AgentReview Task 9 (spec + quality)transcript ↗

You are reviewing one task's implementation (spec compliance + code quality) for the Go CLI ccwhid. Task-scoped gate; a broad whole-branch review happens after this. This is the final implementation task: the Bubble Tea TUI and the cobra RunE wiring.

What Was Requested

Read the task brief: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-9-brief.md

Global constraints binding this task (verbatim):

  • Packages github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/tui; module github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done.
  • TUI browses ALL projects grouped; options screen toggles include-subagents, redact, open, then Generate.
  • Produced API: tui.Selection{Session discovery.SessionInfo; IncludeSubagents, Redact, Open bool; OutDir string; Canceled bool}, tui.Row{IsHeader bool; Label string; Session discovery.SessionInfo}, flattenRows([]discovery.ProjectGroup) []Row, firstSelectable([]Row) int, Run([]discovery.ProjectGroup) (Selection, error); discovery.SessionInfo.DisplayLabel() string.
  • RunE wiring: on the no-selector (needTUI) path, scan → tui.Run → apply Selection to opts (note the mapping opts.noRedact = !sel.Redact) → generate → optionally open; on Canceled, print "canceled" and return nil.
  • Commit trailer: Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>.
  • Go 1.26 builtin min: the implementer omitted the brief's local min and relies on the builtin — this is acceptable.

What the Implementer Claims They Built

Read the implementer's report: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-9-report.md

Diff Under Review

Base: fd591f472cef397c4fdae967b347c4f1c9c18bbb Head: 777b116 Diff file (read once): ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/review-fd591f4..777b116.diff

Read-only review; do not modify the tree. Implementer already ran tests and a --latest smoke test — do not re-run the suite; focused test only on a specific doubt.

Named risks to check:

  1. Selection→opts mapping in RunE: opts.noRedact = !sel.Redact (redact toggle ON must mean noRedact=false). An inversion here would silently disable or force redaction against the user's choice.
  2. Cursor navigation: moveCursor/firstSelectable never land on a header row and never index out of bounds on an all-header or empty list.
  3. Run type assertion: p.Run() result is asserted back to model; confirm the program returns the final model and the assertion can't panic on a normal quit.
  4. Empty groups: Run handles zero groups without panic.
  5. DisplayLabel: falls back Title → FirstPrompt → ID.

Follow the task-reviewer output format: Spec Compliance (✅/❌/⚠️), Strengths, Issues (Critical/Important/Minor with file:line), Assessment (Task quality: Approved | Needs fixes). Your final message IS the report.

Commit trailer confirmed correct. Findings below.

Spec Compliance

  • ✅ Package internal/tui, module path correct.
  • ✅ TUI browses all projects grouped (list screen with per-project headers), options screen has include-subagents / redact / open toggles + Generate action, in that order.
  • ✅ Produced API matches exactly: tui.Selection, tui.Row, flattenRows, firstSelectable, Run, discovery.SessionInfo.DisplayLabel() — all present with the specified field/signature shapes.
  • ✅ RunE wiring: needTUI path scans → tui.Run → applies Selection to opts → generate → optional open; Canceled prints "canceled" and returns nil.
  • ✅ opts.noRedact = !sel.Redact mapping verified correct, not inverted (default Redact: true → noRedact: false → generate runs redact.Session; toggling off flips both correctly).
  • ✅ Commit trailer present verbatim.
  • ✅ Go 1.26 builtin min relied on in place of a local helper, as pre-approved.
  • ⚠️ go.mod/go.sum not fully tidy — bubbletea and lipgloss are directly imported by internal/tui/tui.go but were left in the // indirect require block instead of being promoted to the direct block; go mod tidy (run in an isolated copy, not applied to the tree) also adds a missing golang.org/x/exp go.sum entry. Doesn't affect go build/go test in this environment, but the module metadata is inaccurate. Not called out in the brief's steps (which only ask for go get), so this is a quality gap rather than a spec violation.

Strengths

  • Transcription of the brief's reference implementation is faithful; verified line-by-line against the diff, no accidental deviations beyond the reported min omission.
  • Run() (internal/tui/tui.go:290 region) short-circuits on len(groups) == 0 before ever constructing the Bubble Tea program, correctly avoiding the "all-header, no selectable row" degenerate case for the TUI's only real caller.
  • Confirmed via internal/discovery/discovery.go Scan() that groups are only ever appended when len(sessions) > 0, so in production flattenRows can never produce an all-header row list — the theoretical firstSelectable fallback-to-0 edge case is unreachable through the wired CLI path.
  • moveCursor bounds-checks before dereferencing m.rows[i] and no-ops (keeps current cursor) when it runs off either end — verified no out-of-bounds access for any delta/cursor combination.
  • Run's res.(model) assertion is safe: Update always returns the value-typed m model (never a different concrete type or pointer), so p.Run()'s returned tea.Model is always the same underlying type regardless of which key path (quit, cancel, generate) triggered the return.
  • DisplayLabel() fallback chain (Title → FirstPrompt → ID) matches the brief exactly and is covered by TestDisplayLabel.

Issues

Critical: None.

Important: None.

Minor:

  1. go.mod:13,15 (github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea, github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss marked // indirect) — both are directly imported in internal/tui/tui.go. A go mod tidy run in an isolated copy moves them to the direct require block and adds a missing golang.org/x/exp sum entry to go.sum. No functional impact observed (go build/go vet/go test all clean as-is), but go mod tidy/go mod verify would flag it, and downstream CI that runs go mod tidy --check would fail.
  2. internal/tui/tui.go model.done field is written in three places (updateList's cancel branch, updateOptions's cancel branch, updateOptions's Generate branch) but never read anywhere in Update/View/Run — dead state. Inherited verbatim from the brief's reference code, so not an implementer-introduced defect, but worth flagging since it survived the transcription unquestioned.
  3. cmd/ccwhid/run.go:20 — errNeedTUI (from Task 8) is now an unreferenced package-level var, since this task's run() uses the needTUI bool return from resolveSession instead. Legal Go, doesn't fail build/vet, and out of this task's file scope per the brief, but it's dead code the implementer noticed and chose not to clean up (correctly deferred, per their own report, since Step 7 only touches main.go) — flagging for whoever does the whole-branch pass.

Assessment

Task quality: Approved

The implementation is a faithful, verified-correct transcription of the brief's reference code with no behavioral deviations. All five named risks were checked against the actual diff/build and none materialized: the redact mapping is correct, cursor navigation is bounds-safe and unreachable-degenerate in practice, the type assertion in Run cannot panic under normal Update flow, empty-groups is handled explicitly, and DisplayLabel's fallback order is correct and tested. The only findings are pre-existing/inherited minor tidiness items (go.mod indirect markers, one dead field carried over from the brief, one unused var left from Task 8) — none block this task; they're worth a mention in the whole-branch pass but don't warrant reopening this task. agentId: a519365f4b098044d (use SendMessage with to: 'a519365f4b098044d', summary: '<5-10 word recap>' to continue this agent) subagent_tokens: 63756 tool_uses: 11 duration_ms: 130862

Task 10 Review: End-to-End Verification

Spec Compliance: ✅

All binding constraints satisfied:

  • internal/render/render_e2e_test.go created, package render_test (external), imports github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/... — correct module path.
  • Diff touches exactly one file (49 insertions, internal/render/render_e2e_test.go); git diff 19a5d33e..6678bb5 --stat confirms no changes to README.md, LICENSE, or .gitignore.

Strengths

  • Traced the full pipeline by reading source, not just trusting the report: transcript.decodeMessageContent correctly folds a string content field into a text block (internal/transcript/raw.go:52-53), redact.Session → redactTurn mutates Block.Text and ToolCall.Result.Content in place using pattern [REDACTED:aws-key] (internal/redact/redact.go:20,45-70), and render.renderTool writes tc.Result.Content into <pre class="tool-result"> via html.EscapeString (internal/render/render.go:149-154). Each assertion in the test maps to a real code path, not a tautology.
  • The AWS-key fixture [REDACTED:aws-key] matches the aws-key rule regex (\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b) exactly, and the redaction happens before render.Site is called in the test, mirroring real CLI ordering (redact then render) — so a regression that skipped or reordered redaction would legitimately fail this test.
  • REDACTED:aws-key substring check is safely contained within the actual [REDACTED:aws-key] marker; passed through goldmark markdown rendering without being mangled (confirmed via markdown.go and the existing pattern already used in internal/redact/redact_test.go:23).
  • Ran only the targeted test (go test ./internal/render/ -run TestEndToEndRedactedReport -v) to confirm it passes in isolation, per review-scope instructions — PASS.
  • Test isolation via t.TempDir(), no mocks, exercises real ParseFile → redact.Session → render.Site chain end to end.

Issues

None found — Critical, Important, and Minor: no findings.

Assessment

{
  "file_path": "~/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/6.1.1/skills/requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md"
}
1	# Code Reviewer Prompt Template
2	
3	Use this template when dispatching a code reviewer subagent.
4	
5	**Purpose:** Review completed work against requirements and code quality standards before it cascades into more work.
6	
7	```
8	Subagent (general-purpose):
9	  description: "Review code changes"
10	  prompt: |
11	    You are a Senior Code Reviewer with expertise in software architecture,
12	    design patterns, and best practices. Your job is to review completed work
13	    against its plan or requirements and identify issues before they cascade.
14	
15	    ## What Was Implemented
16	
17	    [DESCRIPTION]
18	
19	    ## Requirements / Plan
20	
21	    [PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS]
22	
23	    ## Git Range to Review
24	
25	    **Base:** [BASE_SHA]
26	    **Head:** [HEAD_SHA]
27	
28	    ```bash
29	    git diff --stat [BASE_SHA]..[HEAD_SHA]
30	    git diff [BASE_SHA]..[HEAD_SHA]
31	    ```
32	
33	    ## Read-Only Review
34	
35	    Your review is read-only on this checkout. Do not mutate the working tree, the index, HEAD, or branch state in any way. Use tools like `git show`, `git diff`, and `git log` to inspect history. If you need a working copy of a different revision, check it out into a separate temporary directory (e.g. `git worktree add /tmp/review-[SHA] [SHA]`) — never move HEAD on this checkout.
36	
37	    ## What to Check
38	
39	    **Plan alignment:**
40	    - Does the implementation match the plan / requirements?
41	    - Are deviations justified improvements, or problematic departures?
42	    - Is all planned functionality present?
43	
44	    **Code quality:**
45	    - Clean separation of concerns?
46	    - Proper error handling?
47	    - Type safety where applicable?
48	    - DRY without premature abstraction?
49	    - Edge cases handled?
50	
51	    **Architecture:**
52	    - Sound design decisions?
53	    - Reasonable scalability and performance?
54	    - Security concerns?
55	    - Integrates cleanly with surrounding code?
56	
57	    **Testing:**
58	    - Tests verify real behavior, not mocks?
59	    - Edge cases covered?
60	    - Integration tests where they matter?
61	    - All tests passing?
62	
63	    **Production readiness:**
64	    - Migration strategy if schema changed?
65	    - Backward compatibility considered?
66	    - Documentation complete?
67	    - No obvious bugs?
68	
69	    ## Calibration
70	
71	    Categorize issues by actual severity. Not everything is Critical.
72	    Acknowledge what was done well before listing issues — accurate praise
73	    helps the implementer trust the rest of the feedback.
74	
75	    If you find significant deviations from the plan, flag them specifically
76	    so the implementer can confirm whether the deviation was intentional.
77	    If you find issues with the plan itself rather than the implementation,
78	    say so.
79	
80	    ## Output Format
81	
82	    ### Strengths
83	    [What's well done? Be specific.]
84	
85	    ### Issues
86	
87	    #### Critical (Must Fix)
88	    [Bugs, security issues, data loss risks, broken functionality]
89	
90	    #### Important (Should Fix)
91	    [Architecture problems, missing features, poor error handling, test gaps]
92	
93	    #### Minor (Nice to Have)
94	    [Code style, optimization opportunities, documentation polish]
95	
96	    For each issue:
97	    - File:line reference
98	    - What's wrong
99	    - Why it matters
100	    - How to fix (if not obvious)
101	
102	    ### Recommendations
103	    [Improvements for code quality, architecture, or process]
104	
105	    ### Assessment
106	
107	    **Ready to merge?** [Yes | No | With fixes]
108	
109	    **Reasoning:** [1-2 sentence technical assessment]
110	
111	    ## Critical Rules
112	
113	    **DO:**
114	    - Categorize by actual severity
115	    - Be specific (file:line, not vague)
116	    - Explain WHY each issue matters
117	    - Acknowledge strengths
118	    - Give a clear verdict
119	
120	    **DON'T:**
121	    - Say "looks good" without checking
122	    - Mark nitpicks as Critical
123	    - Give feedback on code you didn't actually read
124	    - Be vague ("improve error handling")
125	    - Avoid giving a clear verdict
126	```
127	
128	**Placeholders:**
129	- `[DESCRIPTION]` — brief summary of what was built
130	- `[PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS]` — what it should do (plan file path, task text, or requirements)
131	- `[BASE_SHA]` — starting commit
132	- `[HEAD_SHA]` — ending commit
133	
134	**Reviewer returns:** Strengths, Issues (Critical / Important / Minor), Recommendations, Assessment
135	
136	## Example Output
137	
138	```
139	### Strengths
140	- Clean database schema with proper migrations (db.ts:15-42)
141	- Comprehensive test coverage (18 tests, all edge cases)
142	- Good error handling with fallbacks (summarizer.ts:85-92)
143	
144	### Issues
145	
146	#### Important
147	1. **Missing help text in CLI wrapper**
148	   - File: index-conversations:1-31
149	   - Issue: No --help flag, users won't discover --concurrency
150	   - Fix: Add --help case with usage examples
151	
152	2. **Date validation missing**
153	   - File: search.ts:25-27
154	   - Issue: Invalid dates silently return no results
155	   - Fix: Validate ISO format, throw error with example
156	
157	#### Minor
158	1. **Progress indicators**
159	   - File: indexer.ts:130
160	   - Issue: No "X of Y" counter for long operations
161	   - Impact: Users don't know how long to wait
162	
163	### Recommendations
164	- Add progress reporting for user experience
165	- Consider config file for excluded projects (portability)
166	
167	### Assessment
168	
169	**Ready to merge: With fixes**
170	
171	**Reasoning:** Core implementation is solid with good architecture and tests. Important issues (help text, date validation) are easily fixed and don't affect core functionality.
172	```

AgentFinal whole-branch code reviewtranscript ↗

You are a Senior Code Reviewer performing the final whole-branch review of the ccwhid CLI before merge. Per-task reviews already passed; this is the broad, cross-cutting pass.

What Was Implemented

ccwhid (cc-what-have-i-done): a single-binary Go CLI that turns a Claude Code session transcript (.jsonl) into a self-contained static HTML report, styled like Claude's docs. Run with no flags it opens a Bubble Tea TUI to browse and select a session; with flags it renders directly. Pipeline: discovery (find/index sessions) → transcript (fold JSONL into a model.Session timeline, incl. subagent sidechains) → redact (scrub secrets, on by default) → render (goldmark/chroma + embedded template/CSS/JS → index.html + assets). Layers: cmd/ccwhid (cobra + wiring), internal/{model,discovery,transcript,redact,render,tui}.

Requirements / Plan

  • Design spec: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-ccwhid-design.md
  • Implementation plan: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-11-ccwhid.md

Git Range to Review

Base: 2ff127a Head: 6678bb5 Diff package (read once — commit list, stat summary, full diff with context): ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/review-2ff127a..6678bb5.diff

Known Minor findings already triaged (assess whether any should block merge; don't re-litigate as new unless you find them more severe than logged)

  1. redact.go assignment regex over-matches (e.g. MONKEY=, across newlines) — safe over-redaction, never leaks.
  2. discovery.go: no committed regression test for malformed-line tolerance / FindSession ambiguous-vs-not-found (behavior verified correct).
  3. run.go:102 home, _ := os.UserHomeDir() error ignored; matchProject uses broad strings.Contains.
  4. cmd/ccwhid/run.go: errNeedTUI package var is now unreferenced (dead); internal/tui/tui.go: model.done field written but never read (dead).
  5. transcript parallel-Task subagent attribution is a documented v1 limitation (sidechains attach to nearest preceding Task by file order).

Read-Only Review

Read-only on this checkout. Do not mutate the working tree, index, HEAD, or branch. Use git show/diff/log to inspect. The diff package is your primary view; read a full file from the tree only if a hunk you must judge is cut off.

What to Check

Focus on cross-cutting concerns that per-task reviews could miss:

  • Layer boundaries respected (transcript owns JSONL; render consumes model only; tui orchestrates)?
  • Any real correctness bug across the pipeline (e.g. tool_result matching, sidechain attachment, redaction completeness across ALL rendered fields, HTML-injection safety of the generated report)?
  • Error handling and robustness (malformed input never aborts; output dir collision handled)?
  • The generated report's self-containment claim (no network/CDN/external fonts) — verify against the embedded CSS/template/JS.
  • Test quality across the branch (real behavior vs tautology; coverage of the stated global constraints).
  • Any dead code, unused exports, or inconsistencies introduced across tasks.

Output Format

Follow the template: Strengths; Issues (Critical / Important / Minor, each with file:line, what's wrong, why it matters, how to fix); Recommendations; Assessment (Ready to merge? Yes | No | With fixes + reasoning). Be specific and give a clear verdict.

Confirmed all findings against the tree. Build, go vet, and all tests pass. Here is the whole-branch review.

Whole-Branch Review: ccwhid CLI (2ff127a..6678bb5)

Strengths

  • Clean layer boundaries. transcript is the sole owner of the JSONL wire format (raw.go/parse.go); render consumes only model.Session and never imports transcript/discovery; tui orchestrates (discovery + returns a Selection) and contains no parsing or HTML logic. The design's stated boundaries hold in the code.
  • HTML-injection safety is solid. html/template auto-escapes every {{ }} field (including the data-search attribute context). The one hand-built HTML path (renderTool, DiffHTML) routes every dynamic value through html.EscapeString. Markdown bodies go through goldmark with the default Unsafe=false, so raw HTML in transcript text is stripped before being wrapped as template.HTML. This is documented at the Markdown function and is correct.
  • Self-containment claim verified. The template references only assets/styles.css and assets/app.js (both written locally). CSS uses system font stacks (-apple-system, ui-monospace) with no @font-face, no url(), no CDN/import. app.js is dependency-free vanilla JS. Nothing reaches the network — the file:// offline claim is real.
  • tool_result matching is correct across the main chain and sidechains. A single toolIndex keyed by globally-unique tool_use id lets results (including those emitted inside a subagent) attach to the originating ToolCall; testdata/subagent_inner_tool.jsonl exercises exactly this and passes. The pointer aliasing between s.Turns[...].Blocks[i].Tool and the lastTask/sidechainOwner pointer is sound (Block.Tool is a pointer, so Subagents appends are visible in the rendered output).
  • Real behavioral tests, not tautologies: the e2e test (render_e2e_test.go) drives parse→redact→render and asserts the secret is absent AND the redaction marker present AND the tool result survives; rune-safe truncation is checked for validity and ellipsis.

Issues

Important

1. Home-path redaction is bypassed for the project path in the report header. internal/redact/redact.go:55 (Session) only walks s.Turns; it never touches s.ProjectPath, s.Title, or s.GitBranch. The template renders internal/render/assets/report.html.tmpl:31 <span>{{ .Session.ProjectPath }}</span> prominently in the session-head meta. Since ProjectPath is the transcript cwd (e.g. ~/IdeaProjects/...), the OS username is printed verbatim in every report even with redaction on — defeating the design's headline "rewrites the user's home directory to ~" (spec §10) for the most visible path in the whole page. Why it matters: redaction is default-on and advertised; a user committing the report to a repo leaks their home path anyway. Fix: in redact.Session, run r.String() (or at least the home-dir rewrite) over s.ProjectPath, s.Title, and s.GitBranch before rendering.

Minor

2. Subagent.Description is rendered but never redacted. renderTool emits internal/render/render.go:1569 ... subagent: +html.EscapeString(sub.Description). sub.Description is set at parse time from the Task tool's summary (taskDescription → str(in,"description")). redactTool redacts tc.Summary and the subagent Turns, but not tc.Subagents[i].Description (redact.go:1083). So the same text is redacted in the tool header yet emitted raw in the subagent summary. Low leak likelihood (task descriptions rarely hold secrets), but it is a genuine gap in the "all rendered fields" claim. Fix: redact tc.Subagents[i].Description in redactTool.

3. Chroma syntax highlighting is never wired in; Highlight is dead code. render.Highlight (internal/render/markdown.go:32) has zero callers — renderTurnBody sends code through Markdown (plain goldmark), which emits <pre><code class="language-…"> with no colorization. So spec §5 ("fenced code blocks syntax-highlighted via chroma") is unfulfilled, and the entire chroma direct dependency exists only to service an unused exported function. TestMarkdownRendersHeadingAndCode only asserts the code text is present, so it doesn't catch this. Fix: either register a goldmark chroma highlighting extension so code blocks are actually highlighted, or drop Highlight + the chroma dependency and update the design note.

4. SkippedLines is counted but never surfaced. parse.go:73 increments s.SkippedLines, and model.go:32 stores it, but neither run.go/generate nor the template ever reads it. Spec §9 promises malformed lines are "skipped with a counted warning" — the count exists but no warning is ever shown. Fix: print warning: skipped N malformed line(s) from generate when sess.SkippedLines > 0, or render it in the report meta.

5. An over-long line aborts the whole parse (robustness edge). parse.go sets a 64 MiB scanner cap and returns sc.Err(); a single line exceeding it yields bufio.ErrTooLong and fails the entire report, contradicting "never abort the whole report." discovery.indexFile (16 MiB) silently ignores its scanner error, so the two layers also disagree. Rare in practice; worth a follow-up rather than a blocker.

6. Stale comment. parse.go line ~1807: the comment // subByFirstUUID lets sidechain turns append… names a variable that does not exist (the actual var is sidechainOwner). Cosmetic.

On the pre-triaged findings

Re-assessed items 1–5: none rise to blocking. The redact assignment over-match is safe (over-redaction only). The dead errNeedTUI var and unread model.done field (item 4) are confirmed but harmless — fold them into the same cleanup pass as finding #3. The parallel-Task attribution limitation (item 5) is clearly documented at the attachment site and is an acceptable v1 scope call.

Recommendations

  • Fix #1 before merge (small, and it undercuts a default-on advertised feature). Bundle #2 into the same redaction change — both are one-line additions to redactTool/Session.
  • Decide #3 deliberately: wire chroma in or remove it. Shipping an unused syntax-highlighting dependency plus an unmet spec line is the kind of drift that per-task reviews miss.
  • Sweep the confirmed dead symbols (Highlight, errNeedTUI, model.done, stale comment) and surface SkippedLines in one small housekeeping commit.

Assessment

Ready to merge? With fixes.

The architecture is clean, the pipeline is correct on the paths that matter (tool-result matching, sidechain attachment, HTML-escaping, self-containment), and the whole suite builds and passes. The one item I'd gate on is #1 — home-path redaction silently doesn't cover the project-path header, so the default-on redaction promise is materially incomplete for every report. It's a two-line fix. Findings #2–#6 are non-blocking and can land in the same or a follow-up cleanup commit. With #1 (and ideally #2) addressed, this is good to merge. agentId: aee04b4f65e40a812 (use SendMessage with to: 'aee04b4f65e40a812', summary: '<5-10 word recap>' to continue this agent) subagent_tokens: 85919 tool_uses: 7 duration_ms: 167513

The final whole-branch review is thorough and found one genuinely important gap plus a set of cleanups. Most are clear-cut fixes I'll batch into one cleanup commit. One finding is a real product decision I should put to you.

The clear fixes (I'll apply these):

  • Important #1: redact.Session walks turns but never redacts Session.ProjectPath / Title / GitBranch — so the home path (with your username) is printed verbatim in the report header even with redaction on. Two-line fix; it undercuts the default-on redaction promise.
  • #2: Subagent.Description is rendered but not redacted (bundled with #1).
  • #4: SkippedLines is counted but never surfaced — I'll print a warning.
  • #5: an over-long line (>64 MiB) currently aborts the whole parse — I'll make it best-effort per the "never abort" rule.
  • Dead-code sweep: unused errNeedTUI, unread model.done, stale comment.

The decision — finding #3: The design spec (§5) called for chroma syntax-highlighted code blocks, but the implementation renders code as plain (CSS-styled) <pre><code>; the chroma dependency and Highlight() function are pulled in but never wired to goldmark.

Claude
AskUserQuestionSyntax highlighting
Syntax highlighting

The report currently shows code blocks in styled monospace but without per-token syntax coloring, even though the spec asked for chroma highlighting. How should I resolve this before merge?

  • Wire up chroma (deliver spec)

    Add the goldmark-highlighting extension so fenced code blocks get real syntax coloring (self-contained inline styles, still offline). Fulfills spec §5; uses the chroma dep already present. Adds one small dependency.

  • Drop chroma, keep it simple

    Remove the unused chroma dependency and Highlight() function; code renders in clean styled monospace (no per-token color). Fewer deps, smaller binary; update the design note to record this as a v1 simplification.

=== redact.Session + redactTool ===
func Session(s *model.Session, homeDir string) {
	r := New(homeDir)
	for i := range s.Turns {
		redactTurn(r, &s.Turns[i])
	}
}
func redactTool(r *Redactor, tc *model.ToolCall) {
	tc.Summary = r.String(tc.Summary)
	tc.InputJSON = r.String(tc.InputJSON)
	if tc.Result != nil {
		tc.Result.Content = r.String(tc.Result.Content)
	}
	if tc.Diff != nil {
		tc.Diff.OldText = r.String(tc.Diff.OldText)
		tc.Diff.NewText = r.String(tc.Diff.NewText)
		tc.Diff.Path = r.String(tc.Diff.Path)
	}
	for i := range tc.Subagents {
		for j := range tc.Subagents[i].Turns {
			redactTurn(r, &tc.Subagents[i].Turns[j])
		}
	}
}
=== markdown.go top (md var + Highlight) ===
package render

import (
	"bytes"
	"html"
	"html/template"
	"strings"

	chromahtml "github.com/alecthomas/chroma/v2/formatters/html"
	"github.com/alecthomas/chroma/v2/lexers"
	"github.com/alecthomas/chroma/v2/styles"
	"github.com/yuin/goldmark"
	"github.com/yuin/goldmark/extension"

	"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
)

var md = goldmark.New(goldmark.WithExtensions(extension.GFM))

// Markdown renders CommonMark+GFM to HTML. goldmark runs with its default
// Unsafe=false setting, so any raw HTML embedded in the transcript text is
// stripped (replaced with an HTML comment) rather than passed through — the
// rendered Body is therefore safe to emit as template.HTML.
func Markdown(src string) template.HTML {
	var buf bytes.Buffer
	if err := md.Convert([]byte(src), &buf); err != nil {
		return template.HTML("<pre>" + html.EscapeString(src) + "</pre>")
	}
	return template.HTML(buf.String())
}

// Highlight renders a code string with chroma using inline styles (no external
// stylesheet needed). lang may be empty for auto-detection.
func Highlight(code, lang string) template.HTML {
	lexer := lexers.Get(lang)
	if lexer == nil {
		lexer = lexers.Analyse(code)
	}
	if lexer == nil {
		lexer = lexers.Fallback
=== Highlight func ===
func Highlight(code, lang string) template.HTML {
	lexer := lexers.Get(lang)
	if lexer == nil {
		lexer = lexers.Analyse(code)
	}
	if lexer == nil {
		lexer = lexers.Fallback
	}
	style := styles.Get("github")
	if style == nil {
		style = styles.Fallback
	}
	formatter := chromahtml.New(chromahtml.WithClasses(false), chromahtml.TabWidth(2))
	it, err := lexer.Tokenise(nil, code)
	if err != nil {
		return template.HTML("<pre>" + html.EscapeString(code) + "</pre>")
	}
	var buf bytes.Buffer
	if err := formatter.Format(&buf, style, it); err != nil {
		return template.HTML("<pre>" + html.EscapeString(code) + "</pre>")
	}
	return template.HTML(buf.String())
}

=== parse.go 52-66 ===
// Parse reads a transcript stream and folds it into a Session timeline.
func Parse(r io.Reader, opts Options) (model.Session, error) {
	var s model.Session
	// toolIndex maps a tool_use id to the ToolCall pointer so results and
	// sidechains can be attached after the fact.
	toolIndex := map[string]*model.ToolCall{}
	// lastTask tracks the most recent Task tool call for sidechain attachment.
	var lastTask *model.ToolCall
	// subByFirstUUID lets sidechain turns append to the right subagent; keyed
	// by the Task tool that owns them.
	sidechainOwner := lastTask

	sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
	sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 1024*1024), 64*1024*1024)
	for sc.Scan() {
=== parse.go 148-156 ===
				}
			}
		}
	}
	if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
		return s, err
	}
	return s, nil
}

package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"path/filepath"
	"runtime"

)

// errNeedTUI signals that no session selector was provided and the caller
// should launch the interactive browser.
var errNeedTUI = errors.New("no session selected: launch TUI")

func resolveOutDir(opts *options, si discovery.SessionInfo) string {
	if opts.out != "" {
		return opts.out
	}
=== run.go 70-92 ===
func generate(opts *options, si discovery.SessionInfo) (string, error) {
	outDir := resolveOutDir(opts, si)
	if err := ensureOutDir(outDir, opts.force); err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	sess, err := transcript.ParseFile(si.FilePath, transcript.Options{
		IncludeSubagents: opts.includeSubagents,
	})
	if err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	if si.ID != "" {
		sess.ID = si.ID
	}
	if !opts.noRedact {
		home, _ := os.UserHomeDir()
		redact.Session(&sess, home)
	}
	if err := render.Site(sess, outDir, render.Options{Title: opts.title}); err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	return outDir, nil
}

func Session(s *model.Session, homeDir string) {
	r := New(homeDir)
	// Session-level fields are rendered in the report header, so they must be
	// scrubbed too (ProjectPath in particular carries the home path/username).
	s.ProjectPath = r.String(s.ProjectPath)
	s.Title = r.String(s.Title)
	s.GitBranch = r.String(s.GitBranch)
	for i := range s.Turns {
		redactTurn(r, &s.Turns[i])
	}
}

		}
	}

Add this regression test to internal/redact/redact_test.go:

func TestRedactSessionLevelFields(t *testing.T) {
	s := &model.Session{
		ProjectPath: "~/IdeaProjects/app",
		Turns: []model.Turn{{
			Kind: model.TurnAssistant,
			Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: &model.ToolCall{
				Name: "Task",
				Subagents: []model.Subagent{{Description: "run [REDACTED:aws-key]"}},
			}}},
		}},
	}
	Session(s, "~")
	if strings.Contains(s.ProjectPath, "~") {
		t.Errorf("ProjectPath home not rewritten: %q", s.ProjectPath)
	}
	if strings.Contains(s.Turns[0].Blocks[0].Tool.Subagents[0].Description, "AKIA") {
		t.Error("subagent description not redacted")
	}
}

═══════════════════════════════════════════════ FIX 2 (per user decision) — wire chroma syntax highlighting into goldmark; remove dead Highlight() ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ internal/render/markdown.go.

First add the dependency:

go get github.com/yuin/goldmark-highlighting/v2@latest

Replace the import block and the md var. The new import block:

import (
	"bytes"
	"html"
	"html/template"
	"strings"

	chromahtml "github.com/alecthomas/chroma/v2/formatters/html"
	"github.com/yuin/goldmark"
	highlighting "github.com/yuin/goldmark-highlighting/v2"
	"github.com/yuin/goldmark/extension"

	"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
)

// md renders markdown with GFM plus chroma-based syntax highlighting for fenced
// code blocks. Highlighting uses inline styles (WithClasses(false)) so the
// generated report stays fully self-contained — no external stylesheet.
var md = goldmark.New(goldmark.WithExtensions(
	extension.GFM,
	highlighting.NewHighlighting(
		highlighting.WithStyle("github"),
		highlighting.WithFormatOptions(
			chromahtml.WithClasses(false),
		),
	),
))

Note this DROPS the lexers and styles imports (they were only used by Highlight).

DELETE the entire Highlight function (from // Highlight renders a code string... through its closing }). It is dead code now that goldmark highlights fenced code blocks directly.

Leave Markdown and DiffHTML unchanged (they still use bytes/html/template/strings/model).

Update the existing test TestMarkdownRendersHeadingAndCode in internal/render/render_test.go: keep its current assertions and ADD one proving highlighting is now active:

	if !strings.Contains(out, "<span") {
		t.Errorf("expected chroma-highlighted <span> tokens in code output, got %q", out)
	}

(Ensure strings is imported in render_test.go — it already is.)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════ FIX 3 (Minor) — surface SkippedLines as a warning ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ cmd/ccwhid/run.go, in generate, after the block:

	if si.ID != "" {
		sess.ID = si.ID
	}

insert:

	if sess.SkippedLines > 0 {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "warning: skipped %d malformed line(s)\n", sess.SkippedLines)
	}

(fmt and os are already imported.)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════ FIX 4 (Minor, robustness) — an over-long line must not abort the whole parse ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ internal/transcript/parse.go.

Add "errors" to the import block (alongside "bufio").

Replace:

	if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
		return s, err
	}
	return s, nil

with:

	if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
		// A single over-long line (bufio.ErrTooLong) is a pathological record,
		// not a reason to discard the whole report: count it and return what we
		// parsed so far.
		if errors.Is(err, bufio.ErrTooLong) {
			s.SkippedLines++
			return s, nil
		}
		return s, err
	}
	return s, nil

═══════════════════════════════════════════════ FIX 5 (Minor) — stale comment in parse.go ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ internal/transcript/parse.go. Replace:

	// lastTask tracks the most recent Task tool call for sidechain attachment.
	var lastTask *model.ToolCall
	// subByFirstUUID lets sidechain turns append to the right subagent; keyed
	// by the Task tool that owns them.
	sidechainOwner := lastTask

with:

	// lastTask tracks the most recent Task tool call; sidechainOwner is the Task
	// whose Subagents any subsequent sidechain turns are appended to.
	var lastTask *model.ToolCall
	sidechainOwner := lastTask

═══════════════════════════════════════════════ FIX 6 (Minor) — remove dead code: errNeedTUI and model.done ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ cmd/ccwhid/run.go: DELETE the declaration:

// errNeedTUI signals that no session selector was provided and the caller
// should launch the interactive browser.
var errNeedTUI = errors.New("no session selected: launch TUI")

Then REMOVE the now-unused "errors" import from run.go's import block (confirm with grep that errors. appears nowhere else in run.go before removing).

internal/tui/tui.go: DELETE the done bool field from the model struct, and DELETE all three m.done = true statements (each sits right before a return m, tea.Quit — leave the return lines intact). Confirm done is referenced nowhere after removal.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════ VERIFICATION (do this in order) ═══════════════════════════════════════════════

  1. go build ./... — must succeed.
  2. go test ./internal/redact/ -run TestRedactSessionLevelFields -v — new test passes.
  3. go test ./internal/render/ -run TestMarkdownRendersHeadingAndCode -v — passes with the new assertion (proving highlighting is wired).
  4. go test ./... — ALL packages pass.
  5. go vet ./... and gofmt -l internal cmd — both clean (gofmt -l prints nothing).
  6. go mod tidy then confirm go build ./... still succeeds (so the new dep is recorded correctly).

If any step fails, fix it before committing. If a fix instruction conflicts with reality (e.g. an import already present), adapt minimally and note it.

- warn on skipped malformed lines; tolerate over-long lines instead of aborting

You are re-reviewing the consolidated cleanup commit for the ccwhid Go CLI, applied after a whole-branch review. Confirm the fixes are correct and complete, and nothing regressed.

The fixes claimed (commit b2f6fc7)

  1. redact.Session now scrubs ProjectPath, Title, GitBranch (session-level fields rendered in the report header); redactTool now redacts Subagent.Description.
  2. chroma syntax highlighting wired into goldmark via goldmark-highlighting/v2 with inline styles (WithClasses(false)); the dead Highlight() function removed.
  3. generate prints warning: skipped N malformed line(s) to stderr when SkippedLines>0.
  4. parse.go tolerates an over-long line (bufio.ErrTooLong → count as skipped, return partial, no abort).
  5. stale comment fixed.
  6. dead code removed: errNeedTUI var (cmd/ccwhid/run.go) and model.done field (internal/tui/tui.go).

Report

~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/final-cleanup-report.md

Diff Under Review

Base: 6678bb55877f95a4a677c7cfef841cde7e218e7d Head: b2f6fc7 Diff file (read once): ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/review-6678bb5..b2f6fc7.diff

Read-only review; do not modify the tree. The implementer reported go test ./..., go vet ./..., gofmt -l, and go mod tidy all clean — do not re-run the full suite; a focused test only for a specific doubt.

Verify specifically:

  1. Redaction completeness: ProjectPath/Title/GitBranch and Subagent.Description are genuinely redacted; the new test asserts real behavior; no rendered user-visible field is still left unredacted (cross-check what the template/renderTool emit vs what redact walks).
  2. Highlighting self-containment: the goldmark-highlighting config uses INLINE styles (no external stylesheet / class-only mode that would need a missing CSS file); the generated report remains fully offline. Confirm WithClasses(false) is what makes it inline. (I already confirmed there are no http/cdn/@font-face/url() refs in the embedded assets.)
  3. No abort on over-long line: the errors.Is(err, bufio.ErrTooLong) branch returns partial + nil, and SkippedLines is incremented.
  4. Dead code truly gone, no new unused imports (e.g. errors import removed from run.go; lexers/styles removed from markdown.go).

Follow the task-reviewer output format: Spec Compliance (✅/❌/⚠️), Strengths, Issues (Critical/Important/Minor with file:line), Assessment (Task quality: Approved | Needs fixes). Your final message IS the report.

Tree is clean (my temp test files were removed) and modules verify correctly. All four verification points check out with independent confirmation, not just re-reading the diff.

Spec Compliance

  1. Redaction completeness — ✅. Cross-checked model.Session fields against report.html.tmpl and render.go: ProjectPath/GitBranch are rendered directly (.Session.ProjectPath, .Session.GitBranch), and Title reaches the header via DisplayTitle() fallback into d.Title → <h1>{{ .Title }}</h1>. All three are now redacted in Session(). Subagent.Description is rendered in renderTool (<summary>subagent: ...) and is now redacted in redactTool. Session.ID/Version are not rendered anywhere, so leaving them alone is correct, not an omission. New test TestRedactSessionLevelFields exercises real behavior (constructs a Session with ProjectPath and a subagent AKIA... description, asserts both are scrubbed) — not a tautology.
  2. Highlighting self-containment — ✅. WithFormatOptions(chromahtml.WithClasses(false)) is indeed what suppresses class-based output. Verified empirically by rendering a fenced code block through Markdown(): output is <pre style="...">...<span style="color:#1f2328">... — fully inline styles, zero class="chroma" or external stylesheet references. Fully offline.
  3. No abort on over-long line — ✅. Verified with a standalone test (one normal line + one ~70MB line): Parse returns err == nil, SkippedLines == 1, and the earlier turn is retained in the partial result — exactly the claimed partial-result + no-abort semantics.
  4. Dead code removed, no unused imports — ✅. grep -rn "errNeedTUI", grep "\.done\b" in internal/tui, and grep "Highlight(" across the whole repo all return zero hits. go build ./..., go vet ./..., and gofmt -l internal cmd are all clean, which also rules out any leftover unused imports (errors in run.go, lexers/styles in markdown.go). Confirmed at the base commit that Highlight() had no external callers even before removal (it's in an internal/ package), so removing it carries zero regression risk.

Strengths

  • Fix ordering in run.go (generate) is correct: SkippedLines warning is emitted from the pre-redaction sess, and redaction still runs afterward — no interaction bug.
  • The stale-comment fix (Fix 5) actually removes a comment that referenced a nonexistent subByFirstUUID map, replacing it with an accurate description of sidechainOwner.
  • go.sum/go.mod additions are minimal and match exactly what goldmark-highlighting/v2 pulls in; go mod verify passes.
  • Full tree is clean after review; no stray artifacts.

Issues

None found — Critical, Important, and Minor lists are all empty. No unaddressed regressions, no incomplete redaction paths, no reintroduced dead code.

Assessment

Task quality: Approved. All six claimed fixes are present, correct, and independently verified (not just diff-read) — redaction now covers every rendered session-level and subagent field, highlighting is genuinely inline/offline, the parser degrades gracefully on oversized lines instead of discarding the whole session, and both pieces of dead code are fully gone with no residue (no unused imports, no dangling references, go vet/gofmt/build all clean). agentId: ae6e5949b473b0b9e (use SendMessage with to: 'ae6e5949b473b0b9e', summary: '<5-10 word recap>' to continue this agent) subagent_tokens: 57016 tool_uses: 20 duration_ms: 107162

You

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:

  1. Primary Request and Intent: The user wants to build a CLI tool called "cc-what-have-i-done" (short "ccwhid") that transforms a Claude Code session transcript (JSONL) into an easily browsable static HTML artifact. Explicit requirements:

    • Read a specific claude code session transcript and produce browsable HTML showing what the user prompted and what Claude Code did
    • The generated HTML can be included as a documentation artifact in a git project
    • Multiple files OK for structuring, but an index.html must be the entry point
    • Inclusion of subagent session transcripts should be optional
    • CLI configurable through options (session name/id, target name/folder, etc.)
    • Starting the CLI with no options opens a TUI to browse sessions stored in the .claude folder and select one for artifact creation; TUI also lets you choose the other options
    • Styling similar in vibe to Claude artifacts or Claude documentation pages
    • Distributable as a single self-contained binary (Go suggested, open to alternatives)
    • Project folder already created, git init already done

    Follow-up explicit requests: "a git remote is configured, so you can push whenever you like. Also, don't forget to add a .gitignore file suitable for Go projects, and you should also add a README and a MIT license. Let's go with option 1." (Option 1 = subagent-driven development execution.)

    Chroma decision (via AskUserQuestion): user chose to "Wire up chroma (deliver spec)" — real syntax highlighting via goldmark-highlighting extension, fulfilling design spec §5.

  2. Key Technical Concepts:

    • Go 1.26.4 (module github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done, binary at cmd/ccwhid)
    • Claude Code transcript format: JSONL at ~/.claude/projects//.jsonl; encoded-cwd = absolute path with "/" replaced by "-" (LOSSY for paths containing hyphens — real cwd is carried in transcript records' "cwd" field, which is authoritative)
    • Record types: user/assistant (Anthropic message format with content blocks: text/thinking/tool_use/tool_result), ai-title, attachment, last-prompt, mode, permission-mode, file-history-snapshot
    • Subagent sidechains: isSidechain:true, threaded via parentUuid; tool_use ids are globally unique so tool_result matches by id
    • cobra (CLI), Bubble Tea + Lipgloss (TUI), goldmark + goldmark-highlighting/v2 + chroma/v2 (markdown→HTML with syntax highlighting), html/template, go:embed
    • Superpowers skill workflow: brainstorming → writing-plans → subagent-driven-development → finishing-a-development-branch
    • SDD process: fresh implementer subagent per task + task reviewer (spec + quality) + broad final whole-branch review; ledger for durable progress; task-brief/review-package helper scripts
    • Redaction (security): on by default, scrubs AWS keys/tokens/JWTs/KEY=value assignments, rewrites home dir → ~

  3. Files and Code Sections:

    • docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-ccwhid-design.md — approved design doc (committed 70472e8)
    • docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-11-ccwhid.md — 10-task implementation plan with complete code (committed f557296); module path corrected from placeholder github.com/[user]/ccwhid to github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done via perl replace
    • .gitignore, LICENSE (MIT, "Copyright (c) 2026 [user]"), README.md — created during setup
    • .superpowers/sdd/progress.md — durable ledger tracking all 10 tasks complete + final review + roll-up of unfixed minors (gitignored SDD scratch)
    • Package structure (all implemented, tested, reviewed):
      • cmd/ccwhid/main.go (cobra root, options struct with flags: session, project, latest, out, title, include-subagents [default true], no-redact, force, open; run() dispatch to TUI or generate)
      • cmd/ccwhid/run.go (generate, resolveOutDir, resolveSession returns (SessionInfo, needTUI bool, error), latestSession, matchProject, ensureOutDir, openInBrowser)
      • internal/model/model.go (Session{ID,ProjectPath,Title,GitBranch,Version string; StartedAt,EndedAt time.Time; Turns []Turn; SkippedLines int}, Turn, Block, ToolCall, ToolResult, Diff, Subagent; consts TurnUser/TurnAssistant, BlockText/BlockThinking/BlockToolUse; methods Duration(), DisplayTitle())
      • internal/discovery/discovery.go (EncodeProjectDir, DecodeProjectDir [lossy, best-effort], SessionInfo, ProjectGroup, Scan, DefaultRoot, FindSession, DisplayLabel; uses transcript cwd for authoritative ProjectPath)
      • internal/transcript/raw.go (rawRecord, apiBlock, decodeMessageContent, toolResultText, parseTime — unexported)
      • internal/transcript/parse.go (Options{IncludeSubagents}, Parse, ParseFile, buildTurn, buildToolCall; folds JSONL into timeline; tool_use registered in buildTurn so sidechain-internal results attach; tolerates bufio.ErrTooLong)
      • internal/redact/redact.go (Redactor, New, String, Session, redactTurn, redactTool; redacts ALL rendered fields including session-level ProjectPath/Title/GitBranch and Subagent.Description)
      • internal/render/markdown.go (Markdown [goldmark+chroma highlighting, inline styles WithClasses(false), Unsafe=false so raw HTML stripped], DiffHTML; Highlight() removed as dead code)
      • internal/render/render.go (Site, buildViewModel, renderTurnBody, renderTool [html.EscapeString on all dynamic values], preview [rune-safe])
      • internal/render/assets/{report.html.tmpl, styles.css [warm #F5F4EE bg, #D97757 coral accent, light+dark themes], app.js [collapse/filter/theme toggle, vanilla JS]} — all go:embed, no external network refs
      • internal/tui/tui.go (Selection, Row, flattenRows, firstSelectable, Bubble Tea model with list + options screens, Run; uses Go 1.26 builtin min)

    Key fixes' code (Task 5 CRITICAL fix in buildTurn):

    case "tool_use":
    tc := buildToolCall(b)
    toolIndex[tc.ID] = tc  // registers for BOTH main-chain and sidechain
    turn.Blocks = append(turn.Blocks, model.Block{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc})

    Final redaction fix (Session):

    func Session(s *model.Session, homeDir string) {
    r := New(homeDir)
    s.ProjectPath = r.String(s.ProjectPath)
    s.Title = r.String(s.Title)
    s.GitBranch = r.String(s.GitBranch)
    for i := range s.Turns { redactTurn(r, &s.Turns[i]) }
}

  4. Errors and fixes:

    • Wrong module path in plan: I initially used placeholder github.com/[user]/ccwhid; discovered real remote is github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done. Fixed via perl replace across plan file (21 refs).
    • Task 3 lossy encode/decode: DecodeProjectDir(/ ↔ -) mangles hyphenated paths (user's own cc-what-have-i-done). Fixed by using transcript cwd field as authoritative ProjectPath, decode as fallback only (commit 159ee65).
    • Task 5 CRITICAL: subagent-internal tool_use ids never registered → tool results silently dropped for any subagent that runs a tool. Fixed by moving registration into buildTurn (commit 41ed5b8). Parallel-Task attribution documented as known v1 limitation.
    • Task 7 Important: preview()/truncate() byte-index slicing corrupts multi-byte UTF-8 (garbled non-ASCII, relevant for German/international content). Fixed rune-safe in render.preview and discovery.truncate (commit 6d415b6).
    • Final review Important #1: redact.Session never redacted session-level ProjectPath/Title/GitBranch → home path with username leaked in report header despite default-on redaction. Fixed in consolidated commit b2f6fc7.
    • go.mod indirect metadata (cobra, bubbletea/lipgloss): fixed with go mod tidy commits (37ba8bc, 19a5d33).

  5. Problem Solving: Solved: transcript format reverse-engineering; correct subagent nesting; UTF-8 safety; comprehensive redaction coverage; self-contained offline HTML (verified no http/cdn/@font-face/url() refs in embedded assets); chroma highlighting wired with inline styles for offline use. All 10 tasks + final cleanup passed independent adversarial review. Final smoke test rendered a real session (index.html 565KB + assets, redaction active, 38 markers). All roll-up minors documented in ledger as acceptable-v1 backlog (redact assignment-regex over-match; run.go ignored home err + broad matchProject Contains; thin run_test.go coverage; parallel-Task attribution limitation).

Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/tui/tui.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/tui/tui.go"
}
1	// Package tui provides an interactive session browser used when ccwhid is run
2	// with no selector flags.
3	package tui
4	
5	import (
6		"fmt"
7		"strings"
8	
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10		"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
11	
12		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery"
13	)
14	
15	// Row is one line in the browser: either a project header or a session.
16	type Row struct {
17		IsHeader bool
18		Label    string
19		Session  discovery.SessionInfo
20	}
21	
22	// Selection is the result of running the browser.
23	type Selection struct {
24		Session          discovery.SessionInfo
25		IncludeSubagents bool
26		Redact           bool
27		Open             bool
28		OutDir           string
29		Canceled         bool
30	}
31	
32	func flattenRows(groups []discovery.ProjectGroup) []Row {
33		var rows []Row
34		for _, g := range groups {
35			rows = append(rows, Row{IsHeader: true, Label: g.ProjectPath})
36			for _, s := range g.Sessions {
37				rows = append(rows, Row{Session: s})
38			}
39		}
40		return rows
41	}
42	
43	func firstSelectable(rows []Row) int {
44		for i, r := range rows {
45			if !r.IsHeader {
46				return i
47			}
48		}
49		return 0
50	}
51	
52	var (
53		headerStyle   = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#D97757")).MarginTop(1)
54		selectedStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#1A1A18")).Background(lipgloss.Color("#F6E7DF"))
55		mutedStyle    = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("240"))
56		optionStyle   = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#D97757"))
57	)
58	
59	type screen int
60	
61	const (
62		screenList screen = iota
63		screenOptions
64	)
65	
66	type model struct {
67		rows      []Row
68		cursor    int
69		screen    screen
70		sel       Selection
71		optCursor int
72	}
73	
74	func newModel(groups []discovery.ProjectGroup) model {
75		rows := flattenRows(groups)
76		return model{
77			rows:   rows,
78			cursor: firstSelectable(rows),
79			sel: Selection{
80				IncludeSubagents: true,
81				Redact:           true,
82			},
83		}
84	}
85	
86	func (m model) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
87	
88	func (m model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
89		key, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg)
90		if !ok {
91			return m, nil
92		}
93		switch m.screen {
94		case screenList:
95			return m.updateList(key)
96		default:
97			return m.updateOptions(key)
98		}
99	}
100	
101	func (m model) updateList(key tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
102		switch key.String() {
103		case "ctrl+c", "q", "esc":
104			m.sel.Canceled = true
105			return m, tea.Quit
106		case "up", "k":
107			m.moveCursor(-1)
108		case "down", "j":
109			m.moveCursor(1)
110		case "enter":
111			if !m.rows[m.cursor].IsHeader {
112				m.sel.Session = m.rows[m.cursor].Session
113				m.screen = screenOptions
114			}
115		}
116		return m, nil
117	}
118	
119	func (m *model) moveCursor(delta int) {
120		i := m.cursor
121		for {
122			i += delta
123			if i < 0 || i >= len(m.rows) {
124				return // out of bounds; keep current
125			}
126			if !m.rows[i].IsHeader {
127				m.cursor = i
128				return
129			}
130		}
131	}
132	
133	// optionCount is the number of toggle rows plus the Generate action.
134	const optionCount = 4 // subagents, redact, open, generate
135	
136	func (m model) updateOptions(key tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
137		switch key.String() {
138		case "ctrl+c", "q":
139			m.sel.Canceled = true
140			return m, tea.Quit
141		case "esc":
142			m.screen = screenList
143		case "up", "k":
144			if m.optCursor > 0 {
145				m.optCursor--
146			}
147		case "down", "j":
148			if m.optCursor < optionCount-1 {
149				m.optCursor++
150			}
151		case " ", "enter":
152			switch m.optCursor {
153			case 0:
154				m.sel.IncludeSubagents = !m.sel.IncludeSubagents
155			case 1:
156				m.sel.Redact = !m.sel.Redact
157			case 2:
158				m.sel.Open = !m.sel.Open
159			case 3:
160				return m, tea.Quit
161			}
162		}
163		return m, nil
164	}
165	
166	func (m model) View() string {
167		if m.screen == screenOptions {
168			return m.viewOptions()
169		}
170		return m.viewList()
171	}
172	
173	func (m model) viewList() string {
174		var b strings.Builder
175		b.WriteString("Select a session  ↑/↓ move · enter select · q quit\n")
176		for i, r := range m.rows {
177			if r.IsHeader {
178				b.WriteString(headerStyle.Render(r.Label) + "\n")
179				continue
180			}
181			line := fmt.Sprintf("  %s  %s", r.Session.DisplayLabel(), mutedStyle.Render(r.Session.ID[:min(8, len(r.Session.ID))]))
182			if i == m.cursor {
183				line = selectedStyle.Render("▸ " + strings.TrimLeft(line, " "))
184			}
185			b.WriteString(line + "\n")
186		}
187		return b.String()
188	}
189	
190	func (m model) viewOptions() string {
191		var b strings.Builder
192		b.WriteString("Options for: " + m.sel.Session.DisplayLabel() + "\n\n")
193		toggles := []struct {
194			label string
195			on    bool
196		}{
197			{"Include subagents", m.sel.IncludeSubagents},
198			{"Redact secrets", m.sel.Redact},
199			{"Open in browser when done", m.sel.Open},
200		}
201		for i, t := range toggles {
202			check := "[ ]"
203			if t.on {
204				check = "[x]"
205			}
206			line := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s", check, t.label)
207			if m.optCursor == i {
208				line = optionStyle.Render("▸ " + line)
209			} else {
210				line = "  " + line
211			}
212			b.WriteString(line + "\n")
213		}
214		gen := "Generate report"
215		if m.optCursor == 3 {
216			gen = optionStyle.Render("▸ " + gen)
217		} else {
218			gen = "  " + gen
219		}
220		b.WriteString("\n" + gen + "\n\n")
221		b.WriteString(mutedStyle.Render("space toggle · enter confirm · esc back · q quit") + "\n")
222		return b.String()
223	}
224	
225	// Run launches the browser and returns the user's selection.
226	func Run(groups []discovery.ProjectGroup) (Selection, error) {
227		if len(groups) == 0 {
228			return Selection{Canceled: true}, fmt.Errorf("no sessions found under ~/.claude/projects")
229		}
230		m := newModel(groups)
231		p := tea.NewProgram(m)
232		res, err := p.Run()
233		if err != nil {
234			return Selection{}, err
235		}
236		fm := res.(model)
237		return fm.sel, nil
238	}
239
Claude
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery/discovery.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery/discovery.go"
}
1	// Package discovery finds Claude Code session transcripts under
2	// ~/.claude/projects and builds a lightweight index for listing and selection.
3	package discovery
4	
5	import (
6		"bufio"
7		"encoding/json"
8		"fmt"
9		"os"
10		"path/filepath"
11		"sort"
12		"strings"
13		"time"
14	)
15	
16	// SessionInfo is a lightweight index entry for one transcript file. It is built
17	// by a cheap scan and does not parse the full timeline.
18	type SessionInfo struct {
19		ID           string
20		FilePath     string
21		ProjectDir   string // encoded directory name
22		ProjectPath  string // decoded absolute path
23		Title        string
24		FirstPrompt  string
25		ModTime      time.Time
26		MessageCount int
27		HasSubagents bool
28	}
29	
30	// ProjectGroup groups a project's sessions.
31	type ProjectGroup struct {
32		ProjectDir  string
33		ProjectPath string
34		Sessions    []SessionInfo
35	}
36	
37	// EncodeProjectDir maps an absolute cwd to its ~/.claude/projects dir name.
38	func EncodeProjectDir(cwd string) string {
39		return strings.ReplaceAll(cwd, "/", "-")
40	}
41	
42	// DecodeProjectDir maps a projects dir name back to an absolute path.
43	//
44	// This is best-effort and lossy: the encoding used for ~/.claude/projects
45	// directory names replaces every "/" with "-", so a decode cannot distinguish
46	// an original path separator from a literal "-" inside a path segment (e.g.
47	// "cc-what-have-i-done" decodes to "cc/what/have/i/done"). Callers that need
48	// the true path should prefer the "cwd" field recorded in transcript records
49	// and only fall back to this function when no such record is available.
50	func DecodeProjectDir(name string) string {
51		return strings.ReplaceAll(name, "-", "/")
52	}
53	
54	// DefaultRoot returns ~/.claude/projects.
55	func DefaultRoot() (string, error) {
56		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
57		if err != nil {
58			return "", err
59		}
60		return filepath.Join(home, ".claude", "projects"), nil
61	}
62	
63	// scanLine is the subset of fields needed to index a session cheaply.
64	type scanLine struct {
65		Type        string          `json:"type"`
66		AiTitle     string          `json:"aiTitle"`
67		IsSidechain bool            `json:"isSidechain"`
68		Timestamp   string          `json:"timestamp"`
69		Cwd         string          `json:"cwd"`
70		Message     json.RawMessage `json:"message"`
71	}
72	
73	// indexFile reads a jsonl file and produces a SessionInfo without a full parse.
74	func indexFile(path, projectDir string) (SessionInfo, error) {
75		f, err := os.Open(path)
76		if err != nil {
77			return SessionInfo{}, err
78		}
79		defer f.Close()
80	
81		fi, err := f.Stat()
82		if err != nil {
83			return SessionInfo{}, err
84		}
85	
86		info := SessionInfo{
87			ID:          strings.TrimSuffix(filepath.Base(path), ".jsonl"),
88			FilePath:    path,
89			ProjectDir:  projectDir,
90			ProjectPath: DecodeProjectDir(projectDir),
91			ModTime:     fi.ModTime(),
92		}
93	
94		sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
95		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 1024*1024), 16*1024*1024)
96		cwdSet := false
97		for sc.Scan() {
98			var l scanLine
99			if err := json.Unmarshal(sc.Bytes(), &l); err != nil {
100				continue // tolerate malformed lines
101			}
102			switch l.Type {
103			case "ai-title":
104				if l.AiTitle != "" {
105					info.Title = l.AiTitle
106				}
107			case "user", "assistant":
108				info.MessageCount++
109				if l.IsSidechain {
110					info.HasSubagents = true
111				}
112				if l.Type == "user" && info.FirstPrompt == "" {
113					info.FirstPrompt = firstPromptText(l.Message)
114				}
115				if !cwdSet && l.Cwd != "" {
116					info.ProjectPath = l.Cwd
117					cwdSet = true
118				}
119			}
120		}
121		if info.Title == "" {
122			info.Title = truncate(info.FirstPrompt, 60)
123		}
124		return info, nil
125	}
126	
127	// firstPromptText extracts a plain-text preview from a user message whose
128	// content is either a JSON string or an array of blocks.
129	func firstPromptText(raw json.RawMessage) string {
130		if len(raw) == 0 {
131			return ""
132		}
133		var msg struct {
134			Content json.RawMessage `json:"content"`
135		}
136		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &msg); err != nil {
137			return ""
138		}
139		var s string
140		if json.Unmarshal(msg.Content, &s) == nil {
141			return strings.TrimSpace(s)
142		}
143		var blocks []struct {
144			Type string `json:"type"`
145			Text string `json:"text"`
146		}
147		if json.Unmarshal(msg.Content, &blocks) == nil {
148			for _, b := range blocks {
149				if b.Type == "text" {
150					return strings.TrimSpace(b.Text)
151				}
152			}
153		}
154		return ""
155	}
156	
157	func truncate(s string, n int) string {
158		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
159		r := []rune(s)
160		if len(r) <= n {
161			return s
162		}
163		return string(r[:n-1]) + "…"
164	}
165	
166	// Scan indexes every session under root (the projects directory), grouped by
167	// project. Groups are ordered by most-recent session first; sessions within a
168	// group are newest-first.
169	func Scan(root string) ([]ProjectGroup, error) {
170		entries, err := os.ReadDir(root)
171		if err != nil {
172			return nil, err
173		}
174		var groups []ProjectGroup
175		for _, e := range entries {
176			if !e.IsDir() {
177				continue
178			}
179			dir := filepath.Join(root, e.Name())
180			files, err := filepath.Glob(filepath.Join(dir, "*.jsonl"))
181			if err != nil {
182				return nil, err
183			}
184			var sessions []SessionInfo
185			for _, fp := range files {
186				si, err := indexFile(fp, e.Name())
187				if err != nil {
188					continue
189				}
190				sessions = append(sessions, si)
191			}
192			if len(sessions) == 0 {
193				continue
194			}
195			sort.Slice(sessions, func(i, j int) bool {
196				return sessions[i].ModTime.After(sessions[j].ModTime)
197			})
198			groups = append(groups, ProjectGroup{
199				ProjectDir:  e.Name(),
200				ProjectPath: sessions[0].ProjectPath,
201				Sessions:    sessions,
202			})
203		}
204		sort.Slice(groups, func(i, j int) bool {
205			return groups[i].Sessions[0].ModTime.After(groups[j].Sessions[0].ModTime)
206		})
207		return groups, nil
208	}
209	
210	// DisplayLabel returns a human-friendly one-line label for a session.
211	func (s SessionInfo) DisplayLabel() string {
212		if s.Title != "" {
213			return s.Title
214		}
215		if s.FirstPrompt != "" {
216			return s.FirstPrompt
217		}
218		return s.ID
219	}
220	
221	// FindSession resolves a session id or unambiguous prefix across all projects.
222	func FindSession(root, idOrPrefix string) (SessionInfo, error) {
223		groups, err := Scan(root)
224		if err != nil {
225			return SessionInfo{}, err
226		}
227		var matches []SessionInfo
228		for _, g := range groups {
229			for _, s := range g.Sessions {
230				if s.ID == idOrPrefix {
231					return s, nil // exact wins immediately
232				}
233				if strings.HasPrefix(s.ID, idOrPrefix) {
234					matches = append(matches, s)
235				}
236			}
237		}
238		switch len(matches) {
239		case 0:
240			return SessionInfo{}, fmt.Errorf("no session matching %q", idOrPrefix)
241		case 1:
242			return matches[0], nil
243		default:
244			return SessionInfo{}, fmt.Errorf("ambiguous prefix %q matches %d sessions", idOrPrefix, len(matches))
245		}
246	}
247
Claude
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/run.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/run.go"
}
1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"os"
6		"os/exec"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"runtime"
9		"strings"
10	
11		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery"
12		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/redact"
13		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render"
14		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript"
15	)
16	
17	func resolveOutDir(opts *options, si discovery.SessionInfo) string {
18		if opts.out != "" {
19			return opts.out
20		}
21		short := si.ID
22		if len(short) > 8 {
23			short = short[:8]
24		}
25		return filepath.Join("ccwhid-report", short)
26	}
27	
28	// resolveSession picks a session from flags. It returns needTUI=true when the
29	// user gave no selector.
30	func resolveSession(opts *options, root string) (discovery.SessionInfo, bool, error) {
31		switch {
32		case opts.session != "":
33			si, err := discovery.FindSession(root, opts.session)
34			return si, false, err
35		case opts.latest:
36			return latestSession(opts, root)
37		default:
38			return discovery.SessionInfo{}, true, nil
39		}
40	}
41	
42	func latestSession(opts *options, root string) (discovery.SessionInfo, bool, error) {
43		groups, err := discovery.Scan(root)
44		if err != nil {
45			return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, err
46		}
47		for _, g := range groups {
48			if opts.project != "" && !matchProject(g, opts.project) {
49				continue
50			}
51			if len(g.Sessions) > 0 {
52				return g.Sessions[0], false, nil
53			}
54		}
55		return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, fmt.Errorf("no sessions found")
56	}
57	
58	func matchProject(g discovery.ProjectGroup, want string) bool {
59		return g.ProjectPath == want ||
60			strings.EqualFold(filepath.Base(g.ProjectPath), want) ||
61			strings.Contains(g.ProjectPath, want)
62	}
63	
64	// generate parses, redacts, and renders a session, returning the output dir.
65	func generate(opts *options, si discovery.SessionInfo) (string, error) {
66		outDir := resolveOutDir(opts, si)
67		if err := ensureOutDir(outDir, opts.force); err != nil {
68			return "", err
69		}
70		sess, err := transcript.ParseFile(si.FilePath, transcript.Options{
71			IncludeSubagents: opts.includeSubagents,
72		})
73		if err != nil {
74			return "", err
75		}
76		if si.ID != "" {
77			sess.ID = si.ID
78		}
79		if sess.SkippedLines > 0 {
80			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "warning: skipped %d malformed line(s)\n", sess.SkippedLines)
81		}
82		if !opts.noRedact {
83			home, _ := os.UserHomeDir()
84			redact.Session(&sess, home)
85		}
86		if err := render.Site(sess, outDir, render.Options{Title: opts.title}); err != nil {
87			return "", err
88		}
89		return outDir, nil
90	}
91	
92	func ensureOutDir(dir string, force bool) error {
93		entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
94		if err != nil {
95			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
96				return os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755)
97			}
98			return err
99		}
100		if len(entries) > 0 && !force {
101			return fmt.Errorf("output directory %q is not empty (use --force to overwrite)", dir)
102		}
103		return nil
104	}
105	
106	// openInBrowser opens path (a file or dir) in the default browser.
107	func openInBrowser(path string) error {
108		index := filepath.Join(path, "index.html")
109		abs, err := filepath.Abs(index)
110		if err != nil {
111			return err
112		}
113		url := "file://" + abs
114		switch runtime.GOOS {
115		case "darwin":
116			return exec.Command("open", url).Start()
117		case "windows":
118			return exec.Command("rundll32", "url.dll,FileProtocolHandler", url).Start()
119		default:
120			return exec.Command("xdg-open", url).Start()
121		}
122	}
123

Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/main.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/main.go"
}
1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"os"
6	
7		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
8	
9		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery"
10		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/tui"
11	)
12	
13	// options holds all CLI flag values.
14	type options struct {
15		session          string
16		project          string
17		latest           bool
18		out              string
19		title            string
20		includeSubagents bool
21		noRedact         bool
22		force            bool
23		open             bool
24	}
25	
26	func newRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
27		opts := &options{}
28		cmd := &cobra.Command{
29			Use:   "ccwhid",
30			Short: "Turn a Claude Code session transcript into a browsable HTML report",
31			Long: "ccwhid (cc-what-have-i-done) renders a Claude Code session " +
32				"transcript into a self-contained static HTML report. Run with no " +
33				"flags to browse sessions in an interactive TUI.",
34			SilenceUsage: true,
35			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
36				return run(cmd, opts)
37			},
38		}
39		f := cmd.Flags()
40		f.StringVar(&opts.session, "session", "", "session id or unambiguous prefix to render")
41		f.StringVar(&opts.project, "project", "", "project path or name to scope to")
42		f.BoolVar(&opts.latest, "latest", false, "render the most recent session")
43		f.StringVar(&opts.out, "out", "", "output directory (default ./ccwhid-report/<session-short>)")
44		f.StringVar(&opts.title, "title", "", "override the report title")
45		f.BoolVar(&opts.includeSubagents, "include-subagents", true, "include subagent (Task) activity")
46		f.BoolVar(&opts.noRedact, "no-redact", false, "disable secret redaction")
47		f.BoolVar(&opts.force, "force", false, "overwrite a non-empty output directory")
48		f.BoolVar(&opts.open, "open", false, "open the report in a browser when done")
49		return cmd
50	}
51	
52	func run(cmd *cobra.Command, opts *options) error {
53		root, err := discovery.DefaultRoot()
54		if err != nil {
55			return err
56		}
57		si, needTUI, err := resolveSession(opts, root)
58		if err != nil {
59			return err
60		}
61		if needTUI {
62			groups, err := discovery.Scan(root)
63			if err != nil {
64				return err
65			}
66			sel, err := tui.Run(groups)
67			if err != nil {
68				return err
69			}
70			if sel.Canceled {
71				fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "canceled")
72				return nil
73			}
74			si = sel.Session
75			opts.includeSubagents = sel.IncludeSubagents
76			opts.noRedact = !sel.Redact
77			opts.open = sel.Open
78			if sel.OutDir != "" {
79				opts.out = sel.OutDir
80			}
81		}
82		outDir, err := generate(opts, si)
83		if err != nil {
84			return err
85		}
86		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "report written to %s\n", outDir)
87		if opts.open {
88			if err := openInBrowser(outDir); err != nil {
89				fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "warning: could not open browser: %v\n", err)
90			}
91		}
92		return nil
93	}
94	
95	func main() {
96		if err := newRootCmd().Execute(); err != nil {
97			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "error:", err)
98			os.Exit(1)
99		}
100	}
101

These "review" sessions are separate top-level files (not sidechains) — spawned agents that each got their own transcript. I need a reliable marker to distinguish user-initiated "root" sessions from agent-spawned ones. Let me inspect the raw records for distinguishing fields.

Claude

}

Claude4k tok · ~$0.15

I've diagnosed everything. Here's what I found and what I propose.

Findings

--project is effectively broken. It's only consulted inside latestSession(), i.e. it does nothing unless you also pass --latest. Running ccwhid --project foo alone falls through to the default branch in resolveSession (run.go:37) → opens the TUI and ignores --project entirely. That's exactly the "didn't work with name or path" you hit.

The "subagent sessions" are real separate files, not sidechains. Your SDD reviews each ran as SDK-spawned agents that Claude Code wrote as their own top-level transcripts (16 files here; only 1 is yours). They're cleanly distinguishable:

Root (yours) Agent-spawned
entrypoint cli sdk-py
promptSource (absent) sdk

So "root session" = entrypoint not starting with sdk (and promptSource != sdk). That's a reliable filter.

Proposed redesign

TUI navigation (two levels + scrolling):

  1. Opens on a project list only (scrollable viewport, ↑/↓, sized to terminal).
  2. enter / → on a project → session list for that project (scrollable).
  3. ← / esc / h returns to the project list.
  4. Session list shows root sessions only by default; a key toggles all-sessions on/off (I'll use a, shown in the help line). Agent-spawned count shown as a hint (e.g. "+14 agent sessions — press a").
  5. enter on a session → the existing Options screen (subagents/redact/open/generate), unchanged.

Terminal-size-aware viewport via Bubble Tea's WindowSizeMsg, with a scroll window so long lists page properly.

--project fix: improve matching (prefer exact path, then basename, then unambiguous substring) and make it work standalone — see the question below.

I'll also fold the same root/agent classification into discovery so --latest/--project target your sessions by default rather than a review agent's.

One decision I need from you:

Claude
AskUserQuestion--project alone · Root filter scope
--project alone

How should ccwhid --project <name> behave when used alone (no --latest)?

  • Open TUI at that project

    Jump straight into that project's session list in the TUI, skipping the project-picker screen. Most consistent with the new two-level TUI.

    $ ccwhid --project cc-what-have-i-done

[TUI opens directly on the session list
 for cc-what-have-i-done, project step skipped]
  • Render its latest session

    Non-interactive: behave like --latest --project <name> and render that project's most recent root session immediately.

    $ ccwhid --project cc-what-have-i-done
report written to ccwhid-report/41f7e8f4/
  • Error, require --latest

    Print an error telling you to combine --project with --latest or --session. Most explicit, least magic.

    $ ccwhid --project cc-what-have-i-done
error: --project requires --latest or --session
Root filter scope

Should the session list default to your sessions only, or also filter what --latest picks?

  • Both TUI and --latest

    Default everywhere to root (entrypoint=cli) sessions; --latest picks your latest, not a review agent's. Toggle 'a' in TUI reveals agent sessions. Recommended.

    --latest        → your latest session
TUI session list → root only, 'a' shows all
  • TUI only

    Root-only default applies just to the TUI list; --latest keeps current behavior (picks newest file regardless of origin).

    --latest        → newest file (may be an agent)
TUI session list → root only, 'a' shows all
answered
  • --project aloneOpen TUI at that project
  • Root filter scopeBoth TUI and --latest
Claude5k tok · ~$0.22

Both decisions noted: --project alone opens the TUI at that project; root-filtering applies to both the TUI and --latest. Let me check the existing tests so I keep them (or their intent) intact.

Claude

9	func TestFlattenRowsBuildsHeadersAndSessions(t *testing.T) {
10		groups := []discovery.ProjectGroup{
11			{
12				ProjectPath: "/tmp/projA",
13				Sessions: []discovery.SessionInfo{
14					{ID: "s1", Title: "First"},
15					{ID: "s2", Title: "Second"},
16				},
17			},
18			{
19				ProjectPath: "/tmp/projB",
20				Sessions:    []discovery.SessionInfo{{ID: "s3", Title: "Third"}},
21			},
22		}
23		rows := flattenRows(groups)
24		// 2 headers + 3 sessions
25		if len(rows) != 5 {
26			t.Fatalf("got %d rows, want 5", len(rows))
27		}
28		if !rows[0].IsHeader || rows[0].Label != "/tmp/projA" {
29			t.Errorf("row0 = %+v", rows[0])
30		}
31		if rows[1].IsHeader || rows[1].Session.ID != "s1" {
32			t.Errorf("row1 = %+v", rows[1])
33		}
34	}
35	
36	func TestFirstSelectableRow(t *testing.T) {

38		if got := firstSelectable(rows); got != 1 {
39			t.Errorf("firstSelectable = %d, want 1", got)

1	package main

3	import (
4		"os"

7		"testing"
8	
9		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery"
10	)
11	
12	func TestResolveOutDirDefault(t *testing.T) {
13		opts := &options{}
14		si := discovery.SessionInfo{ID: "abcd1234-5678-90ef-0000-000000000000"}
15		got := resolveOutDir(opts, si)
16		if got != filepath.Join("ccwhid-report", "abcd1234") {
17			t.Errorf("resolveOutDir = %q", got)
18		}
19	}
20	
21	func TestGenerateProducesReport(t *testing.T) {
22		// Build a fake transcript file.
23		dir := t.TempDir()
24		src := filepath.Join(dir, "sess.jsonl")
25		lines := strings.Join([]string{
26			`{"type":"ai-title","aiTitle":"Test run"}`,
27			`{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"hello"},"timestamp":"2026-07-11T10:00:00Z"}`,
28			`{"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"world"}]},"timestamp":"2026-07-11T10:00:01Z"}`,
29		}, "\n")
30		if err := os.WriteFile(src, []byte(lines), 0o644); err != nil {
31			t.Fatal(err)
32		}
33		out := filepath.Join(dir, "report")
34		opts := &options{out: out, includeSubagents: true}
35		si := discovery.SessionInfo{ID: "sess", FilePath: src}
36		got, err := generate(opts, si)
37		if err != nil {
38			t.Fatal(err)
39		}
40		if got != out {
41			t.Errorf("generate returned %q, want %q", got, out)
42		}
43		html, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(out, "index.html"))
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatal(err)
46		}
47		if !strings.Contains(string(html), "Test run") || !strings.Contains(string(html), "world") {
48			t.Error("report missing expected content")
49		}
50	}
