{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery/discovery_test.go"

1	package discovery
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"path/filepath"
6		"strings"
7		"testing"
8		"unicode/utf8"
9	)
10	
11	func TestEncodeDecodeRoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
12		// NOTE: deviates from the task brief's literal fixture, which used
13		// "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done" (this repo's own
14		// path). That path contains a literal hyphen in the directory name
15		// ("cc-what-have-i-done"), which the naive '/'<->'-' replace scheme
16		// specified below cannot round-trip: every embedded hyphen gets
17		// decoded back into a '/'. This is a mathematical property of the
18		// scheme, not an implementation bug, so a hyphen-free path is used
19		// here instead to exercise a genuinely invertible case. See the
20		// task report for verification (go run of the exact replace calls
21		// against the original fixture).
22		cwd := "~/IdeaProjects/ccwhid"
23		enc := EncodeProjectDir(cwd)
24		if enc != "-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-ccwhid" {
25			t.Fatalf("EncodeProjectDir = %q", enc)
26		}
27		if dec := DecodeProjectDir(enc); dec != cwd {
28			t.Fatalf("DecodeProjectDir = %q, want %q", dec, cwd)
29		}
30	}
31	
32	// writeSession writes a minimal valid jsonl file with the given lines.
33	func writeSession(t *testing.T, dir, id string, lines ...string) {
34		t.Helper()
35		if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
36			t.Fatal(err)
37		}
38		path := filepath.Join(dir, id+".jsonl")
39		data := ""
40		for _, l := range lines {
41			data += l + "\n"
42		}
43		if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(data), 0o644); err != nil {
44			t.Fatal(err)
45		}
46	}
47	
48	func TestTruncateRuneSafe(t *testing.T) {
49		s := strings.Repeat("ä", 80)
50		got := truncate(s, 60)
51		if !utf8.ValidString(got) {
52			t.Errorf("truncate produced invalid UTF-8: %q", got)
53		}
54	}
55	
56	func TestScanBuildsGroups(t *testing.T) {
57		root := t.TempDir()
58		proj := filepath.Join(root, "-tmp-projA")
59		writeSession(t, proj, "aaaa1111-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
60			`{"type":"ai-title","aiTitle":"Build a parser"}`,
61			`{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"hello"},"timestamp":"2026-07-11T10:00:00Z"}`,
62			`{"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"hi"}]},"timestamp":"2026-07-11T10:00:01Z"}`,
63		)
64		groups, err := Scan(root)
65		if err != nil {
66			t.Fatal(err)
67		}
68		if len(groups) != 1 {
69			t.Fatalf("got %d groups, want 1", len(groups))
70		}
71		g := groups[0]
72		if g.ProjectPath != "/tmp/projA" {
73			t.Errorf("ProjectPath = %q", g.ProjectPath)
74		}
75		if len(g.Sessions) != 1 {
76			t.Fatalf("got %d sessions, want 1", len(g.Sessions))
77		}
78		s := g.Sessions[0]
79		if s.Title != "Build a parser" {
80			t.Errorf("Title = %q", s.Title)
81		}
82		if s.MessageCount != 2 {
83			t.Errorf("MessageCount = %d, want 2", s.MessageCount)
84		}
85	}
86	
87	func TestFindSessionByPrefix(t *testing.T) {
88		root := t.TempDir()
89		proj := filepath.Join(root, "-tmp-projA")
90		writeSession(t, proj, "abcd1111-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
91			`{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"x"},"timestamp":"2026-07-11T10:00:00Z"}`)
92		writeSession(t, proj, " zzzz2222")
93		got, err := FindSession(root, "abcd")
94		if err != nil {
95			t.Fatal(err)
96		}
97		if got.ID != "abcd1111-0000-0000-0000-000000000000" {
98			t.Errorf("ID = %q", got.ID)
99		}
100	}
101	
102	func TestScanUsesTranscriptCwd(t *testing.T) {
103		root := t.TempDir()
104		// Dir name would decode losslessly-wrong; the transcript cwd is authoritative.
105		proj := filepath.Join(root, "-tmp-proj-with-hyphen")
106		writeSession(t, proj, "cccc3333-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
107			`{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"hi"},"cwd":"/tmp/proj-with-hyphen","timestamp":"2026-07-11T10:00:00Z"}`)
108		groups, err := Scan(root)
109		if err != nil {
110			t.Fatal(err)
111		}
112		if len(groups) != 1 {
113			t.Fatalf("got %d groups, want 1", len(groups))
114		}
115		if groups[0].ProjectPath != "/tmp/proj-with-hyphen" {
116			t.Errorf("ProjectPath = %q, want /tmp/proj-with-hyphen (from transcript cwd)", groups[0].ProjectPath)
117		}
118		if groups[0].Sessions[0].ProjectPath != "/tmp/proj-with-hyphen" {
119			t.Errorf("session ProjectPath = %q, want the true cwd", groups[0].Sessions[0].ProjectPath)
120		}
121	}
122	
123	func TestDisplayLabel(t *testing.T) {
124		if got := (SessionInfo{Title: "T"}).DisplayLabel(); got != "T" {
125			t.Errorf("DisplayLabel = %q", got)
126		}
127		if got := (SessionInfo{ID: "id"}).DisplayLabel(); got != "id" {
128			t.Errorf("DisplayLabel fallback = %q", got)
129		}
130	}

- sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
- sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 1024*1024), 16*1024*1024)
- cwdSet := false
- for sc.Scan() {
- var l scanLine
- if err := json.Unmarshal(sc.Bytes(), &l); err != nil {
- continue // tolerate malformed lines
- }
- switch l.Type {
- case "ai-title":
- if l.AiTitle != "" {
- info.Title = l.AiTitle
- }

- info.MessageCount++
- if l.IsSidechain {

- }
+ sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
+ sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 1024*1024), 16*1024*1024)
+ cwdSet := false
+ entrypointSet := false
+ for sc.Scan() {
+ var l scanLine
+ if err := json.Unmarshal(sc.Bytes(), &l); err != nil {
+ continue // tolerate malformed lines
+ }
+ switch l.Type {
+ case "ai-title":
+ if l.AiTitle != "" {
+ info.Title = l.AiTitle
+ }
+ case "user", "assistant":
+ info.MessageCount++
+ if l.IsSidechain {
+ info.HasSubagents = true
+ }
+ // Classify origin: the first record carrying an entrypoint decides
+ // it (interactive "cli"/IDE vs. "sdk-*"), and any prompt sourced
+ // from the SDK confirms an agent-spawned transcript.
+ if !entrypointSet && l.Entrypoint != "" {
+ info.IsAgent = strings.HasPrefix(l.Entrypoint, "sdk")
+ entrypointSet = true
+ }
+ if l.PromptSource == "sdk" {
+ info.IsAgent = true
+ }

Now add FindProject and helper methods to discovery. Let me add after FindSession:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery/discovery.go
- // DisplayLabel returns a human-friendly one-line label for a session.
+ // RootSessions returns the group's interactive (non-agent) sessions, preserving
+ // order.
+ func (g ProjectGroup) RootSessions() []SessionInfo {
+ out := make([]SessionInfo, 0, len(g.Sessions))
+ for _, s := range g.Sessions {
+ if !s.IsAgent {
+ out = append(out, s)
+ }
+ }
+ return out
+ }
+
+ // AgentCount returns how many of the group's sessions were agent-spawned.
+ func (g ProjectGroup) AgentCount() int {
+ n := 0
+ for _, s := range g.Sessions {
+ if s.IsAgent {
+ n++
+ }
+ }
+ return n
+ }
+
+ // FindProject resolves a project selector (full path, basename, or unambiguous
+ // substring of the path) to an index into groups. It errors if nothing matches
+ // or the selector is ambiguous.
+ func FindProject(groups []ProjectGroup, want string) (int, error) {
+ // 1. Exact absolute-path match.
+ for i, g := range groups {
+ if g.ProjectPath == want {
+ return i, nil
+ }
+ }
+ // 2. Case-insensitive basename match.
+ var matches []int
+ for i, g := range groups {
+ if strings.EqualFold(filepath.Base(g.ProjectPath), want) {
+ matches = append(matches, i)
+ }
+ }
+ // 3. Fall back to a case-insensitive substring of the full path.
+ if len(matches) == 0 {
+ lw := strings.ToLower(want)
+ for i, g := range groups {
+ if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(g.ProjectPath), lw) {
+ matches = append(matches, i)
+ }
+ }
+ }
+ switch len(matches) {
+ case 0:
+ return -1, fmt.Errorf("no project matching %q", want)
+ case 1:
+ return matches[0], nil
+ default:
+ return -1, fmt.Errorf("ambiguous project %q matches %d projects", want, len(matches))
+ }
+ }
+
+ // DisplayLabel returns a human-friendly one-line label for a session.

Now the core rewrite — tui.go with two-level navigation, a scrolling viewport, and the root/all toggle:

Claude
Write~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/tui/tui.go
+ // Package tui provides an interactive session browser used when ccwhid is run
+ // with no selector flags. It presents a two-level view: a scrollable list of
+ // projects, then a scrollable list of that project's sessions.
+ package tui
+
+ import (
+ "fmt"
+ "strings"
+
+ tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
+ "github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
+
+ "github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery"
+ )
+
+ // Selection is the result of running the browser.
+ type Selection struct {
+ Session discovery.SessionInfo
+ IncludeSubagents bool
+ Redact bool
+ Open bool
+ OutDir string
+ Canceled bool
+ }
+
+ var (
+ titleStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true)
+ headerStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#D97757"))
+ selectedStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#1A1A18")).Background(lipgloss.Color("#F6E7DF"))
+ mutedStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("240"))
+ optionStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#D97757"))
+ )
+
+ type screen int
+
+ const (
+ screenProjects screen = iota
+ screenSessions
+ screenOptions
+ )
+
+ // reservedLines is how many rows the title and footer occupy, leaving the rest
+ // of the terminal height for the scrollable list body.
+ const reservedLines = 4
+
+ type model struct {
+ groups []discovery.ProjectGroup
+
+ // projects screen
+ projCursor int
+ projTop int
+
+ // sessions screen
+ groupIdx int
+ showAll bool
+ sessions []discovery.SessionInfo // filtered view of groups[groupIdx]
+ sessCursor int
+ sessTop int
+
+ // options screen
+ optCursor int
+
+ screen screen
+ sel Selection
+
+ height int
+ width int
+ }
+
+ func newModel(groups []discovery.ProjectGroup) model {
+ return model{
+ groups: groups,
+ screen: screenProjects,
+ height: 20,
+ width: 80,
+ sel: Selection{
+ IncludeSubagents: true,
+ Redact: true,
+ },
+ }
+ }
+
+ // listHeight is the number of list rows visible in the current terminal.
+ func (m model) listHeight() int {
+ h := m.height - reservedLines
+ if h < 1 {
+ return 1
+ }
+ return h
+ }
+
+ func (m model) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
+
+ func (m model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
+ switch msg := msg.(type) {
+ case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
+ m.height = msg.Height
+ m.width = msg.Width
+ m.adjustProjScroll()
+ m.adjustSessScroll()
+ return m, nil
+ case tea.KeyMsg:
+ switch m.screen {
+ case screenProjects:
+ return m.updateProjects(msg)
+ case screenSessions:
+ return m.updateSessions(msg)
+ default:
+ return m.updateOptions(msg)
+ }
+ }
+ return m, nil
+ }
+
+ // --- projects screen ---
+
+ func (m model) updateProjects(key tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
+ switch key.String() {
+ case "ctrl+c", "q", "esc":
+ m.sel.Canceled = true
+ return m, tea.Quit
+ case "up", "k":
+ if m.projCursor > 0 {
+ m.projCursor--
+ m.adjustProjScroll()
+ }
+ case "down", "j":
+ if m.projCursor < len(m.groups)-1 {
+ m.projCursor++
+ m.adjustProjScroll()
+ }
+ case "enter", "right", "l":
+ if len(m.groups) > 0 {
+ m.enterProject(m.projCursor)
+ }
+ }
+ return m, nil
+ }
+
+ func (m *model) adjustProjScroll() {
+ m.projTop = clampScroll(m.projCursor, m.projTop, m.listHeight(), len(m.groups))
+ }
+
+ func (m *model) enterProject(idx int) {
+ m.groupIdx = idx
+ m.showAll = false
+ m.sessCursor = 0
+ m.sessTop = 0
+ m.refreshSessions()
+ m.screen = screenSessions
+ }
+
+ func (m *model) refreshSessions() {
+ g := m.groups[m.groupIdx]
+ if m.showAll {
+ m.sessions = g.Sessions
+ } else {
+ m.sessions = g.RootSessions()
+ }
+ if m.sessCursor >= len(m.sessions) {
+ m.sessCursor = max(0, len(m.sessions)-1)
+ }
+ m.adjustSessScroll()
+ }
+
+ // --- sessions screen ---
+
+ func (m model) updateSessions(key tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
+ switch key.String() {
+ case "ctrl+c", "q":
+ m.sel.Canceled = true
+ return m, tea.Quit
+ case "esc", "left", "h":
+ m.screen = screenProjects
+ case "up", "k":
+ if m.sessCursor > 0 {
+ m.sessCursor--
+ m.adjustSessScroll()
+ }
+ case "down", "j":
+ if m.sessCursor < len(m.sessions)-1 {
+ m.sessCursor++
+ m.adjustSessScroll()
+ }
+ case "a":
+ m.showAll = !m.showAll
+ m.sessCursor = 0
+ m.sessTop = 0
+ m.refreshSessions()
+ case "enter", "right", "l":
+ if len(m.sessions) > 0 {
+ m.sel.Session = m.sessions[m.sessCursor]
+ m.optCursor = 0
+ m.screen = screenOptions
+ }
+ }
+ return m, nil
+ }
+
+ func (m *model) adjustSessScroll() {
+ m.sessTop = clampScroll(m.sessCursor, m.sessTop, m.listHeight(), len(m.sessions))
+ }
+
+ // --- options screen ---
+
+ // optionCount is the number of toggle rows plus the Generate action.
+ const optionCount = 4 // subagents, redact, open, generate
+
+ func (m model) updateOptions(key tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
+ switch key.String() {
+ case "ctrl+c", "q":
+ m.sel.Canceled = true
+ return m, tea.Quit
+ case "esc", "left", "h":
+ m.screen = screenSessions
+ case "up", "k":
+ if m.optCursor > 0 {
+ m.optCursor--
+ }
+ case "down", "j":
+ if m.optCursor < optionCount-1 {
+ m.optCursor++
+ }
+ case " ", "enter":
+ switch m.optCursor {
+ case 0:
+ m.sel.IncludeSubagents = !m.sel.IncludeSubagents
+ case 1:
+ m.sel.Redact = !m.sel.Redact
+ case 2:
+ m.sel.Open = !m.sel.Open
+ case 3:
+ return m, tea.Quit
+ }
+ }
+ return m, nil
+ }
+
+ // clampScroll returns a scroll offset that keeps cursor within the visible
+ // window [top, top+visible) over a list of length n.
+ func clampScroll(cursor, top, visible, n int) int {
+ if visible < 1 {
+ visible = 1
+ }
+ if cursor < top {
+ top = cursor
+ }
+ if cursor >= top+visible {
+ top = cursor - visible + 1
+ }
+ if top < 0 {
+ top = 0
+ }
+ // Don't scroll past the end when the list shrank.
+ if max := n - visible; top > max && max >= 0 {
+ top = max
+ }
+ return top
+ }
+
+ func (m model) View() string {
+ switch m.screen {
+ case screenProjects:
+ return m.viewProjects()
+ case screenSessions:
+ return m.viewSessions()
+ default:
+ return m.viewOptions()
+ }
+ }
+
+ func (m model) viewProjects() string {
+ var b strings.Builder
+ b.WriteString(titleStyle.Render("Select a project") +
+ mutedStyle.Render(" ↑/↓ move · enter open · q quit") + "\n\n")
+ visible := m.listHeight()
+ end := min(m.projTop+visible, len(m.groups))
+ for i := m.projTop; i < end; i++ {
+ g := m.groups[i]
+ roots := len(g.RootSessions())
+ agents := g.AgentCount()
+ count := fmt.Sprintf("%d session%s", roots, plural(roots))
+ if agents > 0 {
+ count += fmt.Sprintf(", %d agent", agents)
+ }
+ label := headerStyle.Render(g.ProjectPath) + " " + mutedStyle.Render(count)
+ if i == m.projCursor {
+ label = selectedStyle.Render("▸ " + g.ProjectPath + " " + count)
+ } else {
+ label = " " + label
+ }
+ b.WriteString(label + "\n")
+ }
+ b.WriteString(scrollHint(m.projTop, end, len(m.groups)))
+ return b.String()
+ }
+
+ func (m model) viewSessions() string {
+ var b strings.Builder
+ g := m.groups[m.groupIdx]
+ b.WriteString(titleStyle.Render("Sessions in ") + headerStyle.Render(g.ProjectPath) + "\n")
+ toggle := "a show all"
+ if m.showAll {
+ toggle = "a root only"
+ }
+ b.WriteString(mutedStyle.Render("↑/↓ move · enter select · ← back · "+toggle+" · q quit") + "\n\n")
+
+ if len(m.sessions) == 0 {
+ b.WriteString(mutedStyle.Render(" no interactive sessions here — press a to show agent sessions") + "\n")
+ return b.String()
+ }
+ visible := m.listHeight()
+ end := min(m.sessTop+visible, len(m.sessions))
+ for i := m.sessTop; i < end; i++ {
+ s := m.sessions[i]
+ id := s.ID[:min(8, len(s.ID))]
+ tag := ""
+ if s.IsAgent {
+ tag = mutedStyle.Render(" ⟲")
+ }
+ line := fmt.Sprintf(" %s %s%s", s.DisplayLabel(), mutedStyle.Render(id), tag)
+ if i == m.sessCursor {
+ line = selectedStyle.Render("▸ "+s.DisplayLabel()+" "+id) + tag
+ }
+ b.WriteString(line + "\n")
+ }
+ b.WriteString(scrollHint(m.sessTop, end, len(m.sessions)))
+ return b.String()
+ }
+
+ func (m model) viewOptions() string {
+ var b strings.Builder
+ b.WriteString("Options for: " + m.sel.Session.DisplayLabel() + "\n\n")
+ toggles := []struct {
+ label string
+ on bool
+ }{
+ {"Include subagents", m.sel.IncludeSubagents},
+ {"Redact secrets", m.sel.Redact},
+ {"Open in browser when done", m.sel.Open},
+ }
+ for i, t := range toggles {
+ check := "[ ]"
+ if t.on {
+ check = "[x]"
+ }
+ line := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s", check, t.label)
+ if m.optCursor == i {
+ line = optionStyle.Render("▸ " + line)
+ } else {
+ line = " " + line
+ }
+ b.WriteString(line + "\n")
+ }
+ gen := "Generate report"
+ if m.optCursor == 3 {
+ gen = optionStyle.Render("▸ " + gen)
+ } else {
+ gen = " " + gen
+ }
+ b.WriteString("\n" + gen + "\n\n")
+ b.WriteString(mutedStyle.Render("space toggle · enter confirm · esc back · q quit") + "\n")
+ return b.String()
+ }
+
+ // scrollHint renders a one-line position indicator when the list is scrolled or
+ // overflows the viewport.
+ func scrollHint(top, end, n int) string {
+ if top == 0 && end >= n {
+ return ""
+ }
+ return mutedStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("\n %d–%d of %d", top+1, end, n))
+ }
+
+ func plural(n int) string {
+ if n == 1 {
+ return ""
+ }
+ return "s"
+ }
+
+ // Run launches the browser and returns the user's selection. If focusIdx is a
+ // valid index into groups, the browser opens directly on that project's session
+ // list; pass -1 to start on the project list.
+ func Run(groups []discovery.ProjectGroup, focusIdx int) (Selection, error) {
+ if len(groups) == 0 {
+ return Selection{Canceled: true}, fmt.Errorf("no sessions found under ~/.claude/projects")
+ }
+ m := newModel(groups)
+ if focusIdx >= 0 && focusIdx < len(groups) {
+ m.projCursor = focusIdx
+ m.enterProject(focusIdx)
+ }
+ p := tea.NewProgram(m, tea.WithAltScreen())
+ res, err := p.Run()
+ if err != nil {
+ return Selection{}, err
+ }
+ fm := res.(model)
+ return fm.sel, nil
+ }
+

Claude896 tok · ~$0.08
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/run.go
- func latestSession(opts *options, root string) (discovery.SessionInfo, bool, error) {
- groups, err := discovery.Scan(root)
- if err != nil {
- return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, err
- }
- for _, g := range groups {
- if opts.project != "" && !matchProject(g, opts.project) {
- continue
- }
- if len(g.Sessions) > 0 {
- return g.Sessions[0], false, nil
- }
- }
- return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, fmt.Errorf("no sessions found")
- }
-
- func matchProject(g discovery.ProjectGroup, want string) bool {
- return g.ProjectPath == want ||
- strings.EqualFold(filepath.Base(g.ProjectPath), want) ||
- strings.Contains(g.ProjectPath, want)
- }
+ // latestSession returns the most recent interactive (non-agent) session,
+ // optionally scoped to a project. Agent-spawned transcripts are skipped so
+ // --latest targets the user's own sessions.
+ func latestSession(opts *options, root string) (discovery.SessionInfo, bool, error) {
+ groups, err := discovery.Scan(root)
+ if err != nil {
+ return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, err
+ }
+ if opts.project != "" {
+ idx, err := discovery.FindProject(groups, opts.project)
+ if err != nil {
+ return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, err
+ }
+ groups = groups[idx : idx+1]
+ }
+ for _, g := range groups {
+ for _, s := range g.Sessions {
+ if !s.IsAgent {
+ return s, false, nil
+ }
+ }
+ }
+ return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, fmt.Errorf("no sessions found")
+ }

Build clean. Now fix tui_test.go (references removed functions) and add new coverage:

Claude
Write~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/tui/tui_test.go
+ package tui
+
+ import (
+ "testing"
+
+ tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
+
+ "github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery"
+ )
+
+ func testGroups() []discovery.ProjectGroup {
+ return []discovery.ProjectGroup{
+ {
+ ProjectPath: "/tmp/projA",
+ Sessions: []discovery.SessionInfo{
+ {ID: "root1aaa-0000", Title: "Root one"},
+ {ID: "agent1aa-0000", Title: "Agent one", IsAgent: true},
+ {ID: "root2aaa-0000", Title: "Root two"},
+ },
+ },
+ {
+ ProjectPath: "/tmp/projB",
+ Sessions: []discovery.SessionInfo{{ID: "root3bbb-0000", Title: "Root three"}},
+ },
+ }
+ }
+
+ func key(s string) tea.KeyMsg {
+ switch s {
+ case "enter":
+ return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyEnter}
+ case "left":
+ return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyLeft}
+ case "down":
+ return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyDown}
+ case "up":
+ return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyUp}
+ default:
+ return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRunes, Runes: []rune(s)}
+ }
+ }
+
+ func send(m model, msgs ...tea.Msg) model {
+ var tm tea.Model = m
+ for _, msg := range msgs {
+ tm, _ = tm.Update(msg)
+ }
+ return tm.(model)
+ }
+
+ func TestSessionListHidesAgentsByDefault(t *testing.T) {
+ m := newModel(testGroups())
+ m = send(m, key("enter")) // open projA
+ if m.screen != screenSessions {
+ t.Fatalf("screen = %v, want sessions", m.screen)
+ }
+ if len(m.sessions) != 2 {
+ t.Fatalf("root sessions = %d, want 2 (agents hidden)", len(m.sessions))
+ }
+ for _, s := range m.sessions {
+ if s.IsAgent {
+ t.Errorf("agent session %q leaked into default list", s.ID)
+ }
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestToggleShowAllRevealsAgents(t *testing.T) {
+ m := newModel(testGroups())
+ m = send(m, key("enter"), key("a"))
+ if !m.showAll {
+ t.Fatal("showAll not toggled")
+ }
+ if len(m.sessions) != 3 {
+ t.Fatalf("all sessions = %d, want 3", len(m.sessions))
+ }
+ m = send(m, key("a"))
+ if m.showAll || len(m.sessions) != 2 {
+ t.Fatalf("toggle back failed: showAll=%v n=%d", m.showAll, len(m.sessions))
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestLeftReturnsToProjectList(t *testing.T) {
+ m := newModel(testGroups())
+ m = send(m, key("enter"))
+ if m.screen != screenSessions {
+ t.Fatalf("screen = %v, want sessions", m.screen)
+ }
+ m = send(m, key("left"))
+ if m.screen != screenProjects {
+ t.Fatalf("screen = %v, want projects after left", m.screen)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestSelectSessionGoesToOptions(t *testing.T) {
+ m := newModel(testGroups())
+ m = send(m, key("enter"), key("enter")) // open projA, select first session
+ if m.screen != screenOptions {
+ t.Fatalf("screen = %v, want options", m.screen)
+ }
+ if m.sel.Session.ID != "root1aaa-0000" {
+ t.Errorf("selected %q, want root1aaa-0000", m.sel.Session.ID)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestScrollKeepsCursorVisible(t *testing.T) {
+ // 30 sessions, viewport of 10 rows.
+ var ss []discovery.SessionInfo
+ for i := 0; i < 30; i++ {
+ ss = append(ss, discovery.SessionInfo{ID: string(rune('a'+i%26)) + "0000000", Title: "s"})
+ }
+ m := newModel([]discovery.ProjectGroup{{ProjectPath: "/p", Sessions: ss}})
+ m = send(m, tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: 80, Height: reservedLines + 10}, key("enter"))
+ for i := 0; i < 25; i++ {
+ m = send(m, key("down"))
+ }
+ if m.sessCursor != 25 {
+ t.Fatalf("cursor = %d, want 25", m.sessCursor)
+ }
+ // cursor must be within the visible window [top, top+10)
+ if m.sessCursor < m.sessTop || m.sessCursor >= m.sessTop+m.listHeight() {
+ t.Errorf("cursor %d outside window [%d,%d)", m.sessCursor, m.sessTop, m.sessTop+m.listHeight())
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestFocusIdxOpensSessionList(t *testing.T) {
+ m := newModel(testGroups())
+ m.projCursor = 1
+ m.enterProject(1)
+ if m.screen != screenSessions {
+ t.Fatalf("screen = %v, want sessions", m.screen)
+ }
+ if m.sessions[0].ID != "root3bbb-0000" {
+ t.Errorf("focused group session = %q, want root3bbb-0000", m.sessions[0].ID)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestClampScroll(t *testing.T) {
+ // cursor below window scrolls down
+ if got := clampScroll(12, 0, 10, 30); got != 3 {
+ t.Errorf("clampScroll down = %d, want 3", got)
+ }
+ // cursor above window scrolls up
+ if got := clampScroll(2, 5, 10, 30); got != 2 {
+ t.Errorf("clampScroll up = %d, want 2", got)
+ }
+ // list shorter than window pins to 0
+ if got := clampScroll(0, 4, 10, 3); got != 0 {
+ t.Errorf("clampScroll short = %d, want 0", got)
+ }
+ }
+

Now add discovery tests for agent classification and FindProject:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery/discovery_test.go
- func TestDisplayLabel(t *testing.T) {
+ func TestScanClassifiesAgentSessions(t *testing.T) {
+ root := t.TempDir()
+ proj := filepath.Join(root, "-tmp-projA")
+ // Interactive session: entrypoint cli, no promptSource.
+ writeSession(t, proj, "aaaa1111-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
+ `{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"hi"},"entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/tmp/projA","timestamp":"2026-07-11T10:00:00Z"}`)
+ // Agent session: entrypoint sdk-py + promptSource sdk.
+ writeSession(t, proj, "bbbb2222-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
+ `{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"review"},"entrypoint":"sdk-py","promptSource":"sdk","cwd":"/tmp/projA","timestamp":"2026-07-11T11:00:00Z"}`)
+ groups, err := Scan(root)
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ byID := map[string]SessionInfo{}
+ for _, s := range groups[0].Sessions {
+ byID[s.ID[:4]] = s
+ }
+ if byID["aaaa"].IsAgent {
+ t.Error("cli session wrongly classified as agent")
+ }
+ if !byID["bbbb"].IsAgent {
+ t.Error("sdk session not classified as agent")
+ }
+ if got := groups[0].AgentCount(); got != 1 {
+ t.Errorf("AgentCount = %d, want 1", got)
+ }
+ if got := len(groups[0].RootSessions()); got != 1 {
+ t.Errorf("RootSessions = %d, want 1", got)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestFindProject(t *testing.T) {
+ groups := []ProjectGroup{
+ {ProjectPath: "/Users/[user]/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done"},
+ {ProjectPath: "/Users/[user]/IdeaProjects/other-app"},
+ {ProjectPath: "/Users/[user]/Downloads/apparatus"},
+ }
+ cases := []struct {
+ want string
+ wantIdx int
+ wantErr bool
+ }{
+ {"/Users/[user]/IdeaProjects/other-app", 1, false}, // exact path
+ {"cc-what-have-i-done", 0, false}, // basename
+ {"OTHER-APP", 1, false}, // basename, case-insensitive
+ {"Downloads", 2, false}, // substring
+ {"nope", -1, true}, // no match
+ {"IdeaProjects", -1, true}, // ambiguous substring
+ }
+ for _, c := range cases {
+ idx, err := FindProject(groups, c.want)
+ if c.wantErr {
+ if err == nil {
+ t.Errorf("FindProject(%q) = %d, want error", c.want, idx)
+ }
+ continue
+ }
+ if err != nil || idx != c.wantIdx {
+ t.Errorf("FindProject(%q) = %d, %v; want %d", c.want, idx, err, c.wantIdx)
+ }
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestDisplayLabel(t *testing.T) {

Everything works — it correctly picked the cli root session (41f7e8f4), not an agent review session, and the error paths are clean. The interactive TUI needs a real TTY so I can't drive it headless, but its navigation/filtering/scroll logic is unit-tested.

Let me update the README to document the new behavior:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md
- The report is written to `./ccwhid-report/<session-short>/` by default (override
- with `--out`). Open `index.html` in any browser — no server needed.
+ The report is written to `./ccwhid-report/<session-short>/` by default (override
+ with `--out`). Open `index.html` in any browser — no server needed.
+
+ ### Browsing sessions
+
+ Run `ccwhid` with no selector to open the interactive browser:
+
+ - **Project list** — every project with sessions, most-recent first. `↑`/`↓`
+ to move, `enter` (or `→`) to open a project, `q` to quit.
+ - **Session list** — the chosen project's sessions. `enter` selects, `←` (or
+ `esc`) returns to the project list.
+ - Both lists **scroll** to fit any terminal size.
+ - By default only your **interactive sessions** are shown. Claude Code writes
+ each Task subagent / code-review agent as its own transcript; press **`a`**
+ in the session list to show (and hide) those agent sessions — they're marked
+ with a `⟲`.
+
+ `--project <name>` opens the browser directly on that project's session list.

- | `--latest` | Render the most recent session |
+ | `--latest` | Render the most recent interactive session (skips agent transcripts) |

Claude596 tok · ~$0.08

Done. The TUI redesign, agent-session filtering, and --project fix are implemented, tested, committed, and pushed to origin/feat/ccwhid-impl.

What changed:

  • Scrollable two-level browser — a project list, then a per-project session list. Both viewports size to your terminal and scroll to keep the cursor in view. enter/→ to descend, ←/esc to go back, q to quit.
  • Agent sessions hidden by default — your SDD review agents are separate SDK-spawned transcripts (entrypoint: sdk-py, promptSource: sdk). They're now filtered out of the default list; press a to toggle them (marked ⟲).
  • --project fixed — it was silently ignored unless paired with --latest. Now --project foo alone opens the TUI on that project, matching by full path, basename, or unambiguous substring (with clear ambiguous/no-match errors). --latest also skips agent transcripts now.

Tests pass (all 7 packages), vet/gofmt clean, and a real --latest --project cc-what-have-i-done correctly rendered your cli root session rather than a review agent.

One note: I implemented this inline rather than through the SDD subagent + reviewer loop the rest of the branch went through. It's unit-tested and smoke-tested, but not independently reviewed.

Back to finishing the branch — what would you like to do?

  1. Merge back to main locally
  2. Push and create a Pull Request (branch already pushed)
  3. Keep the branch as-is
  4. Discard this work

And separately: want me to run an independent code review of this last change before you decide? I'd recommend it, since it skipped the review loop.

You

Yes, please do another code review.

Claude2k tok · ~$0.12
AgentReview TUI redesign changetranscript ↗

You are performing a focused code review of a single commit in a Go CLI project (ccwhid, module github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done). The commit reworks the interactive TUI and fixes a --project flag. Your job: find real defects — correctness bugs, edge cases, logic errors, security issues, test gaps — and verify each against the actual code. Do NOT nitpick style. Report only issues you can substantiate.

The change under review

Read the review package (commit list, stat, full diff with context): /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done/41f7e8f4-aaf3-4cc6-b237-7184bd309128/scratchpad/review-tui-0f130bc.txt

Then read the current full files as needed to verify (do not trust the diff alone):

  • ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/tui/tui.go
  • ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/tui/tui_test.go
  • ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery/discovery.go
  • ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery/discovery_test.go
  • ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/main.go
  • ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/run.go

What the change is supposed to do (the spec for this change)

  1. TUI is now two-level: a scrollable project list, then a scrollable per-project session list. Both viewports must size to the terminal (via tea.WindowSizeMsg) and scroll to keep the cursor visible. Navigation: enter/→ descends, ←/esc ascends, q/ctrl+c quits. An options screen (subagents/redact/open/generate toggles) follows session selection — unchanged behavior.
  2. Agent-spawned transcripts (Claude Code writes Task subagents / code-review agents as their own top-level .jsonl files) must be distinguished from interactive human sessions. Classification: a session is an agent session if its first record carrying entrypoint has an "sdk"-prefixed value, OR any user/assistant record has promptSource == "sdk". In the session list, agent sessions are hidden by default; pressing a toggles showing all (and hiding again).
  3. --project was previously ignored unless combined with --latest. Now: --project <name> alone opens the TUI focused on the matched project's session list; combined with --latest it scopes latest. Matching is via discovery.FindProject: exact absolute path, else case-insensitive basename, else case-insensitive unambiguous substring of the path; ambiguous or no match is an error.
  4. --latest now skips agent transcripts and targets the newest interactive session (in or out of a project scope).

Verify especially (think hard about these — they are where bugs hide)

  • Scroll math (clampScroll in tui.go and its callers): off-by-one at boundaries; behavior when the list shrinks (e.g. toggling 'a' to a shorter root-only list while scrolled down); behavior when visible >= list length; the if max := n - visible; top > max && max >= 0 clause — is it correct and can it produce a negative or wrong top? Consider n=0.
  • WindowSizeMsg ordering: before the first WindowSizeMsg arrives, height defaults to 20. Does scroll stay correct if keys arrive before any size msg? Does listHeight() ever return <1?
  • Empty states: a project with zero root sessions (all agents) with showAll=false → sessions is empty. Does enter/right guard against index panic? Does viewSessions avoid slicing panics? Does refreshSessions handle sessCursor when the new list is empty (max(0, -1) style)?
  • Agent classification in discovery.indexFile: is entrypointSet logic correct if the first entrypoint-bearing record is an agent but a later record has promptSource != sdk? Can a genuine cli session be misclassified, or an agent session missed? What if entrypoint is absent entirely but promptSource=="sdk"? What about a session file with NO user/assistant records (only queue-operation etc.)?
  • FindProject: exact-path precedence vs. basename vs substring; the ambiguity detection — can it return a match when it should error, or error when exactly one match exists? Case handling.
  • run.go latestSession: groups = groups[idx:idx+1] slice after FindProject — bounds safe? Interaction with the root-session skip loop when the scoped project has only agent sessions (should error "no sessions found").
  • main.go: focusIdx wiring; when --project matches but the group has zero root sessions, the TUI opens on an empty (root-only) session list — is that acceptable and non-crashing? (It should show the "press a" hint.)
  • Test quality: do the new tests actually assert meaningful behavior, or are any vacuous? Is there a gap for any of the above risks that a cheap test would close?

How to report

Classify each finding as Critical / Important / Minor. For each: file:line, what's wrong, a concrete failure scenario (inputs → wrong result/panic), and a suggested fix. If you find nothing above Minor, say so plainly. Be precise and do not invent issues to seem thorough. You may run go test ./..., go vet ./..., and go build ./... from ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done to confirm hypotheses. Return your review as your final message (it is consumed by the controller, not shown to a human — be concise and structured).

All tests pass, build and vet are clean. I verified the classification, scroll math, FindProject, and TUI empty-state paths against the actual code. Here is the review.

Findings

Important — latestSession does not return the globally newest interactive session

File: cmd/ccwhid/run.go:54-63 (the skip loop in latestSession)

Spec item 4 says --latest should "target the newest interactive session (in or out of a project scope)." The implementation instead returns the first non-agent session in group order, and groups are ordered by each group's newest session including agents (Scan sorts by groups[i].Sessions[0].ModTime, and Sessions[0] may be an agent transcript). So it finds "the newest interactive session inside whichever project had the most recent activity of any kind," not the newest interactive session overall.

Failure scenario (no --project):

  • Project A sessions: agent@T=100, interactive@T=50
  • Project B sessions: interactive@T=90

Scan orders A before B (100 > 90). The loop enters A, skips the agent, and returns interactive@T=50. But the newest interactive session is B's @T=90. --latest renders the wrong (older) session. This is very plausible in practice: running a code-review/Task agent in project A writes a fresh top-level transcript with a recent mtime, while your actual latest human session was in project B.

Note the old code (return groups[0].Sessions[0]) was correct for "newest session" because the group sort key is Sessions[0].ModTime; adding the agent-skip broke that invariant without switching to a global max.

Suggested fix: scan all sessions and pick the non-agent one with the maximum ModTime, e.g.

var best *discovery.SessionInfo
for _, g := range groups {
    for i := range g.Sessions {
        s := g.Sessions[i]
        if s.IsAgent {
            continue
        }
        if best == nil || s.ModTime.After(best.ModTime) {
            best = &s
        }
    }
}
if best == nil { return ..., fmt.Errorf("no sessions found") }
return *best, false, nil

(The --project scoped path narrows to one group first, where this reduces to the correct newest-interactive-in-group.)

Verified correct (no defect)

  • clampScroll — Boundary math is correct. For n=0/empty lists it returns 0 (the max := n - visible; max >= 0 guard is skipped, and the cursor-pull clauses keep top=0). I checked the potential "stale large top when visible > n" stranding case: sessTop/sessCursor are reset to 0 on both enterProject and the a toggle (the only places n changes), and WindowSizeMsg changes only visible, never n, so a stale oversized top is unreachable. The max >= 0 guard is effectively dead but harmless.
  • WindowSizeMsg / defaults — height defaults to 20; listHeight() clamps to >= 1; keys before the first size msg are safe.
  • Empty states — enter/right in both screens is guarded by len(...) > 0; viewSessions early-returns the "press a" hint on empty; refreshSessions clamps sessCursor via max(0, len-1). No slicing panics. A --project match with zero root sessions opens a non-crashing empty session list. All confirmed.
  • Agent classification (discovery.go:120-129) — Matches the spec: first entrypoint-bearing user/assistant record decides via HasPrefix("sdk") and latches with entrypointSet; promptSource=="sdk" can only set IsAgent=true (never resets), so an sdk session can't be un-classified by a later non-sdk record, and a cli session flips to agent only if a record actually carries promptSource=="sdk". Entrypoint-absent + promptSource=="sdk" correctly yields agent. A file with no user/assistant records stays IsAgent=false (shown by default) — acceptable per spec.
  • FindProject — Exact path precedence, then case-insensitive basename, then case-insensitive substring; ambiguity/no-match errors are correct. groups[idx:idx+1] is bounds-safe.

Minor

  • Test gap for the Important bug: latestSession in run.go has no test at all — neither the agent-skip nor the ordering. A cheap table test over synthetic groups (like the counterexample above) would have caught the ordering defect. The new discovery/tui tests are meaningful and non-vacuous, but this untested seam is exactly where the bug lives.
  • Entrypoint only read on user/assistant records (discovery.go:110-137): if a real transcript carries entrypoint only on some other record type (e.g. a summary/system line), classification would miss it. The author's test assumes entrypoint-on-user, so this is a schema-assumption risk rather than a confirmed bug — worth a comment or a quick check against a real agent transcript.

No other issues above Minor. agentId: ad1bfe0b3beb4138b (use SendMessage with to: 'ad1bfe0b3beb4138b', summary: '<5-10 word recap>' to continue this agent) subagent_tokens: 59996 tool_uses: 7 duration_ms: 139767

Claude4k tok · ~$0.19

The reviewer found one Important bug: --latest returns the first interactive session in group order, but groups are ordered by their newest session including agents — so --latest can render an older interactive session instead of the globally newest one. Concrete failure: an agent run in project A bumps A ahead of project B, and --latest returns A's older human session instead of B's newer one. That's a real regression from my agent-skip change. Let me fix it and close the test gap.

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/run.go
- if opts.project != "" {
- idx, err := discovery.FindProject(groups, opts.project)
- if err != nil {
- return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, err
- }
- groups = groups[idx : idx+1]
- }
- for _, g := range groups {
- for _, s := range g.Sessions {
- if !s.IsAgent {
- return s, false, nil
- }
- }
- }
- return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, fmt.Errorf("no sessions found")
+ if opts.project != "" {
+ idx, err := discovery.FindProject(groups, opts.project)
+ if err != nil {
+ return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, err
+ }
+ groups = groups[idx : idx+1]
+ }
+ // Pick the newest interactive session by modification time. Group order
+ // alone is insufficient: groups are sorted by their newest session
+ // *including* agent transcripts, so a recent agent run in one project
+ // could otherwise mask a newer human session in another.
+ bestGI, bestSI := -1, -1
+ for gi := range groups {
+ for si := range groups[gi].Sessions {
+ if groups[gi].Sessions[si].IsAgent {
+ continue
+ }
+ if bestGI < 0 || groups[gi].Sessions[si].ModTime.After(groups[bestGI].Sessions[bestSI].ModTime) {
+ bestGI, bestSI = gi, si
+ }
+ }
+ }
+ if bestGI < 0 {
+ return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, fmt.Errorf("no sessions found")
+ }
+ return groups[bestGI].Sessions[bestSI], false, nil

Claude2k tok · ~$0.14

Now add the missing test for latestSession (the untested seam where the bug lived):

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/run_test.go
- import (
- "os"
- "path/filepath"
- "strings"
- "testing"
-
- "github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery"
- )
+ import (
+ "fmt"
+ "os"
+ "path/filepath"
+ "strings"
+ "testing"
+ "time"
+
+ "github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery"
+ )
+
+ // writeLatestSession writes a one-line transcript with a controlled mtime so
+ // latestSession's newest-first selection can be exercised deterministically.
+ func writeLatestSession(t *testing.T, root, projDir, id, entrypoint, promptSource string, mod time.Time) {
+ t.Helper()
+ dir := filepath.Join(root, projDir)
+ if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ p := filepath.Join(dir, id+".jsonl")
+ line := fmt.Sprintf(`{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"x"},"entrypoint":%q,"promptSource":%q,"cwd":%q,"timestamp":"2026-07-11T10:00:00Z"}`,
+ entrypoint, promptSource, "/"+projDir)
+ if err := os.WriteFile(p, []byte(line+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if err := os.Chtimes(p, mod, mod); err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestLatestSessionPicksNewestInteractive(t *testing.T) {
+ root := t.TempDir()
+ base := time.Date(2026, 7, 11, 10, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
+ // Project A: newest file is an agent transcript; its interactive one is older.
+ writeLatestSession(t, root, "-a", "aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000", "sdk-py", "sdk", base.Add(100*time.Minute))
+ writeLatestSession(t, root, "-a", "aaaaaaaa-1111-0000-0000-000000000000", "cli", "", base.Add(50*time.Minute))
+ // Project B: interactive, newer than A's interactive but older than A's agent.
+ writeLatestSession(t, root, "-b", "bbbbbbbb-0000-0000-0000-000000000000", "cli", "", base.Add(90*time.Minute))
+
+ // Unscoped: must pick the globally newest interactive session (B), not A's
+ // older interactive session, and never the agent transcript.
+ si, needTUI, err := latestSession(&options{latest: true}, root)
+ if err != nil || needTUI {
+ t.Fatalf("latestSession err=%v needTUI=%v", err, needTUI)
+ }
+ if si.IsAgent {
+ t.Fatal("latestSession returned an agent transcript")
+ }
+ if !strings.HasPrefix(si.ID, "bbbbbbbb") {
+ t.Errorf("unscoped --latest picked %q, want newest interactive bbbbbbbb...", si.ID)
+ }
+
+ // Scoped to project A: must skip the newer agent and return A's interactive.
+ si, _, err = latestSession(&options{latest: true, project: "-a"}, root)
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if !strings.HasPrefix(si.ID, "aaaaaaaa-1111") {
+ t.Errorf("--project -a --latest picked %q, want aaaaaaaa-1111...", si.ID)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestLatestSessionNoInteractiveErrors(t *testing.T) {
+ root := t.TempDir()
+ base := time.Date(2026, 7, 11, 10, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
+ writeLatestSession(t, root, "-a", "aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000", "sdk-py", "sdk", base)
+ if _, _, err := latestSession(&options{latest: true, project: "-a"}, root); err == nil {
+ t.Error("expected error when project has only agent sessions")
+ }
+ }

Claude408 tok · ~$0.08

Review complete and acted on.

Reviewer verdict: 1 Important, 2 Minor.

  • Important (fixed, 0c147ee): --latest could render an older session — my agent-skip returned the first interactive session in group order, but groups sort by newest-including-agents, so a recent agent run masked a newer human session elsewhere. Now selects by max modification time across non-agent sessions. Added the missing latestSession tests (they'd fail against the pre-fix code).
  • Minor (accepted): entrypoint is only read from user/assistant records — verified against your real transcripts that it's reliably present there for both cli and sdk sessions, so it's a sound assumption.
  • The reviewer independently confirmed clampScroll, empty-state handling, agent classification, and FindProject are all correct.

All 7 packages green, vet/gofmt clean, both fixes pushed to origin/feat/ccwhid-impl.

Back to finishing the branch:

  1. Merge back to main locally
  2. Push and create a Pull Request (branch already pushed)
  3. Keep the branch as-is
  4. Discard this work

Which option?

You

Claude1k tok · ~$0.11
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ngh pr create --base main --head feat/ccwhid-impl \\\n  --title \"ccwhid: Claude Code session transcript → browsable HTML report\" \\\n  --body \"$(cat \u003c\u003c'EOF'\nBuilds **ccwhid** (`cc-what-have-i-done`), a single-binary Go CLI that turns a Claude Code session transcript (JSONL) into a self-contained, browsable static HTML report you can commit into a repo as a documentation artifact.\n\n## What it does\n- Parses `~/.claude/projects/\u003cproj\u003e/\u003csession\u003e.jsonl` into a timeline of prompts, replies, thinking blocks, tool calls, and `Edit`/`Write` diffs.\n- Renders a self-contained site (`index.html` + embedded assets, no network refs) with rendered markdown + syntax highlighting (goldmark + chroma), collapsible tool detail, and optional nested subagent activity.\n- Redacts common secret shapes and rewrites `$HOME` → `~` by default (`--no-redact` to disable).\n- Ships an interactive TUI (Bubble Tea) when run with no selector flags.\n\n## Usage\n```\nccwhid                 # browse sessions in a TUI\nccwhid --latest        # render the most recent interactive session\nccwhid --session \u003cid\u003e  # render a specific session (id or prefix)\nccwhid --project \u003cn\u003e   # open the TUI on a project (or scope --latest)\n```\n\n## Structure\n- `cmd/ccwhid` — cobra CLI, session resolution, generation, browser open\n- `internal/discovery` — scan/index sessions, agent-vs-interactive classification, project resolution\n- `internal/transcript` — fold raw JSONL records into a session model (incl. subagent sidechains)\n- `internal/model` — domain types\n- `internal/redact` — secret/home-[user]\n- `internal/render` — session → HTML (embedded templates/CSS/JS)\n- `internal/tui` — two-level scrollable session browser\n\n## TUI (final iteration)\nTwo-level, terminal-sized, scrollable: project list → per-project session list (`enter`/`→` down, `←`/`esc` up). Claude Code writes each Task/review agent as its own top-level transcript; these are classified via `entrypoint`/`promptSource` and **hidden by default** — press `a` to toggle them (marked `⟲`). `--project` (previously ignored unless combined with `--latest`) now opens the TUI on the matched project.\n\n## Testing \u0026 review\n- Unit + end-to-end tests across all 7 packages; `go vet`/`gofmt` clean.\n- Built task-by-task via subagent-driven development, each task spec- and quality-reviewed, plus a whole-branch review. The final TUI change also went through an independent review that caught and fixed a `--latest` ordering bug.\n\n\u003e Redaction is best-effort defense-in-depth — review generated reports before committing.\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\" 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -5",
  "description": "Create the pull request against main"
}
https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/pull/1
Claude480 tok · ~$0.09

Pull request created: https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/pull/1 (feat/ccwhid-impl → main).

Per the finish flow for Option 2, the branch stays as-is so you can iterate on any PR feedback — nothing merged, nothing deleted, worktree preserved.

Summary of the whole branch (18 commits, ~2,960 insertions):

  • Full ccwhid CLI: transcript parsing → self-contained HTML report, redaction on by default, syntax highlighting, optional nested subagents.
  • Interactive TUI: two-level scrollable browser, agent sessions hidden by default (a to reveal), working --project.
  • Tests green across all 7 packages; vet/gofmt clean; built via subagent-driven development with per-task + whole-branch + one follow-up independent review (which caught the --latest ordering bug, now fixed).

The branch is done and up for review. Anything you'd like to adjust on the PR, or shall I leave it there?

Claude259 tok · ~$0.08

It works end-to-end on a real (large) session:

  • Redaction active — zero occurrences of your home path; ~/... was rewritten to ~/... throughout.
  • Structure rendered — 789 turn blocks and 1,011 tool cards, all in one self-contained 780K index.html (plus assets/), no network dependencies.

+ <!doctype html>
+ <html>
+ <head>
+ <meta charset="utf-8">
+ <style>
+ :root {
+ --bg: #1b1e23;
+ --fg: #cfd2d6;
+ --muted: #7f8792;
+ --coral: #D97757;
+ --sel-bg: #F6E7DF;
+ --sel-fg: #1A1A18;
+ }
+ * { box-sizing: border-box; }
+ body {
+ margin: 0;
+ padding: 28px;
+ background: #0d0f12;
+ font-family: "SF Mono", "JetBrains Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
+ display: flex;
+ flex-direction: column;
+ gap: 40px;
+ width: max-content;
+ }
+ .term {
+ width: 660px;
+ background: var(--bg);
+ border-radius: 10px;
+ overflow: hidden;
+ box-shadow: 0 18px 50px rgba(0,0,0,.55);
+ }
+ .bar {
+ height: 34px;
+ display: flex;
+ align-items: center;
+ gap: 8px;
+ padding: 0 14px;
+ background: #24272d;
+ border-bottom: 1px solid #000;
+ }
+ .dot { width: 12px; height: 12px; border-radius: 50%; }
+ .r { background: #ff5f56; } .y { background: #ffbd2e; } .g { background: #27c93f; }
+ .bar .name { margin-left: 10px; color: var(--muted); font-size: 12px; }
+ .body {
+ padding: 16px 18px 20px;
+ font-size: 14px;
+ line-height: 1.6;
+ color: var(--fg);
+ white-space: pre;
+ }
+ .title { font-weight: 700; color: #fff; }
+ .muted { color: var(--muted); }
+ .coral { color: var(--coral); font-weight: 700; }
+ .row { display: block; }
+ .sel { background: var(--sel-bg); color: var(--sel-fg); border-radius: 3px; }
+ .sel .coral, .sel .muted { color: var(--sel-fg); font-weight: 400; }
+ .gap { display: block; height: 1.6em; }
+ </style>
+ </head>
+ <body>
+
+ <!-- Screen 1: project list -->

Claude
mcp__plugin_playwright_playwright__browser_evaluate
{
  "function": "() =\u003e {\n  const classes = new Set();\n  document.querySelectorAll('[class]').forEach(e =\u003e e.className.split(/\\s+/).forEach(c =\u003e classes.add(c)));\n  const diff = document.querySelector('.diff, [class*=\"diff\"]');\n  const tool = document.querySelector('[class*=\"tool\"]');\n  // expand any \u003cdetails\u003e tool cards near the first diff so detail is visible\n  let target = diff || tool;\n  if (target) {\n    let d = target.closest('details'); if (d) d.open = true;\n    document.querySelectorAll('details').forEach((el,i)=\u003e{ if(i\u003c8) el.open = true; });\n    target.scrollIntoView({block:'center'});\n  }\n  return { classes: [...classes].filter(c=\u003e/tool|diff|code|card|turn/.test(c)).slice(0,40), hasDiff: !!diff, scrollY: window.scrollY };\n}"
}
### Result

  "classes": [
    "turn",
    "turn-user",
    "turn-role",

### Ran Playwright code
```js
await page.evaluate('() => {\n  const classes = new Set();\n  document.querySelectorAll(\'[class]\').forEach(e => e.className.split(/\\s+/).forEach(c => classes.add(c)));\n  const diff = document.querySelector(\'.diff, [class*="diff"]\');\n  const tool = document.querySelector(\'[class*="tool"]\');\n  // expand any <details> tool cards near the first diff so detail is visible\n  let target = diff || tool;\n  if (target) {\n    let d = target.closest(\'details\'); if (d) d.open = true;\n    document.querySelectorAll(\'details\').forEach((el,i)=>{ if(i<8) el.open = true; });\n    target.scrollIntoView({block:\'center\'});\n  }\n  return { classes: [...classes].filter(c=>/tool|diff|code|card|turn/.test(c)).slice(0,40), hasDiff: !!diff, scrollY: window.scrollY };\n}');
```

Claude1k tok · ~$0.12

That shows a diff but landed mid-diff without the tool-card header. Let me pick a compact, self-contained tool card (header + summary + short diff or a Bash result) and frame it properly:

Claude
mcp__plugin_playwright_playwright__browser_evaluate
{
  "function": "() =\u003e {\n  // collapse everything first\n  document.querySelectorAll('details').forEach(d =\u003e d.open = false);\n  const tools = [...document.querySelectorAll('.tool')];\n  const summarize = t =\u003e {\n    const name = (t.querySelector('.tool-name')||{}).textContent || '';\n    const sum = (t.querySelector('.tool-summary')||{}).textContent || '';\n    const diffLines = t.querySelectorAll('.diff-add, .diff-del').length;\n    return {name:name.trim(), sum:sum.trim().slice(0,40), diffLines};\n  };\n  // find a small Edit diff (2..14 changed lines)\n  let pick = tools.find(t =\u003e { const s=summarize(t); return /Edit/.test(s.name) \u0026\u0026 s.diffLines\u003e=2 \u0026\u0026 s.diffLines\u003c=14; });\n  if (!pick) pick = tools.find(t =\u003e /Bash/.test(summarize(t).name));\n  if (!pick) pick = tools[0];\n  const d = pick.closest('details'); if (d) d.open = true;\n  // also open the parent turn if collapsible\n  let p = pick.parentElement;\n  while (p) { if (p.tagName==='DETAILS') p.open = true; p = p.parentElement; }\n  const head = pick.querySelector('.tool-head') || pick;\n  const y = head.getBoundingClientRect().top + window.scrollY - 90;\n  window.scrollTo(0, y);\n  return { picked: summarize(pick), scrollY: window.scrollY };\n}"
}
### Result
{
  "picked": {
    "name": "Edit",
    "sum": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/docs/",
    "diffLines": 2
  },
  "scrollY": 8488.5
}
### Ran Playwright code
```js
await page.evaluate('() => {\n  // collapse everything first\n  document.querySelectorAll(\'details\').forEach(d => d.open = false);\n  const tools = [...document.querySelectorAll(\'.tool\')];\n  const summarize = t => {\n    const name = (t.querySelector(\'.tool-name\')||{}).textContent || \'\';\n    const sum = (t.querySelector(\'.tool-summary\')||{}).textContent || \'\';\n    const diffLines = t.querySelectorAll(\'.diff-add, .diff-del\').length;\n    return {name:name.trim(), sum:sum.trim().slice(0,40), diffLines};\n  };\n  // find a small Edit diff (2..14 changed lines)\n  let pick = tools.find(t => { const s=summarize(t); return /Edit/.test(s.name) && s.diffLines>=2 && s.diffLines<=14; });\n  if (!pick) pick = tools.find(t => /Bash/.test(summarize(t).name));\n  if (!pick) pick = tools[0];\n  const d = pick.closest(\'details\'); if (d) d.open = true;\n  // also open the parent turn if collapsible\n  let p = pick.parentElement;\n  while (p) { if (p.tagName===\'DETAILS\') p.open = true; p = p.parentElement; }\n  const head = pick.querySelector(\'.tool-head\') || pick;\n  const y = head.getBoundingClientRect().top + window.scrollY - 90;\n  window.scrollTo(0, y);\n  return { picked: summarize(pick), scrollY: window.scrollY };\n}');
```

Claude342 tok · ~$0.11

Both mockups are faithful — the session list shows the ⟲ agent markers, the 1–7 of 9 scroll indicator, and the a root only toggle state. Now let me place all four into the README. Let me re-read it to pick insertion points:

Claude
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md"
}
1	# ccwhid — cc-what-have-i-done
2	
3	Turn a Claude Code session transcript into a browsable, self-contained static
4	HTML report. See what you prompted and what Claude Code did — and commit the
5	report into your repo as a documentation artifact.
6	
7	> **Status:** under active development. See
8	> [`docs/superpowers/plans/`](docs/superpowers/plans/) for the implementation plan.
9	
10	## Install
11	
12	```bash
13	go install github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid@latest
14	```
15	
16	Or build locally:
17	
18	```bash
19	go build -o ccwhid ./cmd/ccwhid
20	```
21	
22	## Usage
23	
24	```bash
25	ccwhid                      # browse sessions in an interactive TUI
26	ccwhid --latest             # render the most recent session
27	ccwhid --session <id>       # render a specific session (id or prefix)
28	ccwhid --session <id> --open
29	```
30	
31	The report is written to `./ccwhid-report/<session-short>/` by default (override
32	with `--out`). Open `index.html` in any browser — no server needed.
33	
34	### Browsing sessions
35	
36	Run `ccwhid` with no selector to open the interactive browser:
37	
38	- **Project list** — every project with sessions, most-recent first. `↑`/`↓`
39	  to move, `enter` (or `→`) to open a project, `q` to quit.
40	- **Session list** — the chosen project's sessions. `enter` selects, `←` (or
41	  `esc`) returns to the project list.
42	- Both lists **scroll** to fit any terminal size.
43	- By default only your **interactive sessions** are shown. Claude Code writes
44	  each Task subagent / code-review agent as its own transcript; press **`a`**
45	  in the session list to show (and hide) those agent sessions — they're marked
46	  with a `⟲`.
47	
48	`--project <name>` opens the browser directly on that project's session list.
49	
50	### Flags
51	
52	| Flag | Description |
53	|------|-------------|
54	| `--session <id>` | Session id or unambiguous prefix to render |
55	| `--project <path\|name>` | Scope `--latest` to a project, or open the TUI on it (matches full path, basename, or unambiguous substring) |
56	| `--latest` | Render the most recent interactive session (skips agent transcripts) |
57	| `--out <dir>` | Output directory |
58	| `--title <str>` | Override the report title |
59	| `--include-subagents` | Include subagent (Task) activity (default true; `--include-subagents=false` to omit) |
60	| `--no-redact` | Disable secret redaction |
61	| `--force` | Overwrite a non-empty output directory |
62	| `--open` | Open the report in a browser when done |
63	
64	## Redaction
65	
66	By default, ccwhid scrubs common secret shapes (AWS keys, API tokens, JWTs,
67	`KEY=[REDACTED:assignment] assignments) and rewrites your home directory to `~`.
68	This is best-effort defense-in-depth — **review generated reports before
69	committing them.** Disable with `--no-redact`.
70	
71	## What's included
72	
73	Your prompts, Claude's replies (rendered markdown + syntax-highlighted code),
74	tool calls with collapsible detail, `Edit`/`Write` diffs, collapsed thinking
75	blocks, and — optionally — nested subagent activity. System reminders and
76	attachments are omitted for readability.
77	
78	## License
79	
80	[MIT](LICENSE) © 2026 [user]
81

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md
- > **Status:** under active development. See
- > [`docs/superpowers/plans/`](docs/superpowers/plans/) for the implementation plan.
-
- ## Install
+ > **Status:** under active development. See
+ > [`docs/superpowers/plans/`](docs/superpowers/plans/) for the implementation plan.
+
+ ## Preview
+
+ A full example — ccwhid rendering the very session that built it — is committed
+ at [`docs/session-report/`](docs/session-report/); clone the repo and open
+ `index.html` in a browser.
+
+ | Report overview | Tool calls & diffs |
+ |:---:|:---:|
+ | [![ccwhid report overview](docs/images/report-top.png)](docs/session-report/) | [![tool calls and diffs](docs/images/report-tool.png)](docs/session-report/) |
+
+ The report is one self-contained page: a jump-to-prompt sidebar, a live turn
+ filter, expand/collapse-all, and a light/dark toggle — all client-side, no
+ server.
+
+ ## Install

Claude477 tok · ~$0.10

Done — the README now has screenshots, committed and pushed to PR #1 (8433cbd).

Added four images in docs/images/:

  • Report overview + tool calls & diffs — real captures of the committed docs/session-report demo, placed side-by-side near the top and linked to the demo.
  • Project list + session list — faithful TUI mockups (exact View() layout and lipgloss colors) with sample project names, in the "Browsing sessions" section. The session shot shows the ⟲ agent markers and 1–7 of 9 scroll indicator.

Why mockups for the TUI: a real screenshot of your terminal would bake all your actual ~/.claude project paths and session titles into a public README. The mockups look identical to the real thing but use invented sample data. The report screenshots are the genuine article, since that report is already public in the repo.

Cleanup handled: removed Playwright's stray output that landed in the repo root and added .playwright-mcp/ to .gitignore.

One small thing I noticed while capturing: the demo report shows a couple of raw ANSI codes ([1m, [22m) in the /model command output — leaked escape sequences from that transcript. Harmless and cosmetic, but if you'd like, I can add ANSI-stripping to the renderer as a small follow-up. Otherwise, PR #1 is up to date with the screenshots.

You

Add ANSI-stripping to the renderer

Claude452 tok · ~$0.10

Let me look at the renderer to find the right chokepoint for ANSI stripping.

Claude
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go"
}
1	package render
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"embed"
6		"html"
7		"html/template"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10		"strings"
11	
12		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
13	)
14	
15	//go:embed assets/report.html.tmpl assets/styles.css assets/app.js
16	var assets embed.FS
17	
18	// Options configures a render.
19	type Options struct {
20		Title string
21	}
22	
23	// Site renders the session into outDir as index.html + assets/.
24	func Site(s model.Session, outDir string, opts Options) error {
25		title := opts.Title
26		if title == "" {
27			title = s.DisplayTitle()
28		}
29	
30		tmplSrc, err := assets.ReadFile("assets/report.html.tmpl")
31		if err != nil {
32			return err
33		}
34		tmpl, err := template.New("report").Parse(string(tmplSrc))
35		if err != nil {
36			return err
37		}
38	
39		data := buildViewModel(s, title)
40		var buf bytes.Buffer
41		if err := tmpl.Execute(&buf, data); err != nil {
42			return err
43		}
44	
45		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(outDir, "assets"), 0o755); err != nil {
46			return err
47		}
48		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(outDir, "index.html"), buf.Bytes(), 0o644); err != nil {
49			return err
50		}
51		for _, name := range []string{"styles.css", "app.js"} {
52			b, err := assets.ReadFile("assets/" + name)
53			if err != nil {
54				return err
55			}
56			if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(outDir, "assets", name), b, 0o644); err != nil {
57				return err
58			}
59		}
60		return nil
61	}
62	
63	// view models -------------------------------------------------------------
64	
65	type viewData struct {
66		Title     string
67		Session   model.Session
68		StartedAt string
69		TurnCount int
70		Prompts   []promptRef
71		Turns     []turnView
72	}
73	
74	type promptRef struct {
75		Index   int
76		Preview string
77	}
78	
79	type turnView struct {
80		Index      int
81		Kind       string
82		RoleLabel  string
83		SearchText string
84		Body       template.HTML
85	}
86	
87	func buildViewModel(s model.Session, title string) viewData {
88		d := viewData{
89			Title:     title,
90			Session:   s,
91			TurnCount: len(s.Turns),
92		}
93		if !s.StartedAt.IsZero() {
94			d.StartedAt = s.StartedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04")
95		}
96		for i, t := range s.Turns {
97			if t.Kind == model.TurnUser {
98				d.Prompts = append(d.Prompts, promptRef{Index: i, Preview: preview(turnPlainText(t), 60)})
99			}
100			d.Turns = append(d.Turns, turnView{
101				Index:      i,
102				Kind:       string(t.Kind),
103				RoleLabel:  roleLabel(t.Kind),
104				SearchText: strings.ToLower(turnPlainText(t)),
105				Body:       renderTurnBody(t),
106			})
107		}
108		return d
109	}
110	
111	func roleLabel(k model.TurnKind) string {
112		if k == model.TurnUser {
113			return "You"
114		}
115		return "Claude"
116	}
117	
118	// renderTurnBody renders all blocks of a turn to HTML.
119	func renderTurnBody(t model.Turn) template.HTML {
120		var b strings.Builder
121		for _, blk := range t.Blocks {
122			switch blk.Type {
123			case model.BlockText:
124				b.WriteString(string(Markdown(blk.Text)))
125			case model.BlockThinking:
126				b.WriteString(`<details class="thinking"><summary>thinking</summary>`)
127				b.WriteString(string(Markdown(blk.Text)))
128				b.WriteString(`</details>`)
129			case model.BlockToolUse:
130				b.WriteString(renderTool(blk.Tool))
131			}
132		}
133		return template.HTML(b.String())
134	}
135	
136	func renderTool(tc *model.ToolCall) string {
137		var b strings.Builder
138		b.WriteString(`<details class="tool"><summary class="tool-head">`)
139		b.WriteString(`<span class="tool-name">` + html.EscapeString(tc.Name) + `</span>`)
140		if tc.Summary != "" {
141			b.WriteString(`<span class="tool-summary">` + html.EscapeString(tc.Summary) + `</span>`)
142		}
143		b.WriteString(`</summary><div class="tool-body">`)
144		if tc.Diff != nil {
145			b.WriteString(string(DiffHTML(tc.Diff)))
146		} else if tc.InputJSON != "" {
147			b.WriteString(`<pre class="tool-input">` + html.EscapeString(tc.InputJSON) + `</pre>`)
148		}
149		if tc.Result != nil && tc.Result.Content != "" {
150			cls := "tool-result"
151			if tc.Result.IsError {
152				cls += " tool-result-error"
153			}
154			b.WriteString(`<pre class="` + cls + `">` + html.EscapeString(tc.Result.Content) + `</pre>`)
155		}
156		for _, sub := range tc.Subagents {
157			b.WriteString(`<details class="subagent"><summary>subagent: ` + html.EscapeString(sub.Description) + `</summary>`)
158			for _, st := range sub.Turns {
159				b.WriteString(`<article class="turn turn-` + string(st.Kind) + `"><div class="turn-role">` + roleLabel(st.Kind) + `</div><div class="turn-body">`)
160				b.WriteString(string(renderTurnBody(st)))
161				b.WriteString(`</div></article>`)
162			}
163			b.WriteString(`</details>`)
164		}
165		b.WriteString(`</div></details>`)
166		return b.String()
167	}
168	
169	func turnPlainText(t model.Turn) string {
170		var parts []string
171		for _, blk := range t.Blocks {
172			switch blk.Type {
173			case model.BlockText, model.BlockThinking:
174				parts = append(parts, blk.Text)
175			case model.BlockToolUse:
176				parts = append(parts, blk.Tool.Name, blk.Tool.Summary)
177			}
178		}
179		return strings.Join(parts, " ")
180	}
181	
182	func preview(s string, n int) string {
183		s = strings.TrimSpace(strings.ReplaceAll(s, "\n", " "))
184		r := []rune(s)
185		if len(r) <= n {
186			return s
187		}
188		return string(r[:n-1]) + "…"
189	}
190
Claude
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/markdown.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/markdown.go"
}
1	package render
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"html"
6		"html/template"
7		"strings"
8	
9		chromahtml "github.com/alecthomas/chroma/v2/formatters/html"
10		"github.com/yuin/goldmark"
11		highlighting "github.com/yuin/goldmark-highlighting/v2"
12		"github.com/yuin/goldmark/extension"
13	
14		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
15	)
16	
17	// md renders markdown with GFM plus chroma-based syntax highlighting for fenced
18	// code blocks. Highlighting uses inline styles (WithClasses(false)) so the
19	// generated report stays fully self-contained — no external stylesheet.
20	var md = goldmark.New(goldmark.WithExtensions(
21		extension.GFM,
22		highlighting.NewHighlighting(
23			highlighting.WithStyle("github"),
24			highlighting.WithFormatOptions(
25				chromahtml.WithClasses(false),
26			),
27		),
28	))
29	
30	// Markdown renders CommonMark+GFM to HTML. goldmark runs with its default
31	// Unsafe=false setting, so any raw HTML embedded in the transcript text is
32	// stripped (replaced with an HTML comment) rather than passed through — the
33	// rendered Body is therefore safe to emit as template.HTML.
34	func Markdown(src string) template.HTML {
35		var buf bytes.Buffer
36		if err := md.Convert([]byte(src), &buf); err != nil {
37			return template.HTML("<pre>" + html.EscapeString(src) + "</pre>")
38		}
39		return template.HTML(buf.String())
40	}
41	
42	// DiffHTML renders a simple line-based diff of a Diff's old/new text.
43	func DiffHTML(d *model.Diff) template.HTML {
44		var b strings.Builder
45		b.WriteString(`<div class="diff">`)
46		if d.OldText != "" {
47			for _, line := range strings.Split(d.OldText, "\n") {
48				b.WriteString(`<div class="diff-del">- `)
49				b.WriteString(html.EscapeString(line))
50				b.WriteString("</div>")
51			}
52		}
53		if d.NewText != "" {
54			for _, line := range strings.Split(d.NewText, "\n") {
55				b.WriteString(`<div class="diff-add">+ `)
56				b.WriteString(html.EscapeString(line))
57				b.WriteString("</div>")
58			}
59		}
60		b.WriteString("</div>")
61		return template.HTML(b.String())
62	}
63
Claude5k tok · ~$0.25

ANSI codes can appear in several rendered text paths (markdown text blocks, tool results, tool summaries, diffs). I'll add a single StripANSI helper and apply it at each render boundary. First the helper + test:

Claude
Write~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/ansi.go
+ package render
+
+ import (
+ "regexp"
+ "strings"
+ )
+
+ // ansiEscape matches ANSI/VT escape sequences: CSI (e.g. SGR colour codes like
+ // "\x1b[1m"), OSC sequences terminated by BEL or ST, and two-character Fe
+ // escapes. Terminal output leaks these into transcripts (e.g. a colourised
+ // `/model` command echo); the ESC byte is invisible in a browser but the
+ // trailing "[1m" shows as literal text, so we strip the whole sequence.
+ var ansiEscape = regexp.MustCompile(`\x1b(?:[@-Z\\-_]|\[[0-?]*[ -/]*[@-~]|\][^\x07\x1b]*(?:\x07|\x1b\\))`)
+
+ // StripANSI removes ANSI escape sequences from s. It fast-paths the common case
+ // of text containing no ESC byte at all.
+ func StripANSI(s string) string {
+ if !strings.ContainsRune(s, 0x1b) {
+ return s
+ }
+ return ansiEscape.ReplaceAllString(s, "")
+ }
+

Now add a test covering the helper and the render paths:

Claude
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render_test.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render_test.go"
}
1	package render
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"path/filepath"
6		"strings"
7		"testing"
8		"time"
9		"unicode/utf8"
10	
11		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
12	)
13	
14	func TestMarkdownRendersHeadingAndCode(t *testing.T) {
15		out := string(Markdown("# Title\n\n```go\nfmt.Println(\"hi\")\n```"))
16		if !strings.Contains(out, "<h1") {
17			t.Errorf("expected <h1> in %q", out)
18		}
19		if !strings.Contains(out, "Println") {
20			t.Errorf("expected highlighted code in output")
21		}
22		if !strings.Contains(out, "<span") {
23			t.Errorf("expected chroma-highlighted <span> tokens in code output, got %q", out)
24		}
25	}
26	
27	func TestDiffHTMLMarksLines(t *testing.T) {
28		out := string(DiffHTML(&model.Diff{Path: "x.txt", OldText: "a", NewText: "b"}))
29		if !strings.Contains(out, "diff-del") || !strings.Contains(out, "diff-add") {
30			t.Errorf("diff HTML missing add/del classes: %q", out)
31		}
32	}
33	
34	func sampleSession() model.Session {
35		return model.Session{
36			ID:          "abcd1234",
37			ProjectPath: "/tmp/proj",
38			Title:       "Sample",
39			StartedAt:   time.Date(2026, 7, 11, 10, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
40			EndedAt:     time.Date(2026, 7, 11, 10, 1, 0, 0, time.UTC),
41			Turns: []model.Turn{
42				{Kind: model.TurnUser, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockText, Text: "do it"}}},
43				{Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{
44					{Type: model.BlockText, Text: "on it"},
45					{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: &model.ToolCall{
46						Name: "Bash", Summary: "ls", Result: &model.ToolResult{Content: "file.txt"},
47					}},
48				}},
49			},
50		}
51	}
52	
53	func TestPreviewRuneSafe(t *testing.T) {
54		s := strings.Repeat("ä", 80) // 80 two-byte runes, exceeds n=60
55		got := preview(s, 60)
56		if !utf8.ValidString(got) {
57			t.Errorf("preview produced invalid UTF-8: %q", got)
58		}
59		if []rune(got)[len([]rune(got))-1] != '…' {
60			t.Errorf("preview should end with ellipsis, got %q", got)
61		}
62	}
63	
64	func TestSiteWritesFiles(t *testing.T) {
65		dir := t.TempDir()
66		if err := Site(sampleSession(), dir, Options{}); err != nil {
67			t.Fatal(err)
68		}
69		for _, name := range []string{"index.html", "assets/styles.css", "assets/app.js"} {
70			if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, name)); err != nil {
71				t.Errorf("missing %s: %v", name, err)
72			}
73		}
74		html, _ := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, "index.html"))
75		s := string(html)
76		for _, want := range []string{"Sample", "do it", "on it", "Bash", "assets/styles.css", "assets/app.js"} {
77			if !strings.Contains(s, want) {
78				t.Errorf("index.html missing %q", want)
79			}
80		}
81	}

- func sampleSession() model.Session {
+ func TestStripANSI(t *testing.T) {
+ cases := []struct{ in, want string }{
+ {"\x1b[1mOpus 4.8\x1b[22m", "Opus 4.8"}, // SGR bold on/off
+ {"\x1b[0;31mred\x1b[0m text", "red text"}, // colour codes
+ {"plain text", "plain text"}, // fast path, unchanged
+ {"array[1m] index", "array[1m] index"}, // literal, no ESC → kept
+ {"\x1b]0;title\x07done", "done"}, // OSC + BEL
+ {"a\x1b[Kb", "ab"}, // erase-line CSI
+ }
+ for _, c := range cases {
+ if got := StripANSI(c.in); got != c.want {
+ t.Errorf("StripANSI(%q) = %q, want %q", c.in, got, c.want)
+ }
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestRenderStripsANSIFromReport(t *testing.T) {
+ s := model.Session{
+ ID: "x",
+ Title: "T",
+ Turns: []model.Turn{
+ {Kind: model.TurnUser, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockText, Text: "set model to \x1b[1mOpus\x1b[22m now"}}},
+ {Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: &model.ToolCall{
+ Name: "Bash", Summary: "echo hi", Result: &model.ToolResult{Content: "\x1b[32mgreen\x1b[0m output"},
+ }}}},
+ },
+ }
+ dir := t.TempDir()
+ if err := Site(s, dir, Options{}); err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ html, _ := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, "index.html"))
+ body := string(html)
+ if strings.ContainsRune(body, 0x1b) {
+ t.Error("report still contains a raw ESC byte")
+ }
+ for _, want := range []string{"set model to Opus now", "green output"} {
+ if !strings.Contains(body, want) {
+ t.Errorf("report missing cleaned text %q", want)
+ }
+ }
+ if strings.Contains(body, "[1m") || strings.Contains(body, "[22m") || strings.Contains(body, "[32m") {
+ t.Error("report still shows leaked ANSI parameter text")
+ }
+ }
+
+ func sampleSession() model.Session {

Claude4k tok · ~$0.15

The OSC case exposed an alternation-ordering bug: ] (0x5D) falls in my two-char-escape class, so \x1b] matched the short form before the OSC rule. Fix — try CSI/OSC first and exclude [/] from the Fe class:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/ansi.go
- var ansiEscape = regexp.MustCompile(`\x1b(?:[@-Z\\-_]|\[[0-?]*[ -/]*[@-~]|\][^\x07\x1b]*(?:\x07|\x1b\\))`)
+ // The CSI ("\x1b[…") and OSC ("\x1b]…") alternatives are listed before the
+ // generic two-character Fe escape, whose class deliberately omits "[" and "]"
+ // so those introducers can't be swallowed as a short escape.
+ var ansiEscape = regexp.MustCompile(`\x1b(?:\[[0-?]*[ -/]*[@-~]|\][^\x07\x1b]*(?:\x07|\x1b\\)|[@-Z\\^_])`)
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/ansi.go has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

  "description": "Run full test suite"
}

All green. Let me verify the fix on your real session (temp output, not committing) to confirm the leaked codes are gone:

Claude
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ngo build -o /tmp/ccwhid ./cmd/ccwhid\n/tmp/ccwhid --latest --out /tmp/ccwhid-ansi --force \u003e/dev/null 2\u003e\u00261\necho \"--- raw ESC bytes in report (want 0) ---\"\nLC_ALL=C grep -c $'\\x1b' /tmp/ccwhid-ansi/index.html || echo 0\necho \"--- leaked '[1m'/'[22m' literal fragments (want 0) ---\"\ngrep -o '\\[22m\\|\\[1m\\|\\[2m' /tmp/ccwhid-ansi/index.html | wc -l | tr -d ' '\necho \"--- the previously-affected line now reads: ---\"\ngrep -o 'Set model to[^\u003c]*' /tmp/ccwhid-ansi/index.html | head -1",
  "description": "Verify ANSI stripped in real regenerated report"
}
--- raw ESC bytes in report (want 0) ---
0
0
--- leaked '[1m'/'[22m' literal fragments (want 0) ---

Claude778 tok · ~$0.13

Those are legitimate — claude-opus-4-8[1m] is the literal model ID (the [1m] denotes the 1M-context variant), not an escape sequence. That's exactly why the helper only strips ESC-prefixed sequences: real ANSI goes, literal text stays. Let me vet/fmt and commit:

Claude
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ngo vet ./... 2\u003e\u00261 | head; gofmt -l internal cmd\ngit add internal/render\ngit commit -q -F - \u003c\u003c'EOF'\nfeat(render): strip ANSI escape sequences from report text\n\nTerminal output leaks ANSI/VT escapes into transcripts (e.g. a colourised\n/model command echo). The ESC byte is invisible in a browser but its trailing\n\"[1m\"/\"[22m\" show as literal noise. Add StripANSI and apply it at every text\nboundary: markdown blocks, tool summaries, tool results, diffs, and the\npreview/search plain-text.\n\nOnly ESC-prefixed CSI/OSC/Fe sequences are removed, so legitimate literal\ntext is preserved — e.g. the model id \"claude-opus-4-8[1m]\" stays intact.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) \u003cnoreply@anthropic.com\u003e\nEOF\ngit push 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -2",
  "description": "Vet, fmt, commit and push renderer fix"
}
To https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done.git
   8433cbd..c16bfdb  feat/ccwhid-impl -> feat/ccwhid-impl
Claude520 tok · ~$0.12

ANSI-stripping is in and pushed to PR #1 (c16bfdb).

What it does: a single StripANSI helper (fast-pathed when there's no ESC byte) applied at every render text boundary — markdown blocks, tool summaries, tool results, diffs, and the preview/search index. It removes ESC-prefixed CSI/OSC/Fe sequences only, so genuine literal text survives. Verified on your real session: 0 ESC bytes remain, the Set model to … line renders cleanly, and the literal model ID claude-opus-4-8[1m] is correctly left untouched. Covered by two new tests (TestStripANSI, TestRenderStripsANSIFromReport); full suite green, vet/fmt clean.

One consistency note: the committed docs/session-report demo still shows the old ANSI artifact, since it's the frozen snapshot you asked to keep — the fix only affects newly generated reports. If you'd like the demo (and the one README screenshot that shows that line) to reflect the fix, I can regenerate them; otherwise I'll leave the snapshot as-is. Your call.

You
