mcp__plugin_playwright_playwright__browser_evaluate
{
  "function": "() =\u003e {\n  document.querySelectorAll('details').forEach(d =\u003e d.open = false);\n  const tools = [...document.querySelectorAll('.tool')];\n  const info = t =\u003e ({\n    name: (t.querySelector('.tool-name')||{}).textContent||'',\n    diffLines: t.querySelectorAll('.diff-add, .diff-del').length\n  });\n  let pick = tools.find(t =\u003e { const s=info(t); return /Edit/.test(s.name) \u0026\u0026 s.diffLines\u003e=2 \u0026\u0026 s.diffLines\u003c=14; })\n          || tools.find(t =\u003e /Bash/.test(info(t).name))\n          || tools[0];\n  let p = pick;\n  while (p) { if (p.tagName==='DETAILS') p.open = true; p = p.parentElement; }\n  const head = pick.querySelector('.tool-head') || pick;\n  window.scrollTo(0, head.getBoundingClientRect().top + window.scrollY - 90);\n  return info(pick);\n}"
}

{
  "name": "Edit",

### Ran Playwright code
```js
await page.evaluate('() => {\n  document.querySelectorAll(\'details\').forEach(d => d.open = false);\n  const tools = [...document.querySelectorAll(\'.tool\')];\n  const info = t => ({\n    name: (t.querySelector(\'.tool-name\')||{}).textContent||\'\',\n    diffLines: t.querySelectorAll(\'.diff-add, .diff-del\').length\n  });\n  let pick = tools.find(t => { const s=info(t); return /Edit/.test(s.name) && s.diffLines>=2 && s.diffLines<=14; })\n          || tools.find(t => /Bash/.test(info(t).name))\n          || tools[0];\n  let p = pick;\n  while (p) { if (p.tagName===\'DETAILS\') p.open = true; p = p.parentElement; }\n  const head = pick.querySelector(\'.tool-head\') || pick;\n  window.scrollTo(0, head.getBoundingClientRect().top + window.scrollY - 90);\n  return info(pick);\n}');
```

=== PR reviews ===
{"author":"copilot-pull-request-reviewer","body":"## Pull request overview\n\nThis PR introduces **ccwhid**, a Go-based CLI that parses Claude Code JSONL transcripts into a browsable, self-contained static HTML report, with optional TUI-based session browsing and best-effort redaction.\n\n**Changes:**\n- Adds transcript parsing and modeling (turns/blocks/tool calls, including subagent sidechains) plus secret/home-[user]\n- Adds HTML report rendering (templated page + embedded assets) with client-side filtering, expand/collapse, and theme toggle.\n- Adds an interactive Bubble Tea TUI for browsing projects/sessions and selecting render options, plus documentation and test coverage.\n\n### Reviewed changes\n\nCopilot reviewed 31 out of 38 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eShow a summary per file\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n| File | Description |\r\n| ---- | ----------- |\r\n| README.md | Adds preview screenshots and documents TUI browsing behavior and updated flag semantics. |\r\n| cmd/ccwhid/main.go | Wires Cobra CLI flags and integrates TUI-driven selection into the main execution path. |\r\n| cmd/ccwhid/run.go | Implements session resolution, report generation, output handling, and browser-opening behavior. |\r\n| cmd/ccwhid/main_test.go | Verifies CLI flag registration and help output. |\r\n| cmd/ccwhid/run_test.go | Tests latest-session selection logic, output path defaults, and report generation. |\r\n| internal/discovery/discovery.go | Scans/indexes transcript files and groups them by project, with agent-session classification. |\r\n| internal/discovery/discovery_test.go | Tests scanning/indexing, project/session resolution, and agent classification behavior. |\r\n| internal/model/model.go | Defines shared domain types for sessions, turns, blocks, tools, diffs, and subagents. |\r\n| internal/model/model_test.go | Tests small model helpers (duration/title fallback). |\r\n| internal/redact/redact.go | Adds best-effort secret/home-[user]-visible session fields. |\r\n| internal/redact/redact_test.go | Tests redaction patterns, home rewrite, and full-session traversal. |\r\n| internal/render/render.go | Renders a session into index.html + local assets, building a view model for templates. |\r\n| internal/render/markdown.go | Adds Goldmark+Chroma markdown rendering and simple diff-to-HTML rendering. |\r\n| internal/render/ansi.go | Strips ANSI escape sequences to prevent terminal-control artifacts leaking into reports. |\r\n| internal/render/render_test.go | Unit tests markdown, diff HTML, ANSI stripping, preview rune-safety, and file outputs. |\r\n| internal/render/render_e2e_test.go | End-to-end test for parsing + redaction + rendering (ensures secrets don’t leak). |\r\n| internal/render/assets/report.html.tmpl | Adds the report page template (sidebar prompts, turn list, controls). |\r\n| internal/render/assets/styles.css | Provides light/dark styling and layout for the report. |\r\n| internal/render/assets/app.js | Implements client-side filtering, expand/collapse-all, tool-detail toggle, and theme toggle. |\r\n| internal/transcript/raw.go | Defines raw JSONL decoding helpers for message blocks/tool results/timestamps. |\r\n| internal/transcript/parse.go | Implements folding JSONL into a session timeline, including tool result attachment and sidechains. |\r\n| internal/transcript/*_test.go | Tests raw decoding and parsing behaviors, including subagent attachment and inner tool results. |\r\n| internal/transcript/testdata/*.jsonl | Adds transcript fixtures for parser tests. |\r\n| internal/tui/tui.go | Implements the Bubble Tea TUI (project list → session list → render options). |\r\n| internal/tui/tui_test.go | Tests core TUI navigation and scrolling/filtering behaviors. |\r\n| docs/session-report/assets/* | Adds committed example report assets for the README preview. |\r\n| go.mod / go.sum | Adds module definition and dependencies (Bubble Tea, Cobra, Goldmark, Chroma, etc.). |\r\n| .gitignore | Ignores Playwright MCP scratch output directory. |\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n---\n\n💡 \u003ca href=\"/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/new/main?filename=.github/instructions/*.instructions.md\" class=\"Link--inTextBlock\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\"\u003eAdd Copilot custom instructions\u003c/a\u003e for smarter, more guided reviews. \u003ca href=\"https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/customizing-copilot/adding-repository-custom-instructions-for-github-copilot\" class=\"Link--inTextBlock\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\"\u003eLearn how to get started\u003c/a\u003e.","state":"COMMENTED"}
=== review comments (inline) ===
{"body":"Project list count string uses singular \"agent\" even when multiple agent transcripts exist, which will display incorrect grammar (e.g. \"2 agent\"). Use the existing plural() helper for the agent count too.","line":285,"path":"internal/tui/tui.go","user":"Copilot"}
{"body":"turnPlainText(t) is computed twice per turn (once for prompt preview and again for SearchText), which does redundant work and extra allocations on potentially large transcripts. Compute it once per loop iteration and reuse it.","line":107,"path":"internal/render/render.go","user":"Copilot"}
{"body":"openInBrowser builds a file:// URL from filepath.Abs output and passes it to platform openers. This can be fragile on Windows (backslashes) and with spaces/special characters. Since these openers accept filesystem paths, pass the absolute path directly instead of a file URL.","line":134,"path":"cmd/ccwhid/run.go","user":"Copilot"}

1. tui.go — pluralize the agent count:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/tui/tui.go

- if agents > 0 {

Claude694 tok · ~$0.14

2. render.go — compute turnPlainText once per turn:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go
- for i, t := range s.Turns {
- if t.Kind == model.TurnUser {
- d.Prompts = append(d.Prompts, promptRef{Index: i, Preview: preview(turnPlainText(t), 60)})
- }
- d.Turns = append(d.Turns, turnView{
- Index: i,
- Kind: string(t.Kind),
- RoleLabel: roleLabel(t.Kind),
- SearchText: strings.ToLower(turnPlainText(t)),
- Body: renderTurnBody(t),
- })
- }
+ for i, t := range s.Turns {
+ plain := turnPlainText(t)
+ if t.Kind == model.TurnUser {
+ d.Prompts = append(d.Prompts, promptRef{Index: i, Preview: preview(plain, 60)})
+ }
+ d.Turns = append(d.Turns, turnView{
+ Index: i,
+ Kind: string(t.Kind),
+ RoleLabel: roleLabel(t.Kind),
+ SearchText: strings.ToLower(plain),
+ Body: renderTurnBody(t),
+ })
+ }
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
Claude596 tok · ~$0.13

3. run.go — pass the absolute path to openers instead of a file:// URL:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/run.go
- abs, err := filepath.Abs(index)
- if err != nil {
- return err
- }
- url := "file://" + abs
- switch runtime.GOOS {
- case "darwin":
- return exec.Command("open", url).Start()
- case "windows":
- return exec.Command("rundll32", "url.dll,FileProtocolHandler", url).Start()
- default:
- return exec.Command("xdg-open", url).Start()
- }
+ abs, err := filepath.Abs(index)
+ if err != nil {
+ return err
+ }
+ // Pass the filesystem path directly rather than a file:// URL: exec runs the
+ // opener without a shell, so a single path argument handles spaces and
+ // special characters, and avoids Windows backslash/URL-encoding pitfalls.
+ switch runtime.GOOS {
+ case "darwin":
+ return exec.Command("open", abs).Start()
+ case "windows":
+ return exec.Command("rundll32", "url.dll,FileProtocolHandler", abs).Start()
+ default:
+ return exec.Command("xdg-open", abs).Start()
+ }
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/run.go has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

Claude
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ngh pr comment 1 --body \"Thanks — all three Copilot findings addressed in ba19c76:\n\n1. **tui.go** — project-list agent count now pluralized via the existing \\`plural()\\` helper (\\\"2 agents\\\").\n2. **render.go** — \\`turnPlainText(t)\\` is computed once per turn and reused for both the prompt preview and the search index.\n3. **run.go** — \\`openInBrowser\\` now passes the absolute filesystem path to the platform opener instead of a \\`file://\\` URL, avoiding space/backslash/encoding pitfalls (exec runs without a shell, so a single path arg is safe).\n\nTests, \\`go vet\\`, and \\`gofmt\\` all clean.\" 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -2",

Claude339 tok · ~$0.12

All three Copilot findings addressed and pushed to PR #1 (ba19c76), with a summary comment posted on the PR.

Finding File Fix
Singular "agent" for plural counts tui.go Pluralized via the existing plural() helper
turnPlainText computed twice per turn render.go Compute once, reuse for preview + search index
Fragile file:// URL for opening reports run.go Pass the absolute path to the opener directly

All are minor quality/robustness improvements — no behavior change for the common case. Tests, go vet, and gofmt are clean.

These were reasonable catches. The file:// one is the most substantive: passing a bare path is genuinely more robust across platforms since exec.Command doesn't invoke a shell, so spaces in a path are handled as a single argument rather than needing URL-encoding. The other two are tidy-ups. The PR now reflects all of them.

Agent · Broader search for screenshot filescompleted

Background command "Broader search for screenshot files" completed (exit code 0)

Claude389 tok · ~$0.12
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/run.go
- if len(entries) > 0 && !force {
- return fmt.Errorf("output directory %q is not empty (use --force to overwrite)", dir)
- }
- return nil
- }
+ if len(entries) == 0 {
+ return nil
+ }
+ if !force {
+ return fmt.Errorf("output directory %q is not empty (use --force to overwrite)", dir)
+ }
+ // --force: clear existing entries so the report reflects only this run and
+ // no stale files (e.g. renamed assets) are left behind.
+ for _, e := range entries {
+ if err := os.RemoveAll(filepath.Join(dir, e.Name())); err != nil {
+ return err
+ }
+ }
+ return nil
+ }

Now add a test for the --force clearing behavior, then verify:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/run_test.go
- func TestResolveOutDirDefault(t *testing.T) {
+ func TestEnsureOutDir(t *testing.T) {
+ // Non-existent dir is created.
+ base := t.TempDir()
+ fresh := filepath.Join(base, "new")
+ if err := ensureOutDir(fresh, false); err != nil {
+ t.Fatalf("fresh dir: %v", err)
+ }
+ if fi, err := os.Stat(fresh); err != nil || !fi.IsDir() {
+ t.Fatalf("expected created dir, got %v %v", fi, err)
+ }
+
+ // Non-empty dir without --force errors.
+ nonEmpty := t.TempDir()
+ stale := filepath.Join(nonEmpty, "stale.txt")
+ if err := os.WriteFile(stale, []byte("old"), 0o644); err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if err := ensureOutDir(nonEmpty, false); err == nil {
+ t.Error("expected error for non-empty dir without force")
+ }
+
+ // Non-empty dir with --force clears stale content (incl. nested).
+ nested := filepath.Join(nonEmpty, "assets")
+ if err := os.MkdirAll(nested, 0o755); err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(nested, "old.css"), []byte("x"), 0o644); err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if err := ensureOutDir(nonEmpty, true); err != nil {
+ t.Fatalf("force clear: %v", err)
+ }
+ entries, err := os.ReadDir(nonEmpty)
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if len(entries) != 0 {
+ t.Errorf("force did not clear dir, %d entries remain", len(entries))
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestResolveOutDirDefault(t *testing.T) {

Claude408 tok · ~$0.13

All four new findings from the re-review are addressed and pushed to PR #1 (d60f985), with a summary comment posted.

Finding File Fix
Flag help out of date main.go --project/--latest descriptions now match behavior (verified via --help)
--force left stale files run.go Now clears the output dir before rendering; added ensureOutDir tests
Preview link → dir listing README.md Text link now points at docs/session-report/index.html
Screenshot links → dir listing README.md Both image links now point at index.html

The --force one was the most substantive — a genuine correctness gap: previously it only bypassed the non-empty check, so a renamed or removed asset from a prior run would linger in the report directory. It now removes existing entries (including nested assets/) first, so the output reflects only the current run. Tests, go vet, and gofmt all clean.

Note the first three comments in the API list were the original findings (already fixed in ba19c76); the re-review correctly didn't re-flag those against the current code. PR #1 now reflects both review rounds.

You

=== inline review comments (newest last) ===
2026-07-11T17:27:31Z  internal/tui/tui.go:285  Project list count string uses singular "agent" even when multiple agent transcripts exist, which will display incorrect grammar (e.g. "2 agent"). Use the existing plural() helper for the agent count too.
2026-07-11T17:27:31Z  internal/render/render.go:108  turnPlainText(t) is computed twice per turn (once for prompt preview and again for SearchText), which does redundant work and extra allocations on potentially large transcripts. Compute it once per loop iteration and reuse it.
2026-07-11T17:27:32Z  cmd/ccwhid/run.go:146  openInBrowser builds a file:// URL from filepath.Abs output and passes it to platform openers. This can be fragile on Windows (backslashes) and with spaces/special characters. Since these openers accept filesystem paths, pass the absolute path directly instead of a file URL.
2026-07-11T17:38:08Z  cmd/ccwhid/main.go:null  The flag help strings for --project and --latest no longer match the current behavior: --project can also be used to start the TUI focused on a project, and --latest intentionally skips agent transcripts (see latestSession implementation). Update the descriptions so `ccwhid --help` is accurate.
2026-07-11T17:38:08Z  cmd/ccwhid/run.go:127  `--force` is documented as overwriting a non-empty output directory, but ensureOutDir currently only bypasses the non-empty check. This can leave stale files from previous runs in the report directory. If `force` is true and the directory is non-empty, clear it before rendering.
2026-07-11T17:38:08Z  README.md:null  The README links to the `docs/session-report/` directory, which typically opens a file listing on GitHub rather than the actual report. Linking directly to the committed `index.html` makes the preview easier to access.
2026-07-11T17:38:09Z  README.md:null  These preview screenshots link to the report directory; on GitHub this usually shows a directory listing. Point the links at the committed `docs/session-report/index.html` so clicking the images opens the report file directly.
2026-07-11T17:50:31Z  internal/discovery/discovery.go:142  indexFile() ignores bufio.Scanner errors (sc.Err()) after the scan loop. If the scan stops early (e.g. I/O error), the returned SessionInfo may be incomplete or misclassified without any signal. Consider checking sc.Err() and failing (or explicitly tolerating bufio.ErrTooLong like Parse does) so callers can decide how to handle the file.
2026-07-11T17:50:31Z  internal/tui/tui.go:362  The options screen help text says "enter confirm", but in updateOptions both space and enter toggle the current option unless the cursor is on "Generate report". This is misleading for users trying to confirm/generate with Enter.

Two new findings from the third pass (17:50), both valid:

1. discovery.go — indexFile ignores scanner errors:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery/discovery.go
- import (
- "bufio"
- "encoding/json"
- "fmt"
- "os"
- "path/filepath"
- "sort"
- "strings"
- "time"
- )
+ import (
+ "bufio"
+ "encoding/json"
+ "errors"
+ "fmt"
+ "os"
+ "path/filepath"
+ "sort"
+ "strings"
+ "time"
+ )
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery/discovery.go has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
Claude402 tok · ~$0.17
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery/discovery.go
- if info.Title == "" {
- info.Title = truncate(info.FirstPrompt, 60)
- }
- return info, nil
- }
+ // A single pathologically long line (bufio.ErrTooLong) is tolerated like
+ // the full parser does — index what we read so the session still lists.
+ // Any other scan error means the file was truncated/unreadable, so signal
+ // it and let Scan skip this entry rather than list a misleading index.
+ if err := sc.Err(); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, bufio.ErrTooLong) {
+ return SessionInfo{}, err
+ }
+ if info.Title == "" {
+ info.Title = truncate(info.FirstPrompt, 60)
+ }
+ return info, nil
+ }
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery/discovery.go has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

Claude349 tok · ~$0.13

Both third-pass findings addressed and pushed to PR #1 (9d0cc5b), with a summary comment posted.

Finding File Fix
Scanner errors ignored discovery.go indexFile checks sc.Err() — tolerates ErrTooLong, propagates other errors so Scan skips bad files
Misleading options help tui.go "enter confirm" → "↑/↓ move · space/enter select · esc back · q quit"

The discovery.go one is the meatier fix — it mirrors the tolerance the full parser already had: an oversized line still yields a usable index entry, but a genuine I/O truncation now surfaces instead of silently producing a possibly-misclassified session. Tests, go vet, and gofmt all clean.

That's three review rounds handled now. Copilot tends to converge after a couple of passes; if it flags another round, send it my way. And whenever you're ready, the PR is in good shape to merge.

You

Sigh, even more findings by Copilot. Please have a look. On the last comment, I like the suggestion to add an option/input in the TUI to actually let users override the output directory.

Claude346 tok · ~$0.13
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ngh api repos/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/pulls/1/comments --jq '.[] | select(.created_at \u003e \"2026-07-11T18:00:00Z\") | \"\\(.created_at)  \\(.path):\\(.line)\\n  \\(.body)\\n\"' 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -40",
  "description": "Fetch newest PR review comments (after third pass)"
}
2026-07-11T18:44:48Z  internal/tui/tui.go:285
  `viewProjects` calls `g.RootSessions()` just to count interactive sessions. `RootSessions()` allocates a new slice every render, and combined with `AgentCount()` this walks the sessions twice. In a large transcript directory this can create unnecessary allocations and UI jank when the TUI re-renders.

2026-07-11T18:44:48Z  cmd/ccwhid/run.go:131
  The comment says `openInBrowser` accepts a file or directory, but the implementation always appends `index.html` (so it effectively expects a report directory). This mismatch can mislead future callers and makes the function contract unclear.

2026-07-11T18:44:48Z  internal/tui/tui.go:24
  `Selection.OutDir` is plumbed through to `cmd/ccwhid/main.go` but nothing in the TUI ever sets it, so this field/branch is currently dead code. Either remove `OutDir` and the corresponding `if sel.OutDir != "" { ... }` handling, or add an option/input in the TUI to actually let users override the output directory.
Claude6k tok · ~$0.27

22		OutDir           string

26	var (
27		titleStyle    = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true)
28		headerStyle   = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#D97757"))
29		selectedStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#1A1A18")).Background(lipgloss.Color("#F6E7DF"))
30		mutedStyle    = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("240"))
31		optionStyle   = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#D97757"))

33	
34	type screen int
35	
36	const (
37		screenProjects screen = iota
38		screenSessions
39		screenOptions
40	)
41	
42	// reservedLines is how many rows the title and footer occupy, leaving the rest
43	// of the terminal height for the scrollable list body.
44	const reservedLines = 4
45	
46	type model struct {
47		groups []discovery.ProjectGroup
48	
49		// projects screen
50		projCursor int
51		projTop    int
52	
53		// sessions screen
54		groupIdx   int
55		showAll    bool
56		sessions   []discovery.SessionInfo // filtered view of groups[groupIdx]
57		sessCursor int
58		sessTop    int
59	
60		// options screen
61		optCursor int
62	
63		screen screen
64		sel    Selection
65	
66		height int
67		width  int
68	}
69	
70	func newModel(groups []discovery.ProjectGroup) model {
71		return model{
72			groups: groups,
73			screen: screenProjects,
74			height: 20,
75			width:  80,
76			sel: Selection{
77				IncludeSubagents: true,
78				Redact:           true,
79			},
80		}
81	}
82	
83	// listHeight is the number of list rows visible in the current terminal.
84	func (m model) listHeight() int {
85		h := m.height - reservedLines
86		if h < 1 {
87			return 1
88		}
89		return h
90	}
91	
92	func (m model) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
93	
94	func (m model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
95		switch msg := msg.(type) {
96		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
97			m.height = msg.Height
98			m.width = msg.Width
99			m.adjustProjScroll()
100			m.adjustSessScroll()
101			return m, nil

225			switch m.optCursor {

227				m.sel.IncludeSubagents = !m.sel.IncludeSubagents
228			case 1:
229				m.sel.Redact = !m.sel.Redact

238	
239	// clampScroll returns a scroll offset that keeps cursor within the visible
240	// window [top, top+visible) over a list of length n.
241	func clampScroll(cursor, top, visible, n int) int {
242		if visible < 1 {
243			visible = 1
244		}
245		if cursor < top {
246			top = cursor
247		}
248		if cursor >= top+visible {
249			top = cursor - visible + 1
250		}
251		if top < 0 {
252			top = 0
253		}
254		// Don't scroll past the end when the list shrank.
255		if max := n - visible; top > max && max >= 0 {
256			top = max
257		}
258		return top
259	}
260	
261	func (m model) View() string {
262		switch m.screen {
263		case screenProjects:
264			return m.viewProjects()
265		case screenSessions:
266			return m.viewSessions()
267		default:
268			return m.viewOptions()
269		}
270	}
271	
272	func (m model) viewProjects() string {
273		var b strings.Builder
274		b.WriteString(titleStyle.Render("Select a project") +
275			mutedStyle.Render("   ↑/↓ move · enter open · q quit") + "\n\n")
276		visible := m.listHeight()
277		end := min(m.projTop+visible, len(m.groups))
278		for i := m.projTop; i < end; i++ {
279			g := m.groups[i]
280			roots := len(g.RootSessions())
281			agents := g.AgentCount()
282			count := fmt.Sprintf("%d session%s", roots, plural(roots))
283			if agents > 0 {
284				count += fmt.Sprintf(", %d agent%s", agents, plural(agents))
285			}
286			label := headerStyle.Render(g.ProjectPath) + "  " + mutedStyle.Render(count)
287			if i == m.projCursor {
288				label = selectedStyle.Render("▸ " + g.ProjectPath + "  " + count)
289			} else {
290				label = "  " + label
291			}
292			b.WriteString(label + "\n")
293		}
294		b.WriteString(scrollHint(m.projTop, end, len(m.groups)))

215			m.screen = screenSessions
216		case "up", "k":

218				m.optCursor--
219			}
220		case "down", "j":
221			if m.optCursor < optionCount-1 {
222				m.optCursor++
223			}
224		case " ", "enter":

296	}
297	
298	func (m model) viewSessions() string {
299		var b strings.Builder
300		g := m.groups[m.groupIdx]
301		b.WriteString(titleStyle.Render("Sessions in ") + headerStyle.Render(g.ProjectPath) + "\n")
302		toggle := "a show all"
303		if m.showAll {
304			toggle = "a root only"
305		}
306		b.WriteString(mutedStyle.Render("↑/↓ move · enter select · ← back · "+toggle+" · q quit") + "\n\n")
307	
308		if len(m.sessions) == 0 {
309			b.WriteString(mutedStyle.Render("  no interactive sessions here — press a to show agent sessions") + "\n")
310			return b.String()
311		}
312		visible := m.listHeight()
313		end := min(m.sessTop+visible, len(m.sessions))
314		for i := m.sessTop; i < end; i++ {
315			s := m.sessions[i]
316			id := s.ID[:min(8, len(s.ID))]
317			tag := ""
318			if s.IsAgent {
319				tag = mutedStyle.Render(" ⟲")
320			}
321			line := fmt.Sprintf("  %s  %s%s", s.DisplayLabel(), mutedStyle.Render(id), tag)
322			if i == m.sessCursor {
323				line = selectedStyle.Render("▸ "+s.DisplayLabel()+"  "+id) + tag
324			}
325			b.WriteString(line + "\n")
326		}
327		b.WriteString(scrollHint(m.sessTop, end, len(m.sessions)))
328		return b.String()
329	}
330	
331	func (m model) viewOptions() string {
332		var b strings.Builder
333		b.WriteString("Options for: " + m.sel.Session.DisplayLabel() + "\n\n")
334		toggles := []struct {
335			label string
336			on    bool
337		}{
338			{"Include subagents", m.sel.IncludeSubagents},
339			{"Redact secrets", m.sel.Redact},
340			{"Open in browser when done", m.sel.Open},
341		}
342		for i, t := range toggles {
343			check := "[ ]"
344			if t.on {
345				check = "[x]"
346			}
347			line := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s", check, t.label)
348			if m.optCursor == i {
349				line = optionStyle.Render("▸ " + line)
350			} else {
351				line = "  " + line
352			}
353			b.WriteString(line + "\n")
354		}
355		gen := "Generate report"

366	// scrollHint renders a one-line position indicator when the list is scrolled or
367	// overflows the viewport.
368	func scrollHint(top, end, n int) string {
369		if top == 0 && end >= n {
370			return ""
371		}
372		return mutedStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("\n  %d–%d of %d", top+1, end, n))
373	}
374	
375	func plural(n int) string {
376		if n == 1 {
377			return ""
378		}
379		return "s"
380	}
381	
382	// Run launches the browser and returns the user's selection. If focusIdx is a

Now I have the full picture. Let me implement all three.

Finding #1 — single-pass counts in discovery, used by viewProjects:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery/discovery.go
- // AgentCount returns how many of the group's sessions were agent-spawned.
- func (g ProjectGroup) AgentCount() int {
- n := 0
- for _, s := range g.Sessions {
- if s.IsAgent {
- n++
- }
- }
- return n
- }
+ // AgentCount returns how many of the group's sessions were agent-spawned.
+ func (g ProjectGroup) AgentCount() int {
+ _, agent := g.Counts()
+ return agent
+ }
+
+ // Counts returns the number of interactive (root) and agent-spawned sessions in
+ // a single pass, for callers that only need the tallies and not the slices.
+ func (g ProjectGroup) Counts() (root, agent int) {
+ for _, s := range g.Sessions {
+ if s.IsAgent {
+ agent++
+ } else {
+ root++
+ }
+ }
+ return root, agent
+ }

Now the option indices and the update logic (editing mode + new row):

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/tui/tui.go
- // optionCount is the number of toggle rows plus the Generate action.
- const optionCount = 4 // subagents, redact, open, generate
-
- func (m model) updateOptions(key tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
- switch key.String() {
- case "ctrl+c", "q":
- m.sel.Canceled = true
- return m, tea.Quit
- case "esc", "left", "h":
- m.screen = screenSessions
- case "up", "k":
- if m.optCursor > 0 {
- m.optCursor--
- }
- case "down", "j":
- if m.optCursor < optionCount-1 {
- m.optCursor++
- }
- case " ", "enter":
- switch m.optCursor {
- case 0:
- m.sel.IncludeSubagents = !m.sel.IncludeSubagents
- case 1:
- m.sel.Redact = !m.sel.Redact
- case 2:
- m.sel.Open = !m.sel.Open
- case 3:
- return m, tea.Quit
- }
- }
- return m, nil
- }
+ // Option-screen row indices.
+ const (
+ optSubagents = iota
+ optRedact
+ optOpen
+ optOutDir
+ optGenerate
+ optionCount
+ )
+
+ func (m model) updateOptions(key tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
+ // While editing the output-directory field, keys feed the text buffer;
+ // only Ctrl+C still quits so typed characters (q, h, …) are taken literally.
+ if m.editing {
+ switch key.Type {
+ case tea.KeyCtrlC:
+ m.sel.Canceled = true
+ return m, tea.Quit
+ case tea.KeyEnter, tea.KeyEsc:
+ m.editing = false
+ case tea.KeyBackspace, tea.KeyDelete:
+ if r := []rune(m.outDir); len(r) > 0 {
+ m.outDir = string(r[:len(r)-1])
+ }
+ case tea.KeyCtrlU:
+ m.outDir = ""
+ case tea.KeySpace:
+ m.outDir += " "
+ case tea.KeyRunes:
+ m.outDir += string(key.Runes)
+ }
+ return m, nil
+ }
+
+ switch key.String() {
+ case "ctrl+c", "q":
+ m.sel.Canceled = true
+ return m, tea.Quit
+ case "esc", "left", "h":
+ m.screen = screenSessions
+ case "up", "k":
+ if m.optCursor > 0 {
+ m.optCursor--
+ }
+ case "down", "j":
+ if m.optCursor < optionCount-1 {
+ m.optCursor++
+ }
+ case " ", "enter":
+ switch m.optCursor {
+ case optSubagents:
+ m.sel.IncludeSubagents = !m.sel.IncludeSubagents
+ case optRedact:
+ m.sel.Redact = !m.sel.Redact
+ case optOpen:
+ m.sel.Open = !m.sel.Open
+ case optOutDir:
+ m.editing = true
+ case optGenerate:
+ m.sel.OutDir = strings.TrimSpace(m.outDir)
+ return m, tea.Quit
+ }
+ }
+ return m, nil
+ }

Edit~/IdeaParameterProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/tui/tui.go
- for i, t := range toggles {
- check := "[ ]"
- if t.on {
- check = "[x]"
- }
- line := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s", check, t.label)
- if m.optCursor == i {
- line = optionStyle.Render("▸ " + line)
- } else {
- line = " " + line
- }
- b.WriteString(line + "\n")
- }
- gen := "Generate report"
- if m.optCursor == 3 {
- gen = optionStyle.Render("▸ " + gen)
- } else {
- gen = " " + gen
- }
- b.WriteString("\n" + gen + "\n\n")
- b.WriteString(mutedStyle.Render("↑/↓ move · space/enter select · esc back · q quit") + "\n")
- return b.String()
- }
+ for i, t := range toggles {
+ check := "[ ]"
+ if t.on {
+ check = "[x]"
+ }
+ line := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s", check, t.label)
+ if m.optCursor == i {
+ line = optionStyle.Render("▸ " + line)
+ } else {
+ line = " " + line
+ }
+ b.WriteString(line + "\n")
+ }
+
+ // Output-directory field.
+ outRow := "Output dir: " + m.outDirDisplay()
+ if m.optCursor == optOutDir {
+ outRow = optionStyle.Render("▸ " + outRow)
+ } else {
+ outRow = " " + outRow
+ }
+ b.WriteString(outRow + "\n")
+
+ gen := "Generate report"
+ if m.optCursor == optGenerate {
+ gen = optionStyle.Render("▸ " + gen)
+ } else {
+ gen = " " + gen
+ }
+ b.WriteString("\n" + gen + "\n\n")
+
+ help := "↑/↓ move · space/enter select · esc back · q quit"
+ if m.editing {
+ help = "type a path · enter/esc done · ctrl+u clear"
+ }
+ b.WriteString(mutedStyle.Render(help) + "\n")
+ return b.String()
+ }
+
+ // outDirDisplay renders the current output-directory value: the typed path (with
+ // a cursor while editing), or a muted hint showing the default destination.
+ func (m model) outDirDisplay() string {
+ if m.editing {
+ return m.outDir + "▏"
+ }
+ if m.outDir != "" {
+ return m.outDir
+ }
+ short := m.sel.Session.ID
+ if len(short) > 8 {
+ short = short[:8]
+ }
+ return mutedStyle.Render("ccwhid-report/" + short + " (default)")
+ }

Now main.go already applies sel.OutDir when non-empty, so that branch is live. Let me add tests — one for the new input flow and one for Counts:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/tui/tui_test.go
- func TestClampScroll(t *testing.T) {
+ func TestOptionsOutputDirInput(t *testing.T) {
+ m := newModel(testGroups())
+ m = send(m, key("enter"), key("enter")) // open projA, select first session → options
+ if m.screen != screenOptions {
+ t.Fatalf("screen = %v, want options", m.screen)
+ }
+ // Move down to the output-directory row.
+ m = send(m, key("down"), key("down"), key("down"))
+ if m.optCursor != optOutDir {
+ t.Fatalf("optCursor = %d, want optOutDir (%d)", m.optCursor, optOutDir)
+ }
+ // Enter editing, type a path, finish with enter.
+ m = send(m, key("enter"))
+ if !m.editing {
+ t.Fatal("expected editing mode after enter on output-dir row")
+ }
+ for _, ch := range []string{"o", "u", "t", "/", "r", "u", "n"} {
+ m = send(m, key(ch))
+ }
+ m = send(m, key("enter"))
+ if m.editing {
+ t.Fatal("still editing after enter")
+ }
+ if m.outDir != "out/run" {
+ t.Fatalf("outDir = %q, want out/run", m.outDir)
+ }
+ // Confirm from Generate; the selection carries the override.
+ m = send(m, key("down"))
+ if m.optCursor != optGenerate {
+ t.Fatalf("optCursor = %d, want optGenerate (%d)", m.optCursor, optGenerate)
+ }
+ m = send(m, key("enter"))
+ if m.sel.OutDir != "out/run" {
+ t.Fatalf("sel.OutDir = %q, want out/run", m.sel.OutDir)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestEditingModeTakesLiteralKeys(t *testing.T) {
+ m := newModel(testGroups())
+ m = send(m, key("enter"), key("enter")) // → options
+ m = send(m, key("down"), key("down"), key("down"), key("enter"))
+ // 'q' would normally quit; while editing it must be a literal character.
+ m = send(m, key("q"), key("a"))
+ if m.sel.Canceled {
+ t.Fatal("'q' quit while editing; should be literal input")
+ }
+ if m.outDir != "qa" {
+ t.Fatalf("outDir = %q, want qa", m.outDir)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestClampScroll(t *testing.T) {

Claude484 tok · ~$0.15

All three fourth-pass findings addressed and pushed to PR #1 (74e53a2), with a summary comment posted.

Finding File Resolution
Selection.OutDir was dead code tui.go Built the feature — editable "Output dir" field on the options screen
viewProjects allocated + double-walked discovery.go / tui.go Added single-pass Counts(), used in render
openInBrowser misleading contract run.go Fixed doc + renamed param to dir

The output-dir input is the substantive one you flagged. On the options screen, navigating to Output dir and pressing enter drops into an inline editor: keystrokes build the path, ctrl+u clears, enter/esc finishes, and — importantly — only ctrl+c quits while editing, so literal characters like q are captured as input rather than exiting. Blank keeps the default ccwhid-report/<id>. That makes the previously-dead Selection.OutDir → opts.out path live. Covered by two new tests (the input flow and the literal-keys-while-editing edge case), plus a Counts test and a README note.

Tests, go vet, gofmt all clean. Four review rounds in — the feature addition may prompt Copilot to look again, but the substance is solid. Ready to merge whenever you are.

You

I merged it.

Zu neuem Branch 'chore/ci-dependabot' gewechselt
Claude474 tok · ~$0.15
Write~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+ name: CI
+
+ on:
+ push:
+ branches: [main]
+ pull_request:
+
+ permissions:
+ contents: read
+
+ concurrency:
+ group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
+ cancel-in-progress: true
+
+ jobs:
+ build:
+ runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+ steps:
+ - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+ - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
+ with:
+ go-version-file: go.mod
+ check-latest: true
+
+ - name: Verify formatting
+ run: |
+ unformatted="$(gofmt -l .)"
+ if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
+ echo "These files are not gofmt-clean:"
+ echo "$unformatted"
+ exit 1
+ fi
+
+ - name: Verify go.mod is tidy
+ run: |
+ go mod tidy
+ git diff --exit-code -- go.mod go.sum
+
+ - name: Vet
+ run: go vet ./...
+
+ - name: Build
+ run: go build ./...
+
+ - name: Test (race + coverage)
+ run: go test -race -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...
+ 
File created successfully at: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.github/workflows/ci.yml (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
Claude467 tok · ~$0.16

No untrusted event input is used in any run: step here (the gofmt output comes from the tool, not event payloads), so no injection risk. Now the Dependabot config:

Claude
Write~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.github/dependabot.yml
+ version: 2
+ updates:
+ # Go module dependencies
+ - package-ecosystem: gomod
+ directory: "/"
+ schedule:
+ interval: weekly
+ open-pull-requests-limit: 5
+ commit-message:
+ prefix: "chore(deps)"
+ groups:
+ go-dependencies:
+ patterns:
+ - "*"
+
+ # GitHub Actions used in workflows
+ - package-ecosystem: github-actions
+ directory: "/"
+ schedule:
+ interval: weekly
+ open-pull-requests-limit: 5
+ commit-message:
+ prefix: "chore(ci)"
+ groups:
+ github-actions:
+ patterns:
+ - "*"
+

Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ngit add .github/workflows/ci.yml .github/dependabot.yml\ngit commit -q -F - \u003c\u003c'EOF'\nci: add GitHub Actions workflow and Dependabot config\n\nCI (push to main + PRs): gofmt check, go mod tidy verification, go vet,\nbuild, and `go test -race` with coverage on the go.mod-pinned Go version.\nLeast-privilege permissions and in-progress cancellation.\n\nDependabot: weekly grouped updates for Go modules and GitHub Actions.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) \u003cnoreply@anthropic.com\u003e\nEOF\ngit push -u origin chore/ci-dependabot 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -3\ngh pr create --base main --head chore/ci-dependabot \\\n  --title \"ci: GitHub Actions workflow + Dependabot\" \\\n  --body \"$(cat \u003c\u003c'BODY'\nAdds continuous integration and dependency automation.\n\n## CI (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)\nRuns on pushes to `main` and all PRs:\n- **gofmt** check (fails on unformatted files)\n- **go mod tidy** verification (fails if `go.mod`/`go.sum` would change)\n- **go vet**, **go build**\n- **go test -race** with coverage\n\nUses the Go version pinned in `go.mod` (`go-version-file`), read-only `contents` permission, and cancels superseded in-progress runs.\n\n## Dependabot (`.github/dependabot.yml`)\nWeekly, grouped update PRs for:\n- Go modules (`gomod`)\n- GitHub Actions (`github-actions`)\n\nAll CI steps verified green locally (`gofmt`, `go mod tidy`, `go vet`, `go test -race`).\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nBODY\n)\" 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -3",
  "description": "Commit, push, and open CI/Dependabot PR"
}

Claude775 tok · ~$0.16

CI passed — every step green on PR #2:

✓ checkout · ✓ setup-go · ✓ gofmt · ✓ go mod tidy · ✓ vet · ✓ build · ✓ test (race + coverage)

What's set up:

  • .github/workflows/ci.yml — runs on pushes to main and all PRs: gofmt check, go mod tidy verification, go vet, build, and go test -race with coverage. Uses the Go version pinned in go.mod, read-only contents permission, and cancels superseded runs.
  • .github/dependabot.yml — weekly grouped update PRs for Go modules and GitHub Actions.

One non-blocking annotation: actions/checkout@v4 and setup-go@v5 still target Node 20 (deprecated), so GitHub forced them onto Node 24. It doesn't fail the build, and Dependabot will bump these actions to Node-24 majors on its first run — so it'll self-resolve.

PR #2 is green and ready to merge whenever you like. Want me to enable branch protection on main requiring this CI check, or is that something you'd rather configure in the GitHub UI?

You

I merged and activated branch protection already.

Claude765 tok · ~$0.15

Local main is synced, the merged chore/ci-dependabot branch is deleted, and the stale remote ref pruned — only main remains. (I also see the CI actions were already bumped to checkout@v7 / setup-go@v6, so that Node 20 deprecation is handled too.)

Everything's shipped and buttoned up:

  • ccwhid — full CLI + two-level scrollable TUI, agent-session filtering, redaction, ANSI-clean HTML reports, output-dir override.
  • Demo + docs — committed example report and README with screenshots.
  • CI — required gofmt/tidy/vet/build/test -race gate, enforced by branch protection.
  • Dependabot — weekly grouped updates for modules and Actions.

Nice project. Ping me whenever you want to tag a release, add install docs, or start the next feature.

You

{

  "description": "Create and push annotated v0.1.0 tag"
}

ccwhid v0.1.0 — first release

6d4966f18453190c6460746e7ddc9116c3e2b4dd  (HEAD -> main, tag: v0.1.0, origin/main, origin/HEAD)
Claude754 tok · ~$0.16
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ngh release create v0.1.0 \\\n  --title \"v0.1.0\" \\\n  --verify-tag \\\n  --notes \"$(cat \u003c\u003c'BODY'\nFirst release of **ccwhid** (`cc-what-have-i-done`) — turn a Claude Code session transcript into a browsable, self-contained static HTML report.\n\n### Highlights\n- **HTML report** — one self-contained page (`index.html` + embedded assets, no server): rendered markdown + chroma syntax highlighting, collapsible tool calls, `Edit`/`Write` diffs, collapsed thinking, and optional nested subagent activity. Client-side prompt sidebar, live filter, expand/collapse-all, and light/dark toggle.\n- **Interactive TUI** — two-level scrollable browser (projects → sessions). Agent-spawned transcripts are hidden by default (`a` to reveal, marked `⟲`); an options screen toggles subagents/redaction/open and takes an output-directory override.\n- **Redaction on by default** — scrubs common secret shapes and rewrites `$HOME` → `~`. Disable with `--no-redact`.\n- **ANSI-clean** — strips leaked terminal escape sequences from report text.\n- **Single binary** — Go, no runtime deps.\n\n### Install\n```bash\ngo install github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid@v0.1.0\n```\n\n### Usage\n```bash\nccwhid                 # browse sessions in the TUI\nccwhid --latest        # render the most recent interactive session\nccwhid --session \u003cid\u003e  # render a specific session (id or prefix)\n```\n\nSee the committed [example report](https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/tree/main/docs/session-report) and the README for full flags.\n\n\u003e Redaction is best-effort defense-in-depth — review generated reports before committing.\nBODY\n)\" 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -3",
  "description": "Create GitHub release for v0.1.0"
}
https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/releases/tag/v0.1.0
Claude272 tok · ~$0.15

v0.1.0 is released 🎉 → https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/releases/tag/v0.1.0

  • GitHub release published with highlights, install, and usage notes.

Installable now:

go install github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid@v0.1.0

That's the full journey done — idea → design → implementation → review rounds → merge → CI/Dependabot → tagged release. Congrats on shipping ccwhid v0.1.0! If you later want prebuilt binaries attached to releases, that's a natural next step (a GoReleaser workflow triggered on tags) — just say the word.

Claude514 tok · ~$0.16

Now the GoReleaser config and release workflow:

Claude
Write~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.goreleaser.yaml
+ # GoReleaser configuration — https://goreleaser.com
+ version: 2
+
+ before:
+ hooks:
+ - go mod tidy
+
+ builds:
+ - id: ccwhid
+ main: ./cmd/ccwhid
+ binary: ccwhid
+ env:
+ - CGO_ENABLED=0
+ goos:
+ - linux
+ - darwin
+ - windows
+ goarch:
+ - amd64
+ - arm64
+ ldflags:
+ - -s -w -X main.version={{ .Version }}
+
+ archives:
+ - id: ccwhid
+ formats: [tar.gz]
+ name_template: >-
+ {{ .ProjectName }}_{{ .Version }}_{{ .Os }}_{{ .Arch }}
+ format_overrides:
+ - goos: windows
+ formats: [zip]
+ files:
+ - README.md
+ - LICENSE
+
+ checksum:
+ name_template: checksums.txt
+
+ changelog:
+ sort: asc
+ use: github
+ filters:
+ exclude:
+ - "^docs:"
+ - "^test:"
+ - "^ci:"
+ - "^chore:"
+ - "^Merge "
+
+ release:
+ draft: false
+ prerelease: auto
+ 
File created successfully at: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.goreleaser.yaml (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
Claude337 tok · ~$0.15
Write~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.github/workflows/release.yml
+ name: Release
+
+ on:
+ push:
+ tags: ["v*"]
+
+ permissions:
+ contents: write
+
+ jobs:
+ goreleaser:
+ runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+ steps:
+ - uses: actions/checkout@v7
+ with:
+ fetch-depth: 0
+
+ - uses: actions/setup-go@v6
+ with:
+ go-version-file: go.mod
+ check-latest: true
+
+ - uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v6
+ with:
+ distribution: goreleaser
+ version: "~> v2"
+ args: release --clean
+ env:
+ GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+ 
File created successfully at: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.github/workflows/release.yml (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
Claude656 tok · ~$0.17

The release workflow uses no untrusted event input in any run: step (only the standard GITHUB_TOKEN), and permissions are scoped to contents: write — no injection surface.

Claude713 tok · ~$0.17
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ngh pr create --base main --head chore/goreleaser \\\n  --title \"ci: GoReleaser for prebuilt release binaries\" \\\n  --body \"$(cat \u003c\u003c'BODY'\nAdds automated cross-platform release binaries via GoReleaser, triggered on version tags.\n\n## What it does\nOn pushing a `v*` tag, `.github/workflows/release.yml` runs `goreleaser release --clean` (with `contents: write`) to build and publish to the GitHub release:\n\n- **Binaries:** `linux`, `darwin`, `windows` × `amd64`, `arm64` (CGO disabled, stripped)\n- **Archives:** `.tar.gz` (`.zip` on Windows), each bundling `README.md` + `LICENSE`\n- **`checksums.txt`** and a filtered changelog\n\n## Version stamping\n`main.go` now exposes a `version` var wired to cobra's `Version`, injected at release time via `-ldflags -X main.version={{.Version}}`:\n\n```\n$ ccwhid --version\nccwhid version 0.1.0\n```\n\nLocal builds report `dev`.\n\n## Validation\nVerified locally with GoReleaser v2.17 (`go1.26.4`):\n- `goreleaser check` — config valid\n- `goreleaser release --snapshot --clean --skip=publish` — all six targets cross-compiled into archives + `checksums.txt`\n\n## Note on v0.1.0\nThe workflow fires on *new* tags, so the existing `v0.1.0` release won't get assets automatically. After merge, the simplest way to publish binaries is to cut a fresh tag (e.g. `v0.1.1`), which will run the full pipeline.\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nBODY\n)\" 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -2",
  "description": "Open PR for GoReleaser setup"
}

Claude494 tok · ~$0.16

CI passed. PR #4 is green and ready: https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/pull/4

What's set up:

  • .goreleaser.yaml — builds ccwhid for linux/darwin/windows × amd64/arm64 (CGO off, stripped), .tar.gz/.zip archives bundling README + LICENSE, checksums.txt, and a filtered changelog.
  • .github/workflows/release.yml — on a v* tag, runs goreleaser release --clean with contents: write to publish binaries to the GitHub release.
  • ccwhid --version — version var wired to cobra, injected via -ldflags at release time (dev for local builds).

I validated it locally with GoReleaser v2.17: goreleaser check passes and a full snapshot cross-compiled all six targets into archives + checksums. CI on the PR is green.

One thing to note: the workflow fires on new tags, so the existing v0.1.0 release won't get binaries retroactively. After you merge, the cleanest way to publish assets is to cut a fresh tag — e.g. v0.1.1 — which runs the full pipeline and attaches everything automatically. Want me to do that once it's merged? (I also noticed there's a PR #3 in the numbering — likely a Dependabot PR already opened; let me know if you'd like me to look at it.)

Claude993 tok · ~$0.20

Confirmed the root cause: the only commit was ci: add GoReleaser…, which my ^ci: exclude filter dropped — leaving an empty ## Changelog. My git-log-based filters are the wrong tool here anyway. The fix is to delegate to GitHub's native release-notes generator, which lists the merged PRs since the last tag (with authors + a full-changelog link), configurable via .github/release.yml.

Claude
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ngit checkout -b chore/release-changelog 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -1",
  "description": "Create branch for changelog improvement"
}
Zu neuem Branch 'chore/release-changelog' gewechselt
Claude311 tok · ~$0.17
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.goreleaser.yaml
- changelog:
- sort: asc
- use: github
- filters:
- exclude:
- - "^docs:"
- - "^test:"
- - "^ci:"
- - "^chore:"
- - "^Merge "
+ changelog:
+ # Delegate to GitHub's native release-notes generator so each release lists
+ # the pull requests merged since the previous tag, with contributors and a
+ # full-changelog link. Categorisation is configured in .github/release.yml.
+ use: github-native
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.goreleaser.yaml has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
Claude335 tok · ~$0.16
Write~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.github/release.yml
+ # Configures GitHub's automatic release notes (used by the GoReleaser
+ # github-native changelog). PRs are grouped by label; the "*" catch-all
+ # ensures every merged PR is listed even when it carries no matching label.
+ changelog:
+ categories:
+ - title: 🚀 Features
+ labels: ["enhancement", "feature"]
+ - title: 🐛 Fixes
+ labels: ["bug", "fix"]
+ - title: 📦 Dependencies
+ labels: ["dependencies"]
+ - title: 🔧 Maintenance
+ labels: ["chore", "ci", "docs", "test", "refactor"]
+ - title: Other Changes
+ labels: ["*"]
+ 
File created successfully at: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.github/release.yml (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

Claude1k tok · ~$0.19

Config valid, YAML valid, and the native generator responds (it only shows the compare link now because nothing is merged since v0.1.1 yet — once this PR merges, it'll list under "What's Changed"). Note: GitHub reads .github/release.yml from the default branch, so it takes effect once merged. Commit and PR:

Claude
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ngit add .goreleaser.yaml .github/release.yml\ngit commit -q -F - \u003c\u003c'EOF'\nci: generate release changelog from merged PRs\n\nThe git-log changelog filters excluded ^ci:/^chore:/etc., which dropped the\nsole commit in v0.1.1 and left an empty changelog. Switch GoReleaser to\n`use: github-native` so each release lists the pull requests merged since the\nprevious tag (with contributors and a full-changelog link), and add\n.github/release.yml to group those PRs by label with a catch-all so nothing\nis omitted.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) \u003cnoreply@anthropic.com\u003e\nEOF\ngit push -u origin chore/release-changelog 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -2\ngh pr create --base main --head chore/release-changelog \\\n  --title \"ci: generate release changelog from merged PRs\" \\\n  --body \"$(cat \u003c\u003c'BODY'\nFixes the empty changelog on auto-generated releases.\n\n**Problem:** the GoReleaser `changelog` used git-log parsing with `exclude` filters for `^ci:`, `^chore:`, `^docs:`, etc. For `v0.1.1` the only commit was `ci: add GoReleaser…`, which the `^ci:` filter dropped — so the release body was just an empty `## Changelog`.\n\n**Fix:** switch to `changelog.use: github-native`, delegating to GitHub's release-notes generator. Each release now lists the **PRs merged since the previous tag** under \"What's Changed\", with contributors and a full-changelog compare link. Added `.github/release.yml` to group PRs by label (Features / Fixes / Dependencies / Maintenance) with a `\"*\"` catch-all so every PR is listed even without a matching label.\n\nValidated with `goreleaser check`. Effect is visible on the next tag after merge.\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nBODY\n)\" 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -2",
  "description": "Commit, push, open changelog PR"
}
 * [new branch]      chore/release-changelog -> chore/release-changelog

Claude1k tok · ~$0.18

Fixed and verified. The v0.1.2 release now has a real changelog:

What's Changed

Other Changes

  • ci: generate release changelog from merged PRs by @saigyo in #5

Full Changelog: v0.1.1...v0.1.2

Plus all 7 assets (6 binaries + checksums) attached, as before.

What changed: the old config parsed git-log with exclude filters (^ci:, ^chore:, …) that dropped the very commits your releases contained. It now uses GoReleaser's github-native changelog, which delegates to GitHub's release-notes generator — so every release lists the PRs merged since the previous tag, with author, PR link, and a full-changelog compare link. .github/release.yml groups them into Features / Fixes / Dependencies / Maintenance, with a * catch-all so nothing is ever omitted.

One nuance worth knowing: GitHub categorizes by PR labels, not commit-message prefixes. So a PR titled ci: … lands under "Other Changes" unless it carries a matching label — which is why #5 shows there. This is mostly cosmetic since the catch-all guarantees the PR is listed. Two things that "just work" already: Dependabot PRs carry the dependencies label, so they'll auto-group under 📦 Dependencies; and if you start labeling PRs (enhancement, bug, etc.), they'll sort into the right buckets automatically.

If you'd like the nicer categories without manually labeling, I can add a small auto-labeler (e.g. a workflow that applies a label based on the conventional-commit prefix in the PR title). Want that? Otherwise, the changelog requirement is fully met — you now have v0.1.0, v0.1.1, and v0.1.2 released, with v0.1.2 demonstrating the proper PR-listed notes.

You

New feature idea: I'd like to add a section to the report that displays the number of consumed tokens and the estimated cost, like the the "/usage" command in ClaudeCode does, or the popular ccusage tool (see https://github.com/ccusage/ccusage) does. Is this doable?

Claude1k tok · ~$2.88

Good feature idea. Before designing anything, let me verify feasibility by checking whether the transcript actually carries token-usage data — that determines everything.

Claude
Bashcd ~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done\npython3 - \u003c\u003c'EOF'\nimport json, glob\n# inspect the main session transcript for usage + model fields on assistant records\npath = glob.glob(\"41f7e8f4*.jsonl\")[0]\nseen_models = set()\nsample = None\ntotals = {\"input\":0,\"output\":0,\"cache_read\":0,\"cache_creation\":0}\nn_assist = 0\nfor line in open(path):\n    try: r = json.loads(line)\n    except: continue\n    if r.get(\"type\") != \"assistant\": continue\n    msg = r.get(\"message\", {})\n    u = msg.get(\"usage\")\n    if not u: continue\n    n_assist += 1\n    if msg.get(\"model\"): seen_models.add(msg[\"model\"])\n    if sample is None:\n        sample = {\"model\": msg.get(\"model\"), \"usage\": u}\n    totals[\"input\"] += u.get(\"input_tokens\",0)\n    totals[\"output\"] += u.get(\"output_tokens\",0)\n    totals[\"cache_read\"] += u.get(\"cache_read_input_tokens\",0)\n    totals[\"cache_creation\"] += u.get(\"cache_creation_input_tokens\",0)\nprint(\"assistant records with usage:\", n_assist)\nprint(\"models seen:\", seen_models)\nprint(\"first sample:\", json.dumps(sample, indent=2))\nprint(\"naive totals:\", totals)\nEOF",
  "description": "Inspect transcript for token usage and model fields"
}

Claude2k tok · ~$0.23

Yes — this is very doable, and the data is all there. Let me show you what I found, because it shapes the design.

Every assistant record in the transcript already carries full usage:

"model": "claude-opus-4-8",
"usage": {
  "input_tokens": 10223,
  "output_tokens": 192,
  "cache_creation_input_tokens": 3008,   // cache writes (billed higher)
  "cache_read_input_tokens": 18878,      // cache hits (billed ~0.1×)
  "cache_creation": { "ephemeral_1h_input_tokens": 3008, "ephemeral_5m_input_tokens": 0 },
  "server_tool_use": { "web_search_requests": 0, "web_fetch_requests": 0 }
}

This session has 679 assistant messages with usage — the exact same source ccusage and /usage read from. So token counts are exact and free to compute. (Note: I'd exclude <synthetic> model messages, which are local/no-cost.)

The only hard part is cost, and it's a design decision rather than a blocker. Cost = tokens × per-model, per-token-type prices (input / output / cache-write / cache-read differ, and 1h vs 5m cache writes differ). ccusage pulls a live pricing table from LiteLLM. ccwhid is deliberately offline and self-contained, so we can't fetch at render time — we'd embed a small static pricing table. That makes cost an estimate that can drift when prices change or a model isn't in the table.

Since this is a new feature, let me brainstorm the design with you before building.

Claude
Skillbrainstorming
{
  "args": "Add a token-usage + estimated-cost section to the ccwhid HTML report, using the message.usage data already in transcripts. Feasibility confirmed. Need to design: what to display, cost/pricing approach (embedded static table for offline tool), handling unknown models, and whether cost is opt-in.",
  "skill": "brainstorming"
}
Launching skill: brainstorming
Claude3k tok · ~$0.27

I'm in brainstorming mode now — I'll shape this with a few focused questions before writing a spec. Project context is already clear (I know the render pipeline, the offline/self-contained constraint, and the transcript's usage shape), so let me start with the pivotal decision.

The crux is pricing: tokens are exact, but cost needs a per-model price table, and ccwhid is offline by design.

Claude
AskUserQuestionPricing source
Pricing source

How should ccwhid source model prices for the cost estimate?

  • Embedded table + disclaimer

    Bundle current Claude prices in the binary. Show cost with an 'estimated · prices as of YYYY-MM' note; unknown models render tokens-only with 'cost n/a'. Zero network, refreshed when you cut a release. Recommended — simplest, matches the offline design.

    Usage · 946k tokens · ~$12.4 (est.)
prices as of 2026-07 · opus-4.8

unknown-model rows → tokens shown,
cost marked n/a
  • Embedded + --pricing override

    Same embedded table, plus a --pricing <file.json> flag (and a bundled editable JSON) so users can refresh or add prices without rebuilding. More flexible, slightly more surface area.

    ccwhid --latest --pricing ./prices.json

# prices.json overrides / extends
# the embedded defaults per model
  • Tokens only (no cost)

    Ship exact token counts now; defer cost entirely. Never misleading, zero pricing maintenance. Cost can be added later.

    Usage
  input        205,644
  output       767,986
  cache read   119,760,870
  cache write    2,698,793
answered
  • Pricing sourceEmbedded table + disclaimer

Granularity

How detailed should the usage section be?

  • Summary + per-model table

    Grand totals (input / output / cache-read / cache-write tokens + est. cost) plus a per-model breakdown table. Sessions can span models (this one has opus-4.8 + synthetic), so per-model keeps costs honest. Recommended.

    Total  946k tokens  ~$12.4 (est.)
  input 205,644 · output 767,986
  cache read 119.8M · write 2.7M

By model
  opus-4.8   946k   ~$12.4
  synthetic   —      — (no cost)
  • Summary only

    Just the grand totals and total estimated cost — no per-model table. Simplest and most compact; loses per-model attribution in mixed-model sessions.

    Usage
946k tokens  ·  ~$12.4 (est.)
input/output/cache broken out
  • Summary + per-turn costs

    Everything in the per-model option, plus each assistant turn annotated with its own token count / cost. Most granular; adds visual noise and more rendering work.

    … per-model table, plus:

Claude1k tok · ~$0.21

Collapsible top card + inline per-turn badges. Two smaller decisions left, then I'll write the spec.

First — default behaviour and flags. The report is often committed as a shared artifact, so cost (an estimate) might sometimes be unwanted.

Claude
AskUserQuestionDefault & flags
Default & flags

What should the default be, and which opt-outs do you want?

  • On; --no-usage to omit

    Usage section (tokens + cost) is always computed and shown; a single --no-usage flag omits the whole section. Simplest. Recommended.

    ccwhid --latest            → usage shown
ccwhid --latest --no-usage → no section
  • On; --no-usage and --no-cost

    Shown by default; --no-usage omits the section entirely, and --no-cost keeps token counts but hides dollar figures (useful for committed/shared reports where estimated $ is noise). Two small flags.

    --no-usage → no section at all
--no-cost  → tokens only, no $ (est.)
  • Opt-in via --usage

    Off by default; only rendered when --usage is passed. Most conservative, but hides a free, useful stat by default.

    ccwhid --latest          → no usage
ccwhid --latest --usage  → usage shown
answered
  • Default & flagsOpt-in via --usage
Claude6k tok · ~$0.33

That covers the big decisions. Here's the consolidated design — let me get your sign-off before I write the spec.

ccwhid usage & cost section — design

Scope: an opt-in (--usage) report section showing token usage and estimated cost, computed entirely from the message.usage data already in transcripts.

1. Data extraction (transcript → model)

  • Add to model.Turn: Model string and Usage *Usage.
  • New model.Usage{Input, Output, CacheRead, CacheWrite5m, CacheWrite1h int}.
  • transcript decodes message.model + message.usage (including the cache_creation.ephemeral_5m/1h split) per assistant record and attaches it to the turn. Records without usage → nil.

2. Pricing (internal/usage)

  • Embedded static table keyed by model id → per-MTok USD rates for input / output / cache-write-5m / cache-write-1h / cache-read, plus a PricesAsOf date string.
  • I'll populate it from Anthropic's published list prices at implementation time (dev-time fetch to embed accurate numbers; runtime stays fully offline). Unknown model → no price.

3. Computation (internal/usage)

  • Compute(session) Report: cost = Σ(tokens_type × rate_type ÷ 1e6) per model, summed; cache writes use the 5m/1h split. Returns totals, per-model rows, per-turn costs, PricesAsOf, and a HasUnknownModel flag.
  • v1 excludes server-tool request fees (web search/fetch) — noted as future work.
  • <synthetic> and unknown models: tokens counted, cost shown as n/a.

4. Rendering

  • With --usage, a collapsible "Usage" card after the meta line:
    • Collapsed: ▸ Usage · 973k in+out · ~$12.40 (est.) — headline counts input+output so it isn't drowned by cache-reads (119M here).
    • Expanded: full token breakdown (input / output / cache read / cache write) + per-model table (model · tokens · cost/"n/a") + muted footnote: "Estimated — Anthropic list prices as of 2026-07; excludes server-tool fees."
  • Per assistant turn: a small muted badge in the header — · 12.3k tok · ~$0.18 (synthetic/unknown → tokens only).
  • Reuses existing theme/CSS; no new external assets.

5. CLI & TUI

  • CLI: --usage (default false) → render.Options{Usage: true}.
  • TUI options screen: an "Include usage & cost" toggle (off by default).

6. Edge cases

  • --usage but no usage data anywhere → a small "no usage data in transcript" note (so the flag isn't silently empty).
  • Unknown model → tokens shown, cost n/a, footnote flags that totals exclude unpriced models.
  • Numbers aren't secrets → redaction untouched.

7. Testing — usage: price lookup (known/unknown), Compute over a two-model + 5m/1h-cache fixture → exact token totals & cost, unknown→nil cost. transcript: usage/model decoded onto turns. render: card only with --usage, per-turn badge present, unknown-model footnote.

Files: new internal/usage/{pricing.go,usage.go,prices.json,*_test.go}; edits to internal/model, internal/transcript/{raw,parse}.go, internal/render (+ template/CSS), cmd/ccwhid/main.go, internal/tui/tui.go, README.

Two calls I made on your behalf — flag if you disagree: (a) the collapsed headline counts input+output only (cache-read would dominate); (b) v1 leaves web-search/fetch request fees out of the cost. Does this match your intent, or anything to adjust before I write it up?

You

Yes, that's fine. Add as future idea to the spec the option to load prices from a provided file (to override the built-in table or provide prices for unknown models).

Claude678 tok · ~$0.18

Approved — and I'll add the pricing-file override as a documented future idea. Writing the spec now.

Claude

Claude4k tok · ~$0.26
Write~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-usage-cost-section-design.md
+ # ccwhid Usage & Cost Section — Design
+
+ **Status:** approved (2026-07-12)
+
+ **Goal:** Add an opt-in report section that shows token usage and an estimated
+ cost for a rendered session, computed entirely from the `message.usage` data
+ already present in Claude Code transcripts — comparable to `/usage` or the
+ `ccusage` tool, but self-contained and offline.
+
+ ## Motivation
+
+ Every assistant record in a transcript carries a full `usage` object and the
+ `model` id, e.g.:
+
+ ```json
+ "model": "claude-opus-4-8",
+ "usage": {
+ "input_tokens": 10223,
+ "output_tokens": 192,
+ "cache_creation_input_tokens": 3008,
+ "cache_read_input_tokens": 18878,
+ "cache_creation": { "ephemeral_1h_input_tokens": 3008, "ephemeral_5m_input_tokens": 0 },
+ "server_tool_use": { "web_search_requests": 0, "web_fetch_requests": 0 }
+ }
+ ```
+
+ Token counts are therefore **exact and free to compute**. Only cost requires a
+ price table, which is the one estimated element of the feature.
+
+ ## Scope
+
+ - **Opt-in** via a `--usage` flag (default off). The section is only computed
+ and rendered when requested.
+ - Displays, when enabled:
+ 1. A **collapsible "Usage" card** near the top of the report (summary +
+ per-model breakdown).
+ 2. A **per-turn cost badge** on each assistant turn header.
+ - Cost is an **estimate** from an embedded, dated price table.
+
+ ### Out of scope for v1
+
+ - **Server-tool request fees** (web search / web fetch requests have their own
+ per-request pricing). v1 costs LLM token usage only; excluded fees are called
+ out in the report footnote and revisited later (see Future Ideas).
+ - Live/remote price fetching at runtime (the tool is offline by design).
+
+ ## Data Model
+
+ Extend the domain model so usage travels with each turn:
+
+ - `model.Usage{ Input, Output, CacheRead, CacheWrite5m, CacheWrite1h int }`
+ - `model.Turn` gains `Model string` and `Usage *Usage`.
+
+ The `cache_creation.ephemeral_5m_input_tokens` / `ephemeral_1h_input_tokens`
+ split is preserved because 5-minute and 1-hour cache writes are priced
+ differently. If a record only has the aggregate `cache_creation_input_tokens`
+ and no split, the whole amount is treated as a 5-minute write (the cheaper,
+ default TTL).
+
+ ## Transcript Parsing
+
+ `internal/transcript` decodes `message.model` and `message.usage` for each
+ **assistant** record and attaches a `model.Usage` (plus `Model`) to the turn it
+ produces. Records without a `usage` object yield `Usage == nil` (no badge, not
+ counted). User records carry no usage.
+
+ ## Pricing (`internal/usage`)
+
+ - An **embedded static table** (`prices.json`, `go:embed`) keyed by model id.
+ Each entry gives per-million-token USD rates:
+ `input`, `output`, `cacheWrite5m`, `cacheWrite1h`, `cacheRead`.
+ - A `PricesAsOf` date string (e.g. `"2026-07"`) is embedded and surfaced in the
+ report footnote.
+ - The table is **populated from Anthropic's published list prices at
+ implementation time** (a dev-time lookup to embed accurate numbers); the
+ running binary performs no network access. Refreshing prices is a manual
+ edit + release.
+ - Model resolution is exact-id first. `<synthetic>` and any id absent from the
+ table resolve to **no price** (tokens still counted; cost shown as `n/a`).
+
+ ## Computation (`internal/usage`)
+
+ `Compute(session) Report` walks the session's turns (main chain and, when
+ included, nested subagent turns) and aggregates:
+
+ - **Per turn:** token totals and, if the model is priced, a cost.
+ - **Per model:** summed tokens and cost (or `nil` cost when unpriced).
+ - **Grand totals:** summed tokens; total cost = sum of priced models only.
+
+ Cost for a model = Σ over token types of `tokens_type × rate_type ÷ 1_000_000`,
+ using the 5m/1h split for cache writes.
+
+ ```
+ Report{
+ Total TokenCounts
+ TotalCostUSD *float64 // nil only if nothing was priced
+ ByModel []ModelUsage // { Model, Tokens TokenCounts, CostUSD *float64 }
+ PerTurnCost map[int]*float64 // turn index → cost (nil = unpriced/no usage)
+ PricesAsOf string
+ HasUnknownModel bool // some tokens came from an unpriced model
+ HasAnyUsage bool // false → transcript carried no usage at all
+ }
+ ```
+
+ ## Rendering (`internal/render`)
+
+ Rendering is gated on `render.Options{ Usage bool }`.
+
+ **Usage card** (collapsible, placed after the report meta line):
+
+ - Collapsed one-liner:
+ `▸ Usage · 973k in+out · ~$12.40 (est.)`
+ The headline token figure is **input + output only**, so it is not dominated
+ by cache-read volume (which can be orders of magnitude larger).
+ - Expanded body:
+ - Token breakdown: input / output / cache read / cache write.
+ - Per-model table: `model · tokens · cost` (or `n/a`).
+ - Muted footnote:
+ *"Estimated — Anthropic list prices as of `<PricesAsOf>`; excludes
+ server-tool fees."* When `HasUnknownModel`, append: *"totals exclude
+ unpriced models (shown as n/a)."*
+
+ **Per-turn badge:** a small muted element in each assistant turn header, e.g.
+ `· 12.3k tok · ~$0.18`. Unpriced/synthetic turns show `· 12.3k tok` (no cost).
+ Turns without usage show no badge.
+
+ **No-data case:** with `--usage` set but `HasAnyUsage == false`, render a small
+ muted note ("no token-usage data in this transcript") instead of an empty card,
+ so the flag is never silently inert.
+
+ Styling reuses existing report design tokens (warm palette, muted text, the
+ same collapse mechanism as tool cards); no new external assets, preserving the
+ self-contained/offline guarantee. Usage numbers are not secrets, so redaction
+ does not touch them.
+
+ ## CLI & TUI
+
+ - **CLI:** add `--usage` (bool, default false) wired to `render.Options.Usage`.
+ - **TUI:** add an "Include usage & cost" toggle to the options screen (default
+ off), carried on `tui.Selection` and mapped to the same option.
+
+ ## Error Handling & Edge Cases
+
+ - Unpriced model → tokens shown, cost `n/a`, footnote notes excluded totals.
+ - Missing/partial usage on a record → that turn contributes no cost and no
+ badge; parsing never fails on absent fields.
+ - Malformed `usage` JSON → treated as absent (consistent with the parser's
+ existing tolerance), not a fatal error.
+
+ ## Testing
+
+ - `internal/usage`:
+ - price lookup: known id returns rates; unknown / `<synthetic>` returns
+ not-found.
+ - `Compute` over a fixture session spanning two models with a 5m/1h cache
+ split → exact per-type token totals and cost; an unpriced model yields
+ `nil` cost and sets `HasUnknownModel`.
+ - headline in+out figure excludes cache tokens.
+ - `internal/transcript`: a fixture asserts `model`/`usage` (incl. cache split)
+ are decoded onto the produced turns; a record without usage yields
+ `Usage == nil`.
+ - `internal/render`: the usage card renders only with `Usage: true`; a per-turn
+ badge is present; the unknown-model footnote appears; the no-data note renders
+ when usage is absent.
+
+ ## Component Boundaries
+
+ - `internal/usage` owns pricing and aggregation; it depends only on
+ `internal/model`. It has no rendering or I/O concerns and is unit-tested in
+ isolation.
+ - `internal/transcript` is the only place that knows the raw `usage` JSON shape.
+ - `internal/render` consumes a `usage.Report` and knows nothing about pricing
+ math.
+
+ ## Files
+
+ - New: `internal/usage/pricing.go`, `internal/usage/usage.go`,
+ `internal/usage/prices.json`, `internal/usage/*_test.go`.
+ - Edit: `internal/model/model.go`, `internal/transcript/raw.go`,
+ `internal/transcript/parse.go`, `internal/render/render.go`,
+ `internal/render/assets/report.html.tmpl`, `internal/render/assets/styles.css`,
+ `cmd/ccwhid/main.go`, `internal/tui/tui.go`, `README.md`.
+
+ ## Future Ideas
+
+ - **`--pricing <file>` override.** Allow loading a JSON price file at runtime to
+ override the built-in table and/or supply rates for models the embedded table
+ does not know (so users can correct stale prices or price custom/unknown
+ models without rebuilding). The file would be merged over the embedded
+ defaults by model id; the report footnote would note when an override file was
+ used. Deferred from v1 to keep the initial surface small; the pricing lookup
+ is designed so an override layer can slot in cleanly.
+ - **Server-tool fees.** Include web-search / web-fetch request costs
+ (`usage.server_tool_use`) once per-request rates are added to the table.
+ - **Aggregate across sessions.** A future mode could total usage/cost across all
+ sessions in a project (closer to `ccusage`'s daily rollups).
+ 
File created successfully at: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-usage-cost-section-design.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

It captures everything we agreed — opt-in --usage, exact token extraction, embedded dated price table, collapsible top card + per-turn badges, in+out headline, cost n/a for unpriced/synthetic models, redaction untouched, and the component boundaries (internal/usage owns pricing+math, isolated and unit-tested). Please review the spec and let me know if you want any changes before I write the implementation plan.
