Claude5k tok · ~$0.39

Good catch — those numbers should be reconcilable, and they're not. Before I touch the finish flow, let me debug this properly by going to the source data. Let me analyze the actual transcript and compare against both /usage (truth) and ccwhid's logic.

Claude
Bashcd ~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done\npython3 - \u003c\u003c'EOF'\nimport json, glob\nfrom collections import defaultdict\npath = glob.glob(\"41f7e8f4*.jsonl\")[0]\n\nmodels = defaultdict(lambda: {\"recs\":0,\"in\":0,\"out\":0,\"cr\":0,\"cw\":0})\nsidechain = defaultdict(int)\nmsg_ids = defaultdict(int)          # message.id -\u003e count of records carrying usage\ndup_usage_recs = 0\ntotal = {\"in\":0,\"out\":0,\"cr\":0,\"cw\":0}\n\nfor line in open(path):\n    try: r = json.loads(line)\n    except: continue\n    if r.get(\"type\") != \"assistant\": continue\n    m = r.get(\"message\", {})\n    u = m.get(\"usage\")\n    if not u: continue\n    model = m.get(\"model\",\"?\")\n    mid = m.get(\"id\",\"\")\n    msg_ids[mid]+=1\n    sidechain[bool(r.get(\"isSidechain\"))]+=1\n    d = models[model]\n    d[\"recs\"]+=1\n    d[\"in\"]+=u.get(\"input_tokens\",0)\n    d[\"out\"]+=u.get(\"output_tokens\",0)\n    d[\"cr\"]+=u.get(\"cache_read_input_tokens\",0)\n    d[\"cw\"]+=u.get(\"cache_creation_input_tokens\",0)\n    for k,src in [(\"in\",\"input_tokens\"),(\"out\",\"output_tokens\"),(\"cr\",\"cache_read_input_tokens\"),(\"cw\",\"cache_creation_input_tokens\")]:\n        total[k]+=u.get(src,0)\n\nprint(\"=== per-model (top-level usage summed over ALL assistant records) ===\")\nfor model,d in sorted(models.items(), key=[REDACTED:assignment] x:-x[1][\"out\"]):\n    print(f\"{model:20s} recs={d['recs']:4d} in={d['in']:\u003e9,} out={d['out']:\u003e9,} cache_read={d['cr']:\u003e12,} cache_write={d['cw']:\u003e10,}\")\nprint(\"=== totals ===\", {k:f\"{v:,}\" for k,v in total.items()})\nprint(\"=== sidechain distribution ===\", dict(sidechain))\n# duplicate message.id carrying usage?\ndups = {mid:c for mid,c in msg_ids.items() if c\u003e1 and mid}\nprint(\"=== message.ids carrying usage on \u003e1 record ===\", len(dups), \"ids; total extra recs:\", sum(c-1 for c in dups.values()))\n# show a few dup examples\nfor mid,c in list(dups.items())[:5]:\n    print(\"   dup id\", mid[:24], \"count\", c)\nEOF",
  "description": "Analyze raw transcript per-model usage and duplicate detection"
}

Claude3k tok · ~$0.36

Found it. Two things are going on, one of them a real bug:

The transcript streams each assistant message as multiple records — 242 message ids appear on 2–3 records each (425 extra), and ccwhid counts usage on every record. That's double/triple counting. (ccwhid's numbers are actually lower than the naive all-record sum because it drops records that produced no display blocks — so its totals are an inconsistent partial subset, which is exactly why they look fabricated.)

The fix is to count usage once per assistant message id. Let me verify that dedup reproduces /usage's truth:

Claude
Bashcd ~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done\npython3 - \u003c\u003c'EOF'\nimport json, glob\npath = glob.glob(\"41f7e8f4*.jsonl\")[0]\n\n# dedupe by message.id: keep the record with max output_tokens (final streamed usage)\nbest = {}   # mid -\u003e (usage dict, model)\norder = []\nfor line in open(path):\n    try: r = json.loads(line)\n    except: continue\n    if r.get(\"type\") != \"assistant\": continue\n    m = r.get(\"message\", {})\n    u = m.get(\"usage\")\n    if not u: continue\n    mid = m.get(\"id\",\"\") or f\"__noid__{len(order)}\"\n    model = m.get(\"model\",\"?\")\n    cur = best.get(mid)\n    if cur is None or u.get(\"output_tokens\",0) \u003e= cur[0].get(\"output_tokens\",0):\n        best[mid] = (u, model)\n        if cur is None: order.append(mid)\n\ndef tot(pred=lambda model:True):\n    t={\"in\":0,\"out\":0,\"cr\":0,\"cw\":0}\n    for mid,(u,model) in best.items():\n        if not pred(model): continue\n        t[\"in\"]+=u.get(\"input_tokens\",0); t[\"out\"]+=u.get(\"output_tokens\",0)\n        t[\"cr\"]+=u.get(\"cache_read_input_tokens\",0); t[\"cw\"]+=u.get(\"cache_creation_input_tokens\",0)\n    return t\n\nopus = tot(lambda m: m==\"claude-opus-4-8\")\nprint(\"unique assistant messages:\", len(best))\nprint(\"dedup opus-4-8:\", {k:f\"{v:,}\" for k,v in opus.items()})\nprint(\"  /usage opus  : in=92.8k out=437.3k cache_read=88.2M cache_write=2.1M ($74.25)\")\n\n# cost with embedded ccwhid prices: opus-4-8 input $5, output $25 /MTok; cache write 5m 1.25x, read 0.1x input\n# (assume all cache_write is 5m for a rough check)\ninr=5/1e6; outr=25/1e6\ncost = opus[\"in\"]*inr + opus[\"out\"]*outr + opus[\"cw\"]*inr*1.25 + opus[\"cr\"]*inr*0.1\nprint(f\"dedup opus est. cost (all-cw-as-5m): ${cost:.2f}   (/usage opus $74.25)\")\nEOF",
  "description": "Verify dedup-by-message-id reproduces /usage opus figures"
}
unique assistant messages: 390
dedup opus-4-8: {'in': '55,023', 'out': '401,405', 'cr': '81,970,628', 'cw': '1,567,833'}
  /usage opus  : in=92.8k out=437.3k cache_read=88.2M cache_write=2.1M ($74.25)
dedup opus est. cost (all-cw-as-5m): $61.09   (/usage opus $74.25)
Shell cwd was reset to ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
Claude2k tok · ~$0.31

Dedup gets much closer and removes the inflation, but still undershoots /usage's opus line — because /usage aggregates the whole session including the Task sub-agent runs, which are separate transcript files; ccwhid renders one file. That's a legitimate scope difference. Let me confirm the streaming structure so I pick the correct dedup rule (last vs max-output), by inspecting one duplicated message id:

Claude
Bashcd ~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done\npython3 - \u003c\u003c'EOF'\nimport json, glob\npath = glob.glob(\"41f7e8f4*.jsonl\")[0]\ntarget = \"msg_011Ccv2ksza5b7K63LMP\"  # appeared 3x\nrows=[]\nfor i,line in enumerate(open(path)):\n    try: r=json.loads(line)\n    except: continue\n    if r.get(\"type\")!=\"assistant\": continue\n    m=r.get(\"message\",{})\n    if not (m.get(\"id\",\"\").startswith(target)): continue\n    u=m.get(\"usage\",{})\n    blocks=m.get(\"content\",[])\n    btypes=[b.get(\"type\") for b in blocks if isinstance(b,dict)]\n    rows.append((i, u.get(\"input_tokens\"), u.get(\"output_tokens\"), u.get(\"cache_read_input_tokens\"), u.get(\"cache_creation_input_tokens\"), btypes))\nfor row in rows:\n    print(f\"line={row[0]} in={row[1]} out={row[2]} cr={row[3]} cw={row[4]} blocks={row[5]}\")\nEOF",
  "description": "Inspect the multiple records of one message id"
}
line=20 in=10223 out=192 cr=18878 cw=3008 blocks=['thinking']
line=21 in=10223 out=192 cr=18878 cw=3008 blocks=['text']
line=22 in=10223 out=192 cr=18878 cw=3008 blocks=['tool_use']
Shell cwd was reset to ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
Claude4k tok · ~$0.35

Now it's unambiguous. Claude Code writes one assistant message as multiple records — one per content block (thinking / text / tool_use) — and every record repeats the identical usage. ccwhid counts usage per record, so this one message is counted 3×. That's the inflation (and the "fabricated" feel comes from it dropping block-less records, making the multiplier inconsistent).

The scope difference from /usage is separate and expected: /usage sums the whole session including Task sub-agents (separate transcript files); ccwhid reports the one file it renders. The real defect is the per-record double-counting. Let me fix it — dedup usage by message id at parse time. First, the current parser:

Claude
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse.go",
  "limit": 135,
  "offset": 53
}
53	// Parse reads a transcript stream and folds it into a Session timeline.
54	func Parse(r io.Reader, opts Options) (model.Session, error) {
55		var s model.Session
56		// toolIndex maps a tool_use id to the ToolCall pointer so results and
57		// sidechains can be attached after the fact.
58		toolIndex := map[string]*model.ToolCall{}
59		// lastTask tracks the most recent Task tool call; sidechainOwner is the Task
60		// whose Subagents any subsequent sidechain turns are appended to.
61		var lastTask *model.ToolCall
62		sidechainOwner := lastTask
63	
64		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
65		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 1024*1024), 64*1024*1024)
66		for sc.Scan() {
67			line := sc.Bytes()
68			if len(trimSpace(line)) == 0 {
69				continue
70			}
71			var rec rawRecord
72			if err := json.Unmarshal(line, &rec); err != nil {
73				s.SkippedLines++
74				continue
75			}
76			switch rec.Type {
77			case "ai-title":
78				if rec.AiTitle != "" {
79					s.Title = rec.AiTitle
80				}
81				continue
82			case "user", "assistant":
83				// fallthrough to handling below
84			default:
85				continue // ignore mode/permission/attachment/etc. for the timeline
86			}
87			if rec.IsMeta {
88				continue
89			}
90	
91			// Capture session-level metadata from the first record that has it.
92			if s.GitBranch == "" && rec.GitBranch != "" {
93				s.GitBranch = rec.GitBranch
94			}
95			if s.Version == "" && rec.Version != "" {
96				s.Version = rec.Version
97			}
98			if s.ProjectPath == "" && rec.Cwd != "" {
99				s.ProjectPath = rec.Cwd
100			}
101			ts := parseTime(rec.Timestamp)
102			if !ts.IsZero() {
103				if s.StartedAt.IsZero() {
104					s.StartedAt = ts
105				}
106				s.EndedAt = ts
107			}
108	
109			_, blocks := decodeMessageContent(rec.Message)
110	
111			// Sidechain (subagent) records.
112			if rec.IsSidechain {
113				if !opts.IncludeSubagents || sidechainOwner == nil {
114					continue
115				}
116				turn := buildTurn(rec, blocks, toolIndex)
117				if turn == nil {
118					continue
119				}
120				sub := &sidechainOwner.Subagents[len(sidechainOwner.Subagents)-1]
121				sub.Turns = append(sub.Turns, *turn)
122				continue
123			}
124	
125			// Main-chain records.
126			turn := buildTurn(rec, blocks, toolIndex)
127			if turn == nil {
128				continue // e.g. a user record that only carried a tool_result
129			}
130			s.Turns = append(s.Turns, *turn)
131	
132			// Seed a subagent slot for each Task tool call and make it the current
133			// sidechain owner. Tool-use ids are already registered in buildTurn.
134			// Note: sidechain records are attributed to the nearest preceding Task
135			// by file order. Sequential Task calls attribute correctly; multiple
136			// Task calls dispatched in parallel from one assistant message cannot be
137			// disambiguated from the raw records and will all attach to the last
138			// one — a known v1 limitation.
139			if opts.IncludeSubagents {
140				for i := range turn.Blocks {
141					tool := turn.Blocks[i].Tool
142					if tool != nil && tool.Name == "Task" {
143						tool.Subagents = append(tool.Subagents, model.Subagent{
144							Description: taskDescription(tool),
145						})
146						lastTask = tool
147						sidechainOwner = lastTask
148					}
149				}
150			}
151		}
152		if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
153			// A single over-long line (bufio.ErrTooLong) is a pathological record,
154			// not a reason to discard the whole report: count it and return what we
155			// parsed so far.
156			if errors.Is(err, bufio.ErrTooLong) {
157				s.SkippedLines++
158				return s, nil
159			}
160			return s, err
161		}
162		return s, nil
163	}
164	
165	// messageMeta converts a record's message model/usage into turn fields. Only
166	// assistant records carry usage; the aggregate cache_creation figure (with no
167	// 5m/1h split) is attributed to the cheaper 5-minute write.
168	func messageMeta(raw json.RawMessage) (string, *model.Usage) {
169		id, u := decodeMessageMeta(raw)
170		if u == nil {
171			return id, nil
172		}
173		w5, w1 := u.CacheCreation.Ephemeral5m, u.CacheCreation.Ephemeral1h
174		if w5 == 0 && w1 == 0 && u.CacheCreationInputTokens > 0 {
175			w5 = u.CacheCreationInputTokens
176		}
177		return id, &model.Usage{
178			Input:        u.InputTokens,
179			Output:       u.OutputTokens,
180			CacheRead:    u.CacheReadInputTokens,
181			CacheWrite5m: w5,
182			CacheWrite1h: w1,
183		}
184	}
185	
186	// buildTurn converts a record's blocks into a model.Turn. tool_result blocks are
187	// attached to their originating ToolCall via toolIndex and do not themselves
Claude774 tok · ~$0.31

Let me see buildTurn and decodeMessageMeta to implement the dedup precisely:

Claude
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse.go",
  "limit": 40,
  "offset": 186
}
186	// buildTurn converts a record's blocks into a model.Turn. tool_result blocks are
187	// attached to their originating ToolCall via toolIndex and do not themselves
188	// produce turn content. Returns nil if the record yields no displayable blocks.
189	func buildTurn(rec rawRecord, blocks []apiBlock, toolIndex map[string]*model.ToolCall) *model.Turn {
190		turn := &model.Turn{Timestamp: parseTime(rec.Timestamp)}
191		if rec.Type == "user" {
192			turn.Kind = model.TurnUser
193		} else {
194			turn.Kind = model.TurnAssistant
195		}
196		for _, b := range blocks {
197			switch b.Type {
198			case "text":
199				if b.Text != "" {
200					turn.Blocks = append(turn.Blocks, model.Block{Type: model.BlockText, Text: b.Text})
201				}
202			case "thinking":
203				if b.Thinking != "" {
204					turn.Blocks = append(turn.Blocks, model.Block{Type: model.BlockThinking, Text: b.Thinking})
205				}
206			case "tool_use":
207				tc := buildToolCall(b)
208				// Register the tool call so a later tool_result (in this turn's
209				// chain OR inside a sidechain) can be matched back to it by id.
210				toolIndex[tc.ID] = tc
211				turn.Blocks = append(turn.Blocks, model.Block{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc})
212			case "tool_result":
213				if tc := toolIndex[b.ToolUseID]; tc != nil {
214					tc.Result = &model.ToolResult{Content: toolResultText(b.Content), IsError: b.IsError}
215				}
216			}
217		}
218		if len(turn.Blocks) == 0 {
219			return nil
220		}
221		if rec.Type == "assistant" {
222			turn.Model, turn.Usage = messageMeta(rec.Message)
223		}
224		return turn
225	}

86	type apiUsage struct {
87		InputTokens              int `json:"input_tokens"`
88		OutputTokens             int `json:"output_tokens"`
89		CacheReadInputTokens     int `json:"cache_read_input_tokens"`
90		CacheCreationInputTokens int `json:"cache_creation_input_tokens"`
91		CacheCreation            struct {
92			Ephemeral5m int `json:"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens"`
93			Ephemeral1h int `json:"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens"`
94		} `json:"cache_creation"`
95	}
96	
97	// decodeMessageMeta extracts the model id and usage (if any) from a raw message.
98	func decodeMessageMeta(raw json.RawMessage) (modelID string, usage *apiUsage) {
99		if len(raw) == 0 {
100			return "", nil
101		}
102		var m struct {
103			Model string    `json:"model"`
104			Usage *apiUsage `json:"usage"`
105		}
106		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &m); err != nil {
107			return "", nil
108		}
109		return m.Model, m.Usage
110	}
111	
112	func parseTime(s string) time.Time {
113		if s == "" {
114			return time.Time{}
115		}

Now implementing the fix. First, extract the message id in raw.go:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/raw.go
- // decodeMessageMeta extracts the model id and usage (if any) from a raw message.
- func decodeMessageMeta(raw json.RawMessage) (modelID string, usage *apiUsage) {
- if len(raw) == 0 {
- return "", nil
- }
- var m struct {
- Model string `json:"model"`
- Usage *apiUsage `json:"usage"`
- }
- if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &m); err != nil {
- return "", nil
- }
- return m.Model, m.Usage
- }
+ // decodeMessageMeta extracts the message id, model id, and usage (if any) from a
+ // raw message. The message id lets callers deduplicate usage: Claude Code splits
+ // one assistant message across several records (one per content block), each
+ // repeating the same usage object, so usage must be counted once per message id.
+ func decodeMessageMeta(raw json.RawMessage) (msgID, modelID string, usage *apiUsage) {
+ if len(raw) == 0 {
+ return "", "", nil
+ }
+ var m struct {
+ ID string `json:"id"`
+ Model string `json:"model"`
+ Usage *apiUsage `json:"usage"`
+ }
+ if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &m); err != nil {
+ return "", "", nil
+ }
+ return m.ID, m.Model, m.Usage
+ }

- // messageMeta converts a record's message model/usage into turn fields. Only
- // assistant records carry usage; the aggregate cache_creation figure (with no
- // 5m/1h split) is attributed to the cheaper 5-minute write.
- func messageMeta(raw json.RawMessage) (string, *model.Usage) {
- id, u := decodeMessageMeta(raw)
- if u == nil {
- return id, nil
- }
- w5, w1 := u.CacheCreation.Ephemeral5m, u.CacheCreation.Ephemeral1h
- if w5 == 0 && w1 == 0 && u.CacheCreationInputTokens > 0 {
- w5 = u.CacheCreationInputTokens
- }
- return id, &model.Usage{
- Input: u.InputTokens,
- Output: u.OutputTokens,
- CacheRead: u.CacheReadInputTokens,
- CacheWrite5m: w5,
- CacheWrite1h: w1,
- }
- }
+ // messageMeta converts a record's message model/usage into turn fields, also
+ // returning the message id for deduplication. Only assistant records carry
+ // usage; the aggregate cache_creation figure (with no 5m/1h split) is attributed
+ // to the cheaper 5-minute write.
+ func messageMeta(raw json.RawMessage) (msgID, modelID string, usage *model.Usage) {
+ id, mdl, u := decodeMessageMeta(raw)
+ if u == nil {
+ return id, mdl, nil
+ }
+ w5, w1 := u.CacheCreation.Ephemeral5m, u.CacheCreation.Ephemeral1h
+ if w5 == 0 && w1 == 0 && u.CacheCreationInputTokens > 0 {
+ w5 = u.CacheCreationInputTokens
+ }
+ return id, mdl, &model.Usage{
+ Input: u.InputTokens,
+ Output: u.OutputTokens,
+ CacheRead: u.CacheReadInputTokens,
+ CacheWrite5m: w5,
+ CacheWrite1h: w1,
+ }
+ }

Claude411 tok · ~$0.25
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse.go
- if rec.Type == "assistant" {
- turn.Model, turn.Usage = messageMeta(rec.Message)
- }

- }
+ if rec.Type == "assistant" {
+ msgID, mdl, u := messageMeta(rec.Message)
+ turn.Model = mdl
+ // One assistant message is written as several records (one per content
+ // block), each repeating the same usage. Count usage on the first record
+ // of a message id only; later records of the same message get nil usage
+ // so totals are not multiplied by the block count.
+ if u != nil && msgID != "" {
+ if seenUsage[msgID] {
+ u = nil
+ } else {
+ seenUsage[msgID] = true
+ }
+ }
+ turn.Usage = u
+ }
+ return turn
+ }

Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse_test.go
- func TestParseBasicTimeline(t *testing.T) {
+ func TestParseDeduplicatesUsagePerMessageID(t *testing.T) {
+ // Claude Code writes one assistant message as several records (one per
+ // content block), each repeating the same usage. Usage must be counted
+ // once per message id, not once per record.
+ mkRec := func(block string) string {
+ return `{"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","id":"msg_abc","model":"claude-opus-4-8","content":[` +
+ block + `],"usage":{"input_tokens":100,"output_tokens":20,"cache_read_input_tokens":500,"cache_creation_input_tokens":30}},"timestamp":"2026-07-12T10:00:0` + block[:0] + `0Z"}`
+ }
+ lines := strings.Join([]string{
+ mkRec(`{"type":"thinking","thinking":"hmm"}`),
+ mkRec(`{"type":"text","text":"hello"}`),
+ mkRec(`{"type":"tool_use","id":"t1","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"ls"}}`),
+ }, "\n")
+ s, err := Parse(strings.NewReader(lines), Options{})
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ // Three turns are produced (one per record), but exactly one carries usage.
+ withUsage := 0
+ for _, turn := range s.Turns {
+ if turn.Usage != nil {
+ withUsage++
+ }
+ }
+ if withUsage != 1 {
+ t.Fatalf("usage attached to %d turns, want 1 (deduped by message id)", withUsage)
+ }
+ // The one usage-bearing turn holds the message's full (single-counted) usage.
+ for _, turn := range s.Turns {
+ if turn.Usage != nil && (turn.Usage.Input != 100 || turn.Usage.Output != 20 || turn.Usage.CacheRead != 500) {
+ t.Errorf("deduped usage = %+v, want input100/output20/cacheRead500", *turn.Usage)
+ }
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestParseBasicTimeline(t *testing.T) {

{

  "description": "Regenerate report post-fix and compare"
}

claude-opus-4-8</td><td>472k in+out</td><td>$71.50</td>

raw main-file dedup (opus only, computed earlier): 55k in, 401k out, 82.0M cache read, 1.57M cache write, ~$61
Claude2k tok · ~$0.31

The fix works. Cost went from a broken $109.66 → $71.50, and the token breakdown now closely tracks /usage's opus line:

ccwhid (fixed) /usage opus
output 417k 437k
cache read 89.8M 88.2M
cache write 1.6M 2.1M
cost $71.50 $74.25

The remaining gap is the expected scope difference: /usage also folds in the opus Task sub-agent runs (separate transcript files) and the sonnet/haiku sub-agents — ccwhid reports the one transcript it renders. The 2–3× inflation is gone.

Let me add an honesty note to the README about that scope, then commit:

Claude
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md",
  "limit": 14,
  "offset": 90
}
90	
91	By default, ccwhid scrubs common secret shapes (AWS keys, API tokens, JWTs,
92	`KEY=[REDACTED:assignment] assignments) and rewrites your home directory to `~`.
93	This is best-effort defense-in-depth — **review generated reports before
94	committing them.** Disable with `--no-redact`.
95	
96	## What's included
97	
98	Your prompts, Claude's replies (rendered markdown + syntax-highlighted code),
99	tool calls with collapsible detail, `Edit`/`Write` diffs, collapsed thinking
100	blocks, and — optionally — nested subagent activity. System reminders and
101	attachments are omitted for readability.
102	
103	## Token usage & cost
Claude122 tok · ~$0.27
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md",
  "limit": 14,
  "offset": 103
}
103	## Token usage & cost
104	
105	Pass `--usage` (or tick "Include usage & cost" in the TUI) to add a collapsible
106	**Usage** card and per-turn cost badges. Token counts come straight from the
107	transcript's `usage` data and are exact; cost is an **estimate** from a built-in
108	Anthropic list-price table (dated in the report footnote) — unknown models show
109	tokens with cost `n/a`, and server-tool fees are not included. Prices are
110	embedded, so this works fully offline.
111	
112	## License
113	
114	[MIT](LICENSE) © 2026 [user]
115
Claude564 tok · ~$0.26
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md
- Pass `--usage` (or tick "Include usage & cost" in the TUI) to add a collapsible
- **Usage** card and per-turn cost badges. Token counts come straight from the
- transcript's `usage` data and are exact; cost is an **estimate** from a built-in
- Anthropic list-price table (dated in the report footnote) — unknown models show
- tokens with cost `n/a`, and server-tool fees are not included. Prices are
- embedded, so this works fully offline.
+ Pass `--usage` (or tick "Include usage & cost" in the TUI) to add a collapsible
+ **Usage** card and per-turn cost badges. Token counts come straight from the
+ transcript's `usage` data and are exact; cost is an **estimate** from a built-in
+ Anthropic list-price table (dated in the report footnote) — unknown models show
+ tokens with cost `n/a`, and server-tool fees are not included. Prices are
+ embedded, so this works fully offline.
+
+ These figures cover the **single transcript being rendered**. Claude Code stores
+ each `Task` sub-agent / code-review agent as its own transcript file, so their
+ tokens are not included here — which is why the total can be lower than Claude
+ Code's `/usage`, whose session rollup aggregates those sub-agent sessions too.
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ngofmt -l . ; go vet ./... \u003e/dev/null 2\u003e\u00261 \u0026\u0026 echo \"vet ok\"; go test ./... \u003e/dev/null 2\u003e\u00261 \u0026\u0026 echo \"tests ok\"\ngit add internal/transcript/raw.go internal/transcript/parse.go internal/transcript/parse_test.go README.md\ngit commit -q -F - \u003c\u003c'EOF'\nfix(usage): count token usage once per assistant message, not per record\n\nClaude Code writes one assistant message as several JSONL records — one per\ncontent block (thinking/text/tool_use) — each repeating the identical usage\nobject. Counting usage per record inflated every figure 2-3x (and dropping\nblock-less records made the multiplier inconsistent), so the report's cost was\nbadly overstated (~$110 vs a true ~$72 for this session's transcript).\n\nDeduplicate by message id: attach usage to the first record of a message id\nonly; later records of the same message get nil usage. Verified against Claude\nCode's /usage — cost/cache-read/output now agree closely for the rendered\ntranscript (the remaining gap is Task sub-agent sessions, which live in\nseparate transcript files and /usage's rollup includes). Adds a regression\ntest and a README note on the single-transcript scope.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) \u003cnoreply@anthropic.com\u003e\nEOF\necho \"committed $(git rev-parse --short HEAD)\"",
  "description": "Verify and commit the dedup fix"
}

Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ncat \u003e\u003e .superpowers/sdd/progress.md \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n\n## Post-merge-review bug fix (commit 95dcf2d)\nUser compared report vs Claude Code /usage: costs ~fabulated (report $109.66 vs /usage opus $74.25).\nRoot cause (evidence from raw transcript): Claude Code writes ONE assistant message as MULTIPLE records (one per content block: thinking/text/tool_use), each repeating the IDENTICAL usage object. ccwhid counted usage per-record -\u003e 2-3x inflation; and dropping block-less records made it an inconsistent subset (hence \"fabulated\").\nFix: dedupe usage by message id in parser (decodeMessageMeta now returns msg id; buildTurn takes seenUsage map; first record of a message id keeps usage, rest -\u003e nil). Regression test TestParseDeduplicatesUsagePerMessageID.\nValidated vs /usage: after fix report opus = 55k in / 417k out / 89.8M cache read / 1.6M cache write / $71.50, vs /usage opus 92.8k/437.3k/88.2M/2.1M/$74.25 — close; remaining gap is Task sub-agent SESSIONS (separate transcript files) which /usage's session rollup includes but ccwhid (single transcript) does not. README note added on this scope.\nAll 8 packages green, vet/gofmt clean.\nEOF\ngit add .superpowers/sdd/progress.md \u003e/dev/null 2\u003e\u00261; echo \"ledger updated (gitignored scratch, not committed)\"\necho \"commits ahead of main: $(git rev-list --count main..HEAD)\"",
  "description": "Update ledger with bug fix details"
}

Claude479 tok · ~$0.26

Good catch — that was a genuine bug, and it's now fixed and validated against ground truth.

Root cause: Claude Code writes a single assistant message as multiple JSONL records (one per content block — thinking, text, tool_use), each repeating the identical usage object. ccwhid summed usage per record, inflating everything 2–3× — and because it dropped records that produced no display blocks, the multiplier was inconsistent, which is exactly why the numbers looked fabricated.

Fix: deduplicate by message id in the parser — the first record of a message keeps its usage, later records get nil. Regression test added.

Validated against /usage: cost dropped from a bogus $109.66 to $71.50, and cache-read (89.8M vs 88.2M) and output (417k vs 437k) now line up with /usage's opus line. The residual gap is the expected scope difference — /usage aggregates the Task sub-agent sessions (haiku/sonnet/opus, stored as separate transcript files); ccwhid reports the one transcript it renders. I added a README note making that explicit.

All 8 packages green, vet/gofmt clean. The branch is now 9 commits ahead of main.

Back to finishing the branch:

  1. Merge back to main locally
  2. Push and create a Pull Request
  3. Keep the branch as-is
  4. Discard this work

Which option?

You

One more thing: you aggregated the usage by model line into two numbers. What prevents us from giving the same level of detail per model the "/usage" output gives us? [Image #4]

Claude2k tok · ~$0.31

Nothing prevents it — the per-model data is already computed and carried in usage.ModelUsage.Tokens (full TokenCounts); the render layer just collapsed it to in+out. This is a render-only change. Let me look at the current code:

{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go",
  "limit": 100,

}
88		Badge      string // per-turn usage badge, e.g. "12k tok · ~$0.18"
89	}
90	
91	type usageView struct {
92		HasAny   bool
93		Headline template.HTML // collapsed one-line summary; safe, built only from our own formatted numbers/words
94		Rows     []usageRow    // token breakdown
95		Models   []usageModel  // per-model table rows
96		Footnote string
97	}
98	
99	type usageRow struct {
100		Label string
101		Value string
102	}
103	
104	type usageModel struct {
105		Model  string
106		Tokens template.HTML // safe, built only from our own formatted numbers/words
107		Cost   string        // "$1.23" or "n/a"
108	}
109	
110	func buildViewModel(s model.Session, title string, opts Options) viewData {
111		d := viewData{
112			Title:     title,
113			Session:   s,
114			TurnCount: len(s.Turns),
115		}
116		if !s.StartedAt.IsZero() {
117			d.StartedAt = s.StartedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04")
118		}
119	
120		var rep usage.Report
121		if opts.Usage {
122			rep = usage.Compute(s)
123			d.Usage = buildUsageView(rep)
124		}
125	
126		for i, t := range s.Turns {
127			plain := turnPlainText(t)
128			if t.Kind == model.TurnUser {
129				d.Prompts = append(d.Prompts, promptRef{Index: i, Preview: preview(plain, 60)})
130			}
131			tv := turnView{
132				Index:      i,
133				Kind:       string(t.Kind),
134				RoleLabel:  roleLabel(t.Kind),
135				SearchText: strings.ToLower(plain),
136				Body:       renderTurnBody(t),
137			}
138			if opts.Usage && t.Usage != nil {
139				tv.Badge = turnBadge(*t.Usage, rep.PerTurnCost[i])
140			}
141			d.Turns = append(d.Turns, tv)
142		}
143		return d
144	}
145	
146	// turnBadge formats the per-turn usage badge: "12k tok" plus "· ~$0.18" if priced.
147	func turnBadge(u model.Usage, costUSD *float64) string {
148		b := formatTokens(u.Input+u.Output) + " tok"
149		if costUSD != nil {
150			b += " · ~" + formatCost(*costUSD)
151		}
152		return b
153	}
154	
155	// buildUsageView turns a usage.Report into the template-facing view model.
156	func buildUsageView(r usage.Report) *usageView {
157		v := &usageView{HasAny: r.HasAnyUsage}
158		if !r.HasAnyUsage {
159			v.Headline = template.HTML("Usage · no token-usage data")
160			return v
161		}
162		headline := "Usage · " + formatTokens(r.Total.InOut()) + " in+out"
163		if r.TotalCostUSD != nil {
164			headline += " · ~" + formatCost(*r.TotalCostUSD) + " (est.)"
165		}
166		// html.EscapeString (unlike html/template's auto-escaper) leaves "+" intact
167		// while still escaping "<"/"&" etc.; headline is built solely from our own
168		// formatted numbers and literal words, so this is safe to mark as raw HTML.
169		v.Headline = template.HTML(html.EscapeString(headline))
170	
171		v.Rows = []usageRow{
172			{"input", formatTokens(r.Total.Input)},
173			{"output", formatTokens(r.Total.Output)},
174			{"cache read", formatTokens(r.Total.CacheRead)},
175			{"cache write", formatTokens(r.Total.CacheWrite5m + r.Total.CacheWrite1h)},
176		}
177		for _, m := range r.ByModel {
178			row := usageModel{Model: m.Model, Tokens: template.HTML(html.EscapeString(formatTokens(m.Tokens.InOut()) + " in+out")), Cost: "n/a"}
179			if m.CostUSD != nil {
180				row.Cost = formatCost(*m.CostUSD)
181			}
182			v.Models = append(v.Models, row)
183		}
184		foot := "Estimated — Anthropic list prices as of " + r.PricesAsOf + "; excludes server-tool fees."
185		if r.HasUnknownModel {
186			foot += " Totals exclude unpriced models (shown as n/a)."
187		}
Claude3k tok · ~$0.35

This is purely a presentation change — the full TokenCounts per model is already in the report. I'll expand the per-model table to input / output / cache read / cache write / cost columns with a Total row (matching /usage), which also lets me drop the now-redundant separate totals table and the template.HTML on the cells (only the headline keeps it). Let me refactor the view model:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go
- type usageView struct {
- HasAny bool
- Headline template.HTML // collapsed one-line summary; safe, built only from our own formatted numbers/words
- Rows []usageRow // token breakdown
- Models []usageModel // per-model table rows
- Footnote string
- }
-
- type usageRow struct {
- Label string
- Value string
- }
-
- type usageModel struct {
- Model string
- Tokens template.HTML // safe, built only from our own formatted numbers/words
- Cost string // "$1.23" or "n/a"
- }
+ type usageView struct {
+ HasAny bool
+ Headline template.HTML // collapsed one-line summary; safe, built only from our own formatted numbers/words
+ Models []usageModel // per-model rows, full token breakdown
+ Total usageModel // grand-total row
+ Footnote string
+ }
+
+ // usageModel is one row of the per-model table (or the Total row). All fields
+ // are plain formatted strings, auto-escaped by html/template.
+ type usageModel struct {
+ Model string // model id, or "Total" for the summary row
+ Input string
+ Output string
+ CacheRead string
+ CacheWrite string
+ Cost string // "$1.23" or "n/a"
+ }

{

155	// buildUsageView turns a usage.Report into the template-facing view model.
156	func buildUsageView(r usage.Report) *usageView {
157		v := &usageView{HasAny: r.HasAnyUsage}
158		if !r.HasAnyUsage {
159			v.Headline = template.HTML("Usage · no token-usage data")
160			return v
161		}
162		headline := "Usage · " + formatTokens(r.Total.InOut()) + " in+out"
163		if r.TotalCostUSD != nil {
164			headline += " · ~" + formatCost(*r.TotalCostUSD) + " (est.)"
165		}
166		// html.EscapeString (unlike html/template's auto-escaper) leaves "+" intact
167		// while still escaping "<"/"&" etc.; headline is built solely from our own
168		// formatted numbers and literal words, so this is safe to mark as raw HTML.
169		v.Headline = template.HTML(html.EscapeString(headline))
170	
171		for _, m := range r.ByModel {
172			v.Models = append(v.Models, modelRow(m.Model, m.Tokens, m.CostUSD))
173		}
174		v.Total = modelRow("Total", r.Total, r.TotalCostUSD)
175		foot := "Estimated — Anthropic list prices as of " + r.PricesAsOf + "; excludes server-tool fees."
176		if r.HasUnknownModel {
177			foot += " Totals exclude unpriced models (shown as n/a)."
178		}
179		v.Footnote = foot
180		return v
181	}
182

184		if k == model.TurnUser {

+ // modelRow formats one per-model (or Total) table row from a token bucket and
+ // optional cost.
+ func modelRow(name string, t usage.TokenCounts, costUSD *float64) usageModel {
+ row := usageModel{
+ Model: name,
+ Input: formatTokens(t.Input),
+ Output: formatTokens(t.Output),
+ CacheRead: formatTokens(t.CacheRead),
+ CacheWrite: formatTokens(t.CacheWrite5m + t.CacheWrite1h),
+ Cost: "n/a",
+ }
+ if costUSD != nil {
+ row.Cost = formatCost(*costUSD)
+ }
+ return row
+ }

+ "usage-card", "usage-badge", "in+out", "prices as of", "claude-opus-4-8",
+ // per-model table carries the full breakdown + a Total row
+ "<th>input</th>", "<th>output</th>", "<th>cache read</th>", "<th>cache write</th>",
+ "usage-total", "Total",
+ } {
+ if !strings.Contains(html, want) {
+ t.Errorf("usage output missing %q", want)
+ }
+ }
+ }

Claude300 tok · ~$0.27

Done. The per-model table now gives the same granularity as /usage — input, output, cache read, cache write, and est. cost per model, with a Total row. Nothing prevented it: the full TokenCounts was already computed per model; the render layer had just collapsed it to in+out. Committed (dea9a4a), branch is 10 commits ahead of main, all tests green.

To recap this session's two follow-ups on the usage feature:

  • Enhanced the per-model table to full /usage-style detail.

Claude5k tok · ~$0.39

Two good refinements. Both are in buildUsageView: (1) skip all-zero per-model rows, and (2) since dropping a zero unpriced row shouldn't leave a dangling "unpriced" note, derive that note from the kept rows; plus expand the footnote to note sub-agent sessions are excluded. Let me update:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go
- for _, m := range r.ByModel {
- v.Models = append(v.Models, modelRow(m.Model, m.Tokens, m.CostUSD))
- }
- v.Total = modelRow("Total", r.Total, r.TotalCostUSD)
- foot := "Estimated — Anthropic list prices as of " + r.PricesAsOf + "; excludes server-tool fees."
- if r.HasUnknownModel {
- foot += " Totals exclude unpriced models (shown as n/a)."
- }
- v.Footnote = foot
- return v
- }
+ hasUnpriced := false
+ for _, m := range r.ByModel {
+ if m.Tokens == (usage.TokenCounts{}) {
+ continue // drop all-zero rows (e.g. <synthetic> with no tokens)
+ }
+ v.Models = append(v.Models, modelRow(m.Model, m.Tokens, m.CostUSD))
+ if m.CostUSD == nil {
+ hasUnpriced = true
+ }
+ }
+ v.Total = modelRow("Total", r.Total, r.TotalCostUSD)
+
+ foot := "Estimated — Anthropic list prices as of " + r.PricesAsOf +
+ ". Covers this transcript only; sub-agent sessions stored as separate files, and server-tool fees, are excluded."
+ if hasUnpriced {
+ foot += " Totals exclude unpriced models (shown as n/a)."
+ }
+ v.Footnote = foot
+ return v
+ }

+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }

+ t.Error("all-zero <synthetic> row should be dropped from the usage table")

+ if strings.Contains(html, "unpriced models") {
+ t.Error("footnote should not mention unpriced models when the only unpriced model was dropped")
+ }
+ if !strings.Contains(html, "sub-agent sessions") {
+ t.Error("footnote should note sub-agent sessions are excluded")
+ }
+ }
+

Claude316 tok · ~$0.27

Both refinements shipped (b302da3):

  1. All-zero model rows dropped — <synthetic> (and any zero-token model) no longer clutters the table; it now shows only models that actually consumed tokens, plus the Total.
  2. Disclaimer expanded — now reads "…Covers this transcript only; sub-agent sessions stored as separate files, and server-tool fees, are excluded." — which also directly explains the divergence from /usage.

As a bonus, the "unpriced models" note is now derived from the displayed rows, so dropping a zero unpriced row won't leave a dangling note. Tests pass (added a focused regression test), vet/gofmt clean. Branch is 11 commits ahead of main.

Claude1k tok · ~$0.29
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ngh pr create --base main --head feat/usage-cost \\\n  --title \"Token usage \u0026 estimated cost report section (--usage)\" \\\n  --body \"$(cat \u003c\u003c'BODY'\nAdds an opt-in token-usage \u0026 estimated-cost section to the ccwhid report, computed from the `message.usage` data already in transcripts — comparable to Claude Code's `/usage`, but self-contained and offline.\n\n## Usage\n```\nccwhid --latest --usage        # or tick \"Include usage \u0026 cost\" in the TUI\n```\n\n## What it shows\n- A collapsible **Usage** card: collapsed headline (`Usage · 472k in+out · ~$71.50 (est.)`), and expanded, a **per-model table** with `input / output / cache read / cache write / est. cost` and a **Total** row — matching `/usage`'s detail.\n- Per-turn cost badges on assistant turns.\n- Cost is an **estimate** from an embedded, dated Anthropic price table; unknown/`\u003csynthetic\u003e` models show tokens with cost `n/a`; all-zero rows are dropped.\n\n## Design\n- `internal/transcript` attaches per-turn `Model` + `Usage` (with the 5m/1h cache-write split).\n- `internal/usage` owns an embedded `prices.json` + the aggregation/cost math (`Compute → Report`); cache rates derive from base input via the universal 1.25×/2×/0.1× multipliers.\n- `internal/render` renders the card/badges, gated on `--usage`.\n- Built via subagent-driven development: 5 task-reviewed tasks + a whole-branch review (which caught a dark-mode CSS defect, fixed).\n\n## Correctness note (important)\nClaude Code writes one assistant message as several JSONL records (one per content block), each repeating the identical `usage`. An initial version counted per-record and overstated cost ~1.5×. Fixed to **count usage once per message id**, validated against `/usage`: for this session the opus figures now line up (cache-read 89.8M vs 88.2M, output 417k vs 437k, ~$72 vs $74.25). The residual gap is by design — ccwhid reports the single rendered transcript; `/usage` aggregates Task sub-agent sessions stored as separate files. The report footnote states this scope explicitly.\n\n## Scope / non-goals (v1)\n- Server-tool (web search/fetch) request fees excluded.\n- Sub-agent sessions stored as separate transcript files are not aggregated.\n- A `--pricing \u003cfile\u003e` override to refresh/extend prices is noted as future work in the spec.\n\nDocs: [spec](docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-usage-cost-section-design.md), [plan](docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-12-usage-cost.md). All 8 packages green; `go vet`/`gofmt` clean.\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nBODY\n)\" 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -3",
  "description": "Create the usage-cost PR"
}

Claude441 tok · ~$0.27

Pushed and PR is up — https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/pull/6 — with CI green.

Per the finish flow for Option 2, the branch stays as-is so you can iterate on any review feedback; nothing merged, worktree preserved.

Summary of the branch (11 commits):

  • Opt-in --usage flag + TUI toggle → collapsible Usage card with a full per-model breakdown (input / output / cache read / cache write / cost) + Total row, plus per-turn badges.
  • New internal/usage package: embedded dated price table + cost math (cache rates derived via the universal 1.25×/2×/0.1× multipliers).
  • Built via subagent-driven development (5 task reviews + whole-branch review).
  • Two correctness fixes from your testing: the per-record double-counting bug (now counted once per message id, reconciled against /usage) and the dark-mode CSS defect; plus the two polish items (drop zero rows, sub-agent exclusion note).

The PR body documents the design, the /usage reconciliation, and the v1 scope (server-tool fees and separate sub-agent session files excluded; --pricing override noted as future work).

Anything you'd like tweaked on the PR, or shall I leave it for review?

You

Claude771 tok · ~$0.29

Empty memory dir — I'll create the memory file and the index.

Claude
Write~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done/memory/subagent-commits-coauthored-by.md
+ ---
+ name: subagent-commits-coauthored-by
+ description: When dispatching subagents that commit, require a Co-Authored-By trailer identifying the subagent's own model
+ metadata:
+ type: feedback
+ ---
+
+ Subagents that create commits (e.g. subagent-driven-development implementers dispatched via the Agent tool) must be instructed to add a `Co-Authored-By:` trailer to **every** commit they make, identifying **their own model** — not the controller's.
+
+ **Why:** In the usage-cost SDD run, some implementer subagents omitted the trailer while others added it, leaving the branch with a mix. The user wants every commit to carry a `Co-Authored-By` line, and it should credit the model that actually authored the work. A blanket controller-model line would misattribute a Haiku/Sonnet implementer's commit as Opus.
+
+ **How to apply:** In each committing subagent's dispatch prompt, give the exact trailer for the model you assigned it and require it on every commit, e.g. for a Haiku implementer:
+ `Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>`
+ Match the name to the dispatched model (Opus 4.8, Sonnet 5, Haiku 4.5, Fable 5, …). If a subagent's commits still come back without it, fix them with a message-only rebase (`git rebase <base> --exec '… || git commit --amend --no-edit --trailer "…"'`) and force-push with `--force-with-lease`.
+ 
File created successfully at: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done/memory/subagent-commits-coauthored-by.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

Claude209 tok · ~$0.27
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\necho \"=== PR #6 inline review comments ===\"\ngh api repos/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/pulls/6/comments --jq '.[] | \"\\(.created_at)  \\(.path):\\(.line)\\n  \\(.body)\\n\"' 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -60",

=== PR #6 inline review comments ===
2026-07-12T07:25:43Z  internal/render/format.go:18
  `formatTokens` rounds the "k" value, which can produce awkward boundary outputs like `1000k` for values just under 1M (e.g., 999_999). This is a real display bug in the usage headline/table and badges.

Consider clamping the `k` path so it never rounds up to `1000k` (switching to the `M` formatter instead).

2026-07-12T07:25:43Z  internal/usage/usage.go:79
  `Compute` aggregates usage by `t.Model` verbatim. Since `Lookup` explicitly supports dated model IDs by stripping a trailing `-YYYYMMDD`, sessions that mix dated/undated IDs for the same base model will show as separate per-model rows (and potentially multiple rows for the same priced model).

Normalize the model ID (e.g., via `stripDateSuffix`) before using it as the aggregation key (and before appending to `order`) so the per-model table groups consistently.

2026-07-12T07:25:43Z  docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-usage-cost-section-design.md:71
  The design doc says each `prices.json` entry includes cache read/write rates (`cacheWrite5m`, `cacheWrite1h`, `cacheRead`), but the implementation only stores base `input`/`output` and derives cache pricing from `input` via multipliers. This mismatch can confuse future updates to the embedded pricing table.

Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/format.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/format.go"
}
1	package render
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strconv"
6	)
7	
8	// formatTokens renders a token count compactly: 192, 12k, 973k, 1.2M.
9	func formatTokens(n int) string {
10		switch {
11		case n >= 1_000_000:
12			return fmt.Sprintf("%.1fM", float64(n)/1e6)
13		case n >= 1_000:
14			return fmt.Sprintf("%.0fk", float64(n)/1e3)
15		default:
16			return strconv.Itoa(n)
17		}
18	}
19	
20	// formatCost renders a USD cost as $X.XX, or <$0.01 for tiny non-zero amounts.
21	func formatCost(c float64) string {
22		if c > 0 && c < 0.005 {
23			return "<$0.01"
24		}
25		return fmt.Sprintf("$%.2f", c)
26	}
27
Claude129 tok · ~$0.30
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/usage/usage.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/usage/usage.go",
  "limit": 40,
  "offset": 58
}
58	// Compute aggregates token usage and estimated cost for a session. Totals and
59	// per-model figures include nested subagent turns; per-turn costs are recorded
60	// for top-level turns only.
61	func Compute(s model.Session) Report {
62		r := Report{PricesAsOf: PricesAsOf, PerTurnCost: map[int]*float64{}}
63		byModel := map[string]*TokenCounts{}
64		var order []string
65	
66		accumulate := func(t model.Turn) {
67			if t.Usage == nil {
68				return
69			}
70			r.HasAnyUsage = true
71			r.Total.add(*t.Usage)
72			tc, ok := byModel[t.Model]
73			if !ok {
74				tc = &TokenCounts{}
75				byModel[t.Model] = tc
76				order = append(order, t.Model)
77			}
78			tc.add(*t.Usage)
79		}
80	
81		// Totals + per-model: walk top-level and nested subagent turns.
82		for _, t := range s.Turns {
83			walk(t, accumulate)
84		}
85	
86		// Per-turn cost for top-level turns only.
87		for i, t := range s.Turns {
88			if t.Usage == nil {
89				continue
90			}
91			if p, ok := Lookup(t.Model); ok {
92				c := cost(counts(*t.Usage), p)
93				r.PerTurnCost[i] = &c
94			} else {
95				r.PerTurnCost[i] = nil
96			}
97		}

63	**assistant** record and attaches a `model.Usage` (plus `Model`) to the turn it
64	produces. Records without a `usage` object yield `Usage == nil` (no badge, not
65	counted). User records carry no usage.
66	
67	## Pricing (`internal/usage`)
68	
69	- An **embedded static table** (`prices.json`, `go:embed`) keyed by model id.
70	  Each entry gives per-million-token USD rates:
71	  `input`, `output`, `cacheWrite5m`, `cacheWrite1h`, `cacheRead`.
72	- A `PricesAsOf` date string (e.g. `"2026-07"`) is embedded and surfaced in the
73	  report footnote.
74	- The table is **populated from Anthropic's published list prices at
75	  implementation time** (a dev-time lookup to embed accurate numbers); the
76	  running binary performs no network access. Refreshing prices is a manual
77	  edit + release.
78	- Model resolution is exact-id first. `<synthetic>` and any id absent from the
Claude544 tok · ~$0.28

Now applying all three fixes.

Fix 1 — formatTokens never emits 1000k:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/format.go
- // formatTokens renders a token count compactly: 192, 12k, 973k, 1.2M.
- func formatTokens(n int) string {
- switch {
- case n >= 1_000_000:
- return fmt.Sprintf("%.1fM", float64(n)/1e6)
- case n >= 1_000:
- return fmt.Sprintf("%.0fk", float64(n)/1e3)
- default:
- return strconv.Itoa(n)
- }
- }
+ // formatTokens renders a token count compactly: 192, 12k, 973k, 1.2M. The M
+ // threshold is 999_500 (not 1_000_000) so values that would round up to
+ // "1000k" render as "1.0M" instead.
+ func formatTokens(n int) string {
+ switch {
+ case n >= 999_500:
+ return fmt.Sprintf("%.1fM", float64(n)/1e6)
+ case n >= 1_000:
+ return fmt.Sprintf("%.0fk", float64(n)/1e3)
+ default:
+ return strconv.Itoa(n)
+ }
+ }

Fix 2 — normalize the model id (strip date suffix) before aggregating in Compute:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/usage/usage.go
- r.HasAnyUsage = true
- r.Total.add(*t.Usage)
- tc, ok := byModel[t.Model]
- if !ok {
- tc = &TokenCounts{}
- byModel[t.Model] = tc
- order = append(order, t.Model)

+ r.HasAnyUsage = true
+ r.Total.add(*t.Usage)
+ // Normalize the key so dated and undated ids for the same base model
+ // (e.g. claude-haiku-4-5 and claude-haiku-4-5-20251001) group into one
+ // row, matching how Lookup resolves prices.
+ key := stripDateSuffix(t.Model)
+ tc, ok := byModel[key]
+ if !ok {
+ tc = &TokenCounts{}
+ byModel[key] = tc
+ order = append(order, key)
+ }
+ tc.add(*t.Usage)

Claude429 tok · ~$0.28

Fix 3 — correct the spec's pricing description:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-usage-cost-section-design.md
- - An **embedded static table** (`prices.json`, `go:embed`) keyed by model id.
- Each entry gives per-million-token USD rates:
- `input`, `output`, `cacheWrite5m`, `cacheWrite1h`, `cacheRead`.
+ - An **embedded static table** (`prices.json`, `go:embed`) keyed by model id.
+ Each entry stores only the two base per-million-token USD rates, `input` and
+ `output`. Cache rates are **derived** from `input` via the universal Anthropic
+ multipliers (5m write = 1.25×, 1h write = 2×, cache read = 0.1×) applied in the
+ cost function, so they are not duplicated per model in the table.
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-usage-cost-section-design.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

- func TestFormatTokens(t *testing.T) {
- cases := map[int]string{0: "0", 192: "192", 12300: "12k", 973000: "973k", 1_200_000: "1.2M"}
- for n, want := range cases {
- if got := formatTokens(n); got != want {
- t.Errorf("formatTokens(%d) = %q, want %q", n, got, want)
- }
- }
- }
+ func TestFormatTokens(t *testing.T) {
+ cases := map[int]string{
+ 0: "0", 192: "192", 12300: "12k", 973000: "973k", 1_200_000: "1.2M",
+ // boundary: must never render "1000k"
+ 999_499: "999k", 999_999: "1.0M", 1_000_000: "1.0M",
+ }
+ for n, want := range cases {
+ if got := formatTokens(n); got != want {
+ t.Errorf("formatTokens(%d) = %q, want %q", n, got, want)
+ }
+ }
+ }

Claude446 tok · ~$0.28

All three Copilot findings addressed and pushed to PR #6 (0afbbf8), with a summary comment posted.

Finding Fix
formatTokens could emit 1000k (e.g. 999,999) M threshold lowered to 999,500 → renders 1.0M; boundary tests added
Compute grouped dated vs undated model ids separately Normalize the key via stripDateSuffix before aggregating → one row per base model; test added
Spec claimed prices.json stores cache rates Corrected: it stores only base input/output; cache rates derive via the 1.25×/2×/0.1× multipliers

The model-grouping one was the most substantive — it's a real correctness issue for any transcript mixing dated/undated ids for the same model (they'd have shown as duplicate per-model rows with split, misleading costs); it now aggregates consistently with how prices are looked up. The rounding fix is a display polish, and the spec correction keeps the design doc honest for future price-table edits. Tests, vet, gofmt all clean.

The commit carries the Co-Authored-By line — and per the memory I just saved, any future committing subagents will be instructed to add their own. PR #6 is updated and green; anything else you'd like before merge?

2026-07-12T07:34:24Z  internal/usage/usage.go:76
  If an assistant turn has usage but no model id in the transcript, `key := stripDateSuffix(t.Model)` becomes empty, producing a blank per-model row in the Usage table. Consider mapping empty model ids to a placeholder (e.g. "<unknown>") so the report stays readable and such usage is clearly attributed.

2026-07-12T07:34:24Z  internal/render/render.go:187
  The footnote says "Totals exclude unpriced models", but the token totals still include unpriced models; only the total *estimated cost* excludes them (since `TotalCostUSD` is the sum of priced models). Rewording avoids implying tokens are excluded.
Claude2k tok · ~$0.32

Two more valid findings. Let me fix both.

Fix 1 — empty model id → <unknown> placeholder in Compute:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/usage/usage.go
- // Normalize the key so dated and undated ids for the same base model
- // (e.g. claude-haiku-4-5 and claude-haiku-4-5-20251001) group into one
- // row, matching how Lookup resolves prices.
- key := stripDateSuffix(t.Model)
- tc, ok := byModel[key]
+ // Normalize the key so dated and undated ids for the same base model
+ // (e.g. claude-haiku-4-5 and claude-haiku-4-5-20251001) group into one
+ // row, matching how Lookup resolves prices. A missing model id is
+ // labeled so it never renders as a blank row.
+ key := stripDateSuffix(t.Model)
+ if key == "" {
+ key=[REDACTED:assignment]"
+ }

Claude304 tok · ~$0.28

Both findings addressed and pushed to PR #6 (b26d84c), with a summary comment posted.

Finding Fix
Empty model id → blank per-model row Turns with no model id now group under <unknown>; test added
Footnote implied token totals exclude unpriced models Reworded — only the cost excludes them; tokens are still counted

The footnote one was a genuine accuracy fix: unpriced models' tokens are included in the token totals (input/output/cache), and only TotalCostUSD sums priced models — the old wording ("Totals exclude…") wrongly implied the token counts dropped them too. Tests, vet, gofmt all clean.

Both carry the Co-Authored-By line. PR #6 is updated and green — let me know if the reviewer surfaces anything else, or if you'd like to merge.

## What's Changed

Claude505 tok · ~$0.29

v0.2.0 is released 🎉 → https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/releases/tag/v0.2.0

Fully automated via GoReleaser on the tag push:

  • Published (not draft/prerelease).
  • All 6 platform binaries + checksums.txt attached (linux/darwin/windows × amd64/arm64).
  • Changelog lists the merged PR: "Token usage & estimated cost report section (--usage) by @saigyo in #6" with a full v0.1.2...v0.2.0 compare link.

Install:

go install github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid@v0.2.0

Local main is synced and the feat/usage-cost branch cleaned up. The one annotation is the familiar non-blocking Node 20 deprecation on goreleaser-action — Dependabot will bump it. That wraps up the usage & cost feature end to end: design → SDD implementation → review rounds → two field-tested fixes → merge → tagged release with binaries.

You

/compact

You

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:

  1. Primary Request and Intent: This conversation continued development of ccwhid (cc-what-have-i-done), a Go CLI that transforms Claude Code JSONL session transcripts into browsable, self-contained HTML reports. Explicit user requests in chronological order:

    • Fix the TUI: first screen unusable with many projects/sessions (no scrolling); implement two-level navigation (project list → session list) with scrolling, cursor-left to return to project list; show only "root sessions" by default with a key to expand/collapse all sessions; diagnose why --project didn't work (with name or path).
    • After merge: Add README screenshots of the TUI and the generated HTML report.
    • Add ANSI-stripping to the renderer (leaked terminal escape codes in reports).
    • Regenerate the demo report and screenshots with the ANSI fix.
    • Set up CI as a GitHub Actions workflow and add a Dependabot config.
    • Tag a v0.1.0 release.
    • Set up GoReleaser to attach prebuilt binaries to releases.
    • Merge the GoReleaser PR, cut v0.1.1, verify the release pipeline and binary attachment work.
    • Improve auto-generated release changelogs to list PRs/commits since the last release.
    • New feature — token usage & estimated cost section ("like the /usage command in ClaudeCode does, or the popular ccusage tool"). Add pricing-file override as a future idea to the spec. Decisions via AskUserQuestion: embedded price table + disclaimer; summary + per-turn costs; collapsible top card; opt-in via --usage.
    • Fix "fabulated" usage numbers — compared against /usage output which "is the truth."
    • Give per-model detail like /usage (input/output/cache read/cache write/cost per model).
    • Drop the <synthetic> line if it only contains zeros; disclaimer should mention subagent run costs are excluded.
    • Push and create a Pull Request (PR #6).
    • Make every commit contain the Co-Authored-By line (some SDD subagent commits were missing it).
    • Save to memory: subagents should be advised to add a Co-Authored-By line to commits they create using their proper model identification.
    • Fix two rounds of Copilot review findings on PR #6.
    • "I merged the PR. Please cut a release v0.2.0" (the final request).

  2. Key Technical Concepts:

    • Go 1.26; module github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done; binary cmd/ccwhid.
    • Claude Code transcript format: JSONL; assistant records carry message.model, message.usage (input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_creation.ephemeral_5m/1h_input_tokens, server_tool_use), and message.id. A single assistant message is split across multiple records (one per content block: thinking/text/tool_use), each repeating the IDENTICAL usage object.
    • Agent-session classification: entrypoint starting with "sdk" OR promptSource == "sdk" → agent-spawned (separate transcript file); cli → interactive.
    • Bubble Tea TUI (two-level, WindowSizeMsg-sized scrollable viewports, clampScroll), Lipgloss, cobra, goldmark+chroma, html/template, go:embed.
    • Anthropic pricing: universal cache multipliers (5m write = 1.25× base input, 1h write = 2×, cache read = 0.1×); prices.json stores only base input/output per model; cost = tokens × rate ÷ 1e6.
    • Superpowers skills: brainstorming → writing-plans → subagent-driven-development → finishing-a-development-branch. SDD scripts: task-brief, review-package, sdd-workspace at ~/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/6.1.1/skills/.
    • GoReleaser v2 (multi-platform binaries, checksums, github-native changelog); GitHub Actions CI (gofmt/tidy/vet/build/test-race) + Release workflow on v* tags; Dependabot (gomod + github-actions).
    • File-based memory at ~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done/memory/.
    • git rebase --exec for message-only history rewrite; --force-with-lease.

  3. Files and Code Sections:

    • internal/transcript/parse.go — Core dedup fix. Parse now creates seenUsage := map[string]bool{}; buildTurn(rec, blocks, toolIndex, seenUsage) gains the map param. messageMeta(raw) (msgID, modelID string, usage *model.Usage). In buildTurn: 
      if rec.Type == "assistant" {
    msgID, mdl, u := messageMeta(rec.Message)
    turn.Model = mdl
    if u != nil && msgID != "" {
        if seenUsage[msgID] { u = nil } else { seenUsage[msgID] = true }
    }
    turn.Usage = u
}
      Critical: prevents counting usage per-record (was inflating 2-3×).
    • internal/transcript/raw.go — decodeMessageMeta(raw) (msgID, modelID string, usage *apiUsage) extracts message id, model, usage. apiUsage struct with cache_creation split. Kept free of internal/model import.
    • internal/model/model.go — Turn gained Model string and Usage *Usage; new Usage{Input, Output, CacheRead, CacheWrite5m, CacheWrite1h int}.
    • internal/usage/pricing.go — //go:embed prices.json; Price{Input, Output float64}; const PricesAsOf = "2026-07"; consts cacheWrite5mMult=1.25, cacheWrite1hMult=2.0, cacheReadMult=0.1; Lookup(modelID) (Price, bool) with stripDateSuffix (strips trailing -YYYYMMDD); init() panics on invalid JSON.
    • internal/usage/prices.json — 10 models: opus-4-8/4-7/4-6/4-5 (5/25), opus-4-1 (15/75), sonnet-5 (2/10), sonnet-4-6/4-5 (3/15), haiku-4-5 (1/5), fable-5 (10/50).
    • internal/usage/usage.go — TokenCounts (with InOut()), ModelUsage{Model, Tokens, CostUSD}, Report{Total, TotalCostUSD, ByModel, PerTurnCost, PricesAsOf, HasUnknownModel, HasAnyUsage}, Compute(s), cost(), walk() (recurses subagents). Copilot fixes: aggregation key normalizes via stripDateSuffix(t.Model) and maps empty to "<unknown>": 
      key := stripDateSuffix(t.Model)
if key == "" { key=[REDACTED:assignment]" }
tc, ok := byModel[key]
    • internal/render/render.go — Options.Usage bool; usageView{HasAny, Headline template.HTML, Models []usageModel, Total usageModel, Footnote string}; usageModel{Model, Input, Output, CacheRead, CacheWrite, Cost string} (plain strings now); buildUsageView drops all-zero rows, derives hasUnpriced from kept rows; footnote: "Estimated — Anthropic list prices as of " + r.PricesAsOf + ". Covers this transcript only; sub-agent sessions stored as separate files, and server-tool fees, are excluded." + if hasUnpriced: " Estimated cost excludes unpriced models (shown as n/a); their tokens are still counted."; modelRow() helper; turnBadge() for per-turn badges. Only Headline stays template.HTML (built via html.EscapeString, needed because html/template escapes +→&#43;).
    • internal/render/format.go — formatTokens(n) (M threshold raised to 999_500 so never emits "1000k"); formatCost(c).
    • internal/render/ansi.go — StripANSI via regex \x1b(?:\[[0-?]*[ -/]*[@-~]|\][^\x07\x1b]*(?:\x07|\x1b\\)|[@-Z\\^_]) with fast-path.
    • internal/render/assets/{report.html.tmpl, styles.css} — usage card <details class="usage-card">, single per-model table with columns model|input|output|cache read|cache write|est. cost + <tr class="usage-total"> Total row. CSS uses var(--surface)/var(--text)/var(--muted)/var(--border) (fixed dark-mode bug where var(--card, #fff) was undefined).
    • internal/discovery/discovery.go — SessionInfo.IsAgent; ProjectGroup.Counts() (root, agent int), RootSessions(), AgentCount(); FindProject(groups, want) (int, error); indexFile checks sc.Err() tolerating bufio.ErrTooLong.
    • internal/tui/tui.go — two-level model (screenProjects/screenSessions/screenOptions); option indices optSubagents, optRedact, optOpen, optUsage, optOutDir, optGenerate, optionCount; Selection.Usage; editable output-dir field; clampScroll.
    • cmd/ccwhid/main.go — --usage flag, version var wired to cobra Version, TUI focus via discovery.FindProject.
    • cmd/ccwhid/run.go — latestSession picks max-ModTime non-agent session; ensureOutDir clears dir when --force; openInBrowser(dir) passes absolute path (not file:// URL).
    • .goreleaser.yaml — 6 targets (linux/darwin/windows × amd64/arm64), CGO off, ldflags -X main.version, tar.gz/zip archives + README/LICENSE, checksums, changelog: use: github-native.
    • .github/workflows/{ci.yml,release.yml}, .github/dependabot.yml, .github/release.yml (PR categories).
    • docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-usage-cost-section-design.md — spec; corrected pricing section to say prices.json stores only base input/output (cache rates derived).
    • docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-12-usage-cost.md — 5-task plan.
    • Memory files: subagent-commits-coauthored-by.md (feedback type) + MEMORY.md index at the memory dir.

  4. Errors and fixes:

    • --project silently ignored (only worked with --latest): fixed to open TUI at that project or scope --latest; added FindProject.
    • --latest group-ordering bug (Important, review): returned first non-agent session in group order but groups sort by newest-including-agents → picked older session. Fixed to select max ModTime non-agent session globally.
    • Dark-mode CSS bug (Important, whole-branch review): .usage-card { background: var(--card, #fff) } — --card undefined → white card with inherited near-white text unreadable in dark mode. Fixed to var(--surface)/var(--text); .usage-note/.usage-badge to var(--muted).
    • Usage double-counting bug (user reported "fabulated values"): transcript writes one message as multiple records each repeating usage; ccwhid counted per-record → cost $109.66 vs true ~$72. Fixed by deduping by message id. User feedback: compare against /usage which "is the truth." Validated post-fix ($71.50 vs /usage opus $74.25; gap = separate subagent files).
    • template.HTML + escaping (Task 4 deviation): html/template escapes +→&#43; breaking "in+out" test. Implementer typed Headline/Tokens as template.HTML with html.EscapeString; reviewer verified XSS-safe (Model stays plain auto-escaped). Later, moving to per-model breakdown removed + from cells, so only Headline keeps template.HTML.
    • Missing Co-Authored-By on SDD subagent commits (user feedback): 4 commits (6fef39b, 4de9666, 08d3928, 1753ede) lacked it. Fixed via git rebase main --exec 'git log -1 --pretty=%B | grep -qi "Co-Authored-By: Claude" || git commit --amend --no-edit --trailer "..."', force-pushed with --force-with-lease (tree verified identical).
    • Copilot round 1: formatTokens "1000k" rounding (→ 999_500 threshold); Compute grouped verbatim model id (→ stripDateSuffix key); spec cache-rate mismatch (→ corrected doc).
    • Copilot round 2: empty model id → blank row (→ "" placeholder); footnote "Totals exclude unpriced" wrongly implied tokens excluded (→ "Estimated cost excludes unpriced models... their tokens are still counted").
    • Playwright file:// blocked: started local python http.servers to screenshot.

  5. Problem Solving: Solved: TUI scrolling/two-level nav; agent-session classification; --project fix; ANSI stripping; CI/Dependabot/GoReleaser/changelog setup; full usage-cost feature via SDD; the critical per-record usage double-counting root cause (via raw-transcript analysis showing identical usage on split records); dark-mode readability; per-model /usage-parity table; commit attribution. The residual ccwhid-vs-/usage difference is documented as an intentional scope difference (ccwhid = single rendered transcript; /usage = aggregates separate subagent session files).

  6. All user messages:

    • (After /context, /compact) "Before we merge, we need to work on the TUI: it first screen of the TUI is not really useable if you have many projects with lots of session transcripts... on opening the TUI, it should first only display the list of projects. I select a project, and get to a screen with the list of sessions... scroll up and down. From the session list I should be able to return to the project list with cursor left. Further, it looks like all the subagent sessions are listed as well. Can we have it so that on entering the session list for a project by default only 'root sessions' are listed and the list can be expanded to all sessions with a specific key... Finally, I don't quite get how the '--project' option is supposed to work... Is it broken?" (with image)
    • "Yes, please do another code review." (after being asked)
    • "2." (chose Push and create a Pull Request for TUI branch)
    • "I merged it."
    • "Try it out on my latest session"
    • "Commit the report into docs/ as an artifact"
    • "It's fine, we keep this snapshot as a kind of full-featured demo output in the documentation. Could we enhance the readme by including screenshots of the TUI and from the generated HTML report?"
    • "Add ANSI-stripping to the renderer"
    • "Regenerate the demo and screenshot"
    • "The ownly drawback being, that the automatically created releases don't get a proper changelog..." (actually this was about changelog — appeared later) — Note: exact ordering: "Setup CI as gh action workflow, and add a dependabot config."
    • "I merged and activated branch protection already."
    • "Tag a v0.1.0 release"
    • "Set up GoReleaser to attach prebuilt binaries"
    • "Please merge the PR, then cut a v0.1.1 tag and check that release pipeline and attachment of binaries works."
    • "The ownly drawback being, that the automatically created releases don't get a proper changelog. At least they should list the PRs or commits which went into them since the last release. Can this be improved?"
    • "New feature idea: I'd like to add a section to the report that displays the number of consumed tokens and the estimated cost, like the the '/usage' command in ClaudeCode does, or the popular ccusage tool (see https://github.com/ccusage/ccusage) does. Is this doable?"
    • (AskUserQuestion answers) Pricing=Embedded table + disclaimer; Granularity=Summary + per-turn costs; Placement=Collapsible top card; Default=Opt-in via --usage.
    • "Yes, that's fine. Add as future idea to the spec the option to load prices from a provided file (to override the built-in table or provide prices for unknown models)."
    • "Approved, please go on." (spec approved)
    • "1." (chose Subagent-Driven execution)
    • "Ok, something seems to be quite off: if we assume that the output of the '/usage' command 'is the truth' then the computed values in the report seems to be quite fabulated. Compare the screenshots..." (with /usage + ccwhid images)
    • "One more thing: you aggregated the usage by model line into two numbers. What prevents us from giving the same level of detail per model the '/usage' output gives us?" (with image)
    • "Two minor things: 1. Shouldn't we drop the line, if it only contains zeros? 2. The disclaimer line under the table should mention that usage cost of subagent runs is not included (or in other words 'is excluded')."
    • "Push and create a Pull Request"
    • "Some of the commit messages are missing the 'Co-Authored-By' line. Since all commits have been written by you, please make sure every commit contains the line."
    • "Ok, thanks for the info: so those were commits made by the subagents. Could you please put to memory, that the subagents should be advised to add a Co-Authored-By line to commits they create using their proper model identification."
    • "Copilot reviewer noted a few issues. Please have a look." (round 1)
    • "Copilot reviewer noted a few issues. Please have a look." (round 2)
    • "I merged the PR. Please cut a release v0.2.0"

The next milestone will be all about handling of subagents / subagent sessions and comprises multiple features: 1. The response / result of a subagent run which is feeded back into the main session should always be presented as a proper "card" in the report attributed to the subagent. I already noted that in some reports the response is already presented as a card attributed to me ("You") [Image #5] the screenshot is from a report of the fabulous-writing project, and sometimes in only appears inside the expanded agent run card [Image #6] the screenshot is from a report of this project. 2. The transcripts of all subagent session transcripts of a "root" session transcript should optionally be included into the report. Whether inline in the same static HTML, or as separate linked files we need to discuss and decide. 3. When the subagent sessions are included into the report, their usage cost summary should be included into the usage summary of the main session.
