Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse.go"
}
1	package transcript
2	
3	import (
4		"bufio"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"errors"
7		"io"
8		"os"
9	
10		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
11	)
12	
13	// Options controls how a transcript is folded into a Session.
14	type Options struct {
15		IncludeSubagents bool
16	}
17	
18	// ParseFile parses a transcript file at path.
19	func ParseFile(path string, opts Options) (model.Session, error) {
20		f, err := os.Open(path)
21		if err != nil {
22			return model.Session{}, err
23		}
24		defer f.Close()
25		s, err := Parse(f, opts)
26		if err != nil {
27			return model.Session{}, err
28		}
29		s.ID = sessionIDFromPath(path)
30		return s, nil
31	}
32	
33	func sessionIDFromPath(path string) string {
34		base := path
35		if i := lastSlash(path); i >= 0 {
36			base = path[i+1:]
37		}
38		if len(base) > len(".jsonl") && base[len(base)-len(".jsonl"):] == ".jsonl" {
39			return base[:len(base)-len(".jsonl")]
40		}
41		return base
42	}
43	
44	func lastSlash(s string) int {
45		for i := len(s) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
46			if s[i] == '/' {
47				return i
48			}
49		}
50		return -1
51	}
52	
53	// Parse reads a transcript stream and folds it into a Session timeline.
54	func Parse(r io.Reader, opts Options) (model.Session, error) {
55		var s model.Session
56		// toolIndex maps a tool_use id to the ToolCall pointer so results and
57		// sidechains can be attached after the fact.
58		toolIndex := map[string]*model.ToolCall{}
59		// seenUsage tracks message ids whose usage has already been counted, so an
60		// assistant message split across multiple records is not counted per-record.
61		seenUsage := map[string]bool{}
62		// lastTask tracks the most recent Task tool call; sidechainOwner is the Task
63		// whose Subagents any subsequent sidechain turns are appended to.
64		var lastTask *model.ToolCall
65		sidechainOwner := lastTask
66	
67		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
68		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 1024*1024), 64*1024*1024)
69		for sc.Scan() {
70			line := sc.Bytes()
71			if len(trimSpace(line)) == 0 {
72				continue
73			}
74			var rec rawRecord
75			if err := json.Unmarshal(line, &rec); err != nil {
76				s.SkippedLines++
77				continue
78			}
79			switch rec.Type {
80			case "ai-title":
81				if rec.AiTitle != "" {
82					s.Title = rec.AiTitle
83				}
84				continue
85			case "user", "assistant":
86				// fallthrough to handling below
87			default:
88				continue // ignore mode/permission/attachment/etc. for the timeline
89			}
90			if rec.IsMeta {
91				continue
92			}
93	
94			// Capture session-level metadata from the first record that has it.
95			if s.GitBranch == "" && rec.GitBranch != "" {
96				s.GitBranch = rec.GitBranch
97			}
98			if s.Version == "" && rec.Version != "" {
99				s.Version = rec.Version
100			}
101			if s.ProjectPath == "" && rec.Cwd != "" {
102				s.ProjectPath = rec.Cwd
103			}
104			ts := parseTime(rec.Timestamp)
105			if !ts.IsZero() {
106				if s.StartedAt.IsZero() {
107					s.StartedAt = ts
108				}
109				s.EndedAt = ts
110			}
111	
112			_, blocks := decodeMessageContent(rec.Message)
113	
114			// Sidechain (subagent) records.
115			if rec.IsSidechain {
116				if !opts.IncludeSubagents || sidechainOwner == nil {
117					continue
118				}
119				turn := buildTurn(rec, blocks, toolIndex, seenUsage)
120				if turn == nil {
121					continue
122				}
123				sub := &sidechainOwner.Subagents[len(sidechainOwner.Subagents)-1]
124				sub.Turns = append(sub.Turns, *turn)
125				continue
126			}
127	
128			// Main-chain records.
129			turn := buildTurn(rec, blocks, toolIndex, seenUsage)
130			if turn == nil {
131				continue // e.g. a user record that only carried a tool_result
132			}
133			s.Turns = append(s.Turns, *turn)
134	
135			// Seed a subagent slot for each Task tool call and make it the current
136			// sidechain owner. Tool-use ids are already registered in buildTurn.
137			// Note: sidechain records are attributed to the nearest preceding Task
138			// by file order. Sequential Task calls attribute correctly; multiple
139			// Task calls dispatched in parallel from one assistant message cannot be
140			// disambiguated from the raw records and will all attach to the last
141			// one — a known v1 limitation.
142			if opts.IncludeSubagents {
143				for i := range turn.Blocks {
144					tool := turn.Blocks[i].Tool
145					if tool != nil && tool.Name == "Task" {
146						tool.Subagents = append(tool.Subagents, model.Subagent{
147							Description: taskDescription(tool),
148						})
149						lastTask = tool
150						sidechainOwner = lastTask
151					}
152				}
153			}
154		}
155		if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
156			// A single over-long line (bufio.ErrTooLong) is a pathological record,
157			// not a reason to discard the whole report: count it and return what we
158			// parsed so far.
159			if errors.Is(err, bufio.ErrTooLong) {
160				s.SkippedLines++
161				return s, nil
162			}
163			return s, err
164		}
165		return s, nil
166	}
167	
168	// messageMeta converts a record's message model/usage into turn fields, also
169	// returning the message id for deduplication. Only assistant records carry
170	// usage; the aggregate cache_creation figure (with no 5m/1h split) is attributed
171	// to the cheaper 5-minute write.
172	func messageMeta(raw json.RawMessage) (msgID, modelID string, usage *model.Usage) {
173		id, mdl, u := decodeMessageMeta(raw)
174		if u == nil {
175			return id, mdl, nil
176		}
177		w5, w1 := u.CacheCreation.Ephemeral5m, u.CacheCreation.Ephemeral1h
178		if w5 == 0 && w1 == 0 && u.CacheCreationInputTokens > 0 {
179			w5 = u.CacheCreationInputTokens
180		}
181		return id, mdl, &model.Usage{
182			Input:        u.InputTokens,
183			Output:       u.OutputTokens,
184			CacheRead:    u.CacheReadInputTokens,
185			CacheWrite5m: w5,
186			CacheWrite1h: w1,
187		}
188	}
189	
190	// buildTurn converts a record's blocks into a model.Turn. tool_result blocks are
191	// attached to their originating ToolCall via toolIndex and do not themselves
192	// produce turn content. Returns nil if the record yields no displayable blocks.
193	func buildTurn(rec rawRecord, blocks []apiBlock, toolIndex map[string]*model.ToolCall, seenUsage map[string]bool) *model.Turn {
194		turn := &model.Turn{Timestamp: parseTime(rec.Timestamp)}
195		if rec.Type == "user" {
196			turn.Kind = model.TurnUser
197		} else {
198			turn.Kind = model.TurnAssistant
199		}
200		for _, b := range blocks {
201			switch b.Type {
202			case "text":
203				if b.Text != "" {
204					turn.Blocks = append(turn.Blocks, model.Block{Type: model.BlockText, Text: b.Text})
205				}
206			case "thinking":
207				if b.Thinking != "" {
208					turn.Blocks = append(turn.Blocks, model.Block{Type: model.BlockThinking, Text: b.Thinking})
209				}
210			case "tool_use":
211				tc := buildToolCall(b)
212				// Register the tool call so a later tool_result (in this turn's
213				// chain OR inside a sidechain) can be matched back to it by id.
214				toolIndex[tc.ID] = tc
215				turn.Blocks = append(turn.Blocks, model.Block{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc})
216			case "tool_result":
217				if tc := toolIndex[b.ToolUseID]; tc != nil {
218					tc.Result = &model.ToolResult{Content: toolResultText(b.Content), IsError: b.IsError}
219				}
220			}
221		}
222		if len(turn.Blocks) == 0 {
223			return nil
224		}
225		if rec.Type == "assistant" {
226			msgID, mdl, u := messageMeta(rec.Message)
227			turn.Model = mdl
228			// One assistant message is written as several records (one per content
229			// block), each repeating the same usage. Count usage on the first record
230			// of a message id only; later records of the same message get nil usage
231			// so totals are not multiplied by the block count.
232			if u != nil && msgID != "" {
233				if seenUsage[msgID] {
234					u = nil
235				} else {
236					seenUsage[msgID] = true
237				}
238			}
239			turn.Usage = u
240		}
241		return turn
242	}
243	
244	func trimSpace(b []byte) []byte {
245		start, end := 0, len(b)
246		for start < end && (b[start] == ' ' || b[start] == '\t' || b[start] == '\r' || b[start] == '\n') {
247			start++
248		}
249		for end > start && (b[end-1] == ' ' || b[end-1] == '\t' || b[end-1] == '\r' || b[end-1] == '\n') {
250			end--
251		}
252		return b[start:end]
253	}
254	
255	// buildToolCall constructs a ToolCall from a tool_use block, deriving a summary
256	// and, for Edit/Write, a Diff.
257	func buildToolCall(b apiBlock) *model.ToolCall {
258		tc := &model.ToolCall{
259			ID:        b.ID,
260			Name:      b.Name,
261			InputJSON: prettyJSON(b.Input),
262		}
263		input := decodeInput(b.Input)
264		tc.Summary = toolSummary(b.Name, input)
265		switch b.Name {
266		case "Edit", "Write":
267			tc.Diff = buildDiff(b.Name, input)
268		}
269		return tc
270	}
271	
272	func decodeInput(raw json.RawMessage) map[string]any {
273		m := map[string]any{}
274		if len(raw) > 0 {
275			_ = json.Unmarshal(raw, &m)
276		}
277		return m
278	}
279	
280	func str(m map[string]any, key string) string {
281		if v, ok := m[key].(string); ok {
282			return v
283		}
284		return ""
285	}
286	
287	// toolSummary produces a compact one-line header for a tool card.
288	func toolSummary(name string, in map[string]any) string {
289		switch name {
290		case "Bash":
291			if c := str(in, "command"); c != "" {
292				return firstLine(c)
293			}
294		case "Read", "Edit", "Write", "NotebookEdit":
295			if p := str(in, "file_path"); p != "" {
296				return p
297			}
298		case "Glob":
299			return str(in, "pattern")
300		case "Grep":
301			return str(in, "pattern")
302		case "Task":
303			return str(in, "description")
304		case "WebFetch":
305			return str(in, "url")
306		case "WebSearch":
307			return str(in, "query")
308		}
309		return ""
310	}
311	
312	func buildDiff(name string, in map[string]any) *model.Diff {
313		d := &model.Diff{Path: str(in, "file_path")}
314		if name == "Write" {
315			d.NewText = str(in, "content")
316			return d
317		}
318		d.OldText = str(in, "old_string")
319		d.NewText = str(in, "new_string")
320		return d
321	}
322	
323	func taskDescription(tc *model.ToolCall) string {
324		if tc.Summary != "" {
325			return tc.Summary
326		}
327		return "subagent"
328	}
329	
330	func firstLine(s string) string {
331		for i := 0; i < len(s); i++ {
332			if s[i] == '\n' {
333				return s[:i]
334			}
335		}
336		return s
337	}
338	
339	func prettyJSON(raw json.RawMessage) string {
340		if len(raw) == 0 {
341			return ""
342		}
343		var v any
344		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &v); err != nil {
345			return string(raw)
346		}
347		out, err := json.MarshalIndent(v, "", "  ")
348		if err != nil {
349			return string(raw)
350		}
351		return string(out)
352	}
353

<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Implement Task 12: Profiles view" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>Both consistency items fixed and committed.

## Status: Complete

**Commit:** `99dba930962f7db433dc4d2385e662f6046bd455` — "fix: i18n-wrapped errors for all profile operations" (new commit on top of the coordinator's docs commit `f06f21e`; no amend). 10 files changed, +31/−8.

**What was done:**

1. **i18n key** — `profileChangeFailed: (error: string) =&gt; string` added to the Messages interface (Profiles view section, after `profileSaved`) in `frontend/src/i18n/messages.ts` and to all 7 catalogs (`en.ts`, `de.ts`, `fr.ts`, `es.ts`, `it.ts`, `ja.ts`, `zh.ts`) with exactly the translations specified. One note: for zh I used the fullwidth colon `：` (`配置更改失败：${error}`) to match the catalog's existing fullwidth-punctuation style (e.g. its hint strings use `（）`).

2. **ProfilesView.tsx** — added the local `reportError(e: unknown)` helper using `m.profileChangeFailed(e instanceof Error ? e.message :

Claude3k tok · ~$0.30
AskUserQuestionDelivery · Usage rollup · Flag · Result card
Delivery

How should included subagent transcripts be delivered in the report? Your worst case is 256 subagent transcripts / 82 MB JSONL for one root session, so fully inline HTML could get enormous.

  • Separate linked pages (Recommended)

    One HTML page per subagent session in the output dir (e.g. subagents/agent-.html), linked from the Agent tool card and the result card in the main report. Main report stays fast; scales to hundreds of agents.

  • Inline collapsed in main report

    Embed each subagent transcript as a collapsed inside its Agent tool card. Single self-contained file, but can balloon to tens of MB and slow the browser.

  • Hybrid: inline if small, linked if large

    Inline transcripts below a size threshold, separate pages above it. More code paths and less predictable output layout.

Usage rollup

How should the subagent usage be presented in the usage card when subagent sessions are included?

  • Merged per-model + subagent line (Recommended)

    One per-model table combining main + subagent tokens (like /usage does), plus a summary line 'of which subagents: N tok · $X' so you can still see the split. Footnote updated.

  • Fully merged, no split

    Just fold subagent tokens into the existing per-model table. Simplest; you lose visibility of what the subagents cost vs the main session.

  • Two separate tables

    Main-session table and subagents table shown separately with a combined total row. Most detail, but a bulkier usage card.

Flag

Which flag should control including linked subagent session transcripts? Today --include-subagents already controls in-file sidechain rendering.

  • Reuse --include-subagents (Recommended)

    One flag means 'show subagent work': renders in-file sidechains AND discovers/links agent-*.jsonl transcripts. Simple mental model, no new flag.

  • New separate flag

    Keep --include-subagents for sidechains; add e.g. --link-agent-sessions for the file-based transcripts. Finer control, but two similar-sounding flags.

Result card

How should the fixed task-notification card be presented in the main timeline?

  • Agent-attributed card (Recommended)

    Own card at its timeline position, labeled with the agent description (e.g. 'Agent · Implement Task 12: Profiles view'), status badge, and the rendered as markdown. Distinct styling from You/Claude.

  • Fold into the Agent tool card

    Attach the notification result to the originating Agent tool card (via tool-use-id) instead of showing it at its arrival point. Timeline loses the 'when it came back' moment.

  • Both

    Card in the timeline AND a link/echo inside the originating Agent tool card. Redundant but maximally navigable.

answered
  • DeliverySeparate linked pages (Recommended)
  • Usage rollupMerged per-model + subagent line (Recommended)
  • FlagReuse --include-subagents (Recommended)
  • Result cardAgent-attributed card (Recommended)
Claude3k tok · ~$0.32

All four decisions locked in. Here's the design:

Design: Subagent Sessions Milestone

1. Data model & discovery

A parsed root session gains linked agent sessions:

  • model.Session gets Agents []model.AgentSession where AgentSession{ID, Description, AgentType, ToolUseID, SpawnDepth int, Session Session}.
  • New loader step (in run.go, behind --include-subagents): after parsing X.jsonl, look for <projectDir>/X/subagents/agent-*.jsonl. Each is parsed with the same transcript parser (records inside are ordinary user/assistant records with isSidechain: true — parser treats a whole-file sidechain like a main chain when it's the agent's own file). Metadata comes from the sibling agent-<id>.meta.json; if missing, fall back to ID as description.
  • spawnDepth is informational only — all agent files live flat in one dir, so page generation is flat and cross-links resolve across the whole set.

2. Fix the "You" card (works even without transcript inclusion)

The parser detects a user record whose string content starts with <task-notification> and parses the fields (task-id, tool-use-id, status, summary, result). It becomes a new turn kind TurnAgentResult with AgentID, Status, Summary and the <result> body as a text block (rendered as markdown).

Renderer: distinct card — role label from the summary/description (e.g. Agent · Implement Task 12: Profiles view), status badge, own accent color. This fix applies always, independent of the flags — misattribution is a bug, not an option.

3. Separate linked subagent pages

  • Renderer emits subagents/agent-<id>.html per agent session — same template, same styling/assets (relative paths), title = agent description, with a back-link to the main report.
  • Links into the pages from two places in the main report: the originating Agent/Task tool card (matched via toolUseId) and the agent-result card (matched via task-id = agent id).
  • Subagent pages can themselves contain Agent tool calls and task-notifications (depth-2 agents); those link to sibling pages in the same dir.
  • Each subagent page gets its own usage card when --usage is set.
  • Agent files that are neither referenced by a tool card nor a notification are still rendered (reachable only by file) — noted as acceptable.

4. Usage rollup

When agent sessions are included and --usage is on:

  • usage.Compute also walks s.Agents, merging their tokens into the per-model table (same normalization), and tracks a separate subagent subtotal.
  • The usage card gains one line under the table: "of which subagents: N tok · ~$X (M sessions)".
  • Footnote changes to: subagent sessions included (when found); still excludes server-tool fees. When the flag is off, current wording stays.

5. Flags / TUI

--include-subagents now means "show subagent work" — in-file sidechains and linked agent-session pages. No new flag. TUI option text updated accordingly.

6. Out of scope

  • Flat SDK-spawned session files (review-hook sessions): no parent linkage exists → cannot be attributed; excluded, documented in the spec as a known limitation.
  • Old-style in-file sidechains keep their current inline rendering.

7. Testing

  • Parser: task-notification detection/parsing (incl. non-notification user text unaffected, malformed notification falls back to plain user turn).
  • Loader: subagents dir discovery, meta.json fallback, agentId-from-filename.
  • Usage: rollup math with agents, subtotal line, footnote variants.
  • Render: golden-ish checks that agent pages are emitted and links point correctly; result card attribution.

Does this design look right, or should we adjust anything before I write the spec?

You

Approved, please write the spec.

Claude4k tok · ~$0.32

Writing the spec now.

Claude
Write~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-subagent-sessions-design.md
+ # Subagent Sessions — Design
+
+ **Date:** 2026-07-12
+ **Status:** Approved
+
+ ## Goal
+
+ Make subagent work first-class in ccwhid reports:
+
+ 1. Agent results fed back into the main session always render as a card
+ attributed to the agent — never as a "You" card.
+ 2. The transcripts of a root session's subagent sessions can optionally be
+ included in the report as separate linked HTML pages.
+ 3. When subagent sessions are included, their token usage and estimated cost
+ roll up into the main usage summary.
+
+ ## Background: how subagent data appears on disk
+
+ Three generations of subagent storage exist in Claude Code transcripts:
+
+ 1. **In-file sidechains** — records with `isSidechain: true` inside the root
+ session's own `.jsonl`. Already handled by ccwhid (`--include-subagents`
+ renders them inline inside the Task tool card).
+ 2. **Linked agent-session files (modern Agent tool)** —
+ `<projectDir>/<sessionId>/subagents/agent-<agentId>.jsonl`, one file per
+ agent, each with a sibling `agent-<agentId>.meta.json`:
+
+ ```json
+ {
+ "agentType": "general-purpose",
+ "description": "Implement Task 12: Profiles view",
+ "toolUseId": "toolu_0185fny8NUzysKdpquY27j3q",
+ "spawnDepth": 1
+ }
+ ```
+
+ Records inside the agent file are ordinary user/assistant records
+ (they carry `isSidechain: true` and an `agentId` field). `toolUseId`
+ links the agent to the Task/Agent tool call in the parent transcript.
+ `spawnDepth` can exceed 1 (agents spawned by agents); all files live
+ flat in the one `subagents/` dir regardless of depth.
+
+ Observed scale: a single root session with 256 agent files totalling
+ 82 MB of JSONL. This rules out inlining transcripts into one HTML file.
+ 3. **Flat SDK-spawned session files** — separate top-level session files with
+ `entrypoint: sdk-*` / `promptSource: "sdk"` (e.g. review-hook runs). They
+ carry **no parent-session linkage** and cannot be reliably attributed to a
+ root session.
+
+ **Agent results in the main timeline** arrive in one of two shapes:
+
+ - Synchronous agent: the `tool_result` attaches to the Agent/Task tool call
+ (already rendered inside the tool card — correct today).
+ - Background agent: a `user` record whose message content is a **string**
+ starting with `<task-notification>`, e.g.
+
+ ```
+ <task-notification>
+ <task-id>acb6584f99f2f81fd</task-id>
+ <tool-use-id>toolu_0112a19E...</tool-use-id>
+ <output-file>/private/tmp/.../tasks/acb6584f99f2f81fd.output</output-file>
+ <status>completed</status>
+ <summary>Agent "Implement Task 12: Profiles view" finished</summary>
+ <note>...may notify more than once...</note>
+ <result>...full agent report, markdown...</result>
+ </task-notification>
+ ```
+
+ ccwhid currently renders this as a plain user turn — the "You" card bug.
+ The `<task-id>` value equals the `agentId` in the linked file name;
+ `<tool-use-id>` identifies the originating tool call.
+
+ ## Decisions
+
+ | Decision | Choice |
+ |---|---|
+ | Transcript delivery | Separate linked HTML pages, one per agent session |
+ | Usage presentation | Merged per-model table + "of which subagents" line |
+ | Flag | Reuse `--include-subagents` for both sidechains and linked agent sessions |
+ | Result card | Agent-attributed card at its timeline position |
+
+ ## Design
+
+ ### 1. Data model
+
+ `model.Session` gains:
+
+ ```go
+ Agents []AgentSession
+ ```
+
+ ```go
+ // AgentSession is a subagent session file linked to this root session.
+ type AgentSession struct {
+ ID string // agentId, from the file name agent-<ID>.jsonl
+ Description string // from meta.json; falls back to ID
+ AgentType string // from meta.json; may be empty
+ ToolUseID string // links to the spawning tool call; may be empty
+ SpawnDepth int // from meta.json; informational only
+ Session Session // the parsed agent transcript
+ }
+ ```
+
+ New turn kind and fields for the notification card:
+
+ ```go
+ const TurnAgentResult TurnKind = "agent-result" // alongside "user"/"assistant"
+ ```
+
+ `model.Turn` gains `AgentID`, `AgentStatus`, `AgentSummary string` (set only
+ for `TurnAgentResult` turns). The `<result>` body becomes the turn's single
+ text block, rendered as markdown.
+
+ ### 2. Parser: task-notification detection
+
+ In `internal/transcript`, when a `user` record's message content is a string
+ whose trimmed text starts with `<task-notification>`:
+
+ - Parse the `task-id`, `tool-use-id`, `status`, `summary`, and `result`
+ elements with tolerant string scanning (no XML parser; the payload is
+ pseudo-XML with unescaped markdown inside `<result>`).
+ - Produce a `TurnAgentResult` turn: `AgentID` = task-id, `AgentStatus` =
+ status, `AgentSummary` = summary, blocks = one text block containing the
+ result body. If `<result>` is missing or empty, use the summary text as
+ the body.
+ - If parsing fails to find both `<task-id>` and `<summary>`, fall back to a
+ plain user turn (current behavior) — never drop the record.
+ - A task-id may notify more than once; each notification renders as its own
+ card at its timeline position (no dedup).
+
+ This fix is **unconditional** — it applies regardless of `--include-subagents`
+ and `--usage`. Misattribution is a bug, not an option.
+
+ ### 3. Loader: linked agent sessions
+
+ New package-level function in `internal/transcript`:
+
+ ```go
+ // LoadAgentSessions parses <dir>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl, where dir is the
+ // transcript path minus ".jsonl". Returns them sorted by file name.
+ func LoadAgentSessions(transcriptPath string, opts Options) ([]model.AgentSession, error)
+ ```
+
+ - Called from `run.go` only when `--include-subagents` is set.
+ - Each agent file is parsed with the existing `Parse`. Inside an agent's own
+ file its records are the main chain, even though they carry
+ `isSidechain: true`; the parser must treat them as main-chain records when
+ parsing an agent file. Concretely: `Options` gains `AgentFile bool`; when
+ set, `IsSidechain` on records is ignored.
+ - Metadata from `agent-<ID>.meta.json`; a missing or unreadable meta.json is
+ tolerated (Description falls back to ID, other fields zero).
+ - A missing `subagents/` dir yields an empty slice, no error. An unparsable
+ agent file is skipped with a warning to stderr, not a fatal error.
+
+ ### 4. Renderer: agent-result cards and subagent pages
+
+ **Agent-result card** (always): a `TurnAgentResult` turn renders as a card
+ with role label `Agent` plus the summary (e.g. `Agent · Implement Task 12:
+ Profiles view`), a status badge (`completed` / other), and the result body
+ as markdown. Distinct accent styling from You/Claude cards. Search text and
+ prompt-jump behavior treat it like an assistant card (it is not a prompt).
+
+ **Subagent pages** (when agent sessions are loaded):
+
+ - Output layout: `outDir/subagents/agent-<ID>.html`, sharing
+ `outDir/assets/` via relative paths (`../assets/...`).
+ - Each page uses the same report template: title = agent description,
+ subtitle shows agent type and ID, plus a back-link to `../index.html`.
+ - Each page gets its own usage card when `--usage` is set (computed over
+ that agent session only, current single-session semantics).
+ - Links from the main report into the pages:
+ - On the Agent/Task **tool card** whose `tool_use` ID equals an agent's
+ `ToolUseID`.
+ - On every **agent-result card** whose `AgentID` matches an agent file.
+ - Subagent pages themselves may contain Agent tool cards and agent-result
+ cards for deeper agents (spawnDepth 2); the same matching renders links to
+ sibling pages in the same directory. Link resolution uses the full agent
+ set of the root session.
+ - Agent files referenced by no tool card and no notification are still
+ rendered as pages (unreachable by link); acceptable.
+
+ ### 5. Usage rollup
+
+ When agent sessions are loaded and `--usage` is set:
+
+ - `usage.Compute` gains the agent sessions as input (signature stays
+ session-centric: it reads `s.Agents`). Agent turns merge into the same
+ per-model aggregation (same model-id normalization) and into `Total`.
+ - `Report` gains a subagent subtotal:
+
+ ```go
+ Subagents TokenCounts // tokens from linked agent sessions
+ SubagentsCost *float64 // nil when nothing in them was priced
+ AgentSessions int // number of linked agent sessions counted
+ ```
+
+ - The usage card shows, below the table:
+ `of which subagents: <N tok in+out> · ~$X (M sessions)` — omitted when no
+ agent sessions were counted.
+ - Footnote when agent sessions are included: "Includes M linked subagent
+ sessions. Server-tool fees are excluded." Current wording stays when the
+ flag is off or no agent files exist.
+ - Per-turn badges remain main-session-only.
+ - In-file sidechain turns keep their current treatment (already counted via
+ `walk`).
+
+ ### 6. Flags & TUI
+
+ - `--include-subagents` now means "show subagent work": render in-file
+ sidechains inline (unchanged) **and** discover + render linked
+ agent-session pages. Help text updated.
+ - TUI: the existing subagents option's label is updated to reflect the
+ broader meaning. No new option.
+ - README: document the new behavior and the page layout.
+
+ ## Out of scope
+
+ - Flat SDK-spawned session files (no linkage) — excluded; documented as a
+ known limitation in the README.
+ - Inlining full agent transcripts into the main HTML.
+ - Deduplicating repeat notifications for the same task-id.
+ - Pricing-file override (still a future idea from the usage spec).
+
+ ## Testing
+
+ - **Parser:** task-notification record → `TurnAgentResult` with correct
+ AgentID/Status/Summary and result body; user text that merely mentions
+ `<task-notification>` mid-string stays a user turn; malformed notification
+ falls back to a user turn; multiple notifications for one task-id each
+ produce a turn.
+ - **Loader:** finds `agent-*.jsonl` under `<sessionId>/subagents/`; missing
+ dir → empty; missing meta.json → ID fallback; `AgentFile` option makes
+ sidechain-flagged records parse as main chain.
+ - **Usage:** rollup adds agent-session tokens to totals and per-model rows;
+ subtotal line values; `AgentSessions` count; footnote variants.
+ - **Renderer:** agent-result card carries `Agent ·` label and status badge
+ (not "You"); subagent pages are written to `subagents/agent-<ID>.html`;
+ tool cards and result cards carry links when a matching agent exists and
+ none when not; back-link present on agent pages.
+ 
File created successfully at: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-subagent-sessions-design.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

Spec written and committed to docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-subagent-sessions-design.md (f97c0f8). Please review it and let me know if you want any changes before I write the implementation plan.

Quick orientation for the review — the spec pins down:

  • §2 the unconditional "You"-card fix via <task-notification> parsing → new TurnAgentResult turn kind
  • §3 LoadAgentSessions reading <sessionId>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl + meta.json, with an AgentFile parse option so sidechain-flagged records parse as main chain
  • §4 one HTML page per agent under outDir/subagents/, linked from tool cards (via toolUseId) and result cards (via task-id)
  • §5 usage rollup with the "of which subagents" line and footnote change
  • Out of scope: flat SDK-spawned sessions (no linkage), inlining transcripts, notification dedup

Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model/model.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model/model.go"
}
1	// Package model defines the domain types shared across ccwhid: a parsed
2	// Session and the timeline of Turns, Blocks and ToolCalls within it.
3	package model
4	
5	import "time"
6	
7	type TurnKind string
8	
9	const (
10		TurnUser      TurnKind = "user"
11		TurnAssistant TurnKind = "assistant"
12	)
13	
14	type BlockType string
15	
16	const (
17		BlockText     BlockType = "text"
18		BlockThinking BlockType = "thinking"
19		BlockToolUse  BlockType = "tool_use"
20	)
21	
22	// Session is one fully-parsed Claude Code transcript.
23	type Session struct {
24		ID           string
25		ProjectPath  string // decoded absolute cwd
26		Title        string
27		GitBranch    string
28		Version      string
29		StartedAt    time.Time
30		EndedAt      time.Time
31		Turns        []Turn
32		SkippedLines int // count of malformed lines skipped while parsing
33	}
34	
35	func (s Session) Duration() time.Duration { return s.EndedAt.Sub(s.StartedAt) }
36	
37	func (s Session) DisplayTitle() string {
38		if s.Title != "" {
39			return s.Title
40		}
41		return "Untitled session"
42	}
43	
44	// Turn is a single user or assistant message, holding ordered content blocks.
45	type Turn struct {
46		Kind      TurnKind
47		Timestamp time.Time
48		Blocks    []Block
49		Model     string // assistant model id (empty for user turns / when absent)
50		Usage     *Usage // token usage for this assistant turn; nil when absent
51	}
52	
53	// Usage holds the token counts reported for one assistant message. Cache writes
54	// keep the 5-minute / 1-hour split because they are priced differently.
55	type Usage struct {
56		Input        int
57		Output       int
58		CacheRead    int
59		CacheWrite5m int
60		CacheWrite1h int
61	}
62	
63	// Block is one content unit: assistant/user text, a thinking block, or a tool call.
64	type Block struct {
65		Type BlockType
66		Text string    // for BlockText and BlockThinking
67		Tool *ToolCall // for BlockToolUse
68	}
69	
70	// ToolCall is a single tool invocation and its result.
71	type ToolCall struct {
72		ID        string
73		Name      string // e.g. "Bash", "Edit", "Read", "Task"
74		Summary   string // one-line summary for the collapsed card header
75		InputJSON string // pretty-printed input for generic display
76		Result    *ToolResult
77		Diff      *Diff      // set for Edit/Write
78		Subagents []Subagent // set for Task calls with sidechain activity
79	}
80	
81	type ToolResult struct {
82		Content string
83		IsError bool
84	}
85	
86	type Diff struct {
87		Path    string
88		OldText string
89		NewText string
90	}
91	
92	// Subagent is a nested sidechain run attached to a parent Task tool call.
93	type Subagent struct {
94		Description string
95		Turns       []Turn
96	}
97

Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/raw.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/raw.go"
}
1	package transcript
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"strings"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// rawRecord is one decoded line of a session .jsonl file.
10	type rawRecord struct {
11		Type        string          `json:"type"`
12		UUID        string          `json:"uuid"`
13		ParentUUID  string          `json:"parentUuid"`
14		Timestamp   string          `json:"timestamp"`
15		GitBranch   string          `json:"gitBranch"`
16		Version     string          `json:"version"`
17		Cwd         string          `json:"cwd"`
18		AiTitle     string          `json:"aiTitle"`
19		IsSidechain bool            `json:"isSidechain"`
20		IsMeta      bool            `json:"isMeta"`
21		Message     json.RawMessage `json:"message"`
22	}
23	
24	// apiBlock is one content block of an Anthropic message.
25	type apiBlock struct {
26		Type      string          `json:"type"`
27		Text      string          `json:"text"`
28		Thinking  string          `json:"thinking"`
29		ID        string          `json:"id"`
30		Name      string          `json:"name"`
31		Input     json.RawMessage `json:"input"`
32		ToolUseID string          `json:"tool_use_id"`
33		Content   json.RawMessage `json:"content"`
34		IsError   bool            `json:"is_error"`
35	}
36	
37	// decodeMessageContent extracts the role and content blocks from a raw message
38	// object. Content may be a plain string (wrapped as a single text block) or an
39	// array of blocks.
40	func decodeMessageContent(raw json.RawMessage) (string, []apiBlock) {
41		if len(raw) == 0 {
42			return "", nil
43		}
44		var msg struct {
45			Role    string          `json:"role"`
46			Content json.RawMessage `json:"content"`
47		}
48		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &msg); err != nil {
49			return "", nil
50		}
51		var s string
52		if json.Unmarshal(msg.Content, &s) == nil {
53			return msg.Role, []apiBlock{{Type: "text", Text: s}}
54		}
55		var blocks []apiBlock
56		_ = json.Unmarshal(msg.Content, &blocks) // tolerate; empty on failure
57		return msg.Role, blocks
58	}
59	
60	// toolResultText flattens a tool_result content payload to plain text.
61	func toolResultText(raw json.RawMessage) string {
62		if len(raw) == 0 {
63			return ""
64		}
65		var s string
66		if json.Unmarshal(raw, &s) == nil {
67			return s
68		}
69		var blocks []struct {
70			Type string `json:"type"`
71			Text string `json:"text"`
72		}
73		if json.Unmarshal(raw, &blocks) == nil {
74			var parts []string
75			for _, b := range blocks {
76				if b.Text != "" {
77					parts = append(parts, b.Text)
78				}
79			}
80			return strings.Join(parts, "\n")
81		}
82		return ""
83	}
84	
85	// apiUsage mirrors the message.usage object of an assistant record.
86	type apiUsage struct {
87		InputTokens              int `json:"input_tokens"`
88		OutputTokens             int `json:"output_tokens"`
89		CacheReadInputTokens     int `json:"cache_read_input_tokens"`
90		CacheCreationInputTokens int `json:"cache_creation_input_tokens"`
91		CacheCreation            struct {
92			Ephemeral5m int `json:"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens"`
93			Ephemeral1h int `json:"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens"`
94		} `json:"cache_creation"`
95	}
96	
97	// decodeMessageMeta extracts the message id, model id, and usage (if any) from a
98	// raw message. The message id lets callers deduplicate usage: Claude Code splits
99	// one assistant message across several records (one per content block), each
100	// repeating the same usage object, so usage must be counted once per message id.
101	func decodeMessageMeta(raw json.RawMessage) (msgID, modelID string, usage *apiUsage) {
102		if len(raw) == 0 {
103			return "", "", nil
104		}
105		var m struct {
106			ID    string    `json:"id"`
107			Model string    `json:"model"`
108			Usage *apiUsage `json:"usage"`
109		}
110		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &m); err != nil {
111			return "", "", nil
112		}
113		return m.ID, m.Model, m.Usage
114	}
115	
116	func parseTime(s string) time.Time {
117		if s == "" {
118			return time.Time{}
119		}
120		t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, s)
121		if err != nil {
122			return time.Time{}
123		}
124		return t
125	}
126

Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/run.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/run.go"
}
1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"os"
6		"os/exec"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"runtime"
9	
10		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery"
11		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/redact"
12		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render"
13		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript"
14	)
15	
16	func resolveOutDir(opts *options, si discovery.SessionInfo) string {
17		if opts.out != "" {
18			return opts.out
19		}
20		short := si.ID
21		if len(short) > 8 {
22			short = short[:8]
23		}
24		return filepath.Join("ccwhid-report", short)
25	}
26	
27	// resolveSession picks a session from flags. It returns needTUI=true when the
28	// user gave no selector.
29	func resolveSession(opts *options, root string) (discovery.SessionInfo, bool, error) {
30		switch {
31		case opts.session != "":
32			si, err := discovery.FindSession(root, opts.session)
33			return si, false, err
34		case opts.latest:
35			return latestSession(opts, root)
36		default:
37			return discovery.SessionInfo{}, true, nil
38		}
39	}
40	
41	// latestSession returns the most recent interactive (non-agent) session,
42	// optionally scoped to a project. Agent-spawned transcripts are skipped so
43	// --latest targets the user's own sessions.
44	func latestSession(opts *options, root string) (discovery.SessionInfo, bool, error) {
45		groups, err := discovery.Scan(root)
46		if err != nil {
47			return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, err
48		}
49		if opts.project != "" {
50			idx, err := discovery.FindProject(groups, opts.project)
51			if err != nil {
52				return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, err
53			}
54			groups = groups[idx : idx+1]
55		}
56		// Pick the newest interactive session by modification time. Group order
57		// alone is insufficient: groups are sorted by their newest session
58		// *including* agent transcripts, so a recent agent run in one project
59		// could otherwise mask a newer human session in another.
60		bestGI, bestSI := -1, -1
61		for gi := range groups {
62			for si := range groups[gi].Sessions {
63				if groups[gi].Sessions[si].IsAgent {
64					continue
65				}
66				if bestGI < 0 || groups[gi].Sessions[si].ModTime.After(groups[bestGI].Sessions[bestSI].ModTime) {
67					bestGI, bestSI = gi, si
68				}
69			}
70		}
71		if bestGI < 0 {
72			return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, fmt.Errorf("no sessions found")
73		}
74		return groups[bestGI].Sessions[bestSI], false, nil
75	}
76	
77	// generate parses, redacts, and renders a session, returning the output dir.
78	func generate(opts *options, si discovery.SessionInfo) (string, error) {
79		outDir := resolveOutDir(opts, si)
80		if err := ensureOutDir(outDir, opts.force); err != nil {
81			return "", err
82		}
83		sess, err := transcript.ParseFile(si.FilePath, transcript.Options{
84			IncludeSubagents: opts.includeSubagents,
85		})
86		if err != nil {
87			return "", err
88		}
89		if si.ID != "" {
90			sess.ID = si.ID
91		}
92		if sess.SkippedLines > 0 {
93			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "warning: skipped %d malformed line(s)\n", sess.SkippedLines)
94		}
95		if !opts.noRedact {
96			home, _ := os.UserHomeDir()
97			redact.Session(&sess, home)
98		}
99		if err := render.Site(sess, outDir, render.Options{Title: opts.title, Usage: opts.usage}); err != nil {
100			return "", err
101		}
102		return outDir, nil
103	}
104	
105	func ensureOutDir(dir string, force bool) error {
106		entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
107		if err != nil {
108			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
109				return os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755)
110			}
111			return err
112		}
113		if len(entries) == 0 {
114			return nil
115		}
116		if !force {
117			return fmt.Errorf("output directory %q is not empty (use --force to overwrite)", dir)
118		}
119		// --force: clear existing entries so the report reflects only this run and
120		// no stale files (e.g. renamed assets) are left behind.
121		for _, e := range entries {
122			if err := os.RemoveAll(filepath.Join(dir, e.Name())); err != nil {
123				return err
124			}
125		}
126		return nil
127	}
128	
129	// openInBrowser opens the report's index.html inside dir in the default browser.
130	func openInBrowser(dir string) error {
131		index := filepath.Join(dir, "index.html")
132		abs, err := filepath.Abs(index)
133		if err != nil {
134			return err
135		}
136		// Pass the filesystem path directly rather than a file:// URL: exec runs the
137		// opener without a shell, so a single path argument handles spaces and
138		// special characters, and avoids Windows backslash/URL-encoding pitfalls.
139		switch runtime.GOOS {
140		case "darwin":
141			return exec.Command("open", abs).Start()
142		case "windows":
143			return exec.Command("rundll32", "url.dll,FileProtocolHandler", abs).Start()
144		default:
145			return exec.Command("xdg-open", abs).Start()
146		}
147	}
148

Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/assets/report.html.tmpl
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/assets/report.html.tmpl"
}
1	<!DOCTYPE html>
2	<html lang="en">
3	<head>
4	<meta charset="utf-8">
5	<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
6	<title>{{ .Title }} · ccwhid</title>
7	<link rel="stylesheet" href="assets/styles.css">
8	</head>
9	<body>
10	<header class="topbar">
11	  <div class="topbar-inner">
12	    <div class="brand">ccwhid</div>
13	    <input id="filter" type="search" placeholder="Filter turns…" aria-label="Filter turns">
14	    <button id="toggle-all" class="btn">Expand all</button>
15	    <button id="toggle-tools" class="btn">Hide tool detail</button>
16	    <button id="toggle-theme" class="btn" aria-label="Toggle theme">◐</button>
17	  </div>
18	</header>
19	<div class="layout">
20	  <nav class="sidebar" aria-label="Prompts">
21	    <div class="sidebar-title">Prompts</div>
22	    <ol class="prompt-list">
23	      {{ range .Prompts }}<li><a href="#turn-{{ .Index }}">{{ .Preview }}</a></li>{{ end }}
24	    </ol>
25	  </nav>
26	  <main class="content">
27	    <section class="session-head">
28	      <h1>{{ .Title }}</h1>
29	      <div class="meta">
30	        <span>{{ .Session.ProjectPath }}</span>
31	        {{ if .Session.GitBranch }}<span>⎇ {{ .Session.GitBranch }}</span>{{ end }}
32	        <span>{{ .StartedAt }}</span>
33	        <span>{{ .TurnCount }} turns</span>
34	      </div>
35	    </section>
36	    {{ with .Usage }}
37	    <details class="usage-card">
38	      <summary class="usage-summary">{{ .Headline }}</summary>
39	      <div class="usage-body">
40	        {{ if .HasAny }}
41	        <table class="usage-models">
42	          <tr><th>model</th><th>input</th><th>output</th><th>cache read</th><th>cache write</th><th>est. cost</th></tr>
43	          {{ range .Models }}<tr><td>{{ .Model }}</td><td>{{ .Input }}</td><td>{{ .Output }}</td><td>{{ .CacheRead }}</td><td>{{ .CacheWrite }}</td><td>{{ .Cost }}</td></tr>{{ end }}
44	          <tr class="usage-total"><td>{{ .Total.Model }}</td><td>{{ .Total.Input }}</td><td>{{ .Total.Output }}</td><td>{{ .Total.CacheRead }}</td><td>{{ .Total.CacheWrite }}</td><td>{{ .Total.Cost }}</td></tr>
45	        </table>
46	        {{ end }}
47	        <p class="usage-note">{{ .Footnote }}</p>
48	      </div>
49	    </details>
50	    {{ end }}
51	    {{ range .Turns }}
52	    <article class="turn turn-{{ .Kind }}" id="turn-{{ .Index }}" data-search="{{ .SearchText }}">
53	      <div class="turn-role">{{ .RoleLabel }}{{ if .Badge }}<span class="usage-badge">{{ .Badge }}</span>{{ end }}</div>
54	      <div class="turn-body">{{ .Body }}</div>
55	    </article>
56	    {{ end }}
57	  </main>
58	</div>
59	<script src="assets/app.js"></script>
60	</body>
61	</html>
62

Bashhead -60 internal/redact/redact.go && grep -n "Subagent\|Agents" internal/redact/redact.go; echo ===MAIN; grep -n "includeSubagents\|include-subagents\|usage\b" cmd/ccwhid/main.go | head -20
{
  "command": "head -60 internal/redact/redact.go \u0026\u0026 grep -n \"Subagent\\|Agents\" internal/redact/redact.go; echo ===MAIN; grep -n \"includeSubagents\\|include-subagents\\|usage\\b\" cmd/ccwhid/main.go | head -20",

Exit code 1
// Package redact scrubs likely secrets and the user's home path from transcript
// text before it is rendered. It is best-effort defense-in-depth, not a
// guarantee of complete secret removal.
package redact

import (
	"regexp"
	"strings"

	"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
)

type rule struct {
	kind string
	re   *regexp.Regexp
}

// Ordered: more specific patterns first.
var rules = []rule{
	{"aws-key", regexp.MustCompile(`\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b`)},
	{"token", regexp.MustCompile(`\b(?:sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}|ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}|github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{50,}|xox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{10,})\b`)},
	{"jwt", regexp.MustCompile(`\beyJ[A-Za-z0-9_-]{10,}\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]{10,}\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]{10,}\b`)},
	{"assignment", regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)\b([A-Z0-9_]*(?:KEY|TOKEN|SECRET|PASSWORD|PASSWD))\s*[=:]\s*['"]?([^\s'"]{6,})`)},
}

// Redactor applies redaction rules and a home-directory rewrite.
type Redactor struct {
	homeDir string
}

func New(homeDir string) *Redactor { return &Redactor{homeDir: homeDir} }

// String redacts a single string.
func (r *Redactor) String(s string) string {
	for _, ru := range rules {
		if ru.kind == "assignment" {
			s = ru.re.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1=[REDACTED:assignment]")
			continue
		}
		s = ru.re.ReplaceAllString(s, "[REDACTED:"+ru.kind+"]")
	}
	if r.homeDir != "" {
		s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, r.homeDir, "~")
	}
	return s
}

// Session redacts every user-visible text field of a Session in place.
func Session(s *model.Session, homeDir string) {
	r := New(homeDir)
	// Session-level fields are rendered in the report header, so they must be
	// scrubbed too (ProjectPath in particular carries the home path/username).
	s.ProjectPath = r.String(s.ProjectPath)
	s.Title = r.String(s.Title)
	s.GitBranch = r.String(s.GitBranch)
	for i := range s.Turns {
		redactTurn(r, &s.Turns[i])
	}
}

82:	for i := range tc.Subagents {

Write~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-12-subagent-sessions.md
+ # Subagent Sessions Implementation Plan
+
+ > **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
+
+ **Goal:** Agent results render as agent-attributed cards (never "You"), linked subagent session transcripts become separate HTML pages, and their usage rolls up into the main usage summary.
+
+ **Architecture:** The transcript parser learns to recognize `<task-notification>` user records (new turn kind `agent-result`) and to load `<sessionId>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl` files as `model.AgentSession` values attached to the root `model.Session`. The renderer emits one extra HTML page per agent session under `outDir/subagents/` (same template, `{{ .Base }}`-prefixed asset paths) and links to them from tool cards and result cards. `usage.Compute` additionally walks `s.Agents` and tracks a subagent subtotal.
+
+ **Tech Stack:** Go 1.26, html/template, existing packages `internal/{model,transcript,render,usage,redact,tui}`.
+
+ **Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-subagent-sessions-design.md`
+
+ ## Global Constraints
+
+ - The task-notification fix (Task 1, Task 4 card rendering) is **unconditional** — independent of `--include-subagents` and `--usage`.
+ - Linked agent-session loading is gated on `--include-subagents` (existing flag, default `true`).
+ - Agent page path is exactly `subagents/agent-<ID>.html` relative to the output dir.
+ - A malformed notification, missing `subagents/` dir, or missing/unreadable `meta.json` must never fail the run (fallbacks per task).
+ - Flat SDK-spawned session files stay out of scope; do not touch discovery classification.
+ - Run `gofmt -l .` (must print nothing) before every commit.
+ - Every commit message ends with a `Co-Authored-By:` trailer identifying the authoring model.
+
+ ---
+
+ ### Task 1: Parse `<task-notification>` records into agent-result turns
+
+ **Files:**
+ - Modify: `internal/model/model.go`
+ - Modify: `internal/transcript/parse.go`
+ - Modify: `internal/redact/redact.go` (redact the new summary field)
+ - Test: `internal/transcript/notification_test.go` (new)
+
+ **Interfaces:**
+ - Consumes: existing `buildTurn`, `apiBlock`, `model.Turn`.
+ - Produces: `model.TurnAgentResult TurnKind = "agent-result"`; `model.Turn` fields `AgentID, AgentStatus, AgentSummary string`; unexported `parseTaskNotification(s string) (taskNotification, bool)` with `taskNotification{TaskID, ToolUseID, Status, Summary, Result string}`. Task 4 renders turns of this kind; Task 5 links via `Turn.AgentID`.
+
+ - [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**
+
+ Create `internal/transcript/notification_test.go`:
+
+ ```go
+ package transcript
+
+ import (
+ "strings"
+ "testing"
+
+ "github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
+ )
+
+ const notifBody = `<task-notification>
+ <task-id>acb6584f99f2f81fd</task-id>
+ <tool-use-id>toolu_0112a19E</tool-use-id>
+ <output-file>/tmp/tasks/acb6584f99f2f81fd.output</output-file>
+ <status>completed</status>
+ <summary>Agent "Implement Task 12: Profiles view" finished</summary>
+ <note>may notify more than once</note>
+ <result>Both items fixed.
+
+ ## Status: Complete</result>
+ </task-notification>`
+
+ func notifLine(t *testing.T, body string) string {
+ t.Helper()
+ b, err := jsonMarshalString(body)
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ return `{"type":"user","timestamp":"2026-07-05T18:26:08.000Z","message":{"role":"user","content":` + b + `}}`
+ }
+
+ func TestParseTaskNotificationTurn(t *testing.T) {
+ s, err := Parse(strings.NewReader(notifLine(t, notifBody)), Options{})
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if len(s.Turns) != 1 {
+ t.Fatalf("got %d turns, want 1", len(s.Turns))
+ }
+ turn := s.Turns[0]
+ if turn.Kind != model.TurnAgentResult {
+ t.Fatalf("kind = %q, want %q", turn.Kind, model.TurnAgentResult)
+ }
+ if turn.AgentID != "acb6584f99f2f81fd" {
+ t.Errorf("AgentID = %q", turn.AgentID)
+ }
+ if turn.AgentStatus != "completed" {
+ t.Errorf("AgentStatus = %q", turn.AgentStatus)
+ }
+ if want := `Agent "Implement Task 12: Profiles view" finished`; turn.AgentSummary != want {
+ t.Errorf("AgentSummary = %q", turn.AgentSummary)
+ }
+ if len(turn.Blocks) != 1 || !strings.Contains(turn.Blocks[0].Text, "## Status: Complete") {
+ t.Errorf("body block wrong: %+v", turn.Blocks)
+ }
+ if strings.Contains(turn.Blocks[0].Text, "<task-id>") {
+ t.Error("body must be the <result> payload only, not the raw envelope")
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestParseTaskNotificationEmptyResultFallsBackToSummary(t *testing.T) {
+ body := "<task-notification>\n<task-id>a1</task-id>\n<status>completed</status>\n<summary>Agent \"x\" finished</summary>\n<result></result>\n</task-notification>"
+ s, err := Parse(strings.NewReader(notifLine(t, body)), Options{})
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if len(s.Turns) != 1 || s.Turns[0].Blocks[0].Text != `Agent "x" finished` {
+ t.Fatalf("want summary as body, got %+v", s.Turns)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestParseMalformedNotificationStaysUserTurn(t *testing.T) {
+ // No <task-id> -> must stay a plain user turn, content untouched.
+ body := "<task-notification>\nbroken payload\n</task-notification>"
+ s, err := Parse(strings.NewReader(notifLine(t, body)), Options{})
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if len(s.Turns) != 1 || s.Turns[0].Kind != model.TurnUser {
+ t.Fatalf("want plain user turn, got %+v", s.Turns)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestUserTextMentioningNotificationMidStringStaysUserTurn(t *testing.T) {
+ body := "please explain what a <task-notification> record is"
+ s, err := Parse(strings.NewReader(notifLine(t, body)), Options{})
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if len(s.Turns) != 1 || s.Turns[0].Kind != model.TurnUser {
+ t.Fatalf("want plain user turn, got %+v", s.Turns)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestRepeatNotificationsEachProduceATurn(t *testing.T) {
+ line := notifLine(t, notifBody)
+ s, err := Parse(strings.NewReader(line+"\n"+line), Options{})
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if len(s.Turns) != 2 {
+ t.Fatalf("got %d turns, want 2 (no dedup)", len(s.Turns))
+ }
+ }
+ ```
+
+ Add the small helper at the bottom of the same test file:
+
+ ```go
+ // jsonMarshalString wraps a string as a JSON string literal.
+ func jsonMarshalString(s string) (string, error) {
+ b, err := json.Marshal(s)
+ return string(b), err
+ }
+ ```
+
+ with `"encoding/json"` added to the test file's imports.
+
+ - [ ] **Step 2: Run tests to verify they fail**
+
+ Run: `go test ./internal/transcript/ -run 'Notification|NotificationTurn|StaysUserTurn|EachProduceATurn' -v`
+ Expected: FAIL — `model.TurnAgentResult` undefined (compile error).
+
+ - [ ] **Step 3: Add model fields**
+
+ In `internal/model/model.go`, extend the turn-kind consts:
+
+ ```go
+ const (
+ TurnUser TurnKind = "user"
+ TurnAssistant TurnKind = "assistant"
+ TurnAgentResult TurnKind = "agent-result" // background-agent completion fed back into the session
+ )
+ ```
+
+ Extend `Turn` (after the `Usage` field):
+
+ ```go
+ // Agent-result fields, set only when Kind == TurnAgentResult.
+ AgentID string // task/agent id from the notification
+ AgentStatus string // e.g. "completed"
+ AgentSummary string // e.g. `Agent "Implement Task 12" finished`
+ ```
+
+ - [ ] **Step 4: Implement notification parsing**
+
+ In `internal/transcript/parse.go`, add (near `buildTurn`):
+
+ ```go
+ // taskNotification is the parsed payload of a <task-notification> user record —
+ // the message a background agent's completion injects into the parent session.
+ type taskNotification struct {
+ TaskID string
+ ToolUseID string
+ Status string
+ Summary string
+ Result string
+ }
+
+ // parseTaskNotification extracts fields from a <task-notification> payload with
+ // tolerant string scanning (the payload is pseudo-XML with unescaped markdown
+ // inside <result>). ok is false unless both task-id and summary are present.
+ func parseTaskNotification(s string) (taskNotification, bool) {
+ t := trimLeftSpace(s)
+ if !hasPrefix(t, "<task-notification>") {
+ return taskNotification{}, false
+ }
+ n := taskNotification{
+ TaskID: tagContent(t, "task-id"),
+ ToolUseID: tagContent(t, "tool-use-id"),
+ Status: tagContent(t, "status"),
+ Summary: tagContent(t, "summary"),
+ Result: tagContent(t, "result"),
+ }
+ if n.TaskID == "" || n.Summary == "" {
+ return taskNotification{}, false
+ }
+ return n, true
+ }
+
+ // tagContent returns the text between the first <name> and the last </name>,
+ // trimmed. The last closing tag guards <result> bodies that themselves contain
+ // XML-looking text.
+ func tagContent(s, name string) string {
+ open, close := "<"+name+">", "</"+name+">"
+ i := strings.Index(s, open)
+ if i < 0 {
+ return ""
+ }
+ rest := s[i+len(open):]
+ j := strings.LastIndex(rest, close)
+ if j < 0 {
+ return ""
+ }
+ return strings.TrimSpace(rest[:j])
+ }
+
+ func trimLeftSpace(s string) string { return strings.TrimLeft(s, " \t\r\n") }
+
+ func hasPrefix(s, p string) bool { return strings.HasPrefix(s, p) }
+ ```
+
+ Add `"strings"` to `parse.go`'s imports. (If you prefer, call `strings.HasPrefix`/`strings.TrimLeft` directly and drop the two one-line wrappers — either is fine; do not add other helpers.)
+
+ In `buildTurn`, immediately after the `turn.Kind` assignment block, add:
+
+ ```go
+ // A background agent's completion arrives as a user record whose content is
+ // a single <task-notification> text payload. Surface it as an agent-result
+ // turn so the report attributes it to the agent, not to the user.
+ if turn.Kind == model.TurnUser && len(blocks) == 1 && blocks[0].Type == "text" {
+ if n, ok := parseTaskNotification(blocks[0].Text); ok {
+ turn.Kind = model.TurnAgentResult
+ turn.AgentID = n.TaskID
+ turn.AgentStatus = n.Status
+ turn.AgentSummary = n.Summary
+ body := n.Result
+ if body == "" {
+ body = n.Summary
+ }
+ turn.Blocks = []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockText, Text: body}}
+ return turn
+ }
+ }
+ ```
+
+ - [ ] **Step 5: Redact the new field**
+
+ In `internal/redact/redact.go`, function `redactTurn`, add alongside the existing per-turn string fields:
+
+ ```go
+ t.AgentSummary = r.String(t.AgentSummary)
+ ```
+
+ (Open the file first and place it with the other `r.String` rewrites at the top of `redactTurn`; the result body is a normal text block and is already covered.)
+
+ - [ ] **Step 6: Run the tests**
+
+ Run: `go test ./internal/transcript/ ./internal/redact/ -v`
+ Expected: all PASS, including the five new tests.
+
+ - [ ] **Step 7: Run the full suite and commit**
+
+ Run: `gofmt -l . && go test ./...`
+ Expected: gofmt prints nothing; all packages pass.
+
+ ```bash
+ git add internal/model/model.go internal/transcript/parse.go internal/transcript/notification_test.go internal/redact/redact.go
+ git commit -m "feat: parse task-notification records as agent-result turns"
+ ```
+
+ ---
+
+ ### Task 2: Load linked agent-session files
+
+ **Files:**
+ - Modify: `internal/model/model.go`
+ - Modify: `internal/transcript/parse.go` (Options field, sidechain override)
+ - Create: `internal/transcript/agents.go`
+ - Modify: `internal/redact/redact.go` (walk `s.Agents`)
+ - Test: `internal/transcript/agents_test.go` (new)
+
+ **Interfaces:**
+ - Consumes: `ParseFile(path string, opts Options) (model.Session, error)` (existing), `model.Session`.
+ - Produces:
+ - `model.AgentSession{ID, Description, AgentType, ToolUseID string; SpawnDepth int; Session Session}` and `model.Session.Agents []AgentSession`.
+ - `transcript.Options.AgentFile bool` — when set, `isSidechain` on records is ignored (an agent's own file IS its main chain).
+ - `func LoadAgentSessions(transcriptPath string, opts Options) ([]model.AgentSession, error)` in package `transcript` — reads `<transcriptPath minus .jsonl>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl`, sorted by file name; missing dir → `(nil, nil)`; unparsable file → skip with stderr warning; missing/unreadable meta.json → Description falls back to ID.
+
+ - [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**
+
+ Create `internal/transcript/agents_test.go`:
+
+ ```go
+ package transcript
+
+ import (
+ "os"
+ "path/filepath"
+ "strings"
+ "testing"
+
+ "github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
+ )
+
+ // agentRecord is a minimal agent-file line: sidechain-flagged user text.
+ const agentRecord = `{"type":"user","isSidechain":true,"timestamp":"2026-07-05T18:11:19.567Z","message":{"role":"user","content":"do the thing"}}`
+
+ func writeAgentFixture(t *testing.T) (rootPath string) {
+ t.Helper()
+ dir := t.TempDir()
+ rootPath = filepath.Join(dir, "root-session.jsonl")
+ if err := os.WriteFile(rootPath, []byte(`{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"hi"}}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ sub := filepath.Join(dir, "root-session", "subagents")
+ if err := os.MkdirAll(sub, 0o755); err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ files := map[string]string{
+ "agent-bbb222.jsonl": agentRecord + "\n",
+ "agent-aaa111.jsonl": agentRecord + "\n",
+ "agent-aaa111.meta.json": `{"agentType":"general-purpose","description":"Implement Task 12","toolUseId":"toolu_01x","spawnDepth":1}`,
+ "agent-broken.jsonl": "", // empty file parses to an empty session, still listed
+ "notes.txt": "ignore me",
+ }
+ for name, content := range files {
+ if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(sub, name), []byte(content), 0o644); err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ }
+ return rootPath
+ }
+
+ func TestLoadAgentSessions(t *testing.T) {
+ root := writeAgentFixture(t)
+ agents, err := LoadAgentSessions(root, Options{IncludeSubagents: true})
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if len(agents) != 3 {
+ t.Fatalf("got %d agents, want 3", len(agents))
+ }
+ // Sorted by file name: aaa111, bbb222, broken.
+ a := agents[0]
+ if a.ID != "aaa111" || a.Description != "Implement Task 12" || a.AgentType != "general-purpose" || a.ToolUseID != "toolu_01x" || a.SpawnDepth != 1 {
+ t.Errorf("meta not applied: %+v", a)
+ }
+ if len(a.Session.Turns) != 1 || a.Session.Turns[0].Kind != model.TurnUser {
+ t.Errorf("agent transcript not parsed as main chain: %+v", a.Session.Turns)
+ }
+ // No meta.json -> Description falls back to ID.
+ if agents[1].ID != "bbb222" || agents[1].Description != "bbb222" {
+ t.Errorf("fallback description wrong: %+v", agents[1])
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestLoadAgentSessionsMissingDir(t *testing.T) {
+ dir := t.TempDir()
+ root := filepath.Join(dir, "lonely.jsonl")
+ if err := os.WriteFile(root, []byte("{}\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ agents, err := LoadAgentSessions(root, Options{})
+ if err != nil || agents != nil {
+ t.Fatalf("want (nil, nil), got (%v, %v)", agents, err)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestAgentFileOptionParsesSidechainAsMainChain(t *testing.T) {
+ s, err := Parse(strings.NewReader(agentRecord), Options{AgentFile: true})
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if len(s.Turns) != 1 {
+ t.Fatalf("AgentFile должен parse sidechain records as main chain; got %d turns", len(s.Turns))
+ }
+ // Without the option the sidechain record is dropped (no owner).
+ s2, err := Parse(strings.NewReader(agentRecord), Options{IncludeSubagents: true})
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if len(s2.Turns) != 0 {
+ t.Fatalf("without AgentFile the record must not become a main turn; got %d", len(s2.Turns))
+ }
+ }
+ ```
+
+ (Note: fix the stray non-English word in the failure message — write "AgentFile must parse sidechain records as main chain".)
+
+ - [ ] **Step 2: Run tests to verify they fail**
+
+ Run: `go test ./internal/transcript/ -run 'AgentSessions|AgentFile' -v`
+ Expected: FAIL — `LoadAgentSessions` and `Options.AgentFile` undefined (compile error).
+
+ - [ ] **Step 3: Add the model type**
+
+ In `internal/model/model.go`, add to `Session`:
+
+ ```go
+ Agents []AgentSession // linked subagent sessions (subagents/ dir), when loaded
+ ```
+
+ and after the `Subagent` type:
+
+ ```go
+ // AgentSession is a subagent session file linked to a root session
+ // (<projectDir>/<sessionId>/subagents/agent-<ID>.jsonl).
+ type AgentSession struct {
+ ID string // agentId, from the file name agent-<ID>.jsonl
+ Description string // from meta.json; falls back to ID
+ AgentType string // from meta.json; may be empty
+ ToolUseID string // id of the spawning tool call; may be empty
+ SpawnDepth int // from meta.json; informational only
+ Session Session // the parsed agent transcript
+ }
+ ```
+
+ - [ ] **Step 4: Add the AgentFile option**
+
+ In `internal/transcript/parse.go`:
+
+ ```go
+ // Options controls how a transcript is folded into a Session.
+ type Options struct {
+ IncludeSubagents bool
+ // AgentFile marks the input as an agent's own transcript file: its records
+ // carry isSidechain=true but form the file's main chain, so the flag is
+ // ignored.
+ AgentFile bool
+ }
+ ```
+
+ In `Parse`, at the top of the per-record handling (right after the `rec.IsMeta` check), add:
+
+ ```go
+ if opts.AgentFile {
+ rec.IsSidechain = false
+ }
+ ```
+
+ - [ ] **Step 5: Implement the loader**
+
+ Create `internal/transcript/agents.go`:
+
+ ```go
+ package transcript
+
+ import (
+ "encoding/json"
+ "fmt"
+ "os"
+ "path/filepath"
+ "strings"
+
+ "github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
+ )
+
+ // agentMeta mirrors agent-<ID>.meta.json next to each agent transcript.
+ type agentMeta struct {
+ AgentType string `json:"agentType"`
+ Description string `json:"description"`
+ ToolUseID string `json:"toolUseId"`
+ SpawnDepth int `json:"spawnDepth"`
+ }
+
+ // LoadAgentSessions parses the linked subagent transcripts of a root session:
+ // <transcriptPath minus .jsonl>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl, sorted by file name.
+ // A missing subagents dir yields (nil, nil); an unparsable agent file is
+ // skipped with a warning on stderr. Nested agents (spawnDepth > 1) live in the
+ // same flat directory and are returned alongside depth-1 agents.
+ func LoadAgentSessions(transcriptPath string, opts Options) ([]model.AgentSession, error) {
+ dir := filepath.Join(strings.TrimSuffix(transcriptPath, ".jsonl"), "subagents")
+ entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
+ if err != nil {
+ if os.IsNotExist(err) {
+ return nil, nil
+ }
+ return nil, err
+ }
+ opts.AgentFile = true
+ var out []model.AgentSession
+ for _, e := range entries { // ReadDir sorts by name
+ name := e.Name()
+ if e.IsDir() || !strings.HasPrefix(name, "agent-") || !strings.HasSuffix(name, ".jsonl") {
+ continue
+ }
+ id := strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimPrefix(name, "agent-"), ".jsonl")
+ sess, err := ParseFile(filepath.Join(dir, name), opts)
+ if err != nil {
+ fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "warning: skipping agent transcript %s: %v\n", name, err)
+ continue
+ }
+ a := model.AgentSession{ID: id, Description: id, Session: sess}
+ if b, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, "agent-"+id+".meta.json")); err == nil {
+ var m agentMeta
+ if json.Unmarshal(b, &m) == nil {
+ if m.Description != "" {
+ a.Description = m.Description
+ }
+ a.AgentType = m.AgentType
+ a.ToolUseID = m.ToolUseID
+ a.SpawnDepth = m.SpawnDepth
+ }
+ }
+ out = append(out, a)
+ }
+ return out, nil
+ }
+ ```
+
+ - [ ] **Step 6: Redact agent sessions**
+
+ In `internal/redact/redact.go`, at the end of `func Session(s *model.Session, homeDir string)`, add:
+
+ ```go
+ for i := range s.Agents {
+ s.Agents[i].Description = r.String(s.Agents[i].Description)
+ Session(&s.Agents[i].Session, homeDir)
+ }
+ ```
+
+ Note: `Session` creates a new Redactor per call; that is fine (it is cheap and keeps the recursion simple).
+
+ - [ ] **Step 7: Run the tests**

+ Run: `go test ./internal/transcript/ ./internal/redact/ -v`
+ Expected: PASS.
+

+ Expected: gofmt prints nothing; all pass.

+ git commit -m "feat: load linked subagent session transcripts"
+ ```

+

+ - [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**
+
+ Append to `internal/usage/usage_test.go`:
+
+ ```go
+ func TestComputeRollsUpLinkedAgentSessions(t *testing.T) {
+ agent := model.AgentSession{
+ ID: "a1",
+ Session: model.Session{Turns: []model.Turn{
+ {Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Model: "claude-opus-4-8", Usage: u(1_000_000, 0, 0, 0, 0)},
+ }},
+ }
+ s := model.Session{
+ Turns: []model.Turn{
+ {Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Model: "claude-opus-4-8", Usage: u(1_000_000, 0, 0, 0, 0)},
+ },
+ Agents: []model.AgentSession{agent},
+ }
+ r := Compute(s)
+ if r.Total.Input != 2_000_000 {
+ t.Errorf("Total.Input = %d, want 2000000 (main + agent)", r.Total.Input)
+ }
+ if r.Subagents.Input != 1_000_000 {
+ t.Errorf("Subagents.Input = %d, want 1000000", r.Subagents.Input)
+ }
+ if r.AgentSessions != 1 {
+ t.Errorf("AgentSessions = %d, want 1", r.AgentSessions)
+ }
+ // opus-4-8 input $5/MTok -> subagent cost $5.00.
+ if r.SubagentsCost == nil || *r.SubagentsCost < 4.99 || *r.SubagentsCost > 5.01 {
+ t.Errorf("SubagentsCost = %v, want ~5.00", r.SubagentsCost)
+ }
+ // Per-model table merges both (one opus row with 2M input).
+ if len(r.ByModel) != 1 || r.ByModel[0].Tokens.Input != 2_000_000 {
+ t.Errorf("ByModel = %+v, want single merged opus row with 2M input", r.ByModel)
+ }
+ // Agent turns must not create per-turn cost entries.
+ if len(r.PerTurnCost) != 1 {
+ t.Errorf("PerTurnCost entries = %d, want 1 (main turn only)", len(r.PerTurnCost))
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestComputeUnpricedAgentSessionHasNilSubagentsCost(t *testing.T) {
+ s := model.Session{
+ Agents: []model.AgentSession{{
+ ID: "a1",
+ Session: model.Session{Turns: []model.Turn{
+ {Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Model: "mystery-model", Usage: u(100, 0, 0, 0, 0)},
+ }},
+ }},
+ }
+ r := Compute(s)
+ if r.SubagentsCost != nil {
+ t.Errorf("SubagentsCost = %v, want nil (unpriced)", *r.SubagentsCost)
+ }
+ if r.Subagents.Input != 100 || r.AgentSessions != 1 {
+ t.Errorf("subtotal wrong: %+v sessions=%d", r.Subagents, r.AgentSessions)
+ }
+ if !r.HasUnknownModel {
+ t.Error("HasUnknownModel should be true")
+ }
+ }
+ ```
+

+ - [ ] **Step 3: Implement the rollup**
+
+ In `internal/usage/usage.go`, extend `Report`:
+
+ ```go
+ Subagents TokenCounts // tokens from linked agent sessions
+ SubagentsCost *float64 // nil when nothing in them was priced
+ AgentSessions int // number of linked agent sessions
+ ```
+
+ In `Compute`, make the accumulator subagent-aware. Add just before `accumulate` is defined:
+
+ ```go
+ subByModel := map[string]*TokenCounts{}
+ inAgent := false
+ ```
+
+ Inside `accumulate`, after `tc.add(*t.Usage)`, add:
+
+ ```go
+ if inAgent {
+ r.Subagents.add(*t.Usage)
+ stc, ok := subByModel[key]
+ if !ok {
+ stc = &TokenCounts{}
+ subByModel[key] = stc
+ }
+ stc.add(*t.Usage)
+ }
+ ```
+
+ After the existing "Totals + per-model" loop over `s.Turns` (and before the per-turn cost loop), add:
+
+ ```go
+ // Linked agent sessions merge into the same totals and per-model rows,
+ // tracked separately so the report can show the subagent share.
+ r.AgentSessions = len(s.Agents)
+ inAgent = true
+ for _, a := range s.Agents {
+ for _, t := range a.Session.Turns {
+ walk(t, accumulate)
+ }
+ }
+ var subTotal float64
+ subPriced := false
+ for m, tc := range subByModel {
+ if p, ok := Lookup(m); ok {
+ subTotal += cost(*tc, p)
+ subPriced = true
+ }
+ }
+ if subPriced {
+ r.SubagentsCost = &subTotal
+ }
+ ```

+ func TestAgentResultTurnRendersAsAgentCard(t *testing.T) {
+ s := model.Session{Turns: []model.Turn{{
+ Kind: model.TurnAgentResult,
+ AgentStatus: "completed",
+ AgentSummary: `Agent "Implement Task 12: Profiles view" finished`,
+ Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockText, Text: "All done."}},
+ }}}
+ d := buildViewModel(s, "t", Options{})
+ tv := d.Turns[0]
+ if tv.RoleLabel != "Agent · Implement Task 12: Profiles view" {
+ t.Errorf("RoleLabel = %q", tv.RoleLabel)
+ }
+ if tv.Status != "completed" {
+ t.Errorf("Status = %q", tv.Status)
+ }
+ if tv.Kind != "agent-result" {
+ t.Errorf("Kind = %q", tv.Kind)
+ }
+ if tv.RoleLabel == "You" {
+ t.Error("agent result must never be attributed to the user")
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestAgentRoleLabelFallsBackWithoutQuotes(t *testing.T) {
+ if got := agentRoleLabel("something finished"); got != "Agent" {
+ t.Errorf("agentRoleLabel = %q, want Agent", got)
+ }
+ if got := agentRoleLabel(""); got != "Agent" {
+ t.Errorf("agentRoleLabel(empty) = %q, want Agent", got)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestUsageViewSubagentLineAndFootnote(t *testing.T) {
+ c := 5.0
+ r := usage.Report{
+ HasAnyUsage: true,
+ Total: usage.TokenCounts{Input: 2_000_000},
+ Subagents: usage.TokenCounts{Input: 1_000_000},
+ SubagentsCost: &c,
+ AgentSessions: 3,
+ PricesAsOf: "2026-07",
+ }
+ v := buildUsageView(r)
+ if v.SubLine != "of which subagents: 1.0M in+out · ~$5.00 (3 sessions)" {
+ t.Errorf("SubLine = %q", v.SubLine)
+ }
+ if !strings.Contains(v.Footnote, "Includes 3 linked subagent session(s)") {
+ t.Errorf("footnote = %q", v.Footnote)
+ }
+ if strings.Contains(v.Footnote, "sub-agent sessions stored as separate files") {
+ t.Errorf("old exclusion wording must go when agents are included: %q", v.Footnote)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestUsageViewNoSubagentLineWithoutAgents(t *testing.T) {
+ r := usage.Report{HasAnyUsage: true, Total: usage.TokenCounts{Input: 100}, PricesAsOf: "2026-07"}
+ v := buildUsageView(r)
+ if v.SubLine != "" {
+ t.Errorf("SubLine = %q, want empty", v.SubLine)
+ }
+ if !strings.Contains(v.Footnote, "sub-agent sessions stored as separate files, and server-tool fees, are excluded") {
+ t.Errorf("existing footnote must stay when no agents: %q", v.Footnote)
+ }
+ }
+ ```

+ - [ ] **Step 3: Implement the card and usage-line rendering**
+
+ In `internal/render/render.go`:
+
+ 1. Extend `turnView`:
+
+ ```go
+ type turnView struct {
+ Index int
+ Kind string
+ RoleLabel string
+ Status string // agent-result status chip, e.g. "completed"
+ SearchText string
+ Body template.HTML
+ Badge string // per-turn usage badge, e.g. "12k tok · ~$0.18"
+ }
+ ```
+
+ 2. Extend `usageView` with `SubLine string` (after `Total`):
+
+ ```go
+ SubLine string // "of which subagents: …", empty when no linked agents
+ ```
+
+ 3. Replace `roleLabel` and add `agentRoleLabel`:
+
+ ```go
+ func roleLabel(t model.Turn) string {
+ switch t.Kind {
+ case model.TurnUser:
+ return "You"
+ case model.TurnAgentResult:
+ return agentRoleLabel(t.AgentSummary)
+ default:
+ return "Claude"
+ }
+ }
+
+ // agentRoleLabel derives `Agent · <name>` from a notification summary like
+ // `Agent "Implement Task 12" finished`; plain "Agent" when no quoted name.
+ func agentRoleLabel(summary string) string {
+ if i := strings.Index(summary, `"`); i >= 0 {
+ if j := strings.Index(summary[i+1:], `"`); j > 0 {
+ return "Agent · " + summary[i+1:i+1+j]
+ }
+ }
+ return "Agent"
+ }
+ ```
+
+ Update the two existing `roleLabel(...)` call sites to pass the turn:
+ - in `buildViewModel`: `RoleLabel: roleLabel(t)` and add `Status: t.AgentStatus`
+ - in `renderTool` (subagent inline rendering): `roleLabel(st)`
+
+ 4. In `buildUsageView`, after `v.Total = modelRow(...)`, add:
+
+ ```go
+ if r.AgentSessions > 0 {
+ sub := "of which subagents: " + formatTokens(r.Subagents.InOut()) + " in+out"
+ if r.SubagentsCost != nil {
+ sub += " · ~" + formatCost(*r.SubagentsCost)
+ }
+ v.SubLine = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%d sessions)", sub, r.AgentSessions)
+ }
+ ```
+
+ and change the footnote block to:
+
+ ```go
+ foot := "Estimated — Anthropic list prices as of " + r.PricesAsOf + "."
+ if r.AgentSessions > 0 {
+ foot += fmt.Sprintf(" Includes %d linked subagent session(s); server-tool fees are excluded.", r.AgentSessions)
+ } else {
+ foot += " Covers this transcript only; sub-agent sessions stored as separate files, and server-tool fees, are excluded."
+ }
+ if hasUnpriced {
+ foot += " Estimated cost excludes unpriced models (shown as n/a); their tokens are still counted."
+ }
+ v.Footnote = foot
+ ```
+
+ Add `"fmt"` to render.go's imports.
+

+ - Turn header line becomes:

+ ```
+
+ - Below the usage table (before the footnote paragraph):
+

+ ```
+
+ 6. In `assets/styles.css`, append:
+
+ ```css
+ .turn-agent-result { background: var(--surface); border-left: 3px solid var(--accent); }
+ .turn-agent-result .turn-role { color: var(--accent); }
+ .agent-status { margin-left: 0.5rem; padding: 0 0.4rem; border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 999px; color: var(--muted); font-size: 0.7rem; text-transform: none; letter-spacing: normal; }
+ .usage-sub { color: var(--muted); font-size: 0.85rem; margin-top: 0.5rem; }
+ ```
+
+ (The card class `turn-agent-result` comes for free: the template already emits `turn-{{ .Kind }}` and the kind string is `agent-result`.)

+ ### Task 5: Subagent pages, links, and wiring
+
+ **Files:**
