+ **Interfaces:**
+ - Consumes: `model.Session.Agents`, `model.AgentSession` (Task 2), `Turn.AgentID` (Task 1), `transcript.LoadAgentSessions` (Task 2).
+ - Produces: `render.Site` writes `outDir/subagents/agent-<ID>.html` per agent session; main `index.html` links to them from Task/Agent tool cards (`ToolUseID` match) and agent-result cards (`AgentID` match); agent pages link back via `../index.html` and to sibling pages.
+
+ - [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing e2e test**
+
+ Append to `internal/render/render_e2e_test.go` (it already renders a session to a temp dir; follow its existing helper style — read the file first):
+
+ ```go
+ func TestSiteWritesSubagentPagesAndLinks(t *testing.T) {
+ agent := model.AgentSession{
+ ID: "abc123",
+ Description: "Implement Task 12",
+ AgentType: "general-purpose",
+ ToolUseID: "toolu_9",
+ Session: model.Session{Turns: []model.Turn{
+ {Kind: model.TurnUser, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockText, Text: "brief"}}},
+ {Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockText, Text: "agent work"}}},
+ }},
+ }
+ s := model.Session{
+ Title: "root",
+ Turns: []model.Turn{
+ {Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: &model.ToolCall{
+ ID: "toolu_9", Name: "Agent", Summary: "Implement Task 12",
+ }}}},
+ {Kind: model.TurnAgentResult, AgentID: "abc123", AgentStatus: "completed",
+ AgentSummary: `Agent "Implement Task 12" finished`,
+ Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockText, Text: "done"}}},
+ },
+ Agents: []model.AgentSession{agent},
+ }
+ dir := t.TempDir()
+ if err := Site(s, dir, Options{}); err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+
+ page, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, "subagents", "agent-abc123.html"))
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatalf("agent page missing: %v", err)
+ }
+ for _, want := range []string{"agent work", `href="../index.html"`, `href="../assets/styles.css"`, "Implement Task 12"} {
+ if !strings.Contains(string(page), want) {
+ t.Errorf("agent page missing %q", want)
+ }
+ }
+
+ index, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, "index.html"))
+ if err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ // Both the tool card and the result card link to the agent page.
+ if got := strings.Count(string(index), `href="subagents/agent-abc123.html"`); got < 2 {
+ t.Errorf("index has %d links to the agent page, want >= 2", got)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestSiteNoAgentsWritesNoSubagentsDir(t *testing.T) {
+ dir := t.TempDir()
+ if err := Site(model.Session{Title: "x"}, dir, Options{}); err != nil {
+ t.Fatal(err)
+ }
+ if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, "subagents")); !os.IsNotExist(err) {
+ t.Errorf("subagents dir must not exist for agent-less sessions (err=%v)", err)
+ }
+ }
+ ```
+
+ - [ ] **Step 2: Run tests to verify they fail**
+
+ Run: `go test ./internal/render/ -run 'SubagentPages|NoSubagentsDir' -v`
+ Expected: FAIL — no agent page is written / no links found.
+
+ - [ ] **Step 3: Implement multi-page rendering and links**
+
+ In `internal/render/render.go`:
+
+ 1. Add a page-context type and link resolver:
+
+ ```go
+ // pageInfo describes where the page being rendered lives relative to outDir.
+ type pageInfo struct {
+ Base string // asset prefix: "" on index.html, "../" on agent pages
+ PagePrefix string // href prefix to agent pages: "subagents/" on index, "" on agent pages
+ BackHref string // back-link to the main report; empty on index
+ Subtitle string // agent pages: "<agentType> · agent-<ID>"
+ }
+
+ // agentLinks resolves tool-use ids and agent ids to agent-page hrefs.
+ type agentLinks struct {
+ prefix string
+ byToolUse map[string]string // tool_use id -> agent id
+ byAgentID map[string]bool
+ }
+
+ func newAgentLinks(agents []model.AgentSession, prefix string) *agentLinks {
+ l := &agentLinks{prefix: prefix, byToolUse: map[string]string{}, byAgentID: map[string]bool{}}
+ for _, a := range agents {
+ if a.ToolUseID != "" {
+ l.byToolUse[a.ToolUseID] = a.ID
+ }
+ l.byAgentID[a.ID] = true
+ }
+ return l
+ }
+
+ func (l *agentLinks) forToolUse(id string) string {
+ if aid, ok := l.byToolUse[id]; ok {
+ return l.prefix + "agent-" + aid + ".html"
+ }
+ return ""
+ }
+
+ func (l *agentLinks) forAgent(id string) string {
+ if l.byAgentID[id] {
+ return l.prefix + "agent-" + id + ".html"
+ }
+ return ""
+ }
+ ```
+
+ 2. Thread the links through body rendering. Change signatures:
+
+ ```go
+ func renderTurnBody(t model.Turn, links *agentLinks) template.HTML
+ func renderTool(tc *model.ToolCall, links *agentLinks) string
+ ```
+
+ - `renderTurnBody` passes `links` to `renderTool`; its recursive call for inline sidechain turns passes `links` unchanged.
+ - In `renderTool`, after the `tool-summary` span inside the summary element, add:
+
+ ```go
+ if href := links.forToolUse(tc.ID); href != "" {
+ b.WriteString(`<a class="agent-link" href="` + html.EscapeString(href) + `">transcript ↗</a>`)
+ }
+ ```
+
+ 3. Extend `viewData` and `turnView`:
+
+ ```go
+ // viewData gains:
+ Base string
+ BackHref string
+ Subtitle string
+ // turnView gains:
+ AgentHref string // link to the agent's transcript page, when one exists
+ ```
+
+ 4. Change `buildViewModel` to accept the page context and links:
+
+ ```go
+ func buildViewModel(s model.Session, title string, opts Options, page pageInfo, links *agentLinks) viewData {
+ d := viewData{
+ Title: title,
+ Session: s,
+ TurnCount: len(s.Turns),
+ Base: page.Base,
+ BackHref: page.BackHref,
+ Subtitle: page.Subtitle,
+ }
+ ...
+ tv := turnView{
+ ...
+ Body: renderTurnBody(t, links),
+ }
+ if t.Kind == model.TurnAgentResult {
+ tv.AgentHref = links.forAgent(t.AgentID)
+ }
+ ...
+ }
+ ```
+
+ Existing unit tests that call `buildViewModel(s, "t", Options{})` must be updated to `buildViewModel(s, "t", Options{}, pageInfo{}, newAgentLinks(nil, ""))` — update Task 4's tests accordingly in this task (they are in the same package; keep the assertions unchanged).
+
+ 5. Restructure `Site` to render index + one page per agent:
+
+ ```go
+ // Site renders the session into outDir as index.html + assets/, plus one page
+ // per linked agent session under subagents/.
+ func Site(s model.Session, outDir string, opts Options) error {
+ title := opts.Title
+ if title == "" {
+ title = s.DisplayTitle()
+ }
+ tmplSrc, err := assets.ReadFile("assets/report.html.tmpl")
+ if err != nil {
+ return err
+ }
+ tmpl, err := template.New("report").Parse(string(tmplSrc))
+ if err != nil {
+ return err
+ }
+
+ if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(outDir, "assets"), 0o755); err != nil {
+ return err
+ }
+ for _, name := range []string{"styles.css", "app.js"} {
+ b, err := assets.ReadFile("assets/" + name)
+ if err != nil {
+ return err
+ }
+ if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(outDir, "assets", name), b, 0o644); err != nil {
+ return err
+ }
+ }
+
+ writePage := func(path string, sess model.Session, pageTitle string, page pageInfo) error {
+ links := newAgentLinks(s.Agents, page.PagePrefix)
+ data := buildViewModel(sess, pageTitle, opts, page, links)
+ var buf bytes.Buffer
+ if err := tmpl.Execute(&buf, data); err != nil {
+ return err
+ }
+ return os.WriteFile(path, buf.Bytes(), 0o644)
+ }
+
+ if err := writePage(filepath.Join(outDir, "index.html"), s, title,
+ pageInfo{Base: "", PagePrefix: "subagents/"}); err != nil {
+ return err
+ }
+ if len(s.Agents) == 0 {
+ return nil
+ }
+ subDir := filepath.Join(outDir, "subagents")
+ if err := os.MkdirAll(subDir, 0o755); err != nil {
+ return err
+ }
+ for _, a := range s.Agents {
+ sub := "agent-" + a.ID
+ if a.AgentType != "" {
+ sub = a.AgentType + " · " + sub
+ }
+ if err := writePage(filepath.Join(subDir, "agent-"+a.ID+".html"), a.Session, a.Description,
+ pageInfo{Base: "../", PagePrefix: "", BackHref: "../index.html", Subtitle: sub}); err != nil {
+ return err
+ }
+ }
+ return nil
+ }
+ ```
+
+ Note the resolver on agent pages uses the ROOT session's agent set (`s.Agents`) with prefix `""`, so depth-2 agent references inside an agent page resolve to sibling files.
+
+ 6. In `assets/report.html.tmpl`:
+
+ - Asset paths: `href="{{ .Base }}assets/styles.css"` and `src="{{ .Base }}assets/app.js"`.
+ - In `session-head`, after the `<h1>`:
+
+ ```html
+ {{ if .BackHref }}<a class="back-link" href="{{ .BackHref }}">← main session</a>{{ end }}
+ ```
+
+ and in the `.meta` div add `{{ if .Subtitle }}<span>{{ .Subtitle }}</span>{{ end }}`.
+ - Turn header: add the transcript link after the status chip:
+
+ ```html
+ {{ if .AgentHref }}<a class="agent-link" href="{{ .AgentHref }}">transcript ↗</a>{{ end }}
+ ```
+
+ 7. In `assets/styles.css`, append:
+
+ ```css
+ .agent-link { margin-left: 0.5rem; font-size: 0.75rem; text-transform: none; letter-spacing: normal; }
+ .back-link { display: inline-block; font-size: 0.85rem; margin-bottom: 0.4rem; }
+ ```
+
+ - [ ] **Step 4: Run the render tests**
+
+ Run: `go test ./internal/render/ -v`
+ Expected: all PASS (including Task 4's updated buildViewModel call sites).
+
+ - [ ] **Step 5: Wire loading into the CLI**
+
+ In `cmd/ccwhid/run.go`, in `generate`, after the `ParseFile` block (before redaction):
+
+ ```go
+ if opts.includeSubagents {
+ agents, err := transcript.LoadAgentSessions(si.FilePath, transcript.Options{
+ IncludeSubagents: opts.includeSubagents,
+ })
+ if err != nil {
+ return "", err
+ }
+ sess.Agents = agents
+ }
+ ```
+
+ In `cmd/ccwhid/main.go`, update the flag help text:
+
+ ```go
+ f.BoolVar(&opts.includeSubagents, "include-subagents", true, "include subagent work: inline Task sidechains and linked agent-session pages")
+ ```
+
+ In `internal/tui/tui.go`, change the option label `"Include subagents"` to `"Include subagent work"`.
+
+ - [ ] **Step 6: Update the README**
+
+ In `README.md`:
+ - Flag table row for `--include-subagents`: "Include subagent work: inline Task sidechains and linked agent-session transcript pages under `subagents/` (default true)".
+ - In the report-description paragraph, mention: background-agent results appear as their own "Agent" cards; linked subagent sessions (from `<sessionId>/subagents/`) render as separate pages linked from the Agent tool card and the result card; with `--usage`, their cost is included and shown as an "of which subagents" line.
+ - Known limitation note: SDK-spawned sessions without parent linkage (e.g. review-hook runs) are not attributed or included.
+
+ - [ ] **Step 7: Full verification**
+
+ Run: `gofmt -l . && go vet ./... && go test ./...`
+ Expected: gofmt silent, vet clean, all tests pass.
+
+ Manual smoke (optional but recommended): `go run ./cmd/ccwhid --latest --usage --force -o /tmp/ccwhid-smoke && ls /tmp/ccwhid-smoke/subagents | head` on a session known to have a `subagents/` dir.
+
+ - [ ] **Step 8: Commit**
+
+ ```bash
+ git add internal/render/ cmd/ccwhid/ internal/tui/tui.go README.md
+ git commit -m "feat: subagent transcript pages linked from the report"
+ ```
+

Plan complete and saved to docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-12-subagent-sessions.md (608d700). Five tasks:

  1. Parse <task-notification> → agent-result turns (the unconditional "You"-card fix)
  2. LoadAgentSessions — load subagents/agent-*.jsonl + meta.json, AgentFile parse option, redaction walk
  3. Usage rollup — merge agent-session tokens, track Subagents/SubagentsCost/AgentSessions
  4. Render agent-result cards + "of which subagents" line + footnote variants
  5. Subagent pages — one HTML per agent under subagents/, links from tool & result cards, CLI/TUI/README wiring

Two execution options:

1. Subagent-Driven (recommended) — I dispatch a fresh subagent per task, review between tasks, fast iteration

2. Inline Execution — execute tasks in this session using executing-plans, batch execution with checkpoints

Which approach?

# ccwhid â SDD Progress Ledger

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-11-ccwhid.md
Branch: feat/ccwhid-impl
Base commit (branch start): 2ff127ac3e943c5fc1ccd4c4ad5247ce7dbcf3ac

## Task status
Task 1: complete (commits 2ff127a..37ba8bc, review clean; Minor go.mod indirect fixed via go mod tidy)
Task 2: complete (commit 7b11575, review clean â spec â, no issues)
Task 3: complete (commits 9def244, 159ee65 â includes controller-directed correctness fix: transcript cwd is authoritative for ProjectPath, decode is lossy fallback. Spec â, approved)
Task 4: complete (commit 5980a02, review clean â spec â. Minor: null content â one empty text block, but Task 5 drops empty text blocks so harmless)
Task 5: complete (commits 09d5d88, 41ed5b8 â includes CRITICAL fix: tool_use ids now registered in buildTurn so subagent-internal tool_results attach. Parallel-Task attribution documented as known v1 limitation. Spec â, approved)
Task 6: complete (commit faa3097, review clean â spec â. Deep recursion + no-leak verified by reviewer)
Task 7: complete (commits a713dfe, 6d415b6 â includes Important fix: rune-safe truncation in render.preview + discovery.truncate (byte-slicing corrupted non-ASCII); markdown comment corrected. Spec â, approved. Note: fix also touched discovery.go (Task 3) â same bug class, test-covered)
Task 8: complete (commit fd591f4, review clean â spec â. All 4 named risks verified by reviewer. Minors noted in roll-up)
Task 9: complete (commits 777b116, 19a5d33 â TUI + RunE wiring, go mod tidy for bubbletea/lipgloss. Spec â, all 5 named risks clean. redact mapping verified non-inverted)
Task 10: complete (commit 6678bb5 â e2e pipeline test, README reconciled (no drift). Spec â, no issues)

ALL 10 TASKS COMPLETE.
Final whole-branch review (opus): 1 Important (redaction header gap) + minors. Consolidated fix commit b2f6fc7 addressed ALL of them:
  - redact session-level ProjectPath/Title/GitBranch + Subagent.Description
  - wired chroma highlighting into goldmark (user chose "deliver spec"), removed dead Highlight()
  - SkippedLines warning; over-long-line tolerance; removed dead errNeedTUI/model.done; fixed stale comment
Re-review (sonnet): all 6 fixes verified correct, no issues. Final verify: build/vet/test/gofmt clean; real --latest smoke render OK (index.html + assets, redaction active).
Roll-up minors NOT fixed (acceptable v1, for backlog): redact assignment-regex over-match; run.go home err ignored; thin run_test.go coverage; parallel-Task subagent attribution limitation (documented).

## Post-review enhancement (commit 0f130bc, pushed)
User feedback during finish flow: TUI first screen unusable with many projects/sessions (no scroll), listed agent sessions, and --project didn't work.
Delivered:
- Two-level TUI: scrollable project list â scrollable session list; WindowSizeMsg-sized viewports; â/esc back; enter/â forward.
- Agent-spawn detection in discovery (entrypoint "sdk*" / promptSource "sdk" â SessionInfo.IsAgent). Session list hides agents by default; 'a' toggles (â² marker). Added ProjectGroup.RootSessions()/AgentCount().
- --project fixed: discovery.FindProject (exact path / basename / unambiguous substring, with ambiguous+no-match errors). Alone â opens TUI focused on that project (tui.Run gained focusIdx). --latest now skips agents (both raw and project-scoped). Removed old matchProject (Contains) from run.go.
- Tests: tui_test rewritten (agent-hide, toggle, back-nav, select, scroll window, focus, clampScroll); discovery_test +TestScanClassifiesAgentSessions +TestFindProject. All 7 packages green; vet/gofmt clean; real --latest --project smoke picked the cli root session (not an agent).
Reviewed by independent subagent (general-purpose): 1 Important + 2 Minor.
- Important FIXED (commit 0c147ee): latestSession returned first non-agent session in GROUP order, but groups sort by newest-including-agents â --latest could render an older interactive session when a recent agent run masked it. Now selects max-ModTime non-agent session across (optionally project-scoped) groups. Added latestSession tests (global-newest w/ interfering agent, project-scoped skip, all-agents error) â the seam was previously untested.
- Minor (accepted): entrypoint read only from user/assistant records â verified against real transcripts that entrypoint IS present on user records for both cli and sdk sessions; acceptable schema assumption.
- Minor: reviewer confirmed clampScroll/empty-state/classification/FindProject all correct.
Re-verify: all 7 packages green, vet/gofmt clean.
Next: finish branch.

## Minor findings roll-up
- [Task 3] No committed regression test for malformed-line tolerance and FindSession ambiguous-vs-not-found (both verified correct by reviewer; malformed-line tolerance is a global constraint â worth a permanent test). discovery_test.go
- [Task 3] cwd capture only inspects user/assistant records (consistent with model.TurnKind; fine unless a later task needs cwd from other record types). discovery.go
- [Task 5] lastTask/sidechainOwner kept in lockstep â cosmetic redundancy from the brief, no behavioral effect. parse.go
- [Task 6] assignment regex over-matches: any uppercase word containing KEY/TOKEN/etc (e.g. MONKEY=) and matches across newlines. Safe over-redaction (never leaks), inherited from brief. redact.go:108. Consider word-boundary + [^\n] tightening.
- [Task 8] run.go:102 home err ignored (home-path rewrite silently skipped on failure; UserHomeDir ~never fails). Minor.
- [Task 8] matchProject strings.Contains fallback is broad (--project app matches /x/apparatus). run.go:80-84. Consider tightening when --project UX matters.
- [Task 8] run_test.go covers only 2 happy-paths; ensureOutDir 3-branch + resolveSession needTUI/--latest/--project branches lack committed tests (redaction-default is covered by Task 10 e2e). Consider adding.
- [Task 9] dead code for whole-branch cleanup: model.done field in tui.go never read; errNeedTUI var in run.go now unreferenced (Task 8 left it when resolveSession returned needTUI bool instead). Batch-remove after final review.

# ============================================================
## Usage-cost task status
Task 1: complete (commit 1753ede, review clean â spec ✅, no issues. Minor noted: messageMeta re-parses rec.Message (plan-mandated micro double-parse, non-functional))
Task 2: complete (commit 08d3928, review clean - spec OK, no issues)
Task 3: complete (commit 4140bfb, review clean - spec OK, math + recursion hand-verified)
Task 4: complete (commit 4de9666, review clean - spec OK. Deviation: Headline/Tokens typed template.HTML (html/template escapes + to &#43;); reviewer verified XSS-safe (Model stays plain auto-escaped string). Minors to roll-up)
Task 5: complete (commit 6fef39b, review clean - spec OK. TUI index reconciled via named consts; existing tests down-counts bumped 3->4)
## Usage-cost roll-up minors (for final review triage)
- [Task 4] render.go no-data headline literal skips html.EscapeString wrapper the rest uses (safe now-fixed literal; future-proofing). render.go:~159
- [Task 4] template.HTML fix heavier than a plain-string label without "+"; defensible given brief's "in+out" test assertion.
- [Task 4] no render test for Options.Usage=true + HasAnyUsage=false (no-data) path; pre-existing brief gap.
- [Task 1] messageMeta re-parses rec.Message (plan-mandated micro double-parse; non-functional).
ALL 5 USAGE-COST TASKS COMPLETE.
Final whole-branch review (opus): 1 Important (usage card undefined --card CSS var -> unreadable in dark mode) + 2 minors. Fixed in follow-up commit: var(--surface)/--text/--muted, per-model header "in+out". Logic/escaping/subagent-totals/redaction all verified correct by reviewer. Re-verified: 8 packages green, vet/gofmt clean, real --usage render OK (859k in+out, ~$107 est.).
- [Task 5] main_test.go TestRootCmdHasExpectedFlags does not assert 'usage' flag (optional follow-up)

## Post-merge-review bug fix (commit 95dcf2d)
User compared report vs Claude Code /usage: costs ~fabulated (report $109.66 vs /usage opus $74.25).
Root cause (evidence from raw transcript): Claude Code writes ONE assistant message as MULTIPLE records (one per content block: thinking/text/tool_use), each repeating the IDENTICAL usage object. ccwhid counted usage per-record -> 2-3x inflation; and dropping block-less records made it an inconsistent subset (hence "fabulated").
Fix: dedupe usage by message id in parser (decodeMessageMeta now returns msg id; buildTurn takes seenUsage map; first record of a message id keeps usage, rest -> nil). Regression test TestParseDeduplicatesUsagePerMessageID.
Validated vs /usage: after fix report opus = 55k in / 417k out / 89.8M cache read / 1.6M cache write / $71.50, vs /usage opus 92.8k/437.3k/88.2M/2.1M/$74.25 — close; remaining gap is Task sub-agent SESSIONS (separate transcript files) which /usage's session rollup includes but ccwhid (single transcript) does not. README note added on this scope.
All 8 packages green, vet/gofmt clean.

1	# Implementer Subagent Prompt Template
2	
3	Use this template when dispatching an implementer subagent.
4	
5	```
6	Subagent (general-purpose):
7	  description: "Implement Task N: [task name]"
8	  model: [MODEL — REQUIRED: choose per SKILL.md Model Selection; an omitted
9	         model silently inherits the session's most expensive one]
10	  prompt: |
11	    You are implementing Task N: [task name]
12	
13	    ## Task Description
14	
15	    Read your task brief first: [BRIEF_FILE]
16	    It contains the full task text from the plan.
17	
18	    ## Context
19	
20	    [Scene-setting: where this fits, dependencies, architectural context]
21	
22	    ## Before You Begin
23	
24	    If you have questions about:
25	    - The requirements or acceptance criteria
26	    - The approach or implementation strategy
27	    - Dependencies or assumptions
28	    - Anything unclear in the task description
29	
30	    **Ask them now.** Raise any concerns before starting work.
31	
32	    ## Your Job
33	
34	    Once you're clear on requirements:
35	    1. Implement exactly what the task specifies
36	    2. Write tests (following TDD if task says to)
37	    3. Verify implementation works
38	    4. Commit your work
39	    5. Self-review (see below)
40	    6. Report back
41	
42	    Work from: [directory]
43	
44	    **While you work:** If you encounter something unexpected or unclear, **ask questions**.
45	    It's always OK to pause and clarify. Don't guess or make assumptions.
46	
47	    While iterating, run the focused test for what you're changing; run the
48	    full suite once before committing, not after every edit.
49	
50	    ## Code Organization
51	
52	    You reason best about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more
53	    reliable when files are focused. Keep this in mind:
54	    - Follow the file structure defined in the plan
55	    - Each file should have one clear responsibility with a well-defined interface
56	    - If a file you're creating is growing beyond the plan's intent, stop and report
57	      it as DONE_WITH_CONCERNS — don't split files on your own without plan guidance
58	    - If an existing file you're modifying is already large or tangled, work carefully
59	      and note it as a concern in your report
60	    - In existing codebases, follow established patterns. Improve code you're touching
61	      the way a good developer would, but don't restructure things outside your task.
62	
63	    ## When You're in Over Your Head
64	
65	    It is always OK to stop and say "this is too hard for me." Bad work is worse than
66	    no work. You will not be penalized for escalating.
67	
68	    **STOP and escalate when:**
69	    - The task requires architectural decisions with multiple valid approaches
70	    - You need to understand code beyond what was provided and can't find clarity
71	    - You feel uncertain about whether your approach is correct
72	    - The task involves restructuring existing code in ways the plan didn't anticipate
73	    - You've been reading file after file trying to understand the system without progress
74	
75	    **How to escalate:** Report back with status BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT. Describe
76	    specifically what you're stuck on, what you've tried, and what kind of help you need.
77	    The controller can provide more context, re-dispatch with a more capable model,
78	    or break the task into smaller pieces.
79	
80	    ## Before Reporting Back: Self-Review
81	
82	    Review your work with fresh eyes. Ask yourself:
83	
84	    **Completeness:**
85	    - Did I fully implement everything in the spec?
86	    - Did I miss any requirements?
87	    - Are there edge cases I didn't handle?
88	
89	    **Quality:**
90	    - Is this my best work?
91	    - Are names clear and accurate (match what things do, not how they work)?
92	    - Is the code clean and maintainable?
93	
94	    **Discipline:**
95	    - Did I avoid overbuilding (YAGNI)?
96	    - Did I only build what was requested?
97	    - Did I follow existing patterns in the codebase?
98	
99	    **Testing:**
100	    - Do tests actually verify behavior (not just mock behavior)?
101	    - Did I follow TDD if required?
102	    - Are tests comprehensive?
103	    - Is the test output pristine (no stray warnings or noise)?
104	
105	    If you find issues during self-review, fix them now before reporting.
106	
107	    ## After Review Findings
108	
109	    If a reviewer finds issues and you fix them, re-run the tests that cover
110	    the amended code and append the results to your report file. Reviewers
111	    will not re-run tests for you — your report is the test evidence.
112	
113	    ## Report Format
114	
115	    Write your full report to [REPORT_FILE]:
116	    - What you implemented (or what you attempted, if blocked)
117	    - What you tested and test results
118	    - **TDD Evidence** (if TDD was required for this task):
119	      - RED: command run, relevant failing output before implementation, and why the failure was expected
120	      - GREEN: command run and relevant passing output after implementation
121	    - Files changed
122	    - Self-review findings (if any)
123	    - Any issues or concerns
124	
125	    Then report back with ONLY (under 15 lines — the detail lives in the
126	    report file):
127	    - **Status:** DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
128	    - Commits created (short SHA + subject)
129	    - One-line test summary (e.g. "14/14 passing, output pristine")
130	    - Your concerns, if any
131	    - The report file path
132	
133	    If BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT, put the specifics in the final message
134	    itself — the controller acts on it directly.
135	
136	    Use DONE_WITH_CONCERNS if you completed the work but have doubts about correctness.
137	    Use BLOCKED if you cannot complete the task. Use NEEDS_CONTEXT if you need
138	    information that wasn't provided. Never silently produce work you're unsure about.
139	```

AgentImplement Task 1: notification parsingtranscript ↗

You are implementing Task 1: Parse <task-notification> records into agent-result turns, for the Go CLI ccwhid.

Task Description

Read your task brief first: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-1-brief.md It contains the full task text from the plan, including the exact test code and implementation code to use verbatim.

Context

ccwhid parses Claude Code JSONL transcripts into a model.Session and renders an HTML report. Today, a background agent's completion notification (a user record whose content is a <task-notification> string) renders as a user turn attributed to "You" — this task makes the parser surface it as a new turn kind agent-result instead. This is Task 1 of 5; later tasks build the loader and renderer on top of the model fields you add.

Work from: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done (branch feat/subagent-sessions, already checked out).

Global constraints that bind this task:

  • The fix is unconditional — no dependence on any Options flag.
  • A malformed notification must never fail the parse; it falls back to a plain user turn.
  • Run gofmt -l . (must print nothing) before committing.
  • The commit message must end with the trailer: Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

The brief offers a choice about two one-line wrapper helpers (trimLeftSpace/hasPrefix): prefer calling strings.TrimLeft/strings.HasPrefix directly and drop the wrappers.

Before You Begin

If you have questions about the requirements, approach, dependencies, or anything unclear in the task brief — ask them now.

Your Job

  1. Implement exactly what the task brief specifies (TDD: write the failing tests first, verify they fail, then implement)
  2. Verify the full suite passes: gofmt -l . && go test ./...
  3. Commit your work
  4. Self-review with fresh eyes (completeness, quality, YAGNI, test hygiene); fix anything you find before reporting
  5. Report back

While iterating, run the focused test for what you're changing; run the full suite once before committing, not after every edit.

If you're stuck or the code doesn't match what the brief assumes, STOP and report BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT with specifics. Never silently produce work you're unsure about.

After Review Findings

If a reviewer finds issues and you fix them, re-run the tests that cover the amended code and append the results to your report file.

Report Format

Write your full report to ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-1-report.md:

  • What you implemented, TDD evidence (RED command+output, GREEN command+output), files changed, self-review findings, concerns.

Then report back with ONLY (under 15 lines):

  • Status: DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
  • Commits created (short SHA + subject)
  • One-line test summary
  • Your concerns, if any
  • The report file path

Perfect! All checks passed. Here's my summary:

Status: DONE

Commit: b1bc70e feat: parse task-notification records as agent-result turns

Test Summary: 5 new tests all pass; 19 existing tests continue to pass; all 8 packages pass

Changes:

  • internal/model/model.go — Added TurnAgentResult constant; added AgentID, AgentStatus, AgentSummary fields to Turn
  • internal/transcript/parse.go — Added taskNotification struct, parseTaskNotification(), tagContent() helpers, and notification detection logic in buildTurn()
  • internal/transcript/notification_test.go — New file with 5 comprehensive tests (happy path, empty result fallback, malformed fallback, false positives, deduplication)
  • internal/redact/redact.go — Added AgentSummary redaction in redactTurn()

Test Coverage:

  • TestParseTaskNotificationTurn — Full notification parsing with result extraction
  • TestParseTaskNotificationEmptyResultFallsBackToSummary — Empty result → use summary as body
  • TestParseMalformedNotificationStaysUserTurn — Missing <task-id> → stays plain user turn
  • TestUserTextMentioningNotificationMidStringStaysUserTurn — Mid-string text doesn't trigger parsing
  • TestRepeatNotificationsEachProduceATurn — No deduplication across repeated notifications

Self-Review Findings: None. Implementation meets all requirements: ✅ Unconditional parsing (no Options flag) ✅ Graceful malformed fallback (returns false, stays user turn) ✅ Used strings.TrimLeft/strings.HasPrefix directly (no wrappers) ✅ All tests pass ✅ gofmt -l . prints nothing ✅ Commit has proper Co-Authored-By trailer

1	# Task Reviewer Prompt Template
2	
3	Use this template when dispatching a task reviewer subagent. The reviewer
4	reads the task's diff once and returns two verdicts: spec compliance and
5	code quality.
6	
7	**Purpose:** Verify one task's implementation matches its requirements (nothing
8	more, nothing less) and is well-built (clean, tested, maintainable)
9	
10	```
11	Subagent (general-purpose):
12	  description: "Review Task N (spec + quality)"
13	  model: [MODEL — REQUIRED: choose per SKILL.md Model Selection; an omitted
14	         model silently inherits the session's most expensive one]
15	  prompt: |
16	    You are reviewing one task's implementation: first whether it matches its
17	    requirements, then whether it is well-built. This is a task-scoped gate,
18	    not a merge review — a broad whole-branch review happens separately after
19	    all tasks are complete.
20	
21	    ## What Was Requested
22	
23	    Read the task brief: [BRIEF_FILE]
24	
25	    Global constraints from the spec/design that bind this task:
26	    [GLOBAL_CONSTRAINTS]
27	
28	    ## What the Implementer Claims They Built
29	
30	    Read the implementer's report: [REPORT_FILE]
31	
32	    ## Diff Under Review
33	
34	    **Base:** [BASE_SHA]
35	    **Head:** [HEAD_SHA]
36	    **Diff file:** [DIFF_FILE]
37	
38	    Read the diff file once — it contains the commit list, a stat summary,
39	    and the full diff with surrounding context, and it is your view of the
40	    change. The diff's context lines ARE the changed files: do not Read a
41	    changed file separately unless a hunk you must judge is cut off
42	    mid-function — and say so in your report. Do not re-run git commands.
43	    If the diff file is missing, fetch the diff yourself:
44	    `git diff --stat [BASE_SHA]..[HEAD_SHA]` and `git diff [BASE_SHA]..[HEAD_SHA]`.
45	    Do not crawl the broader codebase. Inspect code outside the diff only
46	    to evaluate a concrete risk you can name — one focused check per named
47	    risk, and name both the risk and what you checked in your report.
48	    Cross-cutting changes are legitimate named risks: if the diff changes
49	    lock ordering, a function or API contract, or shared mutable state,
50	    checking the call sites is the right method.
51	
52	    Your review is read-only on this checkout. Do not mutate the working
53	    tree, the index, HEAD, or branch state in any way.
54	
55	    ## Do Not Trust the Report
56	
57	    Treat the implementer's report as unverified claims about the code. It
58	    may be incomplete, inaccurate, or optimistic. Verify the claims against
59	    the diff. Design rationales in the report are claims too: "left it per
60	    YAGNI," "kept it simple deliberately," or any other justification is the
61	    implementer grading their own work. Judge the code on its merits — a
62	    stated rationale never downgrades a finding's severity.
63	
64	    ## Tests
65	
66	    The implementer already ran the tests and reported results with TDD
67	    evidence for exactly this code. Do not re-run the suite to confirm their
68	    report. Run a test only when reading the code raises a specific doubt
69	    that no existing run answers — and then a focused test, never a
70	    package-wide suite, race detector run, or repeated/high-count loop. If
71	    heavy validation seems warranted, recommend it in your report instead of
72	    running it. If you cannot run commands in this environment, name the
73	    test you would run.
74	
75	    Warnings or other noise in the implementer's reported test output are
76	    findings — test output should be pristine.
77	
78	    ## Part 1: Spec Compliance
79	
80	    Compare the diff against What Was Requested:
81	
82	    - **Missing:** requirements they skipped, missed, or claimed without
83	      implementing
84	    - **Extra:** features that weren't requested, over-engineering, unneeded
85	      "nice to haves"
86	    - **Misunderstood:** right feature built the wrong way, wrong problem
87	      solved
88	
89	    If a requirement cannot be verified from this diff alone (it lives in
90	    unchanged code or spans tasks), report it as a ⚠️ item instead of
91	    broadening your search.
92	
93	    ## Part 2: Code Quality
94	
95	    **Code quality:**
96	    - Clean separation of concerns?
97	    - Proper error handling?
98	    - DRY without premature abstraction?
99	    - Edge cases handled?
100	
101	    **Tests:**
102	    - Do the new and changed tests verify real behavior, not mocks?
103	    - Are the task's edge cases covered?
104	
105	    **Structure:**
106	    - Does each file have one clear responsibility with a well-defined interface?
107	    - Are units decomposed so they can be understood and tested independently?
108	    - Is the implementation following the file structure from the plan?
109	    - Did this change create new files that are already large, or
110	      significantly grow existing files? (Don't flag pre-existing file
111	      sizes — focus on what this change contributed.)
112	
113	    Your report should point at evidence: file:line references for every
114	    finding and for any check you would otherwise answer with a bare
115	    "yes." A tight report that cites lines gives the controller everything
116	    it needs.
117	
118	    Your final message is the report itself: begin directly with the
119	    spec-compliance verdict. Every line is a verdict, a finding with
120	    file:line, or a check you ran — no preamble, no process narration,
121	    no closing summary.
122	
123	    ## Calibration
124	
125	    Categorize issues by actual severity. Not everything is Critical.
126	    Important means this task cannot be trusted until it is fixed: incorrect
127	    or fragile behavior, a missed requirement, or maintainability damage you
128	    would block a merge over — verbatim duplication of a logic block,
129	    swallowed errors, tests that assert nothing. "Coverage could be broader"
130	    and polish suggestions are Minor.
131	    If the plan or brief explicitly mandates something this rubric calls a
132	    defect (a test that asserts nothing, verbatim duplication of a logic
133	    block), that IS a finding — report it as Important, labeled
134	    plan-mandated. The plan's authorship does not grade its own work; the
135	    human decides.
136	    Acknowledge what was done well before listing issues — accurate praise
137	    helps the implementer trust the rest of the feedback.
138	
139	    ## Output Format
140	
141	    ### Spec Compliance
142	
143	    - ✅ Spec compliant | ❌ Issues found: [what's missing/extra/misunderstood,
144	      with file:line references]
145	    - ⚠️ Cannot verify from diff: [requirements you could not verify from the
146	      diff alone, and what the controller should check — report alongside the
147	      ✅/❌ verdict for everything you could verify]
148	
149	    ### Strengths
150	    [What's well done? Be specific.]
151	
152	    ### Issues
153	
154	    #### Critical (Must Fix)
155	    #### Important (Should Fix)
156	    #### Minor (Nice to Have)
157	
158	    For each issue: file:line, what's wrong, why it matters, how to fix
159	    (if not obvious).
160	
161	    ### Assessment
162	
163	    **Task quality:** [Approved | Needs fixes]
164	
165	    **Reasoning:** [1-2 sentence technical assessment]
166	```
167	
168	**Placeholders:**
169	- `[MODEL]` — REQUIRED: reviewer model per SKILL.md Model Selection
170	- `[BRIEF_FILE]` — REQUIRED: the task brief file (`scripts/task-brief PLAN N`
171	  prints the path; same file the implementer worked from)
172	- `[GLOBAL_CONSTRAINTS]` — the binding requirements copied verbatim from
173	  the plan's Global Constraints section or the spec: exact values, formats,
174	  and stated relationships between components (not process rules — those
175	  are already in this template)
176	- `[REPORT_FILE]` — REQUIRED: the file the implementer wrote its detailed
177	  report to
178	- `[BASE_SHA]` — commit before this task
179	- `[HEAD_SHA]` — current commit
180	- `[DIFF_FILE]` — REQUIRED: the path the controller wrote the review
181	  package to (`scripts/review-package BASE HEAD` prints the unique path it
182	  wrote; the package never enters the controller's context)
183	
184	**Reviewer returns:** Spec Compliance verdict (✅/❌/⚠️), Strengths, Issues
185	(Critical/Important/Minor), Task quality verdict
186	
187	A fix dispatch can address spec gaps and quality findings together;
188	re-review after fixes covers both verdicts.
189

AgentReview Task 1 (spec + quality)transcript ↗

You are reviewing one task's implementation: first whether it matches its requirements, then whether it is well-built. This is a task-scoped gate, not a merge review — a broad whole-branch review happens separately after all tasks are complete.

What Was Requested

Read the task brief: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-1-brief.md

Global constraints from the spec/design that bind this task:

  • The task-notification fix is unconditional — independent of --include-subagents and --usage.
  • A malformed notification must never fail the run; it falls back to a plain user turn, content untouched.
  • The parsed body must be the <result> payload only (summary as fallback when result is empty), never the raw envelope.
  • The brief allows dropping the two one-line wrappers (trimLeftSpace/hasPrefix) in favor of direct strings calls; the controller instructed the implementer to drop them.
  • gofmt -l . must print nothing.

What the Implementer Claims They Built

Read the implementer's report: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-1-report.md

Diff Under Review

Base: 608d700 Head: b1bc70e Diff file: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/review-608d700..b1bc70e.diff

Read the diff file once — it contains the commit list, a stat summary, and the full diff with surrounding context, and it is your view of the change. The diff's context lines ARE the changed files: do not Read a changed file separately unless a hunk you must judge is cut off mid-function — and say so in your report. Do not re-run git commands. Do not crawl the broader codebase. Inspect code outside the diff only to evaluate a concrete risk you can name — one focused check per named risk, and name both the risk and what you checked in your report.

Your review is read-only on this checkout. Do not mutate the working tree, the index, HEAD, or branch state in any way.

Do Not Trust the Report

Treat the implementer's report as unverified claims about the code. Verify the claims against the diff. Design rationales in the report are claims too. Judge the code on its merits.

Tests

The implementer already ran the tests and reported results with TDD evidence for exactly this code. Do not re-run the suite to confirm their report. Run a test only when reading the code raises a specific doubt that no existing run answers — and then a focused test, never a package-wide suite. Warnings or other noise in the reported test output are findings — test output should be pristine.

Part 1: Spec Compliance

Compare the diff against What Was Requested: Missing / Extra / Misunderstood. If a requirement cannot be verified from this diff alone, report it as a ⚠️ item.

Part 2: Code Quality

Code quality (separation of concerns, error handling, DRY, edge cases), tests (verify real behavior, edge cases covered), structure (file responsibilities, plan's file structure followed).

Point at evidence: file:line references for every finding. Your final message is the report itself: begin directly with the spec-compliance verdict; no preamble, no process narration.

Calibration

Categorize by actual severity. Important = task cannot be trusted until fixed. "Coverage could be broader" and polish are Minor. If the plan/brief explicitly mandates something this rubric calls a defect, report it as Important, labeled plan-mandated. Acknowledge what was done well before listing issues.

Output Format

Spec Compliance

  • ✅ / ❌ / ⚠️ items

Strengths

Issues

Critical (Must Fix)

Important (Should Fix)

Minor (Nice to Have)

Assessment

Task quality: [Approved | Needs fixes] Reasoning: [1-2 sentences]

This matches the brief exactly and follows the existing pattern. Everything checks out. Implementation is small, focused, and well-tested.

Spec Compliance

  • ✅ Unconditional fix — verified by reading the full Parse() loop (parse.go:80-155, not just the diff context): main-chain (!rec.IsSidechain) records call buildTurn unconditionally at line 130, with no opts.IncludeSubagents or usage-flag gating anywhere near the notification-detection branch. Task-notifications arrive as ordinary parent-session user records, so this always fires.
  • ✅ Malformed notification falls back to plain user turn, content untouched — parseTaskNotification requires non-empty TaskID and Summary; on failure buildTurn falls through to the pre-existing block loop, which processes the original blocks slice verbatim. TestParseMalformedNotificationStaysUserTurn and the mid-string-mention test both confirm this.
  • ✅ Parsed body is <result> payload only, summary as fallback — parse.go:311-316 sets body := n.Result; if body == "" { body = n.Summary }, and TestParseTaskNotificationTurn explicitly asserts the raw envelope (<task-id>) is absent from the body block.
  • ✅ Wrappers dropped per brief — implementer used strings.TrimLeft/strings.HasPrefix directly (parse.go:259-260), matching the controller's instruction to drop trimLeftSpace/hasPrefix.
  • ✅ gofmt -l . clean — re-ran it myself (read-only, non-mutating): no output.
  • ✅ Redaction — redactTurn adds t.AgentSummary = r.String(t.AgentSummary) (redact.go:62), placed with the other per-turn string fields as instructed; the result/summary body text still flows through the existing b.Text = r.String(b.Text) loop, so it's covered twice via different fields as intended.
  • ✅ Model/interface shape matches brief — TurnAgentResult, Turn.AgentID/AgentStatus/AgentSummary, and taskNotification{TaskID, ToolUseID, Status, Summary, Result} all present exactly as specified (model.go:32,55-57; parse.go:193-199).

Strengths

  • Test suite (5 cases) directly exercises every global constraint called out in the task: unconditional-ness (implicitly, no options passed), malformed fallback, result-only body, empty-result fallback, and no-dedup on repeats — this is exactly the right shape of TDD evidence for this task.
  • tagContent's use of LastIndex for the closing tag is a deliberate, documented tradeoff to avoid truncating <result> bodies that contain markdown/XML-looking text (parse.go:203-204, 209-210) — a real edge case in this data (agent summaries routinely contain code fences or angle brackets) handled thoughtfully rather than papered over.
  • Early-return in buildTurn on notification match (parse.go:317) cleanly avoids falling into the generic block-processing loop, keeping the two code paths from interacting.

Issues

Critical (Must Fix)

None.

Important (Should Fix)

None.

Minor (Nice to Have)

  • tagContent's LastIndex-based closing-tag search means a <result> body that itself contains a literal </result> substring (e.g., in a code block pasted verbatim by the agent) would over-capture up to the last occurrence, potentially swallowing the </task-notification> wrapper into the visible body. This is an inherent heuristic tradeoff of tolerant string-scanning (not real XML parsing) and is explicitly called out in the code comment, so it's a known, accepted limitation rather than an oversight — flagging only for awareness in case downstream tasks hit it with real transcripts.
  • taskNotification.ToolUseID is parsed but never consumed in this task (reserved for Task 5's linking per the brief) — correct per the interface contract, just noting it's currently dead weight until that task lands.

Assessment

Task quality: Approved Reasoning: The implementation matches the brief's interface and behavior contracts precisely, including the subtle "unconditional regardless of flags" requirement (verified by reading the full Parse loop, not just the diff), the result-vs-summary fallback, and safe degradation on malformed input. Tests are well-targeted at the stated global constraints, gofmt is clean, and redaction is wired correctly. agentId: a72c1702495c21e82 (use SendMessage with to: 'a72c1702495c21e82', summary: '<5-10 word recap>' to continue this agent) subagent_tokens: 49191 tool_uses: 7 duration_ms: 77192

AgentImplement Task 2: agent-session loadertranscript ↗

You are implementing Task 2: Load linked agent-session files, for the Go CLI ccwhid.

Task Description

Read your task brief first: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-2-brief.md It contains the full task text from the plan, including the exact test code and implementation code to use verbatim.

Context

ccwhid parses Claude Code JSONL transcripts. Modern Claude Code stores each subagent's transcript as <projectDir>/<sessionId>/subagents/agent-<ID>.jsonl with a sibling agent-<ID>.meta.json. This task adds the model.AgentSession type, a transcript.Options.AgentFile flag, and transcript.LoadAgentSessions to load those files. This is Task 2 of 5; Task 1 (already merged on this branch) added model.TurnAgentResult and notification parsing — do not touch that code. Later tasks consume model.Session.Agents.

Work from: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done (branch feat/subagent-sessions, already checked out).

Global constraints that bind this task:

  • A missing subagents/ dir must yield (nil, nil); an unparsable agent file is skipped with a warning on stderr; a missing/unreadable meta.json falls back to Description = ID. None of these may fail the run.
  • gofmt -l . must print nothing before committing.
  • The commit message must end with the trailer: Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Before You Begin

If you have questions about the requirements, approach, dependencies, or anything unclear in the task brief — ask them now.

Your Job

  1. Implement exactly what the task brief specifies (TDD: write the failing tests first, verify they fail, then implement)
  2. Verify the full suite passes: gofmt -l . && go test ./...
  3. Commit your work
  4. Self-review with fresh eyes (completeness, quality, YAGNI, test hygiene); fix anything you find before reporting
  5. Report back

While iterating, run the focused test for what you're changing; run the full suite once before committing, not after every edit.

If you're stuck or the code doesn't match what the brief assumes, STOP and report BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT with specifics. Never silently produce work you're unsure about.

After Review Findings

If a reviewer finds issues and you fix them, re-run the tests that cover the amended code and append the results to your report file.

Report Format

Write your full report to ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-2-report.md:

  • What you implemented, TDD evidence (RED command+output, GREEN command+output), files changed, self-review findings, concerns.

Then report back with ONLY (under 15 lines):

  • Status: DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
  • Commits created (short SHA + subject)
  • One-line test summary
  • Your concerns, if any
  • The report file path

Perfect! Task 2 is complete. Here's the summary:

Status: DONE

Commits Created

  • 10aff40 — feat: load linked subagent session transcripts

Test Summary

All 8 packages pass; 3 new agent-related tests added and passing:

  • TestLoadAgentSessions — Multi-agent discovery, sorting, meta.json parsing
  • TestLoadAgentSessionsMissingDir — Graceful (nil, nil) when dir missing
  • TestAgentFileOptionParsesSidechainAsMainChain — AgentFile option override

Implementation

  1. model.go: Added AgentSession type + Agents field to Session
  2. parse.go: Added AgentFile option + sidechain override logic
  3. agents.go (new): Implemented LoadAgentSessions with metadata loading, fallback Description, error handling
  4. agents_test.go (new): Full test suite from brief
  5. redact.go: Added recursive agent session redaction

Verification

  • ✓ gofmt -l . — no formatting issues
  • ✓ go test ./... — all 8 packages pass

You are reviewing one task's implementation: first whether it matches its requirements, then whether it is well-built. This is a task-scoped gate, not a merge review — a broad whole-branch review happens separately after all tasks are complete.

What Was Requested

Read the task brief: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-2-brief.md

Global constraints from the spec/design that bind this task:

  • A missing subagents/ dir yields (nil, nil); an unparsable agent file is skipped with a warning on stderr; missing/unreadable meta.json falls back to Description = ID. None of these may fail the run.
  • LoadAgentSessions reads <transcriptPath minus .jsonl>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl, sorted by file name.
  • Options.AgentFile makes records with isSidechain: true parse as the file's main chain; without it, behavior is unchanged.
  • Agent-session content must be redacted like the root session (Description + full nested Session walk).
  • gofmt -l . must print nothing.

Strengths

Issues

Critical (Must Fix)

Important (Should Fix)

Minor (Nice to Have)

Assessment

Task quality: [Approved | Needs fixes] Reasoning: [1-2 sentences]

Spec Compliance

  • ✅ model.AgentSession{ID, Description, AgentType, ToolUseID, SpawnDepth, Session} and Session.Agents []AgentSession — added verbatim (model.go:55-64, model.go:32).
  • ✅ Options.AgentFile added; sidechain override placed exactly where the brief specifies, right after the rec.IsMeta check (parse.go:95-100).
  • ✅ LoadAgentSessions(transcriptPath, opts) ([]model.AgentSession, error) reads <transcriptPath minus .jsonl>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl, sorted by name (os.ReadDir sorts by filename) (agents.go:125-134, 136-138).
  • ✅ Missing subagents/ dir → (nil, nil) via os.IsNotExist check (agents.go:128-131); verified by TestLoadAgentSessionsMissingDir.
  • ✅ Missing/unreadable meta.json → Description falls back to ID (agents.go:147, 220); verified by the bbb222 case in TestLoadAgentSessions.
  • ✅ Redaction walks s.Agents: Description + full nested Session (redact.go:80-83), matching the brief's Step 6 exactly.
  • ✅ gofmt -l . on the changed files prints nothing — re-verified directly (read-only) rather than trusting the report.
  • ⚠️ "Unparsable agent file is skipped with a warning on stderr" (agents.go:214-218): the code path matches the brief's dictated implementation, but given Parse's actual error semantics (parse.go:80-83: malformed JSON lines are counted via SkippedLines and never cause a hard error), this branch is effectively unreachable for genuinely malformed transcript content — it only fires on I/O-level failures (e.g. permission denied, file removed after ReadDir listing). No test in this diff exercises it; agent-broken.jsonl is empty, which parses successfully with zero turns rather than erroring, so the "skip + warn" behavior claimed in both the brief and the report is never actually observed running. This is inherited from the brief's own Step-5 code, not an implementer deviation, so I'm flagging it rather than failing it.

Strengths

  • Implementation is a faithful, verbatim match of the brief's specified code across all five touched/created files — no unrequested scope creep.
  • Redaction correctly recurses through nested agent sessions rather than only redacting the top-level Description.
  • Tests meaningfully cover the two most important edge cases from the global constraints: missing subagents/ dir, and meta.json-fallback-to-ID, plus both directions of the AgentFile sidechain override (with vs. without).
  • agents.go correctly filters .meta.json siblings out of the agent-*.jsonl glob (suffix check on .jsonl doesn't match .meta.json), avoiding an easy off-by-pattern bug.
  • gofmt-clean, matches report's claim.

Issues

Critical (Must Fix)

None.

Important (Should Fix)

None.

Minor (Nice to Have)

  1. internal/transcript/agents_test.go — the "unparsable agent file → skip + stderr warning" behavior (agents.go:215-218) has no test coverage; the agent-broken.jsonl fixture is empty content, which parses successfully rather than erroring, so it doesn't exercise the warning branch at all. A fixture that actually causes ParseFile/os.Open to fail (or a doc note acknowledging the branch is I/O-only) would close the gap.
  2. internal/transcript/agents_test.go — the meta.json-present-but-malformed-JSON path (json.Unmarshal failure, agents.go:222) and the os.ReadDir non-NotExist error path (agents.go:131-132) are also untested; both are handled correctly in code but unverified by any test.

Assessment

Task quality: Approved Reasoning: All global constraints and interfaces from the brief are implemented correctly and match the brief's dictated code exactly; the only findings are minor test-coverage gaps around error branches that are largely inherited from the brief's own design rather than implementer shortcuts, and none of them threaten the correctness of the paths that are exercised. agentId: a001edb26aa56558d (use SendMessage with to: 'a001edb26aa56558d', summary: '<5-10 word recap>' to continue this agent) subagent_tokens: 55510 tool_uses: 5 duration_ms: 113045

AgentImplement Task 3: usage rolluptranscript ↗

You are implementing Task 3: Usage rollup for agent sessions, for the Go CLI ccwhid.

Task Description

Read your task brief first: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-3-brief.md It contains the full task text from the plan, including the exact test code and implementation code to use verbatim.

Context

ccwhid computes token usage & estimated cost per session (internal/usage). Task 2 (already on this branch) added model.Session.Agents []model.AgentSession — linked subagent session transcripts, each with a nested Session. This task makes usage.Compute merge those agent sessions into the totals and per-model rows while tracking a separate subagent subtotal. This is Task 3 of 5; Task 4 will render the new Report fields.

Work from: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done (branch feat/subagent-sessions, already checked out).

Global constraints that bind this task:

  • With no Agents, behavior is unchanged: Subagents stays zero, SubagentsCost nil, AgentSessions 0 — existing tests must keep passing untouched.
  • Agent turns must NOT create per-turn cost entries (PerTurnCost stays main-session-only).
  • gofmt -l . must print nothing before committing.
  • The commit message must end with the trailer: Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Before You Begin

If you have questions about the requirements, approach, dependencies, or anything unclear in the task brief — ask them now.

Your Job

  1. Implement exactly what the task brief specifies (TDD: write the failing tests first, verify they fail, then implement)
  2. Verify the full suite passes: gofmt -l . && go test ./...
  3. Commit your work
  4. Self-review with fresh eyes (completeness, quality, YAGNI, test hygiene); fix anything you find before reporting
  5. Report back

While iterating, run the focused test for what you're changing; run the full suite once before committing, not after every edit.

If you're stuck or the code doesn't match what the brief assumes, STOP and report BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT with specifics. Never silently produce work you're unsure about.

After Review Findings

If a reviewer finds issues and you fix them, re-run the tests that cover the amended code and append the results to your report file.

Report Format

Test Results:

AgentReview Task 3 (spec + quality)transcript ↗

You are reviewing one task's implementation: first whether it matches its requirements, then whether it is well-built. This is a task-scoped gate, not a merge review — a broad whole-branch review happens separately after all tasks are complete.

What Was Requested

Read the task brief: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-3-brief.md

Global constraints from the spec/design that bind this task:

  • Linked agent-session tokens merge into the SAME totals (Report.Total) and per-model rows (ByModel, same model-id normalization) while also being tracked separately in Report.Subagents/SubagentsCost/AgentSessions.
  • With no Agents, behavior is unchanged (Subagents zero, SubagentsCost nil, AgentSessions 0); existing tests pass untouched.
  • Agent turns must NOT create PerTurnCost entries (main-session-only).
  • SubagentsCost is nil when nothing in the agent sessions was priced.
  • gofmt -l . must print nothing.

Part 2: Code Quality

Strengths

Issues

Critical (Must Fix)

Important (Should Fix)

Minor (Nice to Have)

Assessment

Task quality: [Approved | Needs fixes] Reasoning: [1-2 sentences]

Spec Compliance

  • ✅ Report gains exactly the three fields specified (Subagents TokenCounts, SubagentsCost *float64, AgentSessions int), matching the brief's shape (internal/usage/usage.go:44-48).
  • ✅ Linked agent-session tokens merge into Total and ByModel using the same normalization key (stripDateSuffix via the shared key variable and shared byModel map) — verified by tracing accumulate at internal/usage/usage.go:72-102, which updates r.Total and byModel unconditionally, and only additionally updates r.Subagents/subByModel when inAgent is true.
  • ✅ With no Agents, behavior is unchanged: inAgent=true is set but the for _, a := range s.Agents loop (usage.go:113-117) is a no-op on an empty/nil slice, so Subagents stays zero, subByModel stays empty, subPriced stays false, SubagentsCost stays nil, AgentSessions is 0.
  • ✅ Agent turns do not create PerTurnCost entries — the per-turn cost loop at usage.go:130-141 iterates only s.Turns, never s.Agents; confirmed by TestComputeRollsUpLinkedAgentSessions asserting len(r.PerTurnCost) == 1.
  • ✅ SubagentsCost is nil when nothing in the agent sessions was priced — subPriced bool gate at usage.go:118-128, exercised by TestComputeUnpricedAgentSessionHasNilSubagentsCost.
  • ⚠️ gofmt -l . output — cannot independently execute per review rules; struct field alignment in the diff (usage.go:44-48) visually looks gofmt-correct and the implementer's report shows empty output, but this is unverified by me directly.
  • ✅ Cost math checked by hand: 1,000,000 input tokens × $5/MTok = $5.00, matching TestComputeRollsUpLinkedAgentSessions's assertion (usage_test.go:164-167).

Strengths

  • Implementation is a near-literal, careful application of the brief's suggested snippet — minimal surface area, no scope creep.
  • Correctly reuses existing normalization/pricing primitives (stripDateSuffix via shared key, Lookup, cost, walk) rather than duplicating logic, so subagent rows get identical model-id normalization to main rows "for free."
  • HasUnknownModel/TotalCostUSD correctly pick up subagent contributions "for free" too, since byModel/order are shared across main and agent turns and the final per-model loop (usage.go:143-158) iterates that shared map — this satisfies the "same totals" requirement even though it isn't explicitly re-tested for TotalCostUSD.
  • Ordering of inAgent mutation (false during main-turn walk, flipped true only for the agent walk) is correctly sequenced so there's no leakage of main-turn tokens into Subagents.
  • Two tests directly exercise both the golden path (merged totals + separate subtotal + cost) and the nil-cost edge case, matching the brief's Step 1 test text exactly.

Issues

Critical (Must Fix)

None.

Important (Should Fix)

None.

Minor (Nice to Have)

  1. Doc comment not updated for the new merge behavior. The Compute doc comment (internal/usage/usage.go:61-63) still reads "Totals and per-model figures include nested subagent turns; per-turn costs are recorded for top-level turns only" — it doesn't mention that linked Agents sessions are now also merged into totals/per-model rows. A reader skimming just the doc comment would miss this. Not a functional defect, since inline comments at usage.go:104 and usage.go:109-110 do explain it.
  2. No test asserts TotalCostUSD/per-row CostUSD after merging. TestComputeRollsUpLinkedAgentSessions checks Total.Input, Subagents.Input, AgentSessions, SubagentsCost, ByModel token count, and PerTurnCost length, but never asserts r.TotalCostUSD or r.ByModel[0].CostUSD reflect the merged $10.00 (main $5 + agent $5). This is the one path most likely to silently regress (e.g., if someone later double-counts or the merge diverges from SubagentsCost), and it's exactly the kind of "same totals" claim the task brief calls out as a global constraint, yet it goes unverified by the tests as written.
  3. No test covers multiple agent sessions or an agent session with a different model than the main session (e.g., two Agents entries, or one agent using a different priced model), which would exercise subByModel's multi-key aggregation loop (usage.go:118-125) more realistically. The current single-agent/single-model test can't distinguish an accumulation bug (e.g., overwrite instead of add) from correct behavior as clearly as a multi-entry case would.

Assessment

Task quality: Approved Reasoning: The implementation matches the brief's interfaces and constraints exactly, all four global constraints are correctly enforced in code (verified by tracing Compute), and the two required tests pass with hand-checked cost math. Remaining gaps are documentation polish and modest test-coverage broadening, not correctness defects. agentId: a32af6bd7209c32e2 (use SendMessage with to: 'a32af6bd7209c32e2', summary: '<5-10 word recap>' to continue this agent) subagent_tokens: 47128 tool_uses: 4 duration_ms: 105236

AgentImplement Task 4: agent-result cardstranscript ↗

You are implementing Task 4: Render agent-result cards and the subagent usage line, for the Go CLI ccwhid.

Task Description

Read your task brief first: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-4-brief.md It contains the full task text from the plan, including the exact test code and implementation code to use verbatim.

Context

ccwhid renders parsed sessions to HTML (internal/render). Earlier tasks on this branch added: model.TurnAgentResult with Turn.AgentStatus/AgentSummary fields (Task 1), and usage.Report.Subagents/SubagentsCost/AgentSessions (Task 3). This task renders agent-result turns as agent-attributed cards (role label like Agent · Implement Task 12: Profiles view, status chip) and adds the "of which subagents" line + footnote variants to the usage card. This is Task 4 of 5; Task 5 will add subagent transcript pages and links (and will further change some of the same code — implement exactly this task, nothing from Task 5).

Note: the brief changes roleLabel to take a model.Turn instead of a model.TurnKind — update BOTH existing call sites (buildViewModel and renderTool's inline sidechain rendering) as the brief shows.

Work from: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done (branch feat/subagent-sessions, already checked out).

Global constraints that bind this task:

  • The agent-result card rendering is unconditional (not gated on any Options flag); the usage SubLine/footnote changes live inside the existing usage view which is already gated on --usage.
  • The card CSS class comes from the existing turn-{{ .Kind }} pattern; kind string is agent-result.
  • Footnote wording exactly as the brief specifies (three variants: with agents / without agents / +unpriced suffix).
  • gofmt -l . must print nothing before committing.
  • The commit message must end with the trailer: Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

If you have questions about the requirements, approach, dependencies, or anything unclear in the task brief — ask them now.

Your Job

  1. Implement exactly what the task brief specifies (TDD: write the failing tests first, verify they fail, then implement)
  2. Verify the full suite passes: gofmt -l . && go test ./...
  3. Commit your work
  4. Self-review with fresh eyes (completeness, quality, YAGNI, test hygiene); fix anything you find before reporting
  5. Report back

You are reviewing one task's implementation: first whether it matches its requirements, then whether it is well-built. This is a task-scoped gate, not a merge review — a broad whole-branch review happens separately after all tasks are complete.

What Was Requested

Read the task brief: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-4-brief.md

Global constraints from the spec/design that bind this task:

  • Agent-result card rendering is unconditional (not gated on any Options flag); the SubLine/footnote live inside the usage view (gated on --usage upstream).
  • Role label format: Agent · <name> extracted from the quoted name in the summary; plain Agent fallback when no quoted name.
  • SubLine format exactly: of which subagents: <tokens> in+out · ~$X.XX (N sessions) with the cost segment omitted when SubagentsCost is nil; SubLine empty when AgentSessions == 0.
  • Footnote variants exactly as the brief specifies: with agents → "Includes N linked subagent session(s); server-tool fees are excluded."; without → the existing "Covers this transcript only; sub-agent sessions stored as separate files, and server-tool fees, are excluded."; the unpriced-models suffix appends to either.
  • The card CSS class comes from the existing turn-{{ .Kind }} pattern (kind string agent-result).
  • gofmt -l . must print nothing.

What the Implementer Claims They Built

Read the implementer's report: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-4-report.md

Task quality: [Approved | Needs fixes] Reasoning: [1-2 sentences]

This confirms the base/head commits match the review scope and the branch has prior tasks already landed (model/usage fields from Tasks 1 and 3 exist as prerequisites).

Spec Compliance

  • ✅ Agent-result card rendering is unconditional — the {{ range .Turns }} loop (report.html.tmpl:34) sits outside the {{ with .Usage }} block; SubLine/Footnote correctly live inside the usage card gated upstream by --usage.
  • ✅ Role label format Agent · <name> / plain Agent fallback — agentRoleLabel (render.go:206-214) matches the brief's reference implementation verbatim, including the j > 0 fallback for empty-quoted or unclosed-quote summaries.
  • ✅ SubLine format exact match, cost segment omitted when SubagentsCost is nil, empty when AgentSessions == 0 — render.go:156-162; confirmed usage.TokenCounts.InOut() (internal/usage/usage.go:20) and formatTokens/formatCost (internal/render/format.go:11,23) are the correct pre-existing helpers.
  • ✅ Footnote variants exact wording for both branches, unpriced-models suffix appended to either — render.go:164-171, matches brief text character-for-character.
  • ✅ Card CSS class via existing turn-{{ .Kind }} pattern, kind agent-result — no new template plumbing needed; .turn-agent-result rule added in styles.css:61-64 targets it correctly.

  • ✅ Named risk (roleLabel signature change TurnKind → Turn): grep confirms only two call sites in internal/render/render.go (lines 137, 288), both updated to pass model.Turn values (t and st); no stray TurnKind call sites remain, and roleLabel is unexported so no cross-package callers exist.

Strengths

  • agentRoleLabel and footnote/SubLine logic are copied faithfully from the brief's suggested code, including edge-case handling (empty summary, unmatched quotes).
  • Tests cover the "never attributed to You" invariant explicitly (render_test.go:303-305), a good regression guard against a plausible mistake in the roleLabel switch.
  • Both SubLine-present and SubLine-absent paths are tested, and both footnote variants are asserted to exclude the other's wording (render_test.go:334-336, 345), which is a stronger check than just presence.

Issues

None found at Critical, Important, or Minor severity — the diff is a faithful, minimal implementation of the brief with no scope creep and no deviation from the specified wording/format.

AgentImplement Task 5: subagent pagestranscript ↗

You are implementing Task 5: Subagent pages, links, and wiring — the final task of the subagent-sessions milestone for the Go CLI ccwhid.

Task Description

Read your task brief first: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-5-brief.md It contains the full task text from the plan, including exact code for the new types and the restructured Site function.

Context

ccwhid renders parsed Claude Code sessions to a static HTML report. Already on this branch: model.Session.Agents []model.AgentSession (each with ID, Description, AgentType, ToolUseID, nested Session) loaded by transcript.LoadAgentSessions (Task 2); model.TurnAgentResult turns with Turn.AgentID (Task 1); agent-attributed cards + roleLabel(model.Turn) (Task 4). This task makes render.Site emit one page per agent session under outDir/subagents/agent-<ID>.html and link to them from Agent/Task tool cards (via ToolUseID) and agent-result cards (via AgentID), then wires loading into the CLI, TUI label, and README.

Integration notes the brief cannot know:

  • buildViewModel currently has signature buildViewModel(s model.Session, title string, opts Options) viewData — you change it to take (s, title, opts, page pageInfo, links *agentLinks). Task 4 added tests calling the 3-arg form (TestAgentResultTurnRendersAsAgentCard in render_test.go); update those call sites to buildViewModel(s, "t", Options{}, pageInfo{}, newAgentLinks(nil, "")) keeping assertions unchanged. There may be other pre-existing buildViewModel calls in render tests — update them the same way.
  • renderTurnBody(t) / renderTool(tc) gain a links *agentLinks param; renderTool has a recursive inline-sidechain path calling renderTurnBody — pass links through.
  • renderTurnBody is also called for inline sidechain turns inside renderTool; there is no other caller outside render.go.
  • The e2e test file internal/render/render_e2e_test.go exists — read it first and follow its import/helper style when appending the two new tests from the brief.
  • In cmd/ccwhid/run.go, generate() currently calls transcript.ParseFile(...) then redaction; insert the LoadAgentSessions block between them exactly as the brief shows (gated on opts.includeSubagents).
  • In internal/tui/tui.go the option label to change is the string "Include subagents" (around line 376).

Work from: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done (branch feat/subagent-sessions, already checked out).

Global constraints that bind this task:

  • Agent page path exactly subagents/agent-<ID>.html relative to outDir; no subagents/ dir is created when the session has no agents.
  • Agent pages reference shared assets via ../assets/... (template {{ .Base }} prefix) and carry a back-link to ../index.html.
  • Link resolution always uses the ROOT session's agent set, so depth-2 agent references inside an agent page resolve to sibling files (prefix "" there, "subagents/" on index).
  • Every href you build in Go must go through html.EscapeString.
  • gofmt -l . must print nothing before committing; run go vet ./... too.
  • The commit message must end with the trailer: Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Before You Begin

If you have questions about the requirements, approach, dependencies, or anything unclear in the task brief — ask them now.

Your Job

  1. Implement exactly what the task brief specifies (TDD: write the failing e2e tests first, verify they fail, then implement)
  2. Verify: gofmt -l . && go vet ./... && go test ./...
  3. Commit your work
  4. Self-review with fresh eyes (completeness, quality, YAGNI, test hygiene); fix anything you find before reporting
  5. Report back

While iterating, run the focused test for what you're changing; run the full suite once before committing, not after every edit.

If you're stuck or the code doesn't match what the brief assumes, STOP and report BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT with specifics. Never silently produce work you're unsure about.

After Review Findings

If a reviewer finds issues and you fix them, re-run the tests that cover the amended code and append the results to your report file.

Report Format

Write your full report to ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-5-report.md:

  • What you implemented, TDD evidence (RED command+output, GREEN command+output), files changed, self-review findings, concerns.

Then report back with ONLY (under 15 lines):

  • Status: DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
  • Commits created (short SHA + subject)
  • One-line test summary
  • Your concerns, if any
  • The report file path

Good — that directory is gitignored entirely, so the report file isn't part of version control and won't be committed. Final git status is clean (only the commit already made). Task complete.

No concerns. AgentReview Task 5 (spec + quality)transcript ↗

You are reviewing one task's implementation: first whether it matches its requirements, then whether it is well-built. This is a task-scoped gate, not a merge review — a broad whole-branch review happens separately after all tasks are complete.

What Was Requested

Read the task brief: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-5-brief.md

Global constraints from the spec/design that bind this task:

  • Agent page path exactly subagents/agent-<ID>.html relative to outDir; NO subagents/ dir created when the session has no agents.
  • Agent pages reference shared assets via ../assets/... (template {{ .Base }} prefix) and carry a back-link to ../index.html.
  • Link resolution always uses the ROOT session's agent set with page-appropriate prefix ("subagents/" on index, "" on agent pages) so depth-2 references resolve to sibling files.
  • Index links to an agent page from BOTH the spawning tool card (ToolUseID match) and the agent-result card (AgentID match).
  • Every href built in Go goes through html.EscapeString.
  • CLI: LoadAgentSessions wired in generate() gated on opts.includeSubagents, before redaction. TUI label and README updated.
  • gofmt -l . silent; go vet ./... clean.

What the Implementer Claims They Built

Diff Under Review

Base: b66ed79 Head: b703c81 Diff file: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/review-b66ed79..b703c81.diff

Read the diff file once — it contains the commit list, a stat summary, and the full diff with surrounding context, and it is your view of the change. The diff's context lines ARE the changed files: do not Read a changed file separately unless a hunk you must judge is cut off mid-function — and say so in your report. Do not re-run git commands. Do not crawl the broader codebase. Inspect code outside the diff only to evaluate a concrete risk you can name — one focused check per named risk, and name both the risk and what you checked in your report. Two concrete named risks worth focused checks: (1) renderTurnBody/renderTool signature changes — verify no call site was missed; (2) redaction ordering in cmd/ccwhid/run.go — agents must be attached to the session BEFORE redact.Session runs, or agent content ships unredacted.

Your review is read-only on this checkout. Do not mutate the working tree, the index, HEAD, or branch state in any way.

Do Not Trust the Report

Treat the implementer's report as unverified claims about the code. Verify the claims against the diff. Design rationales in the report are claims too. Judge the code on its merits.

The implementer already ran the tests and reported results with TDD evidence for exactly this code. Do not re-run the suite to confirm their report. Run a test only when reading the code raises a specific doubt that no existing run answers — and then a focused test, never a package-wide suite. Part 1: Spec Compliance
