Output Format

Spec Compliance

  • ✅ / ❌ / ⚠️ items

Strengths

Issues

Critical (Must Fix)

Important (Should Fix)

Minor (Nice to Have)

Assessment

Task quality: [Approved | Needs fixes] Reasoning: [1-2 sentences]

Spec Compliance

  • ✅ Agent page path exactly subagents/agent-<ID>.html relative to outDir — internal/render/render.go:352-357 writes filepath.Join(subDir, "agent-"+a.ID+".html"); no subagents/ dir created when len(s.Agents)==0 (render.go:344-346), verified by TestSiteNoAgentsWritesNoSubagentsDir (render_e2e_test.go:613-621).
  • ✅ Agent pages use ../assets/... via {{ .Base }} and back-link to ../index.html — report.html.tmpl:158,180,215, render.go:357 (Base: "../", BackHref: "../index.html").
  • ✅ Link resolution always uses the ROOT session's agent set — render.go:331 closes over outer s.Agents (not the per-page sess param) inside writePage, with prefix "subagents/" on index and "" on agent pages (render.go:341,357).
  • ✅ Index links from both the tool card (ToolUseID match, renderTool at render.go:497-499) and the agent-result card (AgentID match, buildViewModel at render.go:447-449). Traced through the new e2e test: both the toolu_9 tool-use and the abc123 result turn resolve to the same href, giving the required count ≥ 2.
  • ✅ Every Go-built href passes html.EscapeString — the only Go-side href concatenation is renderTool's agent-link (render.go:498); all other href-bearing fields (Base, BackHref, AgentHref) are emitted via html/template (confirmed "html/template" import at render.go:9), which auto-escapes/URL-filters attribute context.
  • ✅ CLI wiring: LoadAgentSessions gated on opts.includeSubagents, placed after the skipped-lines warning and before redact.Session — cmd/ccwhid/run.go:95-106. Confirmed redact.Session (internal/redact/redact.go:59-61) recurses into s.Agents, redacting Description and the nested Session, so the ordering is load-bearing and correct, not just cosmetic.
  • ✅ TUI label and README updated as specified — internal/tui/tui.go:664, README.md flag table/what's-included/known-limitation sections.
  • ✅ gofmt -l silent and go vet clean — independently re-verified on the touched files/packages (both produced no output).

Strengths

  • Signature-threading of *agentLinks through renderTurnBody/renderTool/its recursive sidechain call is complete — grep confirms all call sites live in render.go itself and were all updated; no orphaned call sites.
  • The design correctly separates "index → subagents/" vs "agent page → sibling" link prefixes while resolving against a single root agent set, exactly matching the brief's depth-2 resolution requirement, and this was traced end-to-end against the new e2e test.
  • README's stale "Token usage & cost" paragraph (claiming subagent tokens are never included) was corrected to match already-shipped behavior — a reasonable, in-scope accuracy fix rather than scope creep, and it's called out transparently in the report.
  • Test additions match the brief verbatim and actually exercise both link directions (tool-card and result-card) plus the no-agents case.

Issues

Critical (Must Fix)

None.

Important (Should Fix)

None.

Minor (Nice to Have)

  • internal/render/render.go:330-331 — newAgentLinks(s.Agents, page.PagePrefix) is rebuilt from scratch inside writePage for every page written, including once per agent page (all with an identical "" prefix). For sessions with many agents (the report's own smoke test used 50+) this is O(n²) map construction; trivial in practice but could be hoisted to two shared instances (one per prefix) since the brief's own pseudocode already computes it inside the closure — not a deviation, just a possible follow-up polish.
  • internal/render/render.go:352 — the local variable sub is reused both as the intermediate subtitle-string builder and shadows the visually-similar subDir from a few lines up; a distinct name (e.g. subtitle) would read more clearly. Purely cosmetic.
  • Agent page filenames are built directly from a.ID (render.go:356) with no path-traversal sanitization; harmless in practice since AgentSession.ID comes from trusted local session-directory names (UUID-shaped), and this pattern is specified verbatim in the brief itself, but worth flagging as a latent assumption if AgentSession.ID ever becomes less trusted.

Assessment

Task quality: Approved Reasoning: Implementation matches the brief essentially line-for-line, all stated global constraints (page path, asset prefixing, root-agent-set link resolution, dual-source linking, Go-side escaping, redaction ordering, CLI/TUI/README wiring) were independently verified against the diff and, where relevant, against out-of-diff code (redact.go recursion, call-site grep, gofmt/vet re-run). Read~/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/6.1.1/skills/requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md
{
  "file_path": "~/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/6.1.1/skills/requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md"
}
1	# Code Reviewer Prompt Template
2	
3	Use this template when dispatching a code reviewer subagent.
4	
5	**Purpose:** Review completed work against requirements and code quality standards before it cascades into more work.
6	
7	```
8	Subagent (general-purpose):
9	  description: "Review code changes"
10	  prompt: |
11	    You are a Senior Code Reviewer with expertise in software architecture,
12	    design patterns, and best practices. Your job is to review completed work
13	    against its plan or requirements and identify issues before they cascade.
14	
15	    ## What Was Implemented
16	
17	    [DESCRIPTION]
18	
19	    ## Requirements / Plan
20	
21	    [PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS]
22	
23	    ## Git Range to Review
24	
25	    **Base:** [BASE_SHA]
26	    **Head:** [HEAD_SHA]
27	
28	    ```bash
29	    git diff --stat [BASE_SHA]..[HEAD_SHA]
30	    git diff [BASE_SHA]..[HEAD_SHA]
31	    ```
32	
33	    ## Read-Only Review
34	
35	    Your review is read-only on this checkout. Do not mutate the working tree, the index, HEAD, or branch state in any way. Use tools like `git show`, `git diff`, and `git log` to inspect history. If you need a working copy of a different revision, check it out into a separate temporary directory (e.g. `git worktree add /tmp/review-[SHA] [SHA]`) — never move HEAD on this checkout.
36	
37	    ## What to Check
38	
39	    **Plan alignment:**
40	    - Does the implementation match the plan / requirements?
41	    - Are deviations justified improvements, or problematic departures?
42	    - Is all planned functionality present?
43	
44	    **Code quality:**
45	    - Clean separation of concerns?
46	    - Proper error handling?
47	    - Type safety where applicable?
48	    - DRY without premature abstraction?
49	    - Edge cases handled?
50	
51	    **Architecture:**
52	    - Sound design decisions?
53	    - Reasonable scalability and performance?
54	    - Security concerns?
55	    - Integrates cleanly with surrounding code?
56	
57	    **Testing:**
58	    - Tests verify real behavior, not mocks?
59	    - Edge cases covered?
60	    - Integration tests where they matter?
61	    - All tests passing?
62	
63	    **Production readiness:**
64	    - Migration strategy if schema changed?
65	    - Backward compatibility considered?
66	    - Documentation complete?
67	    - No obvious bugs?
68	
69	    ## Calibration
70	
71	    Categorize issues by actual severity. Not everything is Critical.
72	    Acknowledge what was done well before listing issues — accurate praise
73	    helps the implementer trust the rest of the feedback.
74	
75	    If you find significant deviations from the plan, flag them specifically
76	    so the implementer can confirm whether the deviation was intentional.
77	    If you find issues with the plan itself rather than the implementation,
78	    say so.
79	
80	    ## Output Format
81	
82	    ### Strengths
83	    [What's well done? Be specific.]
84	
85	    ### Issues
86	
87	    #### Critical (Must Fix)
88	    [Bugs, security issues, data loss risks, broken functionality]
89	
90	    #### Important (Should Fix)
91	    [Architecture problems, missing features, poor error handling, test gaps]
92	
93	    #### Minor (Nice to Have)
94	    [Code style, optimization opportunities, documentation polish]
95	
96	    For each issue:
97	    - File:line reference
98	    - What's wrong
99	    - Why it matters
100	    - How to fix (if not obvious)
101	
102	    ### Recommendations
103	    [Improvements for code quality, architecture, or process]
104	
105	    ### Assessment
106	
107	    **Ready to merge?** [Yes | No | With fixes]
108	
109	    **Reasoning:** [1-2 sentence technical assessment]
110	
111	    ## Critical Rules
112	
113	    **DO:**
114	    - Categorize by actual severity
115	    - Be specific (file:line, not vague)
116	    - Explain WHY each issue matters
117	    - Acknowledge strengths
118	    - Give a clear verdict
119	
120	    **DON'T:**
121	    - Say "looks good" without checking
122	    - Mark nitpicks as Critical
123	    - Give feedback on code you didn't actually read
124	    - Be vague ("improve error handling")
125	    - Avoid giving a clear verdict
126	```
127	
128	**Placeholders:**
129	- `[DESCRIPTION]` — brief summary of what was built
130	- `[PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS]` — what it should do (plan file path, task text, or requirements)
131	- `[BASE_SHA]` — starting commit
132	- `[HEAD_SHA]` — ending commit
133	
134	**Reviewer returns:** Strengths, Issues (Critical / Important / Minor), Recommendations, Assessment
135	
136	## Example Output
137	
138	```
139	### Strengths
140	- Clean database schema with proper migrations (db.ts:15-42)
141	- Comprehensive test coverage (18 tests, all edge cases)
142	- Good error handling with fallbacks (summarizer.ts:85-92)
143	
144	### Issues
145	
146	#### Important
147	1. **Missing help text in CLI wrapper**
148	   - File: index-conversations:1-31
149	   - Issue: No --help flag, users won't discover --concurrency
150	   - Fix: Add --help case with usage examples
151	
152	2. **Date validation missing**
153	   - File: search.ts:25-27
154	   - Issue: Invalid dates silently return no results
155	   - Fix: Validate ISO format, throw error with example
156	
157	#### Minor
158	1. **Progress indicators**
159	   - File: indexer.ts:130
160	   - Issue: No "X of Y" counter for long operations
161	   - Impact: Users don't know how long to wait
162	
163	### Recommendations
164	- Add progress reporting for user experience
165	- Consider config file for excluded projects (portability)
166	
167	### Assessment
168	
169	**Ready to merge: With fixes**
170	
171	**Reasoning:** Core implementation is solid with good architecture and tests. Important issues (help text, date validation) are easily fixed and don't affect core functionality.
172	```

Claude2k tok · ~$0.38
AgentFinal whole-branch reviewtranscript ↗

You are a Senior Code Reviewer with expertise in software architecture, design patterns, and best practices. Your job is to review a completed feature branch against its spec and identify issues before merge.

What Was Implemented

The subagent-sessions milestone for ccwhid, a Go CLI that renders Claude Code JSONL transcripts as static HTML reports. Five tasks, one commit each, on branch feat/subagent-sessions:

  1. Parse <task-notification> user records into a new agent-result turn kind (fixes agent results being attributed to "You").
  2. transcript.LoadAgentSessions loading <sessionId>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl + meta.json into model.Session.Agents; Options.AgentFile treats sidechain-flagged records as main chain; redaction walks agents.
  3. usage.Compute merges agent-session tokens into totals/per-model rows and tracks a Subagents/SubagentsCost/AgentSessions subtotal.
  4. Renderer: agent-attributed result cards (role label Agent · <name>, status chip) and "of which subagents" usage line + footnote variants.
  5. Multi-page rendering: one subagents/agent-<ID>.html per agent session, linked from spawning tool cards (ToolUseID) and result cards (AgentID); CLI/TUI/README wiring.

Requirements / Plan

  • Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-subagent-sessions-design.md (in repo — read it)
  • Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-12-subagent-sessions.md (skim only if the spec leaves a question open)

Git Range to Review

Base: 608d700 Head: b703c81 Review package (read this first — commit list, stat, full diff with context): ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/review-608d700..b703c81.diff

Read-Only Review

Your review is read-only on this checkout. Do not mutate the working tree, the index, HEAD, or branch state. You may read any file in the repo and run focused read-only commands (go test on a package is OK if a specific doubt warrants it).

Specific attention items (from per-task reviews and a background security scan)

  1. Escaping audit (a background security scanner flagged "XSS via manual HTML building" in render.go): verify every manually-built HTML path escapes untrusted content — renderTool (summary, input JSON, result content, subagent descriptions, the new agent-link href), agentRoleLabel output flowing into {{ .RoleLabel }}, notification-derived text (result body is markdown-rendered — check the markdown pipeline's HTML policy), and template fields typed template.HTML (only usageView.Headline should be). Give a verdict: real XSS vector or false positive, with evidence.
  2. Rolled-up Minor findings from per-task reviews — triage which (if any) must be fixed before merge:
    • tagContent LastIndex could over-capture when a <result> body contains a literal </result> (accepted heuristic, documented).
    • LoadAgentSessions warn-and-skip branch effectively fires only on I/O errors; that branch, malformed meta.json, and non-NotExist ReadDir errors are untested.
    • usage.Compute doc comment not updated for linked-agent merge; no test asserts merged TotalCostUSD; no multi-agent/different-model rollup test.
    • newAgentLinks rebuilt per page (O(n²) map builds, trivial n); sub variable naming near subDir (cosmetic); agent page filename built from a.ID without sanitization (trusted local input).
  3. Cross-task seams the per-task gates could not see: notification tool-use-id is parsed but only task-id is used for linking — confirm result-card links and tool-card links can't disagree; agent pages get their own usage card via Compute(a.Session) — confirm no double-counting or misleading footnote on agent pages; --force output-dir clearing with the new subagents/ subdirectory.

What to Check

Plan alignment (implementation matches spec; deviations justified?), code quality (separation of concerns, error handling, DRY, edge cases), architecture (design, performance on 200+ agent sessions, security, integration), testing (real behavior, edge cases, all passing), production readiness (backward compat: sessions without agents / old-style sidechain transcripts unchanged; docs complete).

Calibration

Categorize by actual severity. Acknowledge strengths first. Flag significant plan deviations specifically. If you find issues with the plan itself, say so.

Output Format

Strengths

Issues

Critical (Must Fix)

Important (Should Fix)

Minor (Nice to Have)

For each: file:line, what's wrong, why it matters, how to fix.

Escaping audit verdict

[real vector / false positive, with the evidence trail]

Minor-rollup triage

[which pre-merge, which backlog]

Recommendations

Assessment

Ready to merge? [Yes | No | With fixes] Reasoning: [1-2 sentences]

I have everything I need. Here's my review.

Code Review: feat/subagent-sessions (608d700..b703c81)

Strengths

  • Faithful to the spec. All five design sections landed as written: the agent-result turn kind and unconditional notification parsing (§2), LoadAgentSessions with the AgentFile main-chain flag (§3), agent-attributed cards + linked pages with relative-asset Base prefixing (§4), the merged usage rollup with Subagents/SubagentsCost/AgentSessions and the footnote/subline variants (§5), and the flag/TUI/README wiring (§6). Deviations are limited to cosmetic footnote wording.
  • Good test coverage of the happy and tricky paths. Notification fallback-to-user-turn, mid-string <task-notification> mention, repeat notifications, AgentFile sidechain-as-main-chain, meta.json fallback-to-ID, usage merge with per-model collapsing, per-turn-cost isolation, and the two-links-per-agent index assertion are all present and passing (go test ./... green).
  • Escaping is otherwise disciplined. Every manually built sink in renderTool — tc.Name, tc.Summary, href, InputJSON, Result.Content, sub.Description — runs through html.EscapeString, and the markdown pipeline runs goldmark with Unsafe=false (raw HTML omitted). usageView.Headline is the only intentionally-template.HTML usage field; SubLine/Footnote are plain strings that auto-escape.
  • Clean seam handling. --force uses RemoveAll per top-level entry, so the new subagents/ subdir is cleared correctly; agent pages compute usage over their own session only (empty .Agents), so no double-count and no misleading "of which subagents" line; result-card links (forAgent, keyed on task-id == filename ID) and tool-card links (forToolUse, keyed on meta.json toolUseId) both resolve to the same agent-<ID>.html and can't produce contradictory targets — a mismatch just yields no link, never a broken one.

Issues

Critical (Must Fix)

None.

Important (Should Fix)

internal/render/render.go:364 — unescaped roleLabel(st) on the sidechain render path (the real hit from the XSS scanner).

b.WriteString(`<article class="turn turn-` + string(st.Kind) + `"><div class="turn-role">` + roleLabel(st) + `</div>...

Before this milestone this line called roleLabel(st.Kind), which returned only the constants "You"/"Claude" — always safe. This commit changed it to roleLabel(st), which for a TurnAgentResult turn returns agentRoleLabel(t.AgentSummary) = "Agent · " + <summary text lifted verbatim from the transcript>. That string is written raw into template.HTML with no html.EscapeString, unlike every sibling sink in this same function.

  • Reachability: an in-file sidechain user record whose content is a single <task-notification> string with a valid <task-id>+<summary> is classified TurnAgentResult by buildTurn (it runs for sidechain records too, parse.go:127), and sidechain turns render through this exact branch. A crafted <summary>x<img src=x onerror=…></summary> from a nested-agent completion would inject unescaped markup. Content is model/tool-generated rather than external-attacker-supplied, and reports are meant to be reviewed before sharing — so this is a defense-in-depth regression, not a remote exploit — but it breaks the escaping invariant the rest of the file upholds, and it's precisely what the scanner flagged.
  • Fix: html.EscapeString(roleLabel(st)). The main-timeline path is unaffected because {{ .RoleLabel }} is auto-escaped by html/template; this only reaches the manually-built subagent branch.

Minor (Nice to Have)

  • internal/render/render.go (markdown links, pre-existing). goldmark strips raw HTML but does not sanitize [x](javascript:…) link hrefs by default. This applies to all assistant/agent markdown, predates this milestone, and the result body inherits the same trust level — backlog, not introduced here.
  • internal/transcript/agents.go:35-46 — warn-and-skip / malformed-meta branches untested. The ParseFile error path, json.Unmarshal failure, and non-NotExist ReadDir error are uncovered. Low risk; backlog.
  • internal/usage/usage.go:47 — Compute doc comment not updated to mention the linked-agent merge (says "for a session"); cosmetic.
  • internal/render/render.go:490 — newAgentLinks rebuilt per page (O(n²) map builds). Trivial at realistic n (256 agents → ~65k inserts); backlog. sub vs subDir naming is cosmetic.

Escaping audit verdict

One real vector, rest false positive. The scanner's "XSS via manual HTML building in render.go" is a true positive at render.go:364 (roleLabel(st) unescaped, detailed above). Every other manually built path is correctly escaped: renderTool's summary/input/result/description/href all use html.EscapeString; the new agent-link href is escaped and is trusted-local anyway; agentRoleLabel output on the main timeline flows into auto-escaped {{ .RoleLabel }}; Status, Base, Subtitle, BackHref, AgentHref, SubLine are all auto-escaped template fields; notification result bodies are markdown-rendered with goldmark Unsafe=false (raw HTML omitted). template.HTML-typed fields are limited to Body (intentional, built from escaped fragments + rendered markdown) and usageView.Headline (built via html.EscapeString). Evidence trail: parse.go:259-271 produces the untrusted AgentSummary → render.go:335-337 agentRoleLabel embeds it → render.go:364 emits it without escaping into a template.HTML string.

Minor-rollup triage

  • Pre-merge: only the render.go:364 escaping fix (promoted out of the "Minor" bucket — it's the scanner's real finding).
  • Backlog: tagContent LastIndex heuristic (documented, acceptable); LoadAgentSessions warn/malformed/ReadDir-error tests; Compute doc comment; per-page newAgentLinks O(n²) and sub/subDir naming; agent-page filename sanitization (trusted local input). The already-added multi-model/merged-cost rollup tests (usage_test.go) cover the earlier "no merged-cost test" concern.

Recommendations

  1. Escape line 364: html.EscapeString(roleLabel(st)), and add a one-line render test that a sidechain agent-result turn's summary containing < is escaped (locks the invariant).
  2. Optional: update the Compute doc comment to note linked-agent merge while touching usage.go.

Assessment

Ready to merge? With fixes. Reasoning: The feature is spec-complete, well-tested, and architecturally clean; the only blocker is the single unescaped roleLabel(st) sink at render.go:364 — a one-line fix that closes the escaping regression the security scan correctly flagged. Everything else is backlog. agentId: a900be2071f19ab09 (use SendMessage with to: 'a900be2071f19ab09', summary: '<5-10 word recap>' to continue this agent) subagent_tokens: 80583 tool_uses: 10 duration_ms: 198533

The security scanner's flag was real: one unescaped sink at render.go:364 on the sidechain render path. Dispatching a single fix subagent:

Claude
AgentFix final-review findingstranscript ↗

You are applying the final-review fixes on the Go CLI ccwhid. Work from ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done (branch feat/subagent-sessions).

Two fixes, one commit:

Fix 1 (Important — escaping regression)

In internal/render/render.go, inside renderTool, the inline-sidechain rendering loop writes the role label raw:

b.WriteString(`<article class="turn turn-` + string(st.Kind) + `"><div class="turn-role">` + roleLabel(st) + `</div><div class="turn-body">`)

roleLabel(st) can now return agentRoleLabel(t.AgentSummary) which embeds transcript-derived text (Agent · <name>), so it must be escaped like every sibling sink in this function:

... + html.EscapeString(roleLabel(st)) + ...

Then add a regression test in internal/render/render_test.go that locks the invariant. Follow the file's existing test style:

func TestSidechainAgentResultSummaryIsEscaped(t *testing.T) {
	tc := &model.ToolCall{Name: "Task", Subagents: []model.Subagent{{
		Description: "d",
		Turns: []model.Turn{{
			Kind:         model.TurnAgentResult,
			AgentSummary: `Agent "<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>" finished`,
			Blocks:       []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockText, Text: "x"}},
		}},
	}}}
	out := renderTool(tc, newAgentLinks(nil, ""))
	if strings.Contains(out, "<img") {
		t.Fatal("agent summary must be HTML-escaped in sidechain rendering")
	}
	if !strings.Contains(out, "&lt;img") {
		t.Error("escaped summary not found in output")
	}
}

TDD: add the test first, run go test ./internal/render/ -run SidechainAgentResultSummaryIsEscaped -v, verify it FAILS (contains raw <img), then apply the one-line fix, verify it PASSES.

Fix 2 (Minor — stale doc comment)

In internal/usage/usage.go, the Compute doc comment still reads:

// Compute aggregates token usage and estimated cost for a session. Totals and
// per-model figures include nested subagent turns; per-turn costs are recorded
// for top-level turns only.

Update it to also mention linked agent sessions, e.g.:

// Compute aggregates token usage and estimated cost for a session. Totals and
// per-model figures include nested subagent turns and linked agent sessions
// (s.Agents), tracked separately in the Subagents subtotal; per-turn costs are
// recorded for top-level main-session turns only.

Verify & commit

Run: gofmt -l . && go vet ./... && go test ./internal/render/ ./internal/usage/ Expected: gofmt silent, vet clean, both packages pass. (No need for the full suite — these are the covering packages.)

Commit both changes as one commit, message ending with the trailer: Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Report

Append a short fix report (what changed, RED/GREEN evidence for the escaping test, commands + output for the verify step) to ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-5-report.md under a heading "## Final-review fixes".

Then report back with ONLY: status, commit SHA + subject, one-line test summary.

Status: All fixes applied and verified.

Commit: 92f3646 — Fix escaping regression in sidechain rendering and update Compute doc

Test summary: Regression test TestSidechainAgentResultSummaryIsEscaped now passes (GREEN); all 28 tests across internal/render/ and internal/usage/ pass. Verification: gofmt silent, go vet clean, both packages pass. agentId: ae36a349eae01457d (use SendMessage with to: 'ae36a349eae01457d', summary: '<5-10 word recap>' to continue this agent) subagent_tokens: 37332 tool_uses: 25 duration_ms: 114155

AgentRe-review final fixestranscript ↗

You are re-reviewing a small fix commit on the Go CLI ccwhid (branch feat/subagent-sessions). A whole-branch review found one Important issue and one Minor; a fix subagent addressed both. Verify the fixes are correct and complete.

The findings that were to be fixed

  1. Important: internal/render/render.go, renderTool's inline-sidechain loop wrote roleLabel(st) unescaped into manually-built HTML. For TurnAgentResult turns, roleLabel returns agentRoleLabel(t.AgentSummary) which embeds transcript-derived text — must be wrapped in html.EscapeString, plus a regression test asserting a summary containing <img ...> renders escaped.
  2. Minor: internal/usage/usage.go Compute doc comment updated to mention linked agent sessions / Subagents subtotal.

Diff Under Review

Base: b703c81 Head: 92f3646 Diff file: ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/review-b703c81..92f3646.diff

Read the diff file once. Check: (a) the escaping fix is on the correct sink and doesn't double-escape anything already escaped elsewhere; (b) the regression test actually exercises the sidechain path (renderTool with a Subagent containing a TurnAgentResult turn) and would fail without the fix; (c) the doc comment is accurate; (d) nothing unrelated changed. The fix report with RED/GREEN evidence is appended under "## Final-review fixes" in ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/.superpowers/sdd/task-5-report.md — treat its claims as unverified and check them against the diff.

Your review is read-only. Do not mutate the working tree, index, HEAD, or branch state.

Output

Begin directly with the verdict. For each of the two findings: FIXED / NOT FIXED / PARTIAL with file:line evidence. Then any new issues the fix introduced (severity-labeled), then a one-line overall verdict (clean / needs another pass).

All green, gofmt/vet clean, working tree unmodified (git status clean, still at 92f3646).

Verdict

1. Important — HTML escaping in sidechain rendering: FIXED

internal/render/render.go:364 now wraps the sink correctly:

b.WriteString(`<article class="turn turn-` + string(st.Kind) + `"><div class="turn-role">` + html.EscapeString(roleLabel(st)) + `</div><div class="turn-body">`)

This is the correct and only manual-HTML sink for roleLabel — the other call site (render.go:207, RoleLabel: roleLabel(t)) feeds an html/template-parsed template, which auto-escapes contextually, so no double-escaping is introduced by this change.

Regression test TestSidechainAgentResultSummaryIsEscaped (internal/render/render_test.go:56-72) genuinely exercises the vulnerable path: it builds a model.ToolCall with a Subagent containing a Turn{Kind: model.TurnAgentResult, AgentSummary: ...<img.../>...} and calls renderTool directly. I independently verified RED/GREEN by building a throwaway git worktree at base commit b703c81, applying only the new test file (no other changes), and confirming it fails there (render_test.go:285: agent summary must be HTML-escaped...) — matching the report's claimed RED evidence — then confirmed it passes at HEAD 92f3646. Worktree was removed afterward; the real working tree/index/HEAD were never touched (git status clean, still on 92f3646).

2. Minor — Compute doc comment: FIXED

internal/usage/usage.go:61-64 now reads: "Totals and per-model figures include nested subagent turns and linked agent sessions (s.Agents), tracked separately in the Subagents subtotal; per-turn costs are recorded for top-level main-session turns only." This accurately reflects the function body: walk covers nested turns (line 107) and s.Agents sessions (lines 112-118) both feed r.Total/byModel, with inAgent gating the separate r.Subagents accumulation (lines 94-102), and PerTurnCost is populated only from s.Turns (line 132).

New issues introduced by the fix: none found. Diff is scoped to exactly the two findings — no unrelated changes.

Overall: clean. agentId: aa107892d6417eb01 (use SendMessage with to: 'aa107892d6417eb01', summary: '<5-10 word recap>' to continue this agent) subagent_tokens: 41386 tool_uses: 13 duration_ms: 81270

Do not mutate the working tree, the index, HEAD, or branch state in any way.\n\n## Do Not Trust the Report\n\nTreat the implementer&#39;s report as unverified claims about the code. Verify the claims against the diff. Design rationales in the report are claims too. Judge the code on its merits.\n\n## Tests\n\nThe implementer already ran the tests and reported results with TDD evidence for exactly this code. Do not re-run the suite to confirm their report. Run a test only when reading the code raises a specific doubt that no existing run answers — and then a focused test, never a package-wide suite. Warnings or other noise in the reported test output are findings — test output should be pristine.\n\n## Part 1: Spec Compliance\n\nCompare the diff against What Was Requested: Missing / Extra / Misunderstood. If a requirement cannot be verified from this diff alone, report it as a ⚠️ item.\n\n## Part 2: Code Quality\n\nCode quality (separation of concerns, error handling, DRY, edge cases), tests (verify real behavior, edge cases covered), structure (file responsibilities, plan&#39;s file structure followed). The template/CSS changes are part of the diff — check the template emits the status chip and SubLine where the brief says.\n\nPoint at evidence: file:line references for every finding. Your final message is the report itself: begin directly with the spec-compliance verdict; no preamble, no process narration.\n\n## Calibration\n\nCategorize by actual severity. Important = task cannot be trusted until fixed. \&#34;Coverage could be broader\&#34; and polish are Minor. If the plan/brief explicitly mandates something this rubric calls a defect, report it as Important, labeled plan-mandated. Acknowledge what was done well before listing issues.\n\n## Output Format\n\n### Spec Compliance\n- ✅ / ❌ / ⚠️ items\n\n### Strengths\n\n### Issues\n#### Critical (Must Fix)\n#### Important (Should Fix)\n#### Minor (Nice to Have)\n\n### Assessment\n**Task quality:** [Approved | Needs fixes]\n**Reasoning:** [1-2 sentences]&#34;,

{
  "command": "gh pr create --title \"Subagent sessions: agent-attributed result cards, linked transcript pages, usage rollup\" --body \"$(cat \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n## Summary\n\nMakes subagent work first-class in ccwhid reports (spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-subagent-sessions-design.md`):\n\n- **Agent-attributed result cards** — background-agent completions arrive as `\u003ctask-notification\u003e` user records and were rendered as \"You\" cards. They now parse into a new `agent-result` turn kind and render as `Agent · \u003cname\u003e` cards with a status chip. Unconditional fix (misattribution was a bug, not an option).\n- **Linked subagent transcript pages** — with `--include-subagents` (default on), the transcripts under `\u003csessionId\u003e/subagents/agent-*.jsonl` render as one HTML page each under `subagents/`, linked from the spawning Agent/Task tool card (via meta.json `toolUseId`) and from the result card (via task-id). Agent pages share assets, carry a back-link, and depth-2 agent references resolve to sibling pages.\n- **Usage rollup** — with `--usage`, linked agent-session tokens merge into the per-model table and totals, with a separate `of which subagents: \u003ctok\u003e in+out · ~$X (N sessions)` line and an updated footnote.\n\n## Out of scope (documented)\n\nFlat SDK-spawned session files (e.g. review-hook runs) carry no parent linkage and are not attributed or included.\n\n## Verification\n\n- TDD throughout; full suite, `go vet`, `gofmt` clean.\n- Security: a scanner flagged manual HTML building in render.go — confirmed one real unescaped sink on the inline-sidechain path, fixed with `html.EscapeString` + regression test (`TestSidechainAgentResultSummaryIsEscaped`); all other manual HTML paths audited as escaped.\n- Real-data smoke: rendered a session with 57 linked subagent transcripts — 57 pages, 53 index links, 5 agent-result cards, subline `657k in+out · ~$15.42 (57 sessions)`.\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\" 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -2",

=== internal/transcript/parse.go:244 (id 3566156964)
tagContent() uses strings.LastIndex for the closing tag for *all* fields. If the <result> markdown contains a literal closing tag like </task-id> or </summary>, LastIndex will match that and the extracted TaskID/Summary will span far beyond the intended tag, producing incorrect AgentID/summary and broken linking.

A safer approach is to use the first closing tag (strings.Index) for all tags except <result>, where LastIndex is useful to tolerate XML-looking content inside the body.
Claude3k tok · ~$0.36
AgentFix Copilot finding: tagContenttranscript ↗

You are fixing one code-review finding on the Go CLI ccwhid. Work from ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done (branch feat/subagent-sessions).

The finding (from Copilot on PR #7, valid)

internal/transcript/parse.go — tagContent() uses strings.LastIndex for the closing tag for ALL fields. If a <task-notification> payload's <result> markdown contains a literal </task-id> or </summary> (e.g. an agent report quoting another notification envelope), LastIndex matches that later occurrence and the extracted TaskID/Summary span far beyond the intended tag — corrupting AgentID and breaking result-card links.

The fix

In tagContent(s, name string), use strings.Index (first closing tag) for all tags EXCEPT result, which keeps strings.LastIndex to tolerate XML-looking content inside the body. Keep it in one function, e.g.:

// tagContent returns the text between the first <name> and its closing
// </name>, trimmed. Simple fields match the first closing tag; <result> keeps
// matching the last one so bodies that quote XML-looking text (even a literal
// </result>) stay intact.
func tagContent(s, name string) string {
	open, close := "<"+name+">", "</"+name+">"
	i := strings.Index(s, open)
	if i < 0 {
		return ""
	}
	rest := s[i+len(open):]
	var j int
	if name == "result" {
		j = strings.LastIndex(rest, close)
	} else {
		j = strings.Index(rest, close)
	}
	if j < 0 {
		return ""
	}
	return strings.TrimSpace(rest[:j])
}

TDD

First add a failing regression test to internal/transcript/notification_test.go (follow the file's existing helpers — it has notifLine(t, body) to wrap a payload into a JSONL record and parses via Parse):

func TestParseNotificationResultQuotingOtherTags(t *testing.T) {
	// A <result> body that quotes another notification's envelope must not
	// corrupt the simple fields: task-id/summary match their FIRST closing tag.
	body := "<task-notification>\n<task-id>real-id</task-id>\n<status>completed</status>\n" +
		"<summary>Agent \"x\" finished</summary>\n" +
		"<result>the agent saw `</task-id>` and `</summary>` in a quoted envelope</result>\n" +
		"</task-notification>"
	s, err := Parse(strings.NewReader(notifLine(t, body)), Options{})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if len(s.Turns) != 1 || s.Turns[0].Kind != model.TurnAgentResult {
		t.Fatalf("want one agent-result turn, got %+v", s.Turns)
	}
	if s.Turns[0].AgentID != "real-id" {
		t.Errorf("AgentID = %q, want real-id (must not span into the result body)", s.Turns[0].AgentID)
	}
	if want := `Agent "x" finished`; s.Turns[0].AgentSummary != want {
		t.Errorf("AgentSummary = %q, want %q", s.Turns[0].AgentSummary, want)
	}
}

Run go test ./internal/transcript/ -run QuotingOtherTags -v — verify it FAILS before the fix (AgentID spans into the body), then apply the fix and verify it PASSES. Also confirm the existing notification tests still pass: go test ./internal/transcript/.

Verify & commit

gofmt -l . && go vet ./... && go test ./internal/transcript/ Expected: gofmt silent, vet clean, package passes.

Commit as one commit (message like "fix: match first closing tag for simple notification fields"), ending with the trailer: Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Then push: git push

Report

Report back with ONLY: status, commit SHA + subject, one-line test summary (RED confirmed? GREEN? existing tests?).

Status: Complete

Commit: 9778609 — fix: match first closing tag for simple notification fields

Test Summary: RED → GREEN. Regression test confirms the bug (AgentID was spanning into result body), then passes post-fix. All 19 transcript tests pass, including 9 existing notification tests.

Verification: gofmt silent, go vet clean, package passes. agentId: a5c71d55e0b26ef8e (use SendMessage with to: 'a5c71d55e0b26ef8e', summary: '<5-10 word recap>' to continue this agent) subagent_tokens: 29953 tool_uses: 13 duration_ms: 62042

review 4679919677 state: COMMENTED
--- new comments in this review:
=== README.md:null (id 3566164791)
The `--include-subagents` flag description no longer mentions how to disable it even though it defaults to true. This is a doc regression compared to the prior wording and can confuse users who want to omit subagent content (the CLI supports `--include-subagents=false`).

=== internal/render/render.go:null (id 3566164805)
`renderTool` inserts an `<a>` link inside the `<summary>` element. Per HTML semantics this can lead to unreliable click/keyboard behavior (clicking the link may toggle the `<details>` instead of navigating, and nested interactive content in `<summary>` is problematic for accessibility). Consider rendering the link outside `<summary>` and overriding the default `details:not([open]) > :not(summary){display:none}` rule in CSS so the link stays visible when collapsed, or otherwise adjust structure/JS to make the link reliably clickable.

AgentFix Copilot round-2 findingstranscript ↗

You are fixing two code-review findings on the Go CLI ccwhid. Work from ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done (branch feat/subagent-sessions).

Fix 1 — README flag description regression

In README.md, the flag table row for --include-subagents was reworded on this branch and dropped the disable hint. Find that row and make sure it ends with the previous guidance, i.e. the description should read:

Include subagent work: inline Task sidechains and linked agent-session transcript pages under subagents/ (default true; --include-subagents=false to omit)

(Keep the row's existing table formatting/backticks style consistent with neighboring rows.)

Fix 2 — link inside toggles the details

internal/render/render.go (renderTool) emits an <a class="agent-link" href="...">transcript ↗</a> inside the <summary> of the tool card, and the report template (internal/render/assets/report.html.tmpl) emits the same class inside .turn-role (that one is NOT inside a summary — leave it). Clicking the in-summary link can toggle the <details> instead of / in addition to navigating, and keyboard behavior is unreliable.

Keep the link in the summary (it must stay visible while collapsed) and fix the behavior in internal/render/assets/app.js: add a delegated click handler that stops the event from activating the summary for .agent-link clicks. Read app.js first and follow its existing style. E.g.:

// Links inside <summary> (agent transcript links) must navigate, not toggle
// the surrounding <details>.
document.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
  const link = e.target.closest('a.agent-link');
  if (link) e.stopPropagation();
}, true);

Note: use capture phase (third arg true) so the handler runs before the summary's default activation; verify the file doesn't already have a conflicting delegated handler pattern — integrate cleanly with what's there.

Verify & commit

There is an e2e test that asserts the link markup exists (TestSiteWritesSubagentPagesAndLinks in internal/render/render_e2e_test.go) — it must keep passing; the JS behavior itself has no Go test (fine).

Run: gofmt -l . && go vet ./... && go test ./internal/render/ Expected: gofmt silent, vet clean, package passes.

Commit both changes as ONE commit (message like "fix: keep --include-subagents=false doc hint; make in-summary agent links clickable"), ending with the trailer: Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Then push: git push

Report

Report back with ONLY: status, commit SHA + subject, one-line test summary.

Report

Status: ✓ Both fixes complete, verified, committed, and pushed.

Commit: d8fddef — fix: keep --include-subagents=false doc hint; make in-summary agent links clickable

Test Summary: All 18 tests in ./internal/render/ pass, including TestSiteWritesSubagentPagesAndLinks (link markup assertion), gofmt clean, go vet clean. agentId: a2e10427331a80ab4 (use SendMessage with to: 'a2e10427331a80ab4', summary: '<5-10 word recap>' to continue this agent) subagent_tokens: 29223 tool_uses: 19 duration_ms: 71035

1	copilot round-3 review after 110s
2	=== internal/render/assets/app.js:null (id 3566170871)
3	The capture-phase click handler assumes `e.target` is an Element and calls `.closest(...)` unconditionally. In some browsers the click target can be a Text node, which would throw `TypeError: e.target.closest is not a function` and break the rest of the script (including filtering/toggles). Guard for non-Element targets before calling `closest`.
4	
5

Claude262 tok · ~$0.27
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/assets/app.js
- document.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
- const link = e.target.closest('a.agent-link');
- if (link) e.stopPropagation();
- }, true);
+ document.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
+ if (!(e.target instanceof Element)) return;
+ const link = e.target.closest('a.agent-link');
+ if (link) e.stopPropagation();
+ }, true);

Claude476 tok · ~$0.28

All three reviewer rounds are handled — PR #7 is ready for your merge decision: https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/pull/7

Copilot round summary (every finding was valid, all fixed + replied on-thread):

Round Finding Fix
1 tagContent used LastIndex for all closing tags — a <result> quoting </task-id>/</summary> would corrupt AgentID and break links 9778609 — first-match for simple fields, LastIndex kept only for <result>, plus regression test
2a README flag row lost the --include-subagents=false disable hint d8fddef — hint restored
2b <a> inside <summary> can toggle the card instead of navigating d8fddef — capture-phase stopPropagation handler in app.js, link stays visible while collapsed
3 e.target.closest throws if the click target is a Text node eaa7349 — instanceof Element guard (one-liner, committed directly)

Per your cap I've stopped processing re-reviews after round 3; if Copilot comments again on eaa7349, that's for the merge-time glance. Full suite, vet and gofmt are green at eaa7349. Once you merge, say the word and I'll sync main and we can cut a release.

Done — 79e49cc pushed to PR #7. The demo report at docs/session-report/ is now regenerated from this very session with all features active:

  • Usage card with the per-model table and the new of which subagents: 660k in+out · ~$15.57 (59 sessions) line
  • 10 agent-result cards (the "Agent · …" attribution fix in action)
  • 59 linked subagent transcript pages under docs/session-report/subagents/, reachable from the tool cards and result cards

Nice meta-touch: the demo now documents the session that built the feature it demonstrates — you can click into the transcript of the very subagent that fixed the XSS finding.

Heads-up: the commit will likely trigger one more Copilot re-review pass on the PR; per your three-round cap I won't act on it unless you say otherwise. Ready to merge whenever you are.

go install github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid@v0.3.0

That closes the subagent-sessions milestone end to end: spec → plan → 5 SDD tasks (all reviews clean) → final review that confirmed and fixed a real XSS sink the background scanner flagged → 3 Copilot rounds + demo-report regeneration → merged and released. Local main is synced to 500a5ae and the feature branch is cleaned up.

You

/compact

You

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:

  1. Primary Request and Intent: This session continued development of ccwhid (cc-what-have-i-done, github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done), a Go CLI turning Claude Code JSONL transcripts into self-contained HTML reports. Chronological user requests in this session:

    • (Carried from prior context: confirm v0.2.0 release — done.)
    • Subagent-sessions milestone (three features): (1) The response/result of a subagent run fed back into the main session should ALWAYS render as a proper card attributed to the subagent — user showed two screenshots: in a fabulous-writing report the agent result rendered as a "You" card; in this project's report it appeared only inside the expanded agent-run tool card. (2) Optionally include all subagent session transcripts of a root session into the report — inline vs. separate linked files to be discussed/decided. (3) When subagent sessions are included, their usage cost must be included in the main session's usage summary.
    • Via AskUserQuestion, user chose all recommended options: separate linked HTML pages per subagent; merged per-model table + "of which subagents" line; reuse --include-subagents flag; agent-attributed card at its timeline position.
    • "Approved, please write the spec." then "Approved, please write the implementation plan." then chose "1." (Subagent-Driven Development execution).
    • After completion, chose: "Push and create a pull request. Copilot reviewer is triggered automatically. Wait for its review, read the comments and handle them appropriately. Do at most three reviewer rounds (if there are new findings on automatic re-review)."
    • "There is only one thing missing that we should add in this PR: update the example session report under doc/session-report with a regenerated report showing all features (usage and sub-agent transcripts activated)."
    • Pointed at one final Copilot comment (README backticks) to fix.
    • "PR is merged. Please cut v0.3.0 release." (final request — completed; plain-text confirmation still owed to user).

  2. Key Technical Concepts:

    • Claude Code transcript format: background-agent completions arrive as user records whose content is a string starting with <task-notification> containing <task-id>, <tool-use-id>, <output-file>, <status>, <summary>, <note>, <result> (pseudo-XML with unescaped markdown in result).
    • Modern subagent storage: <projectDir>/<sessionId>/subagents/agent-<agentId>.jsonl + sibling agent-<agentId>.meta.json {agentType, description, toolUseId, spawnDepth}; records inside carry isSidechain: true + agentId but form the file's main chain; toolUseId links to the spawning tool call; spawnDepth can be 2, all files flat in one dir. Observed scale: 256 files / 82 MB for one root session.
    • Three subagent generations: in-file sidechains (already handled), linked agent files (this milestone), flat SDK-spawned sessions (entrypoint: sdk-*, no parent linkage — OUT OF SCOPE, documented limitation).
    • Superpowers SDD workflow: task-brief/review-package scripts at ~/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/6.1.1/skills/subagent-driven-development/scripts/, progress ledger at .superpowers/sdd/progress.md, per-task implementer (haiku for transcription tasks, sonnet for integration) + reviewer (sonnet), final whole-branch review (opus).
    • Memory rule: subagent commits must end with Co-Authored-By trailer identifying THEIR model (e.g. Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>); controller commits use Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>.
    • XSS/escaping invariant: all manually-built HTML in render.go must go through html.EscapeString; goldmark runs with Unsafe=false; only usageView.Headline and turn Body are template.HTML.
    • GoReleaser v2 release pipeline on v* tags; github-native changelog listing PRs; PR was rebase-merged (same commit subjects, new SHAs).
    • GitHub PR review threading: gh api repos/.../pulls/7/comments/<id>/replies; Copilot re-reviews automatically on push.

  3. Files and Code Sections:

    • docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-subagent-sessions-design.md (created, commit f97c0f8) — full design: background/storage findings, decisions table, model/parser/loader/renderer/usage design, out-of-scope, testing.
    • docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-12-subagent-sessions.md (created, commit 608d700) — 5-task TDD plan with complete code per step.
    • internal/model/model.go — added TurnAgentResult TurnKind = "agent-result"; Turn fields AgentID, AgentStatus, AgentSummary string; Session.Agents []AgentSession; type: 
      type AgentSession struct {
    ID          string  // agentId, from file name agent-<ID>.jsonl
    Description string  // from meta.json; falls back to ID
    AgentType   string
    ToolUseID   string
    SpawnDepth  int
    Session     Session
}
    • internal/transcript/parse.go — Options.AgentFile bool (when set, rec.IsSidechain = false after IsMeta check); taskNotification{TaskID, ToolUseID, Status, Summary, Result}; parseTaskNotification(s) (taskNotification, bool) requiring TaskID+Summary; in buildTurn, user records with single text block starting <task-notification> become TurnAgentResult with result body (summary fallback) as sole text block; tagContent after Copilot R1 fix: 
      func tagContent(s, name string) string {
    open, close := "<"+name+">", "</"+name+">"
    i := strings.Index(s, open)
    if i < 0 { return "" }
    rest := s[i+len(open):]
    var j int
    if name == "result" {
        j = strings.LastIndex(rest, close) // tolerate XML-looking body text
    } else {
        j = strings.Index(rest, close)     // first closing tag for simple fields
    }
    if j < 0 { return "" }
    return strings.TrimSpace(rest[:j])
}
    • internal/transcript/agents.go (new) — agentMeta struct; LoadAgentSessions(transcriptPath, opts) ([]model.AgentSession, error): dir = strings.TrimSuffix(transcriptPath, ".jsonl")/subagents, missing dir → (nil, nil), sets opts.AgentFile=true, iterates sorted ReadDir entries matching agent-*.jsonl, unparsable file → stderr warning + skip, meta.json fallback Description=ID.
    • internal/transcript/notification_test.go, agents_test.go (new) — notification parsing tests (happy, empty-result fallback, malformed→user turn, mid-string mention, repeat no-dedup, TestParseNotificationResultQuotingOtherTags for the R1 fix), loader tests (fixture with meta/no-meta/missing-dir/AgentFile-option).
    • internal/redact/redact.go — redactTurn adds t.AgentSummary = r.String(t.AgentSummary); Session() ends with loop redacting s.Agents[i].Description + recursive Session(&s.Agents[i].Session, homeDir).
    • internal/usage/usage.go — Report gains Subagents TokenCounts, SubagentsCost *float64, AgentSessions int; Compute uses subByModel map[string]*TokenCounts + inAgent bool flag inside accumulate (adds to r.Subagents/subByModel when inAgent); after main-turn walk: r.AgentSessions = len(s.Agents), walks agent turns with inAgent=true, computes SubagentsCost from priced subByModel entries. Doc comment updated to mention linked agent sessions.
    • internal/render/render.go — turnView.Status, usageView.SubLine; roleLabel(t model.Turn) (switch on kind; agent-result → agentRoleLabel(t.AgentSummary)); agentRoleLabel extracts quoted name → "Agent · <name>" else "Agent"; SubLine: "of which subagents: " + formatTokens(r.Subagents.InOut()) + " in+out" + optional " · ~" + formatCost(*r.SubagentsCost) + fmt.Sprintf(" (%d sessions)", r.AgentSessions); footnote variants (with agents: "Includes %d linked subagent session(s); server-tool fees are excluded."); pageInfo{Base, PagePrefix, BackHref, Subtitle}; agentLinks{prefix, byToolUse, byAgentID} with newAgentLinks(agents, prefix), forToolUse(id), forAgent(id); renderTurnBody(t, links)/renderTool(tc, links); renderTool emits <a class="agent-link" href="...">transcript ↗</a> in summary (escaped); Site restructured with writePage closure — index gets pageInfo{Base:"", PagePrefix:"subagents/"}, each agent page pageInfo{Base:"../", PagePrefix:"", BackHref:"../index.html", Subtitle: agentType+" · agent-"+ID}; links always resolve against ROOT s.Agents; no subagents dir when no agents. Security fix (92f3646): sidechain loop line now html.EscapeString(roleLabel(st)).
    • internal/render/assets/report.html.tmpl — {{ .Base }}assets/... asset paths; back-link; Subtitle in meta; turn header {{ .RoleLabel }}{{ if .Status }}<span class="agent-status">…{{ end }}{{ if .AgentHref }}<a class="agent-link"…{{ end }}{{ if .Badge }}…; {{ if .SubLine }}<p class="usage-sub">… before footnote.
    • internal/render/assets/styles.css — .turn-agent-result (border-left accent), .agent-status chip, .usage-sub, .agent-link, .back-link.
    • internal/render/assets/app.js — capture-phase delegated handler (post R2+R3 fixes): 
      document.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
  if (!(e.target instanceof Element)) return;
  const link = e.target.closest('a.agent-link');
  if (link) e.stopPropagation();
}, true);
    • internal/render/render_test.go / render_e2e_test.go — agent-card tests, SubLine/footnote tests, TestSidechainAgentResultSummaryIsEscaped (XSS regression), TestSiteWritesSubagentPagesAndLinks (≥2 index links, back-link, ../assets), TestSiteNoAgentsWritesNoSubagentsDir.
    • cmd/ccwhid/run.go — in generate() after ParseFile/warnings, before redaction: 
      if opts.includeSubagents {
    agents, err := transcript.LoadAgentSessions(si.FilePath, transcript.Options{IncludeSubagents: opts.includeSubagents})
    if err != nil { return "", err }
    sess.Agents = agents
}
    • cmd/ccwhid/main.go — flag help: "include subagent work: inline Task sidechains and linked agent-session pages". internal/tui/tui.go — label "Include subagent work".
    • README.md — flag row ends (default true; --include-subagents=false to omit) (backticks per final Copilot nit); sections on agent cards, subagent pages, usage inclusion, SDK-session limitation.
    • docs/session-report/ — regenerated demo (commit 79e49cc): this session's transcript, 59 subagent pages, usage subline "660k in+out · ~$15.57 (59 sessions)", 10 agent-result cards.
    • .superpowers/sdd/progress.md — ledger with all task statuses, review outcomes, backlog minors, Copilot round log.

  4. Errors and fixes:

    • XSS true positive: a background security scan flagged "XSS via manual HTML building in render.go" mid-execution; I deferred to the final whole-branch review which confirmed one real sink — Task 4 changed roleLabel(st.Kind) (safe constants) to roleLabel(st) (can embed transcript-derived AgentSummary) written raw in renderTool's sidechain loop. Fixed 92f3646 with html.EscapeString(roleLabel(st)) + regression test; re-review verified RED/GREEN via throwaway worktree.
    • Copilot R1 (valid): tagContent used LastIndex for ALL closing tags — a <result> quoting literal </task-id>/`` corrupts AgentID/summary. Fixed 9778609: Index for simple fields, LastIndex only for result + TestParseNotificationResultQuotingOtherTags.
    • Copilot R2 (both valid): README dropped the --include-subagents=false hint (restored); <a> inside <summary> toggles the details (fixed with capture-phase stopPropagation in app.js). Both d8fddef.
    • Copilot R3 (valid): e.target.closest throws if target is a Text node → if (!(e.target instanceof Element)) return; guard, eaa7349 (one-liner committed directly by controller with Opus trailer).
    • Final README nit: missing backticks around --include-subagents=false in flag table → 6e8c103.
    • Plan self-review: stray Russian word "должен" in a planned test message — fixed in plan before execution.
    • -o flag error in smoke test: ccwhid has no -o shorthand; used --out.
    • Poll false-positive: first re-review poll matched my own reply as a "review"; re-polled filtering user.login == "copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]".
    • Diverged main after merge: PR #7 was rebase-merged (new SHAs); local main held the 2 docs commits. Verified spec/plan files exist on origin/main (git cat-file -e), then git reset --hard origin/main (tip 500a5ae) and deleted the branch.
    • Model-unavailability transient: one Bash call failed ("claude-opus-4-8[1m] temporarily unavailable"); session was later resumed with "Please continue" and I retried successfully.

  5. Problem Solving:

    • Root-caused the "You" card bug via raw transcript inspection (task-notification user records) and identified the modern subagents/ storage layout + meta.json linkage (toolUseId) enabling clean page linking.
    • Chose separate-pages architecture based on measured worst case (256 files/82 MB).
    • Full SDD execution: 5/5 tasks approved (fix loops never needed at task level), final review 1 Important (the XSS sink) fixed + re-reviewed clean.
    • Smoke-verified against this session's own transcript (57→59 subagent pages, correct links/subline/footnote/back-links).
    • Handled exactly 3 Copilot rounds per user cap; noted that further re-reviews after 79e49cc/6e8c103 are NOT to be processed (cap reached, user only pointed out the one backtick nit which was fixed).
    • Released v0.3.0: tag pushed, GoReleaser run watched to success (only known non-blocking Node.js 20 deprecation warning on goreleaser-action@v6), release verified: not draft/prerelease, 7 assets (cc-what-have-i-done_0.3.0_{darwin,linux,windows}_{amd64,arm64} archives + checksums.txt), changelog: "* Subagent sessions: agent-attributed result cards, linked transcript pages, usage rollup by @saigyo in …/pull/7" + Full Changelog v0.2.0...v0.3.0.
    • Backlog minors recorded in ledger (accepted, not fixed): tagContent LastIndex heuristic for result bodies containing literal </result>; LoadAgentSessions error-branch tests; newAgentLinks rebuilt per page (O(n²), trivial); markdown javascript: link hrefs (pre-existing); agent-page filename from trusted a.ID.

  6. All user messages:

    • "The next milestone will be all about handling of subagents / subagent sessions and comprises multiple features: 1. The response / result of a subagent run which is feeded back into the main session should always be presented as a proper 'card' in the report attributed to the subagent. I already noted that in some reports the response is already presented as a card attributed to me ('You') [screenshot: fabulous-writing] and sometimes in only appears inside the expanded agent run card [screenshot: this project]. 2. The transcripts of all subagent session transcripts of a 'root' session transcript should optionally be included into the report. Whether inline in the same static HTML, or as separate linked files we need to discuss and decide. 3. When the subagent sessions are included into the report, their usage cost summary should be included into the usage summary of the main session."
    • (AskUserQuestion answers): Separate linked pages; Merged per-model + subagent line; Reuse --include-subagents; Agent-attributed card.
    • "Approved, please write the spec."
    • "Approved, please write the implementation plan."
    • "1." (chose Subagent-Driven execution)
    • "Please continue. The session was interrupted due to an exhausted session limit."
    • "Push and create a pull request. Copilot reviewer is triggered automatically. Wait for its review, read the comments and handle them appropriately. Do at most three reviewer rounds (if there are new findings on automatic re-review)."
    • "There is only one thing missing that we should add in this PR: update the example session report under doc/session-report with a regenerated report showing all features (usage and sub-agent transcripts activated)."
    • "Only one very minor finding remains: https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/pull/7#discussion_r3566184317"
    • "PR is merged. Please cut v0.3.0 release."

  7. Pending Tasks:

    • None outstanding. The v0.3.0 release is complete and verified; only the plain-text confirmation to the user remains.

  8. Current Work: Immediately before this summary request, I completed the v0.3.0 release per "PR is merged. Please cut v0.3.0 release.": synced main via git reset --hard origin/main (rebase-merged tip 500a5ae, after verifying the two local docs commits' content exists on origin/main), deleted feat/subagent-sessions, ran gofmt -l . && go vet ./... && go test ./... (all green), created annotated tag v0.3.0 ("v0.3.0: subagent sessions — agent-attributed result cards, linked subagent transcript pages, usage rollup"), pushed it, watched the GoReleaser workflow to successful completion (exit 0; only the recurring non-blocking "Node.js 20 is deprecated" warning on goreleaser-action@v6), and verified the release in the final tool result: {"draft":false,"prerelease":false,"tag":"v0.3.0"}, 7 assets (darwin/linux tar.gz + windows zip for amd64/arm64 + checksums.txt), changelog body: "## What's Changed / ### Other Changes / * Subagent sessions: agent-attributed result cards, linked transcript pages, usage rollup by @saigyo in https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/pull/7" + "Full Changelog: https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/compare/v0.2.0...v0.3.0".

  9. Optional Next Step: Report the successful v0.3.0 release to the user in plain text — release URL https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/releases/tag/v0.3.0, the 7 attached assets, and the changelog correctly listing PR #7 — concluding the explicit request "PR is merged. Please cut v0.3.0 release." No further action beyond that confirmation without new user direction.

You

Next, some usability improvements: 1. The cards for Skill invocation should carry the name of the skill in the title line, 2. The cards for Agents should carry the Agent description in the title line 3. In the expanded cards for Agents, can you render the task description, so that it is more readable (instead of just showing the json snippet, where the prompt can be very long and is hard to read in a single line. 4. In the expanded cards for Agents, the result, which often is properly formatted Markdown, also should be rendered properly. [Image #7]

I'll look at the rendering code to understand how tool cards and agent cards are currently built, then make these four improvements.

Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go"
}
1	package render
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"embed"
6		"fmt"
7		"html"
8		"html/template"
9		"os"
10		"path/filepath"
11		"strings"
12	
13		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
14		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/usage"
15	)
16	
17	//go:embed assets/report.html.tmpl assets/styles.css assets/app.js
18	var assets embed.FS
19	
20	// Options configures a render.
21	type Options struct {
22		Title string
23		Usage bool // render the token-usage & cost section
24	}
25	
26	// pageInfo describes where the page being rendered lives relative to outDir.
27	type pageInfo struct {
28		Base       string // asset prefix: "" on index.html, "../" on agent pages
29		PagePrefix string // href prefix to agent pages: "subagents/" on index, "" on agent pages
30		BackHref   string // back-link to the main report; empty on index
31		Subtitle   string // agent pages: "<agentType> · agent-<ID>"
32	}
33	
34	// agentLinks resolves tool-use ids and agent ids to agent-page hrefs.
35	type agentLinks struct {
36		prefix    string
37		byToolUse map[string]string // tool_use id -> agent id
38		byAgentID map[string]bool
39	}
40	
41	func newAgentLinks(agents []model.AgentSession, prefix string) *agentLinks {
42		l := &agentLinks{prefix: prefix, byToolUse: map[string]string{}, byAgentID: map[string]bool{}}
43		for _, a := range agents {
44			if a.ToolUseID != "" {
45				l.byToolUse[a.ToolUseID] = a.ID
46			}
47			l.byAgentID[a.ID] = true
48		}
49		return l
50	}
51	
52	func (l *agentLinks) forToolUse(id string) string {
53		if aid, ok := l.byToolUse[id]; ok {
54			return l.prefix + "agent-" + aid + ".html"
55		}
56		return ""
57	}
58	
59	func (l *agentLinks) forAgent(id string) string {
60		if l.byAgentID[id] {
61			return l.prefix + "agent-" + id + ".html"
62		}
63		return ""
64	}
65	
66	// Site renders the session into outDir as index.html + assets/, plus one page
67	// per linked agent session under subagents/.
68	func Site(s model.Session, outDir string, opts Options) error {
69		title := opts.Title
70		if title == "" {
71			title = s.DisplayTitle()
72		}
73		tmplSrc, err := assets.ReadFile("assets/report.html.tmpl")
74		if err != nil {
75			return err
76		}
77		tmpl, err := template.New("report").Parse(string(tmplSrc))
78		if err != nil {
79			return err
80		}
81	
82		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(outDir, "assets"), 0o755); err != nil {
83			return err
84		}
85		for _, name := range []string{"styles.css", "app.js"} {
86			b, err := assets.ReadFile("assets/" + name)
87			if err != nil {
88				return err
89			}
90			if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(outDir, "assets", name), b, 0o644); err != nil {
91				return err
92			}
93		}
94	
95		writePage := func(path string, sess model.Session, pageTitle string, page pageInfo) error {
96			links := newAgentLinks(s.Agents, page.PagePrefix)
97			data := buildViewModel(sess, pageTitle, opts, page, links)
98			var buf bytes.Buffer
99			if err := tmpl.Execute(&buf, data); err != nil {
100				return err
101			}
102			return os.WriteFile(path, buf.Bytes(), 0o644)
103		}
104	
105		if err := writePage(filepath.Join(outDir, "index.html"), s, title,
106			pageInfo{Base: "", PagePrefix: "subagents/"}); err != nil {
107			return err
108		}
109		if len(s.Agents) == 0 {
110			return nil
111		}
112		subDir := filepath.Join(outDir, "subagents")
113		if err := os.MkdirAll(subDir, 0o755); err != nil {
114			return err
115		}
116		for _, a := range s.Agents {
117			sub := "agent-" + a.ID
118			if a.AgentType != "" {
119				sub = a.AgentType + " · " + sub
120			}
121			if err := writePage(filepath.Join(subDir, "agent-"+a.ID+".html"), a.Session, a.Description,
122				pageInfo{Base: "../", PagePrefix: "", BackHref: "../index.html", Subtitle: sub}); err != nil {
123				return err
124			}
125		}
126		return nil
127	}
128	
129	// view models -------------------------------------------------------------
130	
131	type viewData struct {
132		Title     string
133		Session   model.Session
134		StartedAt string
135		TurnCount int
136		Prompts   []promptRef
137		Turns     []turnView
138		Usage     *usageView
139		Base      string
140		BackHref  string
141		Subtitle  string
142	}
143	
144	type promptRef struct {
145		Index   int
146		Preview string
147	}
148	
149	type turnView struct {
150		Index      int
151		Kind       string
152		RoleLabel  string
153		Status     string // agent-result status chip, e.g. "completed"
154		SearchText string
155		Body       template.HTML
156		Badge      string // per-turn usage badge, e.g. "12k tok · ~$0.18"
157		AgentHref  string // link to the agent's transcript page, when one exists
158	}
159	
160	type usageView struct {
161		HasAny   bool
162		Headline template.HTML // collapsed one-line summary; safe, built only from our own formatted numbers/words
163		Models   []usageModel  // per-model rows, full token breakdown
164		Total    usageModel    // grand-total row
165		SubLine  string        // "of which subagents: …", empty when no linked agents
166		Footnote string
167	}
168	
169	// usageModel is one row of the per-model table (or the Total row). All fields
170	// are plain formatted strings, auto-escaped by html/template.
171	type usageModel struct {
172		Model      string // model id, or "Total" for the summary row
173		Input      string
174		Output     string
175		CacheRead  string
176		CacheWrite string
177		Cost       string // "$1.23" or "n/a"
178	}
179	
180	func buildViewModel(s model.Session, title string, opts Options, page pageInfo, links *agentLinks) viewData {
181		d := viewData{
182			Title:     title,
183			Session:   s,
184			TurnCount: len(s.Turns),
185			Base:      page.Base,
186			BackHref:  page.BackHref,
187			Subtitle:  page.Subtitle,
188		}
189		if !s.StartedAt.IsZero() {
190			d.StartedAt = s.StartedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04")
191		}
192	
193		var rep usage.Report
194		if opts.Usage {
195			rep = usage.Compute(s)
196			d.Usage = buildUsageView(rep)
197		}
198	
199		for i, t := range s.Turns {
200			plain := turnPlainText(t)
201			if t.Kind == model.TurnUser {
202				d.Prompts = append(d.Prompts, promptRef{Index: i, Preview: preview(plain, 60)})
203			}
204			tv := turnView{
205				Index:      i,
206				Kind:       string(t.Kind),
207				RoleLabel:  roleLabel(t),
208				Status:     t.AgentStatus,
209				SearchText: strings.ToLower(plain),
210				Body:       renderTurnBody(t, links),
211			}
212			if t.Kind == model.TurnAgentResult {
213				tv.AgentHref = links.forAgent(t.AgentID)
214			}
215			if opts.Usage && t.Usage != nil {
216				tv.Badge = turnBadge(*t.Usage, rep.PerTurnCost[i])
217			}
218			d.Turns = append(d.Turns, tv)
219		}
220		return d
221	}
222	
223	// turnBadge formats the per-turn usage badge: "12k tok" plus "· ~$0.18" if priced.
224	func turnBadge(u model.Usage, costUSD *float64) string {
225		b := formatTokens(u.Input+u.Output) + " tok"
226		if costUSD != nil {
227			b += " · ~" + formatCost(*costUSD)
228		}
229		return b
230	}
231	
232	// buildUsageView turns a usage.Report into the template-facing view model.
233	func buildUsageView(r usage.Report) *usageView {
234		v := &usageView{HasAny: r.HasAnyUsage}
235		if !r.HasAnyUsage {
236			v.Headline = template.HTML("Usage · no token-usage data")
237			return v
238		}
239		headline := "Usage · " + formatTokens(r.Total.InOut()) + " in+out"
240		if r.TotalCostUSD != nil {
241			headline += " · ~" + formatCost(*r.TotalCostUSD) + " (est.)"
242		}
243		// html.EscapeString (unlike html/template's auto-escaper) leaves "+" intact
244		// while still escaping "<"/"&" etc.; headline is built solely from our own
245		// formatted numbers and literal words, so this is safe to mark as raw HTML.
246		v.Headline = template.HTML(html.EscapeString(headline))
247	
248		hasUnpriced := false
249		for _, m := range r.ByModel {
250			if m.Tokens == (usage.TokenCounts{}) {
251				continue // drop all-zero rows (e.g. <synthetic> with no tokens)
252			}
253			v.Models = append(v.Models, modelRow(m.Model, m.Tokens, m.CostUSD))
254			if m.CostUSD == nil {
255				hasUnpriced = true
256			}
257		}
258		v.Total = modelRow("Total", r.Total, r.TotalCostUSD)
259	
260		if r.AgentSessions > 0 {
261			sub := "of which subagents: " + formatTokens(r.Subagents.InOut()) + " in+out"
262			if r.SubagentsCost != nil {
263				sub += " · ~" + formatCost(*r.SubagentsCost)
264			}
265			v.SubLine = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%d sessions)", sub, r.AgentSessions)
266		}
267	
268		foot := "Estimated — Anthropic list prices as of " + r.PricesAsOf + "."
269		if r.AgentSessions > 0 {
270			foot += fmt.Sprintf(" Includes %d linked subagent session(s); server-tool fees are excluded.", r.AgentSessions)
271		} else {
272			foot += " Covers this transcript only; sub-agent sessions stored as separate files, and server-tool fees, are excluded."
273		}
274		if hasUnpriced {
275			foot += " Estimated cost excludes unpriced models (shown as n/a); their tokens are still counted."
276		}
277		v.Footnote = foot
278		return v
279	}
280	
281	// modelRow formats one per-model (or Total) table row from a token bucket and
282	// optional cost.
283	func modelRow(name string, t usage.TokenCounts, costUSD *float64) usageModel {
284		row := usageModel{
285			Model:      name,
286			Input:      formatTokens(t.Input),
287			Output:     formatTokens(t.Output),
288			CacheRead:  formatTokens(t.CacheRead),
289			CacheWrite: formatTokens(t.CacheWrite5m + t.CacheWrite1h),
290			Cost:       "n/a",
291		}
292		if costUSD != nil {
293			row.Cost = formatCost(*costUSD)
294		}
295		return row
296	}
297	
298	func roleLabel(t model.Turn) string {
299		switch t.Kind {
300		case model.TurnUser:
301			return "You"
302		case model.TurnAgentResult:
303			return agentRoleLabel(t.AgentSummary)
304		default:
305			return "Claude"
306		}
307	}
308	
309	// agentRoleLabel derives `Agent · <name>` from a notification summary like
310	// `Agent "Implement Task 12" finished`; plain "Agent" when no quoted name.
311	func agentRoleLabel(summary string) string {
312		if i := strings.Index(summary, `"`); i >= 0 {
313			if j := strings.Index(summary[i+1:], `"`); j > 0 {
314				return "Agent · " + summary[i+1:i+1+j]
315			}
316		}
317		return "Agent"
318	}
319	
320	// renderTurnBody renders all blocks of a turn to HTML.
321	func renderTurnBody(t model.Turn, links *agentLinks) template.HTML {
322		var b strings.Builder
323		for _, blk := range t.Blocks {
324			switch blk.Type {
325			case model.BlockText:
326				b.WriteString(string(Markdown(blk.Text)))
327			case model.BlockThinking:
328				b.WriteString(`<details class="thinking"><summary>thinking</summary>`)
329				b.WriteString(string(Markdown(blk.Text)))
330				b.WriteString(`</details>`)
331			case model.BlockToolUse:
332				b.WriteString(renderTool(blk.Tool, links))
333			}
334		}
335		return template.HTML(b.String())
336	}
337	
338	func renderTool(tc *model.ToolCall, links *agentLinks) string {
339		var b strings.Builder
340		b.WriteString(`<details class="tool"><summary class="tool-head">`)
341		b.WriteString(`<span class="tool-name">` + html.EscapeString(tc.Name) + `</span>`)
342		if tc.Summary != "" {
343			b.WriteString(`<span class="tool-summary">` + html.EscapeString(StripANSI(tc.Summary)) + `</span>`)
344		}
345		if href := links.forToolUse(tc.ID); href != "" {
346			b.WriteString(`<a class="agent-link" href="` + html.EscapeString(href) + `">transcript ↗</a>`)
347		}
348		b.WriteString(`</summary><div class="tool-body">`)
349		if tc.Diff != nil {
350			b.WriteString(string(DiffHTML(tc.Diff)))
351		} else if tc.InputJSON != "" {
352			b.WriteString(`<pre class="tool-input">` + html.EscapeString(tc.InputJSON) + `</pre>`)
353		}
354		if tc.Result != nil && tc.Result.Content != "" {
355			cls := "tool-result"
356			if tc.Result.IsError {
357				cls += " tool-result-error"
358			}
359			b.WriteString(`<pre class="` + cls + `">` + html.EscapeString(StripANSI(tc.Result.Content)) + `</pre>`)
360		}
361		for _, sub := range tc.Subagents {
362			b.WriteString(`<details class="subagent"><summary>subagent: ` + html.EscapeString(sub.Description) + `</summary>`)
363			for _, st := range sub.Turns {
364				b.WriteString(`<article class="turn turn-` + string(st.Kind) + `"><div class="turn-role">` + html.EscapeString(roleLabel(st)) + `</div><div class="turn-body">`)
365				b.WriteString(string(renderTurnBody(st, links)))
366				b.WriteString(`</div></article>`)
367			}
368			b.WriteString(`</details>`)
369		}
370		b.WriteString(`</div></details>`)
371		return b.String()
372	}
373	
374	func turnPlainText(t model.Turn) string {
375		var parts []string
376		for _, blk := range t.Blocks {
377			switch blk.Type {
378			case model.BlockText, model.BlockThinking:
379				parts = append(parts, blk.Text)
380			case model.BlockToolUse:
381				parts = append(parts, blk.Tool.Name, blk.Tool.Summary)
382			}
383		}
384		// Strip ANSI so previews and the search index carry clean text.
385		return StripANSI(strings.Join(parts, " "))
386	}
387	
388	func preview(s string, n int) string {
389		s = strings.TrimSpace(strings.ReplaceAll(s, "\n", " "))
390		r := []rune(s)
391		if len(r) <= n {
392			return s
393		}
394		return string(r[:n-1]) + "…"
395	}
396
