Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model/model.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model/model.go"
}
1	// Package model defines the domain types shared across ccwhid: a parsed
2	// Session and the timeline of Turns, Blocks and ToolCalls within it.
3	package model
4	
5	import "time"
6	
7	type TurnKind string
8	
9	const (
10		TurnUser        TurnKind = "user"
11		TurnAssistant   TurnKind = "assistant"
12		TurnAgentResult TurnKind = "agent-result" // background-agent completion fed back into the session
13	)
14	
15	type BlockType string
16	
17	const (
18		BlockText     BlockType = "text"
19		BlockThinking BlockType = "thinking"
20		BlockToolUse  BlockType = "tool_use"
21	)
22	
23	// Session is one fully-parsed Claude Code transcript.
24	type Session struct {
25		ID           string
26		ProjectPath  string // decoded absolute cwd
27		Title        string
28		GitBranch    string
29		Version      string
30		StartedAt    time.Time
31		EndedAt      time.Time
32		Turns        []Turn
33		SkippedLines int            // count of malformed lines skipped while parsing
34		Agents       []AgentSession // linked subagent sessions (subagents/ dir), when loaded
35	}
36	
37	func (s Session) Duration() time.Duration { return s.EndedAt.Sub(s.StartedAt) }
38	
39	func (s Session) DisplayTitle() string {
40		if s.Title != "" {
41			return s.Title
42		}
43		return "Untitled session"
44	}
45	
46	// Turn is a single user or assistant message, holding ordered content blocks.
47	type Turn struct {
48		Kind      TurnKind
49		Timestamp time.Time
50		Blocks    []Block
51		Model     string // assistant model id (empty for user turns / when absent)
52		Usage     *Usage // token usage for this assistant turn; nil when absent
53		// Agent-result fields, set only when Kind == TurnAgentResult.
54		AgentID      string // task/agent id from the notification
55		AgentStatus  string // e.g. "completed"
56		AgentSummary string // e.g. `Agent "Implement Task 12" finished`
57	}
58	
59	// Usage holds the token counts reported for one assistant message. Cache writes
60	// keep the 5-minute / 1-hour split because they are priced differently.
61	type Usage struct {
62		Input        int
63		Output       int
64		CacheRead    int
65		CacheWrite5m int
66		CacheWrite1h int
67	}
68	
69	// Block is one content unit: assistant/user text, a thinking block, or a tool call.
70	type Block struct {
71		Type BlockType
72		Text string    // for BlockText and BlockThinking
73		Tool *ToolCall // for BlockToolUse
74	}
75	
76	// ToolCall is a single tool invocation and its result.
77	type ToolCall struct {
78		ID        string
79		Name      string // e.g. "Bash", "Edit", "Read", "Task"
80		Summary   string // one-line summary for the collapsed card header
81		InputJSON string // pretty-printed input for generic display
82		Result    *ToolResult
83		Diff      *Diff      // set for Edit/Write
84		Subagents []Subagent // set for Task calls with sidechain activity
85	}
86	
87	type ToolResult struct {
88		Content string
89		IsError bool
90	}
91	
92	type Diff struct {
93		Path    string
94		OldText string
95		NewText string
96	}
97	
98	// Subagent is a nested sidechain run attached to a parent Task tool call.
99	type Subagent struct {
100		Description string
101		Turns       []Turn
102	}
103	
104	// AgentSession is a subagent session file linked to a root session
105	// (<projectDir>/<sessionId>/subagents/agent-<ID>.jsonl).
106	type AgentSession struct {
107		ID          string  // agentId, from the file name agent-<ID>.jsonl
108		Description string  // from meta.json; falls back to ID
109		AgentType   string  // from meta.json; may be empty
110		ToolUseID   string  // id of the spawning tool call; may be empty
111		SpawnDepth  int     // from meta.json; informational only
112		Session     Session // the parsed agent transcript
113	}
114
Claude137 tok · ~$0.08
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse.go"
}
1	package transcript
2	
3	import (
4		"bufio"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"errors"
7		"io"
8		"os"
9		"strings"
10	
11		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
12	)
13	
14	// Options controls how a transcript is folded into a Session.
15	type Options struct {
16		IncludeSubagents bool
17		// AgentFile marks the input as an agent's own transcript file: its records
18		// carry isSidechain=true but form the file's main chain, so the flag is
19		// ignored.
20		AgentFile bool
21	}
22	
23	// ParseFile parses a transcript file at path.
24	func ParseFile(path string, opts Options) (model.Session, error) {
25		f, err := os.Open(path)
26		if err != nil {
27			return model.Session{}, err
28		}
29		defer f.Close()
30		s, err := Parse(f, opts)
31		if err != nil {
32			return model.Session{}, err
33		}
34		s.ID = sessionIDFromPath(path)
35		return s, nil
36	}
37	
38	func sessionIDFromPath(path string) string {
39		base := path
40		if i := lastSlash(path); i >= 0 {
41			base = path[i+1:]
42		}
43		if len(base) > len(".jsonl") && base[len(base)-len(".jsonl"):] == ".jsonl" {
44			return base[:len(base)-len(".jsonl")]
45		}
46		return base
47	}
48	
49	func lastSlash(s string) int {
50		for i := len(s) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
51			if s[i] == '/' {
52				return i
53			}
54		}
55		return -1
56	}
57	
58	// Parse reads a transcript stream and folds it into a Session timeline.
59	func Parse(r io.Reader, opts Options) (model.Session, error) {
60		var s model.Session
61		// toolIndex maps a tool_use id to the ToolCall pointer so results and
62		// sidechains can be attached after the fact.
63		toolIndex := map[string]*model.ToolCall{}
64		// seenUsage tracks message ids whose usage has already been counted, so an
65		// assistant message split across multiple records is not counted per-record.
66		seenUsage := map[string]bool{}
67		// lastTask tracks the most recent Task tool call; sidechainOwner is the Task
68		// whose Subagents any subsequent sidechain turns are appended to.
69		var lastTask *model.ToolCall
70		sidechainOwner := lastTask
71	
72		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
73		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 1024*1024), 64*1024*1024)
74		for sc.Scan() {
75			line := sc.Bytes()
76			if len(trimSpace(line)) == 0 {
77				continue
78			}
79			var rec rawRecord
80			if err := json.Unmarshal(line, &rec); err != nil {
81				s.SkippedLines++
82				continue
83			}
84			switch rec.Type {
85			case "ai-title":
86				if rec.AiTitle != "" {
87					s.Title = rec.AiTitle
88				}
89				continue
90			case "user", "assistant":
91				// fallthrough to handling below
92			default:
93				continue // ignore mode/permission/attachment/etc. for the timeline
94			}
95			if rec.IsMeta {
96				continue
97			}
98			if opts.AgentFile {
99				rec.IsSidechain = false
100			}
101	
102			// Capture session-level metadata from the first record that has it.
103			if s.GitBranch == "" && rec.GitBranch != "" {
104				s.GitBranch = rec.GitBranch
105			}
106			if s.Version == "" && rec.Version != "" {
107				s.Version = rec.Version
108			}
109			if s.ProjectPath == "" && rec.Cwd != "" {
110				s.ProjectPath = rec.Cwd
111			}
112			ts := parseTime(rec.Timestamp)
113			if !ts.IsZero() {
114				if s.StartedAt.IsZero() {
115					s.StartedAt = ts
116				}
117				s.EndedAt = ts
118			}
119	
120			_, blocks := decodeMessageContent(rec.Message)
121	
122			// Sidechain (subagent) records.
123			if rec.IsSidechain {
124				if !opts.IncludeSubagents || sidechainOwner == nil {
125					continue
126				}
127				turn := buildTurn(rec, blocks, toolIndex, seenUsage)
128				if turn == nil {
129					continue
130				}
131				sub := &sidechainOwner.Subagents[len(sidechainOwner.Subagents)-1]
132				sub.Turns = append(sub.Turns, *turn)
133				continue
134			}
135	
136			// Main-chain records.
137			turn := buildTurn(rec, blocks, toolIndex, seenUsage)
138			if turn == nil {
139				continue // e.g. a user record that only carried a tool_result
140			}
141			s.Turns = append(s.Turns, *turn)
142	
143			// Seed a subagent slot for each Task tool call and make it the current
144			// sidechain owner. Tool-use ids are already registered in buildTurn.
145			// Note: sidechain records are attributed to the nearest preceding Task
146			// by file order. Sequential Task calls attribute correctly; multiple
147			// Task calls dispatched in parallel from one assistant message cannot be
148			// disambiguated from the raw records and will all attach to the last
149			// one — a known v1 limitation.
150			if opts.IncludeSubagents {
151				for i := range turn.Blocks {
152					tool := turn.Blocks[i].Tool
153					if tool != nil && tool.Name == "Task" {
154						tool.Subagents = append(tool.Subagents, model.Subagent{
155							Description: taskDescription(tool),
156						})
157						lastTask = tool
158						sidechainOwner = lastTask
159					}
160				}
161			}
162		}
163		if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
164			// A single over-long line (bufio.ErrTooLong) is a pathological record,
165			// not a reason to discard the whole report: count it and return what we
166			// parsed so far.
167			if errors.Is(err, bufio.ErrTooLong) {
168				s.SkippedLines++
169				return s, nil
170			}
171			return s, err
172		}
173		return s, nil
174	}
175	
176	// messageMeta converts a record's message model/usage into turn fields, also
177	// returning the message id for deduplication. Only assistant records carry
178	// usage; the aggregate cache_creation figure (with no 5m/1h split) is attributed
179	// to the cheaper 5-minute write.
180	func messageMeta(raw json.RawMessage) (msgID, modelID string, usage *model.Usage) {
181		id, mdl, u := decodeMessageMeta(raw)
182		if u == nil {
183			return id, mdl, nil
184		}
185		w5, w1 := u.CacheCreation.Ephemeral5m, u.CacheCreation.Ephemeral1h
186		if w5 == 0 && w1 == 0 && u.CacheCreationInputTokens > 0 {
187			w5 = u.CacheCreationInputTokens
188		}
189		return id, mdl, &model.Usage{
190			Input:        u.InputTokens,
191			Output:       u.OutputTokens,
192			CacheRead:    u.CacheReadInputTokens,
193			CacheWrite5m: w5,
194			CacheWrite1h: w1,
195		}
196	}
197	
198	// taskNotification is the parsed payload of a <task-notification> user record —
199	// the message a background agent's completion injects into the parent session.
200	type taskNotification struct {
201		TaskID    string
202		ToolUseID string
203		Status    string
204		Summary   string
205		Result    string
206	}
207	
208	// parseTaskNotification extracts fields from a <task-notification> payload with
209	// tolerant string scanning (the payload is pseudo-XML with unescaped markdown
210	// inside <result>). ok is false unless both task-id and summary are present.
211	func parseTaskNotification(s string) (taskNotification, bool) {
212		t := strings.TrimLeft(s, " \t\r\n")
213		if !strings.HasPrefix(t, "<task-notification>") {
214			return taskNotification{}, false
215		}
216		n := taskNotification{
217			TaskID:    tagContent(t, "task-id"),
218			ToolUseID: tagContent(t, "tool-use-id"),
219			Status:    tagContent(t, "status"),
220			Summary:   tagContent(t, "summary"),
221			Result:    tagContent(t, "result"),
222		}
223		if n.TaskID == "" || n.Summary == "" {
224			return taskNotification{}, false
225		}
226		return n, true
227	}
228	
229	// tagContent returns the text between the first <name> and its closing
230	// </name>, trimmed. Simple fields match the first closing tag; <result> keeps
231	// matching the last one so bodies that quote XML-looking text (even a literal
232	// </result>) stay intact.
233	func tagContent(s, name string) string {
234		open, close := "<"+name+">", "</"+name+">"
235		i := strings.Index(s, open)
236		if i < 0 {
237			return ""
238		}
239		rest := s[i+len(open):]
240		var j int
241		if name == "result" {
242			j = strings.LastIndex(rest, close)
243		} else {
244			j = strings.Index(rest, close)
245		}
246		if j < 0 {
247			return ""
248		}
249		return strings.TrimSpace(rest[:j])
250	}
251	
252	// buildTurn converts a record's blocks into a model.Turn. tool_result blocks are
253	// attached to their originating ToolCall via toolIndex and do not themselves
254	// produce turn content. Returns nil if the record yields no displayable blocks.
255	func buildTurn(rec rawRecord, blocks []apiBlock, toolIndex map[string]*model.ToolCall, seenUsage map[string]bool) *model.Turn {
256		turn := &model.Turn{Timestamp: parseTime(rec.Timestamp)}
257		if rec.Type == "user" {
258			turn.Kind = model.TurnUser
259		} else {
260			turn.Kind = model.TurnAssistant
261		}
262		// A background agent's completion arrives as a user record whose content is
263		// a single <task-notification> text payload. Surface it as an agent-result
264		// turn so the report attributes it to the agent, not to the user.
265		if turn.Kind == model.TurnUser && len(blocks) == 1 && blocks[0].Type == "text" {
266			if n, ok := parseTaskNotification(blocks[0].Text); ok {
267				turn.Kind = model.TurnAgentResult
268				turn.AgentID = n.TaskID
269				turn.AgentStatus = n.Status
270				turn.AgentSummary = n.Summary
271				body := n.Result
272				if body == "" {
273					body = n.Summary
274				}
275				turn.Blocks = []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockText, Text: body}}
276				return turn
277			}
278		}
279		for _, b := range blocks {
280			switch b.Type {
281			case "text":
282				if b.Text != "" {
283					turn.Blocks = append(turn.Blocks, model.Block{Type: model.BlockText, Text: b.Text})
284				}
285			case "thinking":
286				if b.Thinking != "" {
287					turn.Blocks = append(turn.Blocks, model.Block{Type: model.BlockThinking, Text: b.Thinking})
288				}
289			case "tool_use":
290				tc := buildToolCall(b)
291				// Register the tool call so a later tool_result (in this turn's
292				// chain OR inside a sidechain) can be matched back to it by id.
293				toolIndex[tc.ID] = tc
294				turn.Blocks = append(turn.Blocks, model.Block{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc})
295			case "tool_result":
296				if tc := toolIndex[b.ToolUseID]; tc != nil {
297					tc.Result = &model.ToolResult{Content: toolResultText(b.Content), IsError: b.IsError}
298				}
299			}
300		}
301		if len(turn.Blocks) == 0 {
302			return nil
303		}
304		if rec.Type == "assistant" {
305			msgID, mdl, u := messageMeta(rec.Message)
306			turn.Model = mdl
307			// One assistant message is written as several records (one per content
308			// block), each repeating the same usage. Count usage on the first record
309			// of a message id only; later records of the same message get nil usage
310			// so totals are not multiplied by the block count.
311			if u != nil && msgID != "" {
312				if seenUsage[msgID] {
313					u = nil
314				} else {
315					seenUsage[msgID] = true
316				}
317			}
318			turn.Usage = u
319		}
320		return turn
321	}
322	
323	func trimSpace(b []byte) []byte {
324		start, end := 0, len(b)
325		for start < end && (b[start] == ' ' || b[start] == '\t' || b[start] == '\r' || b[start] == '\n') {
326			start++
327		}
328		for end > start && (b[end-1] == ' ' || b[end-1] == '\t' || b[end-1] == '\r' || b[end-1] == '\n') {
329			end--
330		}
331		return b[start:end]
332	}
333	
334	// buildToolCall constructs a ToolCall from a tool_use block, deriving a summary
335	// and, for Edit/Write, a Diff.
336	func buildToolCall(b apiBlock) *model.ToolCall {
337		tc := &model.ToolCall{
338			ID:        b.ID,
339			Name:      b.Name,
340			InputJSON: prettyJSON(b.Input),
341		}
342		input := decodeInput(b.Input)
343		tc.Summary = toolSummary(b.Name, input)
344		switch b.Name {
345		case "Edit", "Write":
346			tc.Diff = buildDiff(b.Name, input)
347		}
348		return tc
349	}
350	
351	func decodeInput(raw json.RawMessage) map[string]any {
352		m := map[string]any{}
353		if len(raw) > 0 {
354			_ = json.Unmarshal(raw, &m)
355		}
356		return m
357	}
358	
359	func str(m map[string]any, key string) string {
360		if v, ok := m[key].(string); ok {
361			return v
362		}
363		return ""
364	}
365	
366	// toolSummary produces a compact one-line header for a tool card.
367	func toolSummary(name string, in map[string]any) string {
368		switch name {
369		case "Bash":
370			if c := str(in, "command"); c != "" {
371				return firstLine(c)
372			}
373		case "Read", "Edit", "Write", "NotebookEdit":
374			if p := str(in, "file_path"); p != "" {
375				return p
376			}
377		case "Glob":
378			return str(in, "pattern")
379		case "Grep":
380			return str(in, "pattern")
381		case "Task":
382			return str(in, "description")
383		case "WebFetch":
384			return str(in, "url")
385		case "WebSearch":
386			return str(in, "query")
387		}
388		return ""
389	}
390	
391	func buildDiff(name string, in map[string]any) *model.Diff {
392		d := &model.Diff{Path: str(in, "file_path")}
393		if name == "Write" {
394			d.NewText = str(in, "content")
395			return d
396		}
397		d.OldText = str(in, "old_string")
398		d.NewText = str(in, "new_string")
399		return d
400	}
401	
402	func taskDescription(tc *model.ToolCall) string {
403		if tc.Summary != "" {
404			return tc.Summary
405		}
406		return "subagent"
407	}
408	
409	func firstLine(s string) string {
410		for i := 0; i < len(s); i++ {
411			if s[i] == '\n' {
412				return s[:i]
413			}
414		}
415		return s
416	}
417	
418	func prettyJSON(raw json.RawMessage) string {
419		if len(raw) == 0 {
420			return ""
421		}
422		var v any
423		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &v); err != nil {
424			return string(raw)
425		}
426		out, err := json.MarshalIndent(v, "", "  ")
427		if err != nil {
428			return string(raw)
429		}
430		return string(out)
431	}
432

render.go
---
package render

import (
	"bytes"
	"html"
	"html/template"
	"strings"

	chromahtml "github.com/alecthomas/chroma/v2/formatters/html"
	"github.com/yuin/goldmark"
	highlighting "github.com/yuin/goldmark-highlighting/v2"
	"github.com/yuin/goldmark/extension"

	"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
)

// md renders markdown with GFM plus chroma-based syntax highlighting for fenced
// code blocks. Highlighting uses inline styles (WithClasses(false)) so the
// generated report stays fully self-contained — no external stylesheet.
var md = goldmark.New(goldmark.WithExtensions(
	extension.GFM,
	highlighting.NewHighlighting(
		highlighting.WithStyle("github"),
		highlighting.WithFormatOptions(
			chromahtml.WithClasses(false),
		),
	),
))

// Markdown renders CommonMark+GFM to HTML. goldmark runs with its default
// Unsafe=false setting, so any raw HTML embedded in the transcript text is
// stripped (replaced with an HTML comment) rather than passed through — the
// rendered Body is therefore safe to emit as template.HTML.
func Markdown(src string) template.HTML {
	src = StripANSI(src)
	var buf bytes.Buffer
	if err := md.Convert([]byte(src), &buf); err != nil {
		return template.HTML("<pre>" + html.EscapeString(src) + "</pre>")
	}
	return template.HTML(buf.String())
}

// DiffHTML renders a simple line-based diff of a Diff's old/new text.
func DiffHTML(d *model.Diff) template.HTML {
	var b strings.Builder
	b.WriteString(`<div class="diff">`)
	oldText, newText := StripANSI(d.OldText), StripANSI(d.NewText)
	if oldText != "" {
		for _, line := range strings.Split(oldText, "\n") {
			b.WriteString(`<div class="diff-del">- `)
			b.WriteString(html.EscapeString(line))
			b.WriteString("</div>")
		}
	}
	if newText != "" {
		for _, line := range strings.Split(newText, "\n") {
			b.WriteString(`<div class="diff-add">+ `)
			b.WriteString(html.EscapeString(line))
			b.WriteString("</div>")
		}
	}
	b.WriteString("</div>")
	return template.HTML(b.String())
}

Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model/model.go
- // ToolCall is a single tool invocation and its result.
- type ToolCall struct {
- ID string
- Name string // e.g. "Bash", "Edit", "Read", "Task"
- Summary string // one-line summary for the collapsed card header
- InputJSON string // pretty-printed input for generic display
- Result *ToolResult
- Diff *Diff // set for Edit/Write
- Subagents []Subagent // set for Task calls with sidechain activity
- }
+ // ToolCall is a single tool invocation and its result.
+ type ToolCall struct {
+ ID string
+ Name string // e.g. "Bash", "Edit", "Read", "Task"
+ Summary string // one-line summary for the collapsed card header
+ InputJSON string // pretty-printed input for generic display
+ AgentPrompt string // for Task/Agent calls: the subagent prompt, rendered as markdown
+ Result *ToolResult
+ Diff *Diff // set for Edit/Write
+ Subagents []Subagent // set for Task calls with sidechain activity
+ }
+
+ // IsAgent reports whether this tool call spawns a subagent — the historical
+ // "Task" tool or the modern "Agent" tool.
+ func (t *ToolCall) IsAgent() bool {
+ return t.Name == "Task" || t.Name == "Agent"
+ }

Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go
- b.WriteString(`</summary><div class="tool-body">`)
- if tc.Diff != nil {
- b.WriteString(string(DiffHTML(tc.Diff)))
- } else if tc.InputJSON != "" {
- b.WriteString(`<pre class="tool-input">` + html.EscapeString(tc.InputJSON) + `</pre>`)
- }
- if tc.Result != nil && tc.Result.Content != "" {
- cls := "tool-result"
- if tc.Result.IsError {
- cls += " tool-result-error"
- }

- }
+ b.WriteString(`</summary><div class="tool-body">`)
+ switch {
+ case tc.Diff != nil:
+ b.WriteString(string(DiffHTML(tc.Diff)))
+ case tc.IsAgent() && tc.AgentPrompt != "":
+ // The agent prompt is markdown and often long; render it readably
+ // instead of dumping the raw one-line JSON input.
+ b.WriteString(`<div class="agent-prompt">` + string(Markdown(tc.AgentPrompt)) + `</div>`)
+ case tc.InputJSON != "":
+ b.WriteString(`<pre class="tool-input">` + html.EscapeString(tc.InputJSON) + `</pre>`)
+ }
+ if tc.Result != nil && tc.Result.Content != "" {
+ if tc.IsAgent() {
+ // A subagent's result is markdown too; render it rather than showing
+ // a monospace block.
+ b.WriteString(`<div class="agent-result-body">` + string(Markdown(tc.Result.Content)) + `</div>`)
+ } else {
+ cls := "tool-result"
+ if tc.Result.IsError {
+ cls += " tool-result-error"
+ }
+ b.WriteString(`<pre class="` + cls + `">` + html.EscapeString(StripANSI(tc.Result.Content)) + `</pre>`)
+ }
+ }

internal/transcript/parse_test.go:10:func TestParseDeduplicatesUsagePerMessageID(t *testing.T) {
internal/transcript/parse_test.go:45:func TestParseBasicTimeline(t *testing.T) {
internal/transcript/parse_test.go:87:func TestParseSubagentAttachment(t *testing.T) {
internal/transcript/parse_test.go:108:func TestParseExcludeSubagents(t *testing.T) {
internal/transcript/parse_test.go:119:func TestParseSubagentInnerToolResult(t *testing.T) {
internal/transcript/parse_test.go:144:func TestParseAttachesModelAndUsage(t *testing.T) {
internal/transcript/parse_test.go:177:func TestParseAggregateCacheCreationDefaultsTo5m(t *testing.T) {
internal/transcript/agents_test.go:41:func TestLoadAgentSessions(t *testing.T) {
internal/transcript/agents_test.go:64:func TestLoadAgentSessionsMissingDir(t *testing.T) {
internal/transcript/agents_test.go:76:func TestAgentFileOptionParsesSidechainAsMainChain(t *testing.T) {
internal/render/render_test.go:15:func TestMarkdownRendersHeadingAndCode(t *testing.T) {
internal/render/render_test.go:28:func TestDiffHTMLMarksLines(t *testing.T) {

internal/render/render_test.go:51:func TestRenderStripsANSIFromReport(t *testing.T) {
internal/render/render_test.go:100:func TestPreviewRuneSafe(t *testing.T) {
internal/render/render_test.go:111:func TestSiteRendersUsageWhenEnabled(t *testing.T) {
internal/render/render_test.go:138:func TestSiteDropsZeroTokenModelRow(t *testing.T) {
internal/render/render_test.go:166:func TestSiteOmitsUsageByDefault(t *testing.T) {

209	func TestAgentResultTurnRendersAsAgentCard(t *testing.T) {
210		s := model.Session{Turns: []model.Turn{{
211			Kind:         model.TurnAgentResult,
212			AgentStatus:  "completed",
213			AgentSummary: `Agent "Implement Task 12: Profiles view" finished`,
214			Blocks:       []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockText, Text: "All done."}},
215		}}}
216		d := buildViewModel(s, "t", Options{}, pageInfo{}, newAgentLinks(nil, ""))
217		tv := d.Turns[0]
218		if tv.RoleLabel != "Agent · Implement Task 12: Profiles view" {
219			t.Errorf("RoleLabel = %q", tv.RoleLabel)
220		}
221		if tv.Status != "completed" {
222			t.Errorf("Status = %q", tv.Status)
223		}
224		if tv.Kind != "agent-result" {
225			t.Errorf("Kind = %q", tv.Kind)
226		}
227		if tv.RoleLabel == "You" {
228			t.Error("agent result must never be attributed to the user")
229		}
230	}
231	
232	func TestAgentRoleLabelFallsBackWithoutQuotes(t *testing.T) {
233		if got := agentRoleLabel("something finished"); got != "Agent" {
234			t.Errorf("agentRoleLabel = %q, want Agent", got)
235		}
236		if got := agentRoleLabel(""); got != "Agent" {
237			t.Errorf("agentRoleLabel(empty) = %q, want Agent", got)
238		}
239	}
240	
241	func TestUsageViewSubagentLineAndFootnote(t *testing.T) {
242		c := 5.0
243		r := usage.Report{
244			HasAnyUsage:   true,
245			Total:         usage.TokenCounts{Input: 2_000_000},
246			Subagents:     usage.TokenCounts{Input: 1_000_000},
247			SubagentsCost: &c,
248			AgentSessions: 3,
249			PricesAsOf:    "2026-07",
250		}
251		v := buildUsageView(r)
252		if v.SubLine != "of which subagents: 1.0M in+out · ~$5.00 (3 sessions)" {
253			t.Errorf("SubLine = %q", v.SubLine)
254		}
255		if !strings.Contains(v.Footnote, "Includes 3 linked subagent session(s)") {
256			t.Errorf("footnote = %q", v.Footnote)
257		}
258		if strings.Contains(v.Footnote, "sub-agent sessions stored as separate files") {
259			t.Errorf("old exclusion wording must go when agents are included: %q", v.Footnote)
260		}
261	}
262	
263	func TestUsageViewNoSubagentLineWithoutAgents(t *testing.T) {
264		r := usage.Report{HasAnyUsage: true, Total: usage.TokenCounts{Input: 100}, PricesAsOf: "2026-07"}
265		v := buildUsageView(r)
266		if v.SubLine != "" {
267			t.Errorf("SubLine = %q, want empty", v.SubLine)
268		}

{

}
12:	UUID        string          `json:"uuid"`
13:	ParentUUID  string          `json:"parentUuid"`
25:type apiBlock struct {
29:	ID        string          `json:"id"`
30:	Name      string          `json:"name"`
31:	Input     json.RawMessage `json:"input"`

101:func decodeMessageMeta(raw json.RawMessage) (msgID, modelID string, usage *apiUsage) {
106:		ID    string    `json:"id"`
---
type apiBlock struct {
	Type      string          `json:"type"`
	Text      string          `json:"text"`
	Thinking  string          `json:"thinking"`
	ID        string          `json:"id"`
	Name      string          `json:"name"`
	Input     json.RawMessage `json:"input"`
	ToolUseID string          `json:"tool_use_id"`
	Content   json.RawMessage `json:"content"`
	IsError   bool            `json:"is_error"`
}

{
  "command": "cat \u003e\u003e internal/transcript/parse_test.go \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n\nfunc TestBuildToolCallAgentSummaryAndPrompt(t *testing.T) {\n\tb := apiBlock{\n\t\tType:  \"tool_use\",\n\t\tID:    \"t1\",\n\t\tName:  \"Agent\",\n\t\tInput: json.RawMessage(`{\"description\":\"Implement Task 1\",\"subagent_type\":\"general-purpose\",\"prompt\":\"# Do it\\n\\nWith **care**.\"}`),\n\t}\n\ttc := buildToolCall(b)\n\tif tc.Summary != \"Implement Task 1\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Summary = %q, want the agent description\", tc.Summary)\n\t}\n\tif tc.AgentPrompt != \"# Do it\\n\\nWith **care**.\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"AgentPrompt = %q\", tc.AgentPrompt)\n\t}\n\tif !tc.IsAgent() {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Agent tool should report IsAgent\")\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestBuildToolCallSkillSummaryIsSkillName(t *testing.T) {\n\tb := apiBlock{\n\t\tType:  \"tool_use\",\n\t\tID:    \"t2\",\n\t\tName:  \"Skill\",\n\t\tInput: json.RawMessage(`{\"skill\":\"brainstorming\",\"args\":\"an idea\"}`),\n\t}\n\ttc := buildToolCall(b)\n\tif tc.Summary != \"brainstorming\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Summary = %q, want the skill name\", tc.Summary)\n\t}\n\tif tc.AgentPrompt != \"\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Skill call should not set AgentPrompt, got %q\", tc.AgentPrompt)\n\t}\n}\nEOF\necho done"
}
done

  \"Agent\",\n\t\tSummary:     \"Implement Task 1: scaffold\",\n\t\tAgentPrompt: \"# Do the thing\\n\\nWith **emphasis**.\",\n\t\tInputJSON:   `{\"prompt\":\"# Do the thing\"}`,\n\t\tResult:      \u0026model.ToolResult{Content: \"**Status:** DONE\"},\n\t}\n\tturn := model.Turn{Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc}}}\n\tout := string(renderTurnBody(turn, newAgentLinks(nil, \"\")))\n\n\tif !strings.Contains(out, `class=\"agent-prompt\"`) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected agent-prompt block, got %q\", out)\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(out, \"\u003cstrong\u003eemphasis\u003c/strong\u003e\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"prompt should be rendered as markdown: %q\", out)\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(out, `class=\"agent-result-body\"`) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected agent-result-body block: %q\", out)\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(out, \"\u003cstrong\u003eStatus:\u003c/strong\u003e\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"result should be rendered as markdown: %q\", out)\n\t}\n\tif strings.Contains(out, `class=\"tool-input\"`) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"agent card must not dump raw JSON input: %q\", out)\n\t}\n\tif strings.Contains(out, `class=\"tool-result\"`) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"agent result must not use the monospace tool-result block: %q\", out)\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(out, \"Implement Task 1: scaffold\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"tool header should show the agent description: %q\", out)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestNonAgentToolResultStaysMonospace(t *testing.T) {\n\ttc := \u0026model.ToolCall{\n\t\tName:      \"Bash\",\n\t\tSummary:   \"ls\",\n\t\tInputJSON: `{\"command\":\"ls\"}`,\n\t\tResult:    \u0026model.ToolResult{Content: \"file1\\nfile2\"},\n\t}\n\tturn := model.Turn{Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc}}}\n\tout := string(renderTurnBody(turn, newAgentLinks(nil, \"\")))\n\tif !strings.Contains(out, `class=\"tool-result\"`) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"non-agent result should keep the monospace tool-result block: %q\", out)\n\t}\n\tif strings.Contains(out, `class=\"agent-result-body\"`) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"non-agent result must not be markdown-rendered: %q\", out)\n\t}\n}\nEOF\ngofmt -w internal/render/render_test.go internal/transcript/parse_test.go \u0026\u0026 go test Claude694 tok · ~$0.10

All four usability improvements are implemented, tested, and verified on a real transcript. Everything is green (gofmt/vet clean, full suite passes).

What changed

# Request Change
1 Skill card shows skill name in title toolSummary now returns the skill input for Skill calls → e.g. header reads Skill superpowers:writing-plans
2 Agent card shows description in title toolSummary handles the modern Agent tool (not just Task), returning description → e.g. Agent Implement Task 1: scaffold
3 Expanded agent card renders the prompt readably New ToolCall.AgentPrompt field; renderer renders it as markdown in an .agent-prompt block instead of dumping one-line JSON
4 Expanded agent card renders the result as markdown Agent-tool results now render through Markdown() (in .agent-result-body) instead of a monospace <pre>; non-agent results still stay monospace

Files touched: internal/model/model.go (field + IsAgent() helper), internal/transcript/parse.go (summaries + prompt capture), internal/render/render.go (agent-aware body rendering), internal/render/assets/styles.css (labelled prompt/result blocks), plus new tests in parse_test.go and render_test.go.

Smoke test (this project's own 54-agent SDD session): 54 agent cards each with a markdown prompt + markdown result, 10 skill cards all showing their skill name, and every agent card titled with its description.

Two notes:

  • The scheduled continuation (3f5d77dd, fires once at 17:20) is session-only — it won't fire if this session isn't running then.
  • I haven't committed or regenerated the committed demo under docs/session-report/ — these changes would visibly alter it. Say the word and I'll regenerate the demo and commit (and follow your usual PR/release flow).
You

Please regenerate the demo, commit, then follow usual flow.

Bashgh pr create --base main --head feat/agent-skill-card-rendering \
{
  "command": "gh pr create --base main --head feat/agent-skill-card-rendering \\\n  --title \"Name skill/agent tool cards and render agent bodies as markdown\" \\\n  --body \"$(cat \u003c\u003c'EOF'\nFour report-readability improvements to how tool cards render.\n\n## Changes\n\n1. **Skill cards carry the skill name** in the header (e.g. `Skill  superpowers:writing-plans`) — `toolSummary` returns the `skill` input.\n2. **Agent cards carry the subagent description** in the header (e.g. `Agent  Implement Task 1: scaffold`) — `toolSummary` now also handles the modern `Agent` tool, not just `Task`.\n3. **Expanded agent card renders the prompt as markdown** in an `.agent-prompt` block instead of a one-line JSON dump (new `ToolCall.AgentPrompt` field, populated in `buildToolCall`).\n4. **Expanded agent card renders the result as markdown** in an `.agent-result-body` block; non-agent tool results stay in the monospace `\u003cpre\u003e`.\n\n## Notes\n\n- Agent results contain raw tags like the `\u003cusage\u003e…\u003c/usage\u003e` trailer; goldmark runs `Unsafe=false`, so these are stripped exactly as every other markdown body in the report already is — no new escaping surface.\n- Demo under `docs/session-report/` regenerated: 54 agent cards with markdown prompt+result, 10 named skill cards.\n\n## Tests\n\nNew coverage in `internal/transcript/parse_test.go` (agent/skill summaries + prompt capture) and `internal/render/render_test.go` (agent card renders markdown prompt/result; non-agent results stay monospace). Full suite green; `gofmt`/`go vet` clean.\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\" 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -3"
}

{"body":"## Pull request overview\n\nImproves session report readability by enriching tool card headers for Skill/Agent calls and rendering agent prompts/results as markdown in expanded tool cards, with accompanying styling and tests.\n\n**Changes:**\n- Extend transcript parsing to capture agent prompts and produce better summaries for `Agent`/`Skill` tool calls.\n- Update tool rendering to show agent prompt/result as markdown (while keeping non-agent tool results monospace) and add CSS for the new blocks.\n- Add focused tests for transcript parsing and rendering behavior; regenerate demo report HTML/CSS under `docs/session-report/`.\n\n### Reviewed changes\n\nCopilot reviewed 11 out of 12 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eShow a summary per file\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n| File | Description |\r\n| ---- | ----------- |\r\n| internal/transcript/parse.go | Capture agent prompt and improve tool summaries for `Agent`/`Skill`. |\r\n| internal/transcript/parse_test.go | Add tests for agent summary/prompt capture and skill summary. |\r\n| internal/model/model.go | Add `AgentPrompt` to `ToolCall` and `IsAgent()` helper. |\r\n| internal/render/render.go | Render agent prompt/result as markdown; keep other tools monospace. |\r\n| internal/render/render_test.go | Add coverage for markdown rendering vs monospace results. |\r\n| internal/render/assets/styles.css | Style agent prompt/result blocks. |\r\n| docs/session-report/subagents/agent-af0beb7f8d1b0d24b.html | Regenerated demo output reflecting new agent card rendering. |\r\n| docs/session-report/subagents/agent-ace1d6a2e9d4eca91.html | Regenerated demo output reflecting new agent card rendering. |\r\n| docs/session-report/subagents/agent-a7a0fa1f230b1881e.html | Regenerated demo output reflecting new agent card rendering. |\r\n| docs/session-report/subagents/agent-a4a0f2c1a18950550.html | Regenerated demo output reflecting new agent card rendering. |\r\n| docs/session-report/assets/styles.css | Regenerated demo stylesheet with agent prompt/result styles. |\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n---\n\n💡 \u003ca href=\"/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/new/main?filename=.github/instructions/*.instructions.md\" class=\"Link--inTextBlock\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\"\u003eAdd Copilot custom instructions\u003c/a\u003e for smarter, more guided reviews. \u003ca href=\"https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/customizing-copilot/adding-repository-custom-instructions-for-github-copilot\" class=\"Link--inTextBlock\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\"\u003eLearn how to get started\u003c/a\u003e.","state":"COMMENTED"}

Claude1k tok · ~$0.12

Two distinct findings: (1) agent error results lose the tool-result-error affordance, and (2) the demo HTML embeds absolute ~/... paths. Finding 2 is the important one — it suggests the new AgentPrompt field may be bypassing redaction. Let me check the redaction code:

Claude
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/redact/redact.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/redact/redact.go"
}
1	// Package redact scrubs likely secrets and the user's home path from transcript
2	// text before it is rendered. It is best-effort defense-in-depth, not a
3	// guarantee of complete secret removal.
4	package redact
5	
6	import (
7		"regexp"
8		"strings"
9	
10		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
11	)
12	
13	type rule struct {
14		kind string
15		re   *regexp.Regexp
16	}
17	
18	// Ordered: more specific patterns first.
19	var rules = []rule{
20		{"aws-key", regexp.MustCompile(`\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b`)},
21		{"token", regexp.MustCompile(`\b(?:sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}|ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}|github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{50,}|xox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{10,})\b`)},
22		{"jwt", regexp.MustCompile(`\beyJ[A-Za-z0-9_-]{10,}\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]{10,}\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]{10,}\b`)},
23		{"assignment", regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)\b([A-Z0-9_]*(?:KEY|TOKEN|SECRET|PASSWORD|PASSWD))\s*[=:]\s*['"]?([^\s'"]{6,})`)},
24	}
25	
26	// Redactor applies redaction rules and a home-directory rewrite.
27	type Redactor struct {
28		homeDir string
29	}
30	
31	func New(homeDir string) *Redactor { return &Redactor{homeDir: homeDir} }
32	
33	// String redacts a single string.
34	func (r *Redactor) String(s string) string {
35		for _, ru := range rules {
36			if ru.kind == "assignment" {
37				s = ru.re.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1=[REDACTED:assignment]")
38				continue
39			}
40			s = ru.re.ReplaceAllString(s, "[REDACTED:"+ru.kind+"]")
41		}
42		if r.homeDir != "" {
43			s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, r.homeDir, "~")
44		}
45		return s
46	}
47	
48	// Session redacts every user-visible text field of a Session in place.
49	func Session(s *model.Session, homeDir string) {
50		r := New(homeDir)
51		// Session-level fields are rendered in the report header, so they must be
52		// scrubbed too (ProjectPath in particular carries the home path/username).
53		s.ProjectPath = r.String(s.ProjectPath)
54		s.Title = r.String(s.Title)
55		s.GitBranch = r.String(s.GitBranch)
56		for i := range s.Turns {
57			redactTurn(r, &s.Turns[i])
58		}
59		for i := range s.Agents {
60			s.Agents[i].Description = r.String(s.Agents[i].Description)
61			Session(&s.Agents[i].Session, homeDir)
62		}
63	}
64	
65	func redactTurn(r *Redactor, t *model.Turn) {
66		t.AgentSummary = r.String(t.AgentSummary)
67		for i := range t.Blocks {
68			b := &t.Blocks[i]
69			b.Text = r.String(b.Text)
70			if b.Tool != nil {
71				redactTool(r, b.Tool)
72			}
73		}
74	}
75	
76	func redactTool(r *Redactor, tc *model.ToolCall) {
77		tc.Summary = r.String(tc.Summary)
78		tc.InputJSON = r.String(tc.InputJSON)
79		if tc.Result != nil {
80			tc.Result.Content = r.String(tc.Result.Content)
81		}
82		if tc.Diff != nil {
83			tc.Diff.OldText = r.String(tc.Diff.OldText)
84			tc.Diff.NewText = r.String(tc.Diff.NewText)
85			tc.Diff.Path = r.String(tc.Diff.Path)
86		}
87		for i := range tc.Subagents {
88			tc.Subagents[i].Description = r.String(tc.Subagents[i].Description)
89			for j := range tc.Subagents[i].Turns {
90				redactTurn(r, &tc.Subagents[i].Turns[j])
91			}
92		}
93	}
94
Claude2k tok · ~$0.14

Confirmed: Finding 2 is a real regression — redactTool scrubs InputJSON and Result.Content but not my new AgentPrompt field, so the newly-rendered prompt leaks ~/... (the old demo showed the prompt via the redacted InputJSON, so it was scrubbed before). Finding 1 is also valid — agent error results drop the tool-result-error affordance. Both are worth fixing.

Fix 1 — add AgentPrompt to redaction:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/redact/redact.go
- func redactTool(r *Redactor, tc *model.ToolCall) {
- tc.Summary = r.String(tc.Summary)
- tc.InputJSON = r.String(tc.InputJSON)
+ func redactTool(r *Redactor, tc *model.ToolCall) {
+ tc.Summary = r.String(tc.Summary)
+ tc.InputJSON = r.String(tc.InputJSON)
+ tc.AgentPrompt = r.String(tc.AgentPrompt)

Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go
- if tc.IsAgent() {
- // A subagent's result is markdown too; render it rather than showing
- // a monospace block.
- b.WriteString(`<div class="agent-result-body">` + string(Markdown(tc.Result.Content)) + `</div>`)
- } else {
+ if tc.IsAgent() {
+ // A subagent's result is markdown too; render it rather than showing
+ // a monospace block — but keep the error affordance for failed runs.
+ cls := "agent-result-body"
+ if tc.Result.IsError {
+ cls += " tool-result-error"
+ }
+ b.WriteString(`<div class="` + cls + `">` + string(Markdown(tc.Result.Content)) + `</div>`)
+ } else {

36	func TestRedactSessionWalksBlocks(t *testing.T) {
37		s := &model.Session{Turns: []model.Turn{{
38			Kind: model.TurnAssistant,
39			Blocks: []model.Block{
40				{Type: model.BlockText, Text: "my key [REDACTED:aws-key]"},
41				{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: &model.ToolCall{
42					Name:    "Bash",
43					Summary: "echo [REDACTED:aws-key]",
44					Result:  &model.ToolResult{Content: "[REDACTED:aws-key]"},
45				}},
46			},
47		}}}
48		Session(s, "~")
49		if strings.Contains(s.Turns[0].Blocks[0].Text, "AKIA") {
50			t.Error("block text not redacted")
51		}
52		tool := s.Turns[0].Blocks[1].Tool
53		if strings.Contains(tool.Summary, "AKIA") || strings.Contains(tool.Result.Content, "AKIA") {
54			t.Error("tool fields not redacted")
55		}
56	}

58	func TestRedactSessionLevelFields(t *testing.T) {
59		s := &model.Session{
60			ProjectPath: "~/IdeaProjects/app",
61			Turns: []model.Turn{{
62				Kind: model.TurnAssistant,
63				Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: &model.ToolCall{
64					Name:      "Task",
65					Subagents: []model.Subagent{{Description: "run [REDACTED:aws-key]"}},
66				}}},
67			}},
68		}
69		Session(s, "~")
70		if strings.Contains(s.ProjectPath, "~") {
71			t.Errorf("ProjectPath home not rewritten: %q", s.ProjectPath)
72		}
73		if strings.Contains(s.Turns[0].Blocks[0].Tool.Subagents[0].Description, "AKIA") {
74			t.Error("subagent description not redacted")
75		}
76	}

Round 1 handled. Here's where PR #8 stands:

Copilot round 1 — 2 valid findings, both fixed:

  1. Redaction bypass (important): my new AgentPrompt field was rendered but not scrubbed, leaking ~/... paths into the demo. The prompt used to display via the already-redacted InputJSON, so this was a genuine regression. Fixed in 69e22ab (added AgentPrompt to redactTool), demo regenerated in b9c2e95 — zero /Users/[user]-result-error styling. Fixed in 69e22ab — the class is now applied to the markdown result block.

Both fixes have regression tests; full suite + gofmt + vet are green. I replied to all 5 review threads and pushed.

I'm now polling in the background for Copilot's automatic re-review. I'll process any new findings (up to the 3-round cap) and report back when it's clean or the cap is reached.

PR #8 is clean and ready to merge: https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/pull/8

  • CI: build ✅ SUCCESS
  • Mergeable: CLEAN / MERGEABLE
  • Copilot: round 2 came back with no new comments — both round-1 findings fixed and replied to.

What this PR delivers (4 commits):

  • 852d3b1 — feat: skill cards named, agent cards show description, agent prompt + result rendered as markdown
  • 991adac — demo regenerated with the new rendering
  • 69e22ab — fix: redact AgentPrompt (the review's real find — a redaction bypass I'd introduced) + restore the error affordance on agent results
  • b9c2e95 — demo regenerated with prompts scrubbed (zero /Users/[user]/private/tmp/claude-501/... scratch paths (Copilot flagged these alongside the home paths). Those carry no username and redaction rewrites only the home dir by design, so I left them — but if you'd prefer the demo free of scratch paths too, that's a small follow-up (either extend redaction or regenerate from a transcript without them). Let me know.
You

Hm, ok, this is really not so good, when it comes to security and data protection. Could we integrate some heuristic matching into the data scrubbing/redaction code, that redacts things that look like user paths and user names from paths and file names? (usually patterns like /-[/-][A-Za-z] where the last part "looks like a name"? [Image #8]

Claude14k tok · ~$0.47

You're right — my last fix only handled the exact $HOME string, but the transcript is full of the username in other forms the current redaction never touches: Claude Code's dash-encoded project dirs (-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-…), the ls -l owner column ([user] staff), and any other user's /Users/[user]/ path. Since the demo in this very PR still leaks those, this belongs in PR #8. Let me build the heuristic into the redact package.

Let me rewrite the core of redact.go:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/redact/redact.go
- // Ordered: more specific patterns first.
- var rules = []rule{
- {"aws-key", regexp.MustCompile(`\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b`)},
- {"token", regexp.MustCompile(`\b(?:sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}|ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}|github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{50,}|xox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{10,})\b`)},
- {"jwt", regexp.MustCompile(`\beyJ[A-Za-z0-9_-]{10,}\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]{10,}\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]{10,}\b`)},
- {"assignment", regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)\b([A-Z0-9_]*(?:KEY|TOKEN|SECRET|PASSWORD|PASSWD))\s*[=:]\s*['"]?([^\s'"]{6,})`)},
- }
-
- // Redactor applies redaction rules and a home-directory rewrite.
- type Redactor struct {
- homeDir string
- }
-
- func New(homeDir string) *Redactor { return &Redactor{homeDir: homeDir} }
-
- // String redacts a single string.
- func (r *Redactor) String(s string) string {
- for _, ru := range rules {
- if ru.kind == "assignment" {
- s = ru.re.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1=[REDACTED:assignment]")
- continue
- }
- s = ru.re.ReplaceAllString(s, "[REDACTED:"+ru.kind+"]")
- }
- if r.homeDir != "" {
- s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, r.homeDir, "~")
- }
- return s
- }
+ // Ordered: more specific patterns first.
+ var rules = []rule{
+ {"aws-key", regexp.MustCompile(`\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b`)},
+ {"token", regexp.MustCompile(`\b(?:sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}|ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}|github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{50,}|xox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{10,})\b`)},
+ {"jwt", regexp.MustCompile(`\beyJ[A-Za-z0-9_-]{10,}\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]{10,}\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]{10,}\b`)},
+ {"assignment", regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)\b([A-Z0-9_]*(?:KEY|TOKEN|SECRET|PASSWORD|PASSWD))\s*[=:]\s*['"]?([^\s'"]{6,})`)},
+ }
+
+ // userPathRe matches a home-directory segment in slash, backslash, or Claude
+ // Code's dash-encoded form — "/Users/[user]\Users\[user]// "-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects" — capturing the account name that follows
+ // "Users"/"home". It scrubs account names even in paths outside our own home
+ // (other users, dash-encoded project dirs) that the $HOME rewrite never sees.
+ var userPathRe = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)([/\\-])(Users|home)([/\\-])([^/\\-]+)`)
+
+ // userPlaceholder replaces a scrubbed account name.
+ const userPlaceholder = "[user]"
+
+ // Redactor applies redaction rules, a home-directory rewrite, and account-name
+ // heuristics.
+ type Redactor struct {
+ homeDir string
+ userRe *regexp.Regexp // standalone mentions of our own account name; nil when unknown/unsafe
+ }
+
+ func New(homeDir string) *Redactor {
+ r := &Redactor{homeDir: homeDir}
+ if name := accountName(homeDir); name != "" {
+ r.userRe = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)\b` + regexp.QuoteMeta(name) + `\b`)
+ }
+ return r
+ }
+
+ // accountName extracts the login name from a home-directory path. It returns ""
+ // for very short names or common system accounts, where scrubbing the bare word
+ // would mangle unrelated output (e.g. "root" in log lines).
+ func accountName(homeDir string) string {
+ name := strings.TrimRight(homeDir, `/\`)
+ if i := strings.LastIndexAny(name, `/\`); i >= 0 {
+ name = name[i+1:]
+ }
+ if len(name) < 3 {
+ return ""
+ }
+ switch strings.ToLower(name) {
+ case "root", "home", "user", "users", "admin", "ubuntu", "shared", "guest":
+ return ""
+ }
+ return name
+ }
+
+ // String redacts a single string.
+ func (r *Redactor) String(s string) string {
+ for _, ru := range rules {
+ if ru.kind == "assignment" {
+ s = ru.re.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1=[REDACTED:assignment]")
+ continue
+ }
+ s = ru.re.ReplaceAllString(s, "[REDACTED:"+ru.kind+"]")
+ }
+ // Rewrite our own home directory to ~ first, so the common case stays
+ // readable ("~/foo" rather than "/Users/[user]/foo").
+ if r.homeDir != "" {
+ s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, r.homeDir, "~")
+ }
+ // Scrub account names in any remaining home-style path: dash-encoded project
+ // dirs, other users' home paths, Windows paths.
+ s = userPathRe.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1$2$3"+userPlaceholder)
+ // Scrub standalone mentions of our own account name that carry no path
+ // context — e.g. the owner column of `ls -l` output.
+ if r.userRe != nil {
+ s = r.userRe.ReplaceAllString(s, userPlaceholder)
+ }
+ return s
+ }
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/redact/redact.go has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
Claude1k tok · ~$0.25

Now tests for the new heuristics:

Claude
Bashcat >> internal/redact/redact_test.go <<'EOF'
{
  "command": "cat \u003e\u003e internal/redact/redact_test.go \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n\nfunc TestRedactDashEncodedHomePath(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Claude Code encodes project dirs by replacing \"/\" with \"-\", so the $HOME\n\t// rewrite never sees them.\n\tr := New(\"~\")\n\tgot := r.String(\"~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done/x.jsonl\")\n\tif strings.Contains(got, \"[user]\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"dash-encoded username not scrubbed: %q\", got)\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(got, \"-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected scrubbed encoded segment: %q\", got)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestRedactOtherUsersPath(t *testing.T) {\n\t// A foreign home path (different account) is scrubbed even though it isn't\n\t// our $HOME.\n\tr := New(\"~\")\n\tfor _, in := range []string{\"/Users/[user]/secret\", `C:\\Users\\[user]\\file`, \"/home/[user]/x\"} {\n\t\tgot := r.String(in)\n\t\tfor _, leak := range []string{\"alice\", \"bob\", \"carol\"} {\n\t\t\tif strings.Contains(got, leak) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"foreign account name leaked: %q -\u003e %q\", in, got)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestRedactOwnerColumn(t *testing.T) {\n\t// `ls -l` owner column carries the username with no path context.\n\tr := New(\"~\")\n\tgot := r.String(\"drwx------@ 8 [user] staff 256 baufinanzierung\")\n\tif strings.Contains(got, \"[user]\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"bare username in owner column not scrubbed: %q\", got)\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(got, \"[user] staff\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected scrubbed owner column: %q\", got)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestRedactKeepsHomeTilde(t *testing.T) {\n\t// The friendly ~ rewrite for our own home must survive.\n\tr := New(\"~\")\n\tif got := r.String(\"~/IdeaProjects/app\"); got != \"~/IdeaProjects/app\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"home tilde rewrite broken: %q\", got)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestAccountNameSkipsSystemAndShortNames(t *testing.T) {\n\tfor _, h := range []string{\"/root\", \"/home/[user]\", \"/var/empty\", \"\"} {\n\t\tif got := accountName(h); got != \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"accountName(%q) = %q, want empty (system/short)\", h, got)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif got := accountName(\"~\"); got != \"[user]\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"accountName = %q, want [user]\", got)\n\t}\n}\nEOF\ngo test ./internal/redact/ 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -8"
}

Claude3k tok · ~$0.20

All the real macOS-path leaks are scrubbed. Two residuals remain — let me look at them:

  1. [user] appears only inside a Go module path github.com/[user]/ccwhid (from the original project scaffolding) — that's a URL/module path, not a home path, so the heuristic (correctly) doesn't touch it. Scrubbing github.com/<name>/ generically would also destroy every legitimate github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done reference, so I won't auto-scrub it.
  2. C:\Users\[user]-backslash** Windows paths. Worth hardening since real Windows transcripts JSON-escape as \`.

Let me make the separator groups tolerate runs:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/redact/redact.go
- var userPathRe = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)([/\\-])(Users|home)([/\\-])([^/\\-]+)`)
+ // The separator runs use "+" so JSON-escaped Windows paths (`\\Users\\[user]// match as well as single-separator forms.
+ var userPathRe = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)([/\\-]+)(Users|home)([/\\-]+)([^/\\-]+)`)

Claude568 tok · ~$0.15

Let me update the README redaction section to document the new heuristic, then commit:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md
- By default, ccwhid scrubs common secret shapes (AWS keys, API tokens, JWTs,
- `KEY=[REDACTED:assignment] assignments) and rewrites your home directory to `~`.
- This is best-effort defense-in-depth — **review generated reports before
- committing them.** Disable with `--no-redact`.
+ By default, ccwhid scrubs common secret shapes (AWS keys, API tokens, JWTs,
+ `KEY=[REDACTED:assignment] assignments) and rewrites your home directory to `~`.
+ It also scrubs account names from home-directory paths in any form — plain
+ (`/Users/[user]/home/[user]\Users\[user]-encoded project dirs (`-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-…`) — plus standalone
+ mentions of your own login name (e.g. the owner column of `ls -l` output),
+ replacing them with `[user]`. This is best-effort defense-in-depth — **review
+ generated reports before committing them** (a real name embedded in a URL or
+ module path, for instance, is out of scope). Disable with `--no-redact`.
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
Claude502 tok · ~$0.11
Bashgit add internal/redact/ README.md && \
{
  "command": "git add internal/redact/ README.md \u0026\u0026 \\\ngit commit -q -m \"feat: scrub account names from paths and owner columns\n\nThe \\$HOME rewrite only caught the literal home path, so the username still\nleaked in other forms: Claude Code's dash-encoded project dirs\n(-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-...), other users' /Users//[user]\npaths, and the owner column of ls -l output.\n\nAdd two heuristics to the redactor:\n- userPathRe matches a home segment in slash, backslash, or dash-encoded\n  form and replaces the account-name segment with [user] — covering paths\n  outside our own \\$HOME that the home rewrite never sees.\n- a derived account name (basename of \\$HOME, guarded against short/system\n  names like root/nobody) scrubs standalone mentions that carry no path\n  context.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) \u003cnoreply@anthropic.com\u003e\" \u0026\u0026 \\\ngit add docs/session-report \u0026\u0026 \\\ngit commit -q -m \"docs: regenerate demo report with account-name scrubbing\n\nLocal machine paths and the login name are now scrubbed to [user] in all\nforms (dash-encoded project dirs, ls -l owner columns).\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) \u003cnoreply@anthropic.com\u003e\" \u0026\u0026 \\\ngit log --oneline -6"
}
ec6762c docs: regenerate demo report with account-name scrubbing
c56280e feat: scrub account names from paths and owner columns
b9c2e95 docs: regenerate demo report with agent prompts redacted
69e22ab fix: redact agent prompt and keep error affordance on agent results
991adac docs: regenerate demo report with new agent/skill card rendering
852d3b1 feat: name skill/agent tool cards and render agent bodies as markdown
Claude1k tok · ~$0.13

The PR now covers more than its title. Let me update it and push:

Claude
Bashgit push 2>&1 | tail -2 && \
{
  "command": "git push 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -2 \u0026\u0026 \\\ngh pr edit 8 --title \"Improve agent/skill card rendering and harden path redaction\" --body \"$(cat \u003c\u003c'EOF'\nStarted as report-readability improvements for tool cards; the review surfaced a redaction gap that this PR now also closes.\n\n## Rendering (readability)\n\n1. **Skill cards** carry the skill name in the header (`Skill  superpowers:writing-plans`).\n2. **Agent cards** carry the subagent description in the header (`Agent  Implement Task 1: scaffold`) — `toolSummary` now handles the modern `Agent` tool, not just `Task`.\n3. **Expanded agent card renders the prompt as markdown** (`.agent-prompt`) instead of a one-line JSON dump (new `ToolCall.AgentPrompt`).\n4. **Expanded agent card renders the result as markdown** (`.agent-result-body`); non-agent results stay monospace, and error results keep the `tool-result-error` affordance.\n\n## Redaction (data protection)\n\nThe `$HOME` rewrite only caught the literal home path, so the login name still leaked in other forms — this PR scrubs account names to `[user]` across:\n\n- Claude Code's **dash-encoded** project dirs (`-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-…`)\n- other users' and **Windows** home paths (`/home/[user]\\Users\\[user]-backslash `\\\\Users\\\\[user]\n- standalone mentions of your **own login name** (e.g. the `ls -l` owner column)\n\nGuarded against short/system names (`root`, `nobody`, …) to avoid mangling unrelated output.\n\n**Out of scope / known limitation:** a real name embedded in a URL or Go module path (`github.com/\u003cname\u003e/…`) is not scrubbed — a generic rule there would also destroy legitimate `github.com/saigyo/…` references.\n\n## Tests\n\nNew coverage in `internal/redact/redact_test.go` (dash-encoded, foreign-user, Windows, owner-column, `~` preservation, system-name guard), `internal/transcript/parse_test.go` (agent/skill summaries + prompt capture, `AgentPrompt` redaction), and `internal/render/render_test.go` (markdown prompt/result, error affordance, non-agent monospace). Demo under `docs/session-report/` regenerated — free of local machine paths and login-name leaks. Full suite + `gofmt`/`go vet` green.\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\" 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -2"
}

Redaction hardening is committed, pushed, and the PR is updated. Here's the state of PR #8 while Copilot re-reviews:

New in this round — account-name scrubbing (c56280e, tests + README, demo regen ec6762c):

  • Scrubs the login name to [user] in every form the $HOME rewrite missed: dash-encoded project dirs (-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-…), other users' and Windows home paths, and the bare login name in ls -l owner columns.
  • Guarded against short/system names (root, nobody, empty, …) so it won't mangle unrelated output.

The path heuristic can't touch agithub.com//URL without also destroying the legitimategithub.com/saigyo/…references. It's worth noting this is **already public** in the repo — every commit is authored[user] <acm.ackermann@…>— so it's not new exposure.   • CI build ✅ · mergeable CLEAN · Copilot round 3 came back with no new comments.

Full arc of the PR (6 commits):

  1. 852d3b1 feat — named skill/agent cards + markdown agent bodies

That wraps this milestone end to end: the four card-rendering improvements (named skill/agent cards, markdown prompt + result) plus the redaction hardening that closes the username-in-paths leak — merged as PR #8 (3 clean Copilot rounds) and shipped as v0.3.1. Local main is synced to dda7ee0.

You

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:

  1. Primary Request and Intent: Continued development of ccwhid (cc-what-have-i-done, github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done), a Go CLI that turns Claude Code JSONL transcripts into self-contained HTML reports. Four chronological user requests in this session:

    • (A) Four usability/rendering improvements: (1) Skill invocation cards must carry the skill name in the title line; (2) Agent cards must carry the Agent description in the title line; (3) expanded Agent cards must render the task description (prompt) readably as markdown instead of a one-line JSON snippet; (4) expanded Agent cards must render the result (often markdown) properly. A screenshot showed the raw-JSON prompt and raw-text result problem.
    • (B) Mid-turn: "set a one-shot scheduled continuation at 17:20."
    • (C) "Please regenerate the demo, commit, then follow usual flow." (usual flow = branch, commit, push, PR, wait for automatic Copilot review, handle findings, up to 3 reviewer rounds).
    • (D) Data-protection redaction heuristic: After seeing username leaks in the committed demo, the user requested: "Could we integrate some heuristic matching into the data scrubbing/redaction code, that redacts things that look like user paths and user names from paths and file names? (usually patterns like /-[/-][A-Za-z] where the last part 'looks like a name'?" — screenshot showed dash-encoded -Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-... paths and ls -l owner columns ([user] staff).
    • (E) "PR is merged, please cut a v0.3.1 release."

  2. Key Technical Concepts:

    • Claude Code transcript tool cards: renderTool() in render.go builds <details class="tool"> with tool-name + tool-summary header; the modern subagent tool is named "Agent" (historical: "Task"); Skill invocations use tool name "Skill" with a skill input param.
    • toolSummary(name, in) in parse.go derives the collapsed-card header summary per tool type.
    • Markdown(src) (render/markdown.go) runs goldmark with Unsafe=false, so raw HTML (e.g. <usage>...</usage> trailers in agent results) is stripped and replaced with <!-- raw HTML omitted --> — consistent with all other markdown text bodies in the report.
    • Redaction (internal/redact): best-effort defense-in-depth; scrubs secret shapes + rewrites $HOME→~; must run through every user-visible field. XSS invariant: manually-built HTML must go through html.EscapeString; only Markdown output and specific fields are template.HTML.
    • Claude Code encodes project dirs by replacing / with - (e.g. ~/IdeaProjects/x → -Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-x), which the plain $HOME string-replace never matches.
    • GoReleaser v2 pipeline on v* tags; produces 7 assets (darwin/linux tar.gz + windows zip × amd64/arm64 + checksums.txt); recurring non-blocking "Node.js 20 deprecated" warning on goreleaser-action@v6.
    • CronCreate: session-only in-memory one-shot scheduling; cron in local time; recurring: false for one-shot.
    • Git commit trailer used: Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> (per system prompt git instructions). PRs rebase-merged (new SHAs on main).
    • Copilot re-reviews automatically on each push; poll via gh api repos/.../pulls/8/reviews filtering user.login=="copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]"; comments via .../pulls/8/comments filtering user.login=="Copilot"; replies via .../pulls/8/comments/{id}/replies.

  3. Files and Code Sections:

    • internal/model/model.go — Added AgentPrompt string field to ToolCall and an IsAgent() method: 
      // ToolCall is a single tool invocation and its result.
type ToolCall struct {
    ID          string
    Name        string
    Summary     string
    InputJSON   string
    AgentPrompt string // for Task/Agent calls: the subagent prompt, rendered as markdown
    Result      *ToolResult
    Diff        *Diff
    Subagents   []Subagent
}
func (t *ToolCall) IsAgent() bool { return t.Name == "Task" || t.Name == "Agent" }
    • internal/transcript/parse.go — toolSummary extended: case "Task", "Agent": return str(in, "description") and case "Skill": return str(in, "skill"). In buildToolCall, after diff handling: if tc.IsAgent() { tc.AgentPrompt = str(input, "prompt") }.
    • internal/render/render.go — renderTool body rewritten to a switch: 
      switch {
case tc.Diff != nil:
    b.WriteString(string(DiffHTML(tc.Diff)))
case tc.IsAgent() && tc.AgentPrompt != "":
    b.WriteString(`<div class="agent-prompt">` + string(Markdown(tc.AgentPrompt)) + `</div>`)
case tc.InputJSON != "":
    b.WriteString(`<pre class="tool-input">` + html.EscapeString(tc.InputJSON) + `</pre>`)
}
      And the result branch: if tc.IsAgent(), render <div class="agent-result-body"> (plus tool-result-error class when tc.Result.IsError) via Markdown(tc.Result.Content); else the original <pre> monospace block.
    • internal/render/assets/styles.css — Added after .tool-result-error: 
      .agent-prompt { border-left: 3px solid var(--accent-soft); padding: 0 .8rem; margin: .2rem 0 .8rem; font-size: .9rem; }
.agent-prompt::before, .agent-result-body::before { content: "prompt"; display: block; color: var(--muted); font-size: .7rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .05em; margin-bottom: .2rem; }
.agent-result-body { font-size: .9rem; }
.agent-result-body::before { content: "result"; }
    • internal/redact/redact.go — The most heavily modified file. Added AgentPrompt to redactTool (fixing the round-1 leak). Then added the account-name heuristic: 
      // The separator runs use "+" so JSON-escaped Windows paths (`\\Users\\[user]/\\-]+)(Users|home)([/\\-]+)([^/\\-]+)`)
const userPlaceholder = "[user]"

type Redactor struct {
    homeDir string
    userRe  *regexp.Regexp // standalone mentions of our own account name; nil when unknown/unsafe
}
func New(homeDir string) *Redactor {
    r := &Redactor{homeDir: homeDir}
    if name := accountName(homeDir); name != "" {
        r.userRe = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)\b` + regexp.QuoteMeta(name) + `\b`)
    }
    return r
}
func accountName(homeDir string) string {
    name := strings.TrimRight(homeDir, `/\`)
    if i := strings.LastIndexAny(name, `/\`); i >= 0 { name = name[i+1:] }
    if len(name) < 3 { return "" }
    switch strings.ToLower(name) {
    case "root", "home", "user", "users", "admin", "ubuntu", "shared", "guest", "empty", "nobody", "daemon":
        return ""
    }
    return name
}
      In String(), ordering after secret rules: homeDir→~ first, then s = userPathRe.ReplaceAllString(s, "$1$2$3"+userPlaceholder), then bare userRe→[user].
    • internal/redact/redact_test.go — Added TestRedactAgentPrompt, TestRedactDashEncodedHomePath, TestRedactOtherUsersPath, TestRedactOwnerColumn, TestRedactKeepsHomeTilde, TestAccountNameSkipsSystemAndShortNames, TestRedactDoubledBackslashWindowsPath.
    • internal/render/render_test.go — Added TestAgentToolCardRendersPromptAndResultAsMarkdown, TestNonAgentToolResultStaysMonospace, TestAgentErrorResultKeepsErrorAffordance.
    • internal/transcript/parse_test.go — Added encoding/json import; TestBuildToolCallAgentSummaryAndPrompt, TestBuildToolCallSkillSummaryIsSkillName.
    • README.md — Updated Redaction section to document account-name scrubbing across plain/Windows/dash-encoded path forms + owner columns, noting a real name in a URL/module path is out of scope.
    • docs/session-report/ — Regenerated 4 times (from session 41f7e8f4 with --include-subagents --usage --force); final version free of local machine paths and login-name leaks (0 bare [user], 0 unscrubbed -Users-[user]- **Round-1 Copilot finding (IMPORTANT, valid):** New AgentPromptfield was rendered but not passed throughredactTool, so home paths leaked (the prompt was previously shown via the already-redacted InputJSON). Fixed by adding tc.AgentPrompt = r.String(tc.AgentPrompt)toredactTool` + regression test.
    • Round-1 Copilot finding (minor, valid): Agent error results dropped the tool-result-error affordance. Fixed by appending tool-result-error to the agent-result-body class when tc.Result.IsError + test.
    • Test failure accountName("/var/empty") returned "empty": My own test asserted it should be empty. Fixed the code (not the test) by adding "empty"/"nobody"/"daemon" to the system-name denylist.
    • **Doubled-backslash Windows path (C:\\Users\\[user]-separator regex left bob. Hardened by changing separator groups from [/\-]to[/\-]+` + test.
    • User feedback (data protection): The user explicitly said the leaked username in paths was "really not so good" and requested a general heuristic — this drove the entire redaction-hardening work in request (D). Preserved constraint: redaction is defense-in-depth and reports must be reviewed before committing.

  4. Problem Solving:

    • Root-caused the four rendering requests to renderTool/toolSummary and identified "Agent" vs "Task" naming.
    • Identified the redaction bypass as a genuine regression introduced by the PR's new field.
    • Designed a layered redaction heuristic: (1) $HOME→~ (keeps friendly output), (2) userPathRe for path-form usernames including dash-encoded/Windows/foreign-user, (3) derived bare-username scrub for owner columns — guarded against short/system names to avoid over-redaction.
    • Determined [user] (real name in Go module path github.com/[user]/ccwhid) is out of scope (generic github scrubbing would destroy legitimate github.com/saigyo/... refs) and already public via commit authorship — flagged, not auto-scrubbed. alice/bob/carol residuals are fake names from the assistant's own test source (harmless).
    • Handled exactly 3 Copilot rounds (round 1: 2 findings fixed; rounds 2 & 3: clean) per the user's cap.

  5. All user messages:

    • "Next, some usability improvements: 1. The cards for Skill invocation should carry the name of the skill in the title line, 2. The cards for Agents should carry the Agent description in the title line 3. In the expanded cards for Agents, can you render the task description, so that it is more readable (instead of just showing the json snippet, where the prompt can be very long and is hard to read in a single line. 4. In the expanded cards for Agents, the result, which often is properly formatted Markdown, also should be rendered properly. [Image #7]" (with screenshot)
    • "set a one-shot scheduled continuation at 17:20." (delivered mid-turn as a system-surfaced user message)
    • "Please regenerate the demo, commit, then follow usual flow."
    • "Hm, ok, this is really not so good, when it comes to security and data protection. Could we integrate some heuristic matching into the data scrubbing/redaction code, that redacts things that look like user paths and user names from paths and file names? (usually patterns like /-[/-][A-Za-z] where the last part "looks like a name"? [Image #8]" (with screenshot)
    • "PR is merged, please cut a v0.3.1 release."
    • (Note: the "Scheduled continuation (17:20)..." message was the fired CronCreate job prompt authored by the assistant, NOT a genuine user request; and /context, /compact, /model opus were local command outputs, not user messages.)

  6. Pending Tasks:

    • None outstanding. The v0.3.1 release is complete and verified. The only owed action is the plain-text confirmation to the user.
    • Optional/offered (NOT yet requested to execute): scrub the committer name ([user]) from the demo via a git-user.name-derived redaction term — offered as a follow-up, awaiting user decision.

  7. Current Work: Immediately before this summary request, the assistant completed the v0.3.1 release per "PR is merged, please cut a v0.3.1 release." Steps performed: git checkout main + git fetch + git reset --hard origin/main (tip dda7ee0, rebase-merged 6 commits: c1ab555/22e5893/f46da77/9049fe7/b703630/dda7ee0); verified gofmt -l . && go vet ./... && go test ./... all green; created annotated tag v0.3.1 with message "v0.3.1: readable agent/skill cards + hardened path redaction"; pushed the tag; watched GoReleaser workflow run 29199748058 to success (exit 0, only the recurring non-blocking Node.js 20 deprecation warning); and verified the release with the final tool result: {"tag":"v0.3.1","draft":false,"prerelease":false,"url":"https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/releases/tag/v0.3.1","assets":["cc-what-have-i-done_0.3.1_darwin_amd64.tar.gz","...darwin_arm64.tar.gz","...linux_amd64.tar.gz","...linux_arm64.tar.gz","...windows_amd64.zip","...windows_arm64.zip","checksums.txt"]}.

  8.
