Claude
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go"
}
1	package render
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"embed"
6		"fmt"
7		"html"
8		"html/template"
9		"os"
10		"path/filepath"
11		"strings"
12	
13		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
14		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/usage"
15	)
16	
17	//go:embed assets/report.html.tmpl assets/styles.css assets/app.js
18	var assets embed.FS
19	
20	// Options configures a render.
21	type Options struct {
22		Title string
23		Usage bool // render the token-usage & cost section
24	}
25	
26	// pageInfo describes where the page being rendered lives relative to outDir.
27	type pageInfo struct {
28		Base       string // asset prefix: "" on index.html, "../" on agent pages
29		PagePrefix string // href prefix to agent pages: "subagents/" on index, "" on agent pages
30		BackHref   string // back-link to the main report; empty on index
31		Subtitle   string // agent pages: "<agentType> · agent-<ID>"
32	}
33	
34	// agentLinks resolves tool-use ids and agent ids to agent-page hrefs.
35	type agentLinks struct {
36		prefix    string
37		byToolUse map[string]string // tool_use id -> agent id
38		byAgentID map[string]bool
39	}
40	
41	func newAgentLinks(agents []model.AgentSession, prefix string) *agentLinks {
42		l := &agentLinks{prefix: prefix, byToolUse: map[string]string{}, byAgentID: map[string]bool{}}
43		for _, a := range agents {
44			if a.ToolUseID != "" {
45				l.byToolUse[a.ToolUseID] = a.ID
46			}
47			l.byAgentID[a.ID] = true
48		}
49		return l
50	}
51	
52	func (l *agentLinks) forToolUse(id string) string {
53		if aid, ok := l.byToolUse[id]; ok {
54			return l.prefix + "agent-" + aid + ".html"
55		}
56		return ""
57	}
58	
59	func (l *agentLinks) forAgent(id string) string {
60		if l.byAgentID[id] {
61			return l.prefix + "agent-" + id + ".html"
62		}
63		return ""
64	}
65	
66	// Site renders the session into outDir as index.html + assets/, plus one page
67	// per linked agent session under subagents/.
68	func Site(s model.Session, outDir string, opts Options) error {
69		title := opts.Title
70		if title == "" {
71			title = s.DisplayTitle()
72		}
73		tmplSrc, err := assets.ReadFile("assets/report.html.tmpl")
74		if err != nil {
75			return err
76		}
77		tmpl, err := template.New("report").Parse(string(tmplSrc))
78		if err != nil {
79			return err
80		}
81	
82		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(outDir, "assets"), 0o755); err != nil {
83			return err
84		}
85		for _, name := range []string{"styles.css", "app.js"} {
86			b, err := assets.ReadFile("assets/" + name)
87			if err != nil {
88				return err
89			}
90			if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(outDir, "assets", name), b, 0o644); err != nil {
91				return err
92			}
93		}
94	
95		writePage := func(path string, sess model.Session, pageTitle string, page pageInfo) error {
96			links := newAgentLinks(s.Agents, page.PagePrefix)
97			data := buildViewModel(sess, pageTitle, opts, page, links)
98			var buf bytes.Buffer
99			if err := tmpl.Execute(&buf, data); err != nil {
100				return err
101			}
102			return os.WriteFile(path, buf.Bytes(), 0o644)
103		}
104	
105		if err := writePage(filepath.Join(outDir, "index.html"), s, title,
106			pageInfo{Base: "", PagePrefix: "subagents/"}); err != nil {
107			return err
108		}
109		if len(s.Agents) == 0 {
110			return nil
111		}
112		subDir := filepath.Join(outDir, "subagents")
113		if err := os.MkdirAll(subDir, 0o755); err != nil {
114			return err
115		}
116		for _, a := range s.Agents {
117			sub := "agent-" + a.ID
118			if a.AgentType != "" {
119				sub = a.AgentType + " · " + sub
120			}
121			if err := writePage(filepath.Join(subDir, "agent-"+a.ID+".html"), a.Session, a.Description,
122				pageInfo{Base: "../", PagePrefix: "", BackHref: "../index.html", Subtitle: sub}); err != nil {
123				return err
124			}
125		}
126		return nil
127	}
128	
129	// view models -------------------------------------------------------------
130	
131	type viewData struct {
132		Title     string
133		Session   model.Session
134		StartedAt string
135		TurnCount int
136		Prompts   []promptRef
137		Turns     []turnView
138		Usage     *usageView
139		Base      string
140		BackHref  string
141		Subtitle  string
142	}
143	
144	type promptRef struct {
145		Index   int
146		Preview string
147	}
148	
149	type turnView struct {
150		Index      int
151		Kind       string
152		RoleLabel  string
153		Status     string // agent-result status chip, e.g. "completed"
154		SearchText string
155		Body       template.HTML
156		Badge      string // per-turn usage badge, e.g. "12k tok · ~$0.18"
157		AgentHref  string // link to the agent's transcript page, when one exists
158	}
159	
160	type usageView struct {
161		HasAny   bool
162		Headline template.HTML // collapsed one-line summary; safe, built only from our own formatted numbers/words
163		Models   []usageModel  // per-model rows, full token breakdown
164		Total    usageModel    // grand-total row
165		SubLine  string        // "of which subagents: …", empty when no linked agents
166		Footnote string
167	}
168	
169	// usageModel is one row of the per-model table (or the Total row). All fields
170	// are plain formatted strings, auto-escaped by html/template.
171	type usageModel struct {
172		Model      string // model id, or "Total" for the summary row
173		Input      string
174		Output     string
175		CacheRead  string
176		CacheWrite string
177		Cost       string // "$1.23" or "n/a"
178	}
179	
180	func buildViewModel(s model.Session, title string, opts Options, page pageInfo, links *agentLinks) viewData {
181		d := viewData{
182			Title:     title,
183			Session:   s,
184			TurnCount: len(s.Turns),
185			Base:      page.Base,
186			BackHref:  page.BackHref,
187			Subtitle:  page.Subtitle,
188		}
189		if !s.StartedAt.IsZero() {
190			d.StartedAt = s.StartedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04")
191		}
192	
193		var rep usage.Report
194		if opts.Usage {
195			rep = usage.Compute(s)
196			d.Usage = buildUsageView(rep)
197		}
198	
199		for i, t := range s.Turns {
200			plain := turnPlainText(t)
201			if t.Kind == model.TurnUser {
202				d.Prompts = append(d.Prompts, promptRef{Index: i, Preview: preview(plain, 60)})
203			}
204			tv := turnView{
205				Index:      i,
206				Kind:       string(t.Kind),
207				RoleLabel:  roleLabel(t),
208				Status:     t.AgentStatus,
209				SearchText: strings.ToLower(plain),
210				Body:       renderTurnBody(t, links),
211			}
212			if t.Kind == model.TurnAgentResult {
213				tv.AgentHref = links.forAgent(t.AgentID)
214			}
215			if opts.Usage && t.Usage != nil {
216				tv.Badge = turnBadge(*t.Usage, rep.PerTurnCost[i])
217			}
218			d.Turns = append(d.Turns, tv)
219		}
220		return d
221	}
222	
223	// turnBadge formats the per-turn usage badge: "12k tok" plus "· ~$0.18" if priced.
224	func turnBadge(u model.Usage, costUSD *float64) string {
225		b := formatTokens(u.Input+u.Output) + " tok"
226		if costUSD != nil {
227			b += " · ~" + formatCost(*costUSD)
228		}
229		return b
230	}
231	
232	// buildUsageView turns a usage.Report into the template-facing view model.
233	func buildUsageView(r usage.Report) *usageView {
234		v := &usageView{HasAny: r.HasAnyUsage}
235		if !r.HasAnyUsage {
236			v.Headline = template.HTML("Usage · no token-usage data")
237			return v
238		}
239		headline := "Usage · " + formatTokens(r.Total.InOut()) + " in+out"
240		if r.TotalCostUSD != nil {
241			headline += " · ~" + formatCost(*r.TotalCostUSD) + " (est.)"
242		}
243		// html.EscapeString (unlike html/template's auto-escaper) leaves "+" intact
244		// while still escaping "<"/"&" etc.; headline is built solely from our own
245		// formatted numbers and literal words, so this is safe to mark as raw HTML.
246		v.Headline = template.HTML(html.EscapeString(headline))
247	
248		hasUnpriced := false
249		for _, m := range r.ByModel {
250			if m.Tokens == (usage.TokenCounts{}) {
251				continue // drop all-zero rows (e.g. <synthetic> with no tokens)
252			}
253			v.Models = append(v.Models, modelRow(m.Model, m.Tokens, m.CostUSD))
254			if m.CostUSD == nil {
255				hasUnpriced = true
256			}
257		}
258		v.Total = modelRow("Total", r.Total, r.TotalCostUSD)
259	
260		if r.AgentSessions > 0 {
261			sub := "of which subagents: " + formatTokens(r.Subagents.InOut()) + " in+out"
262			if r.SubagentsCost != nil {
263				sub += " · ~" + formatCost(*r.SubagentsCost)
264			}
265			v.SubLine = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%d sessions)", sub, r.AgentSessions)
266		}
267	
268		foot := "Estimated — Anthropic list prices as of " + r.PricesAsOf + "."
269		if r.AgentSessions > 0 {
270			foot += fmt.Sprintf(" Includes %d linked subagent session(s); server-tool fees are excluded.", r.AgentSessions)
271		} else {
272			foot += " Covers this transcript only; sub-agent sessions stored as separate files, and server-tool fees, are excluded."
273		}
274		if hasUnpriced {
275			foot += " Estimated cost excludes unpriced models (shown as n/a); their tokens are still counted."
276		}
277		v.Footnote = foot
278		return v
279	}
280	
281	// modelRow formats one per-model (or Total) table row from a token bucket and
282	// optional cost.
283	func modelRow(name string, t usage.TokenCounts, costUSD *float64) usageModel {
284		row := usageModel{
285			Model:      name,
286			Input:      formatTokens(t.Input),
287			Output:     formatTokens(t.Output),
288			CacheRead:  formatTokens(t.CacheRead),
289			CacheWrite: formatTokens(t.CacheWrite5m + t.CacheWrite1h),
290			Cost:       "n/a",
291		}
292		if costUSD != nil {
293			row.Cost = formatCost(*costUSD)
294		}
295		return row
296	}
297	
298	func roleLabel(t model.Turn) string {
299		switch t.Kind {
300		case model.TurnUser:
301			return "You"
302		case model.TurnAgentResult:
303			return agentRoleLabel(t.AgentSummary)
304		default:
305			return "Claude"
306		}
307	}
308	
309	// agentRoleLabel derives `Agent · <name>` from a notification summary like
310	// `Agent "Implement Task 12" finished`; plain "Agent" when no quoted name.
311	func agentRoleLabel(summary string) string {
312		if i := strings.Index(summary, `"`); i >= 0 {
313			if j := strings.Index(summary[i+1:], `"`); j > 0 {
314				return "Agent · " + summary[i+1:i+1+j]
315			}
316		}
317		return "Agent"
318	}
319	
320	// renderTurnBody renders all blocks of a turn to HTML.
321	func renderTurnBody(t model.Turn, links *agentLinks) template.HTML {
322		var b strings.Builder
323		for _, blk := range t.Blocks {
324			switch blk.Type {
325			case model.BlockText:
326				b.WriteString(string(Markdown(blk.Text)))
327			case model.BlockThinking:
328				b.WriteString(`<details class="thinking"><summary>thinking</summary>`)
329				b.WriteString(string(Markdown(blk.Text)))
330				b.WriteString(`</details>`)
331			case model.BlockToolUse:
332				b.WriteString(renderTool(blk.Tool, links))
333			}
334		}
335		return template.HTML(b.String())
336	}
337	
338	func renderTool(tc *model.ToolCall, links *agentLinks) string {
339		var b strings.Builder
340		b.WriteString(`<details class="tool"><summary class="tool-head">`)
341		b.WriteString(`<span class="tool-name">` + html.EscapeString(tc.Name) + `</span>`)
342		if tc.Summary != "" {
343			b.WriteString(`<span class="tool-summary">` + html.EscapeString(StripANSI(tc.Summary)) + `</span>`)
344		}
345		if href := links.forToolUse(tc.ID); href != "" {
346			b.WriteString(`<a class="agent-link" href="` + html.EscapeString(href) + `">transcript ↗</a>`)
347		}
348		b.WriteString(`</summary><div class="tool-body">`)
349		switch {
350		case tc.Diff != nil:
351			b.WriteString(string(DiffHTML(tc.Diff)))
352		case tc.IsAgent() && tc.AgentPrompt != "":
353			// The agent prompt is markdown and often long; render it readably
354			// instead of dumping the raw one-line JSON input.
355			b.WriteString(`<div class="agent-prompt">` + string(Markdown(tc.AgentPrompt)) + `</div>`)
356		case tc.InputJSON != "":
357			b.WriteString(`<pre class="tool-input">` + html.EscapeString(tc.InputJSON) + `</pre>`)
358		}
359		if tc.Result != nil && tc.Result.Content != "" {
360			if tc.IsAgent() {
361				// A subagent's result is markdown too; render it rather than showing
362				// a monospace block — but keep the error affordance for failed runs.
363				cls := "agent-result-body"
364				if tc.Result.IsError {
365					cls += " tool-result-error"
366				}
367				b.WriteString(`<div class="` + cls + `">` + string(Markdown(tc.Result.Content)) + `</div>`)
368			} else {
369				cls := "tool-result"
370				if tc.Result.IsError {
371					cls += " tool-result-error"
372				}
373				b.WriteString(`<pre class="` + cls + `">` + html.EscapeString(StripANSI(tc.Result.Content)) + `</pre>`)
374			}
375		}
376		for _, sub := range tc.Subagents {
377			b.WriteString(`<details class="subagent"><summary>subagent: ` + html.EscapeString(sub.Description) + `</summary>`)
378			for _, st := range sub.Turns {
379				b.WriteString(`<article class="turn turn-` + string(st.Kind) + `"><div class="turn-role">` + html.EscapeString(roleLabel(st)) + `</div><div class="turn-body">`)
380				b.WriteString(string(renderTurnBody(st, links)))
381				b.WriteString(`</div></article>`)
382			}
383			b.WriteString(`</details>`)
384		}
385		b.WriteString(`</div></details>`)
386		return b.String()
387	}
388	
389	func turnPlainText(t model.Turn) string {
390		var parts []string
391		for _, blk := range t.Blocks {
392			switch blk.Type {
393			case model.BlockText, model.BlockThinking:
394				parts = append(parts, blk.Text)
395			case model.BlockToolUse:
396				parts = append(parts, blk.Tool.Name, blk.Tool.Summary)
397			}
398		}
399		// Strip ANSI so previews and the search index carry clean text.
400		return StripANSI(strings.Join(parts, " "))
401	}
402	
403	func preview(s string, n int) string {
404		s = strings.TrimSpace(strings.ReplaceAll(s, "\n", " "))
405		r := []rune(s)
406		if len(r) <= n {
407			return s
408		}
409		return string(r[:n-1]) + "…"
410	}
411
Claude
Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse.go"
}
1	package transcript
2	
3	import (
4		"bufio"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"errors"
7		"io"
8		"os"
9		"strings"
10	
11		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
12	)
13	
14	// Options controls how a transcript is folded into a Session.
15	type Options struct {
16		IncludeSubagents bool
17		// AgentFile marks the input as an agent's own transcript file: its records
18		// carry isSidechain=true but form the file's main chain, so the flag is
19		// ignored.
20		AgentFile bool
21	}
22	
23	// ParseFile parses a transcript file at path.
24	func ParseFile(path string, opts Options) (model.Session, error) {
25		f, err := os.Open(path)
26		if err != nil {
27			return model.Session{}, err
28		}
29		defer f.Close()
30		s, err := Parse(f, opts)
31		if err != nil {
32			return model.Session{}, err
33		}
34		s.ID = sessionIDFromPath(path)
35		return s, nil
36	}
37	
38	func sessionIDFromPath(path string) string {
39		base := path
40		if i := lastSlash(path); i >= 0 {
41			base = path[i+1:]
42		}
43		if len(base) > len(".jsonl") && base[len(base)-len(".jsonl"):] == ".jsonl" {
44			return base[:len(base)-len(".jsonl")]
45		}
46		return base
47	}
48	
49	func lastSlash(s string) int {
50		for i := len(s) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
51			if s[i] == '/' {
52				return i
53			}
54		}
55		return -1
56	}
57	
58	// Parse reads a transcript stream and folds it into a Session timeline.
59	func Parse(r io.Reader, opts Options) (model.Session, error) {
60		var s model.Session
61		// toolIndex maps a tool_use id to the ToolCall pointer so results and
62		// sidechains can be attached after the fact.
63		toolIndex := map[string]*model.ToolCall{}
64		// seenUsage tracks message ids whose usage has already been counted, so an
65		// assistant message split across multiple records is not counted per-record.
66		seenUsage := map[string]bool{}
67		// lastTask tracks the most recent Task tool call; sidechainOwner is the Task
68		// whose Subagents any subsequent sidechain turns are appended to.
69		var lastTask *model.ToolCall
70		sidechainOwner := lastTask
71	
72		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
73		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 1024*1024), 64*1024*1024)
74		for sc.Scan() {
75			line := sc.Bytes()
76			if len(trimSpace(line)) == 0 {
77				continue
78			}
79			var rec rawRecord
80			if err := json.Unmarshal(line, &rec); err != nil {
81				s.SkippedLines++
82				continue
83			}
84			switch rec.Type {
85			case "ai-title":
86				if rec.AiTitle != "" {
87					s.Title = rec.AiTitle
88				}
89				continue
90			case "user", "assistant":
91				// fallthrough to handling below
92			default:
93				continue // ignore mode/permission/attachment/etc. for the timeline
94			}
95			if rec.IsMeta {
96				continue
97			}
98			if opts.AgentFile {
99				rec.IsSidechain = false
100			}
101	
102			// Capture session-level metadata from the first record that has it.
103			if s.GitBranch == "" && rec.GitBranch != "" {
104				s.GitBranch = rec.GitBranch
105			}
106			if s.Version == "" && rec.Version != "" {
107				s.Version = rec.Version
108			}
109			if s.ProjectPath == "" && rec.Cwd != "" {
110				s.ProjectPath = rec.Cwd
111			}
112			ts := parseTime(rec.Timestamp)
113			if !ts.IsZero() {
114				if s.StartedAt.IsZero() {
115					s.StartedAt = ts
116				}
117				s.EndedAt = ts
118			}
119	
120			_, blocks := decodeMessageContent(rec.Message)
121	
122			// Sidechain (subagent) records.
123			if rec.IsSidechain {
124				if !opts.IncludeSubagents || sidechainOwner == nil {
125					continue
126				}
127				turn := buildTurn(rec, blocks, toolIndex, seenUsage)
128				if turn == nil {
129					continue
130				}
131				sub := &sidechainOwner.Subagents[len(sidechainOwner.Subagents)-1]
132				sub.Turns = append(sub.Turns, *turn)
133				continue
134			}
135	
136			// Main-chain records.
137			turn := buildTurn(rec, blocks, toolIndex, seenUsage)
138			if turn == nil {
139				continue // e.g. a user record that only carried a tool_result
140			}
141			s.Turns = append(s.Turns, *turn)
142	
143			// Seed a subagent slot for each Task tool call and make it the current
144			// sidechain owner. Tool-use ids are already registered in buildTurn.
145			// Note: sidechain records are attributed to the nearest preceding Task
146			// by file order. Sequential Task calls attribute correctly; multiple
147			// Task calls dispatched in parallel from one assistant message cannot be
148			// disambiguated from the raw records and will all attach to the last
149			// one — a known v1 limitation.
150			if opts.IncludeSubagents {
151				for i := range turn.Blocks {
152					tool := turn.Blocks[i].Tool
153					if tool != nil && tool.Name == "Task" {
154						tool.Subagents = append(tool.Subagents, model.Subagent{
155							Description: taskDescription(tool),
156						})
157						lastTask = tool
158						sidechainOwner = lastTask
159					}
160				}
161			}
162		}
163		if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
164			// A single over-long line (bufio.ErrTooLong) is a pathological record,
165			// not a reason to discard the whole report: count it and return what we
166			// parsed so far.
167			if errors.Is(err, bufio.ErrTooLong) {
168				s.SkippedLines++
169				return s, nil
170			}
171			return s, err
172		}
173		return s, nil
174	}
175	
176	// messageMeta converts a record's message model/usage into turn fields, also
177	// returning the message id for deduplication. Only assistant records carry
178	// usage; the aggregate cache_creation figure (with no 5m/1h split) is attributed
179	// to the cheaper 5-minute write.
180	func messageMeta(raw json.RawMessage) (msgID, modelID string, usage *model.Usage) {
181		id, mdl, u := decodeMessageMeta(raw)
182		if u == nil {
183			return id, mdl, nil
184		}
185		w5, w1 := u.CacheCreation.Ephemeral5m, u.CacheCreation.Ephemeral1h
186		if w5 == 0 && w1 == 0 && u.CacheCreationInputTokens > 0 {
187			w5 = u.CacheCreationInputTokens
188		}
189		return id, mdl, &model.Usage{
190			Input:        u.InputTokens,
191			Output:       u.OutputTokens,
192			CacheRead:    u.CacheReadInputTokens,
193			CacheWrite5m: w5,
194			CacheWrite1h: w1,
195		}
196	}
197	
198	// taskNotification is the parsed payload of a <task-notification> user record —
199	// the message a background agent's completion injects into the parent session.
200	type taskNotification struct {
201		TaskID    string
202		ToolUseID string
203		Status    string
204		Summary   string
205		Result    string
206	}
207	
208	// parseTaskNotification extracts fields from a <task-notification> payload with
209	// tolerant string scanning (the payload is pseudo-XML with unescaped markdown
210	// inside <result>). ok is false unless both task-id and summary are present.
211	func parseTaskNotification(s string) (taskNotification, bool) {
212		t := strings.TrimLeft(s, " \t\r\n")
213		if !strings.HasPrefix(t, "<task-notification>") {
214			return taskNotification{}, false
215		}
216		n := taskNotification{
217			TaskID:    tagContent(t, "task-id"),
218			ToolUseID: tagContent(t, "tool-use-id"),
219			Status:    tagContent(t, "status"),
220			Summary:   tagContent(t, "summary"),
221			Result:    tagContent(t, "result"),
222		}
223		if n.TaskID == "" || n.Summary == "" {
224			return taskNotification{}, false
225		}
226		return n, true
227	}
228	
229	// tagContent returns the text between the first <name> and its closing
230	// </name>, trimmed. Simple fields match the first closing tag; <result> keeps
231	// matching the last one so bodies that quote XML-looking text (even a literal
232	// </result>) stay intact.
233	func tagContent(s, name string) string {
234		open, close := "<"+name+">", "</"+name+">"
235		i := strings.Index(s, open)
236		if i < 0 {
237			return ""
238		}
239		rest := s[i+len(open):]
240		var j int
241		if name == "result" {
242			j = strings.LastIndex(rest, close)
243		} else {
244			j = strings.Index(rest, close)
245		}
246		if j < 0 {
247			return ""
248		}
249		return strings.TrimSpace(rest[:j])
250	}
251	
252	// buildTurn converts a record's blocks into a model.Turn. tool_result blocks are
253	// attached to their originating ToolCall via toolIndex and do not themselves
254	// produce turn content. Returns nil if the record yields no displayable blocks.
255	func buildTurn(rec rawRecord, blocks []apiBlock, toolIndex map[string]*model.ToolCall, seenUsage map[string]bool) *model.Turn {
256		turn := &model.Turn{Timestamp: parseTime(rec.Timestamp)}
257		if rec.Type == "user" {
258			turn.Kind = model.TurnUser
259		} else {
260			turn.Kind = model.TurnAssistant
261		}
262		// A background agent's completion arrives as a user record whose content is
263		// a single <task-notification> text payload. Surface it as an agent-result
264		// turn so the report attributes it to the agent, not to the user.
265		if turn.Kind == model.TurnUser && len(blocks) == 1 && blocks[0].Type == "text" {
266			if n, ok := parseTaskNotification(blocks[0].Text); ok {
267				turn.Kind = model.TurnAgentResult
268				turn.AgentID = n.TaskID
269				turn.AgentStatus = n.Status
270				turn.AgentSummary = n.Summary
271				body := n.Result
272				if body == "" {
273					body = n.Summary
274				}
275				turn.Blocks = []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockText, Text: body}}
276				return turn
277			}
278		}
279		for _, b := range blocks {
280			switch b.Type {
281			case "text":
282				if b.Text != "" {
283					turn.Blocks = append(turn.Blocks, model.Block{Type: model.BlockText, Text: b.Text})
284				}
285			case "thinking":
286				if b.Thinking != "" {
287					turn.Blocks = append(turn.Blocks, model.Block{Type: model.BlockThinking, Text: b.Thinking})
288				}
289			case "tool_use":
290				tc := buildToolCall(b)
291				// Register the tool call so a later tool_result (in this turn's
292				// chain OR inside a sidechain) can be matched back to it by id.
293				toolIndex[tc.ID] = tc
294				turn.Blocks = append(turn.Blocks, model.Block{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc})
295			case "tool_result":
296				if tc := toolIndex[b.ToolUseID]; tc != nil {
297					tc.Result = &model.ToolResult{Content: toolResultText(b.Content), IsError: b.IsError}
298				}
299			}
300		}
301		if len(turn.Blocks) == 0 {
302			return nil
303		}
304		if rec.Type == "assistant" {
305			msgID, mdl, u := messageMeta(rec.Message)
306			turn.Model = mdl
307			// One assistant message is written as several records (one per content
308			// block), each repeating the same usage. Count usage on the first record
309			// of a message id only; later records of the same message get nil usage
310			// so totals are not multiplied by the block count.
311			if u != nil && msgID != "" {
312				if seenUsage[msgID] {
313					u = nil
314				} else {
315					seenUsage[msgID] = true
316				}
317			}
318			turn.Usage = u
319		}
320		return turn
321	}
322	
323	func trimSpace(b []byte) []byte {
324		start, end := 0, len(b)
325		for start < end && (b[start] == ' ' || b[start] == '\t' || b[start] == '\r' || b[start] == '\n') {
326			start++
327		}
328		for end > start && (b[end-1] == ' ' || b[end-1] == '\t' || b[end-1] == '\r' || b[end-1] == '\n') {
329			end--
330		}
331		return b[start:end]
332	}
333	
334	// buildToolCall constructs a ToolCall from a tool_use block, deriving a summary
335	// and, for Edit/Write, a Diff.
336	func buildToolCall(b apiBlock) *model.ToolCall {
337		tc := &model.ToolCall{
338			ID:        b.ID,
339			Name:      b.Name,
340			InputJSON: prettyJSON(b.Input),
341		}
342		input := decodeInput(b.Input)
343		tc.Summary = toolSummary(b.Name, input)
344		switch b.Name {
345		case "Edit", "Write":
346			tc.Diff = buildDiff(b.Name, input)
347		}
348		if tc.IsAgent() {
349			tc.AgentPrompt = str(input, "prompt")
350		}
351		return tc
352	}
353	
354	func decodeInput(raw json.RawMessage) map[string]any {
355		m := map[string]any{}
356		if len(raw) > 0 {
357			_ = json.Unmarshal(raw, &m)
358		}
359		return m
360	}
361	
362	func str(m map[string]any, key string) string {
363		if v, ok := m[key].(string); ok {
364			return v
365		}
366		return ""
367	}
368	
369	// toolSummary produces a compact one-line header for a tool card.
370	func toolSummary(name string, in map[string]any) string {
371		switch name {
372		case "Bash":
373			if c := str(in, "command"); c != "" {
374				return firstLine(c)
375			}
376		case "Read", "Edit", "Write", "NotebookEdit":
377			if p := str(in, "file_path"); p != "" {
378				return p
379			}
380		case "Glob":
381			return str(in, "pattern")
382		case "Grep":
383			return str(in, "pattern")
384		case "Task", "Agent":
385			return str(in, "description")
386		case "Skill":
387			return str(in, "skill")
388		case "WebFetch":
389			return str(in, "url")
390		case "WebSearch":
391			return str(in, "query")
392		}
393		return ""
394	}
395	
396	func buildDiff(name string, in map[string]any) *model.Diff {
397		d := &model.Diff{Path: str(in, "file_path")}
398		if name == "Write" {
399			d.NewText = str(in, "content")
400			return d
401		}
402		d.OldText = str(in, "old_string")
403		d.NewText = str(in, "new_string")
404		return d
405	}
406	
407	func taskDescription(tc *model.ToolCall) string {
408		if tc.Summary != "" {
409			return tc.Summary
410		}
411		return "subagent"
412	}
413	
414	func firstLine(s string) string {
415		for i := 0; i < len(s); i++ {
416			if s[i] == '\n' {
417				return s[:i]
418			}
419		}
420		return s
421	}
422	
423	func prettyJSON(raw json.RawMessage) string {
424		if len(raw) == 0 {
425			return ""
426		}
427		var v any
428		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &v); err != nil {
429			return string(raw)
430		}
431		out, err := json.MarshalIndent(v, "", "  ")
432		if err != nil {
433			return string(raw)
434		}
435		return string(out)
436	}
437

Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model/model.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model/model.go"
}
1	// Package model defines the domain types shared across ccwhid: a parsed
2	// Session and the timeline of Turns, Blocks and ToolCalls within it.
3	package model
4	
5	import "time"
6	
7	type TurnKind string
8	
9	const (
10		TurnUser        TurnKind = "user"
11		TurnAssistant   TurnKind = "assistant"
12		TurnAgentResult TurnKind = "agent-result" // background-agent completion fed back into the session
13	)
14	
15	type BlockType string
16	
17	const (
18		BlockText     BlockType = "text"
19		BlockThinking BlockType = "thinking"
20		BlockToolUse  BlockType = "tool_use"
21	)
22	
23	// Session is one fully-parsed Claude Code transcript.
24	type Session struct {
25		ID           string
26		ProjectPath  string // decoded absolute cwd
27		Title        string
28		GitBranch    string
29		Version      string
30		StartedAt    time.Time
31		EndedAt      time.Time
32		Turns        []Turn
33		SkippedLines int            // count of malformed lines skipped while parsing
34		Agents       []AgentSession // linked subagent sessions (subagents/ dir), when loaded
35	}
36	
37	func (s Session) Duration() time.Duration { return s.EndedAt.Sub(s.StartedAt) }
38	
39	func (s Session) DisplayTitle() string {
40		if s.Title != "" {
41			return s.Title
42		}
43		return "Untitled session"
44	}
45	
46	// Turn is a single user or assistant message, holding ordered content blocks.
47	type Turn struct {
48		Kind      TurnKind
49		Timestamp time.Time
50		Blocks    []Block
51		Model     string // assistant model id (empty for user turns / when absent)
52		Usage     *Usage // token usage for this assistant turn; nil when absent
53		// Agent-result fields, set only when Kind == TurnAgentResult.
54		AgentID      string // task/agent id from the notification
55		AgentStatus  string // e.g. "completed"
56		AgentSummary string // e.g. `Agent "Implement Task 12" finished`
57	}
58	
59	// Usage holds the token counts reported for one assistant message. Cache writes
60	// keep the 5-minute / 1-hour split because they are priced differently.
61	type Usage struct {
62		Input        int
63		Output       int
64		CacheRead    int
65		CacheWrite5m int
66		CacheWrite1h int
67	}
68	
69	// Block is one content unit: assistant/user text, a thinking block, or a tool call.
70	type Block struct {
71		Type BlockType
72		Text string    // for BlockText and BlockThinking
73		Tool *ToolCall // for BlockToolUse
74	}
75	
76	// ToolCall is a single tool invocation and its result.
77	type ToolCall struct {
78		ID          string
79		Name        string // e.g. "Bash", "Edit", "Read", "Task"
80		Summary     string // one-line summary for the collapsed card header
81		InputJSON   string // pretty-printed input for generic display
82		AgentPrompt string // for Task/Agent calls: the subagent prompt, rendered as markdown
83		Result      *ToolResult
84		Diff        *Diff      // set for Edit/Write
85		Subagents   []Subagent // set for Task calls with sidechain activity
86	}
87	
88	// IsAgent reports whether this tool call spawns a subagent — the historical
89	// "Task" tool or the modern "Agent" tool.
90	func (t *ToolCall) IsAgent() bool {
91		return t.Name == "Task" || t.Name == "Agent"
92	}
93	
94	type ToolResult struct {
95		Content string
96		IsError bool
97	}
98	
99	type Diff struct {
100		Path    string
101		OldText string
102		NewText string
103	}
104	
105	// Subagent is a nested sidechain run attached to a parent Task tool call.
106	type Subagent struct {
107		Description string
108		Turns       []Turn
109	}
110	
111	// AgentSession is a subagent session file linked to a root session
112	// (<projectDir>/<sessionId>/subagents/agent-<ID>.jsonl).
113	type AgentSession struct {
114		ID          string  // agentId, from the file name agent-<ID>.jsonl
115		Description string  // from meta.json; falls back to ID
116		AgentType   string  // from meta.json; may be empty
117		ToolUseID   string  // id of the spawning tool call; may be empty
118		SpawnDepth  int     // from meta.json; informational only
119		Session     Session // the parsed agent transcript
120	}
121

Read~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/markdown.go
{
  "file_path": "~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/markdown.go"
}
1	package render
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"html"
6		"html/template"
7		"strings"
8	
9		chromahtml "github.com/alecthomas/chroma/v2/formatters/html"
10		"github.com/yuin/goldmark"
11		highlighting "github.com/yuin/goldmark-highlighting/v2"
12		"github.com/yuin/goldmark/extension"
13	
14		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
15	)
16	
17	// md renders markdown with GFM plus chroma-based syntax highlighting for fenced
18	// code blocks. Highlighting uses inline styles (WithClasses(false)) so the
19	// generated report stays fully self-contained — no external stylesheet.
20	var md = goldmark.New(goldmark.WithExtensions(
21		extension.GFM,
22		highlighting.NewHighlighting(
23			highlighting.WithStyle("github"),
24			highlighting.WithFormatOptions(
25				chromahtml.WithClasses(false),
26			),
27		),
28	))
29	
30	// Markdown renders CommonMark+GFM to HTML. goldmark runs with its default
31	// Unsafe=false setting, so any raw HTML embedded in the transcript text is
32	// stripped (replaced with an HTML comment) rather than passed through — the
33	// rendered Body is therefore safe to emit as template.HTML.
34	func Markdown(src string) template.HTML {
35		src = StripANSI(src)
36		var buf bytes.Buffer
37		if err := md.Convert([]byte(src), &buf); err != nil {
38			return template.HTML("<pre>" + html.EscapeString(src) + "</pre>")
39		}
40		return template.HTML(buf.String())
41	}
42	
43	// DiffHTML renders a simple line-based diff of a Diff's old/new text.
44	func DiffHTML(d *model.Diff) template.HTML {
45		var b strings.Builder
46		b.WriteString(`<div class="diff">`)
47		oldText, newText := StripANSI(d.OldText), StripANSI(d.NewText)
48		if oldText != "" {
49			for _, line := range strings.Split(oldText, "\n") {
50				b.WriteString(`<div class="diff-del">- `)
51				b.WriteString(html.EscapeString(line))
52				b.WriteString("</div>")
53			}
54		}
55		if newText != "" {
56			for _, line := range strings.Split(newText, "\n") {
57				b.WriteString(`<div class="diff-add">+ `)
58				b.WriteString(html.EscapeString(line))
59				b.WriteString("</div>")
60			}
61		}
62		b.WriteString("</div>")
63		return template.HTML(b.String())
64	}
65

Now I'll implement. Starting with the model types:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model/model.go
- // ToolCall is a single tool invocation and its result.
- type ToolCall struct {
- ID string
- Name string // e.g. "Bash", "Edit", "Read", "Task"
- Summary string // one-line summary for the collapsed card header
- InputJSON string // pretty-printed input for generic display
- AgentPrompt string // for Task/Agent calls: the subagent prompt, rendered as markdown
- Result *ToolResult
- Diff *Diff // set for Edit/Write
- Subagents []Subagent // set for Task calls with sidechain activity
- }
-
- // IsAgent reports whether this tool call spawns a subagent — the historical
- // "Task" tool or the modern "Agent" tool.
- func (t *ToolCall) IsAgent() bool {
- return t.Name == "Task" || t.Name == "Agent"
- }
+ // ToolCall is a single tool invocation and its result.
+ type ToolCall struct {
+ ID string
+ Name string // e.g. "Bash", "Edit", "Read", "Task"
+ Summary string // one-line summary for the collapsed card header
+ InputJSON string // pretty-printed input for generic display
+ AgentPrompt string // for Task/Agent calls: the subagent prompt, rendered as markdown
+ Questions []Question // set for AskUserQuestion calls
+ Result *ToolResult
+ Diff *Diff // set for Edit/Write
+ Subagents []Subagent // set for Task calls with sidechain activity
+ }
+
+ // IsAgent reports whether this tool call spawns a subagent — the historical
+ // "Task" tool or the modern "Agent" tool.
+ func (t *ToolCall) IsAgent() bool {
+ return t.Name == "Task" || t.Name == "Agent"
+ }
+
+ // IsAskUserQuestion reports whether this tool call is an AskUserQuestion prompt.
+ func (t *ToolCall) IsAskUserQuestion() bool {
+ return t.Name == "AskUserQuestion"
+ }
+
+ // Question is one question posed by an AskUserQuestion call, with its options.
+ type Question struct {
+ Header string // short chip label, e.g. "Language"
+ Prompt string // the full question text
+ MultiSelect bool
+ Options []QuestionOption
+ }
+
+ // QuestionOption is one selectable answer of a Question.
+ type QuestionOption struct {
+ Label string
+ Description string
+ Preview string // optional monospace preview block
+ }
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model/model.go has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
Claude678 tok · ~$0.08

Now the parser:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse.go
- if tc.IsAgent() {
- tc.AgentPrompt = str(input, "prompt")
- }
- return tc
- }
+ if tc.IsAgent() {
+ tc.AgentPrompt = str(input, "prompt")
+ }
+ if tc.IsAskUserQuestion() {
+ tc.Questions = parseQuestions(input)
+ }
+ return tc
+ }
+
+ // parseQuestions extracts the structured question list from an AskUserQuestion
+ // input map. Missing or malformed fields degrade to empty values rather than
+ // failing, so a partial card still renders.
+ func parseQuestions(in map[string]any) []model.Question {
+ raw, ok := in["questions"].([]any)
+ if !ok {
+ return nil
+ }
+ var out []model.Question
+ for _, rq := range raw {
+ qm, ok := rq.(map[string]any)
+ if !ok {
+ continue
+ }
+ q := model.Question{
+ Header: str(qm, "header"),
+ Prompt: str(qm, "question"),
+ }
+ if v, ok := qm["multiSelect"].(bool); ok {
+ q.MultiSelect = v
+ }
+ if opts, ok := qm["options"].([]any); ok {
+ for _, ro := range opts {
+ om, ok := ro.(map[string]any)
+ if !ok {
+ continue
+ }
+ q.Options = append(q.Options, model.QuestionOption{
+ Label: str(om, "label"),
+ Description: str(om, "description"),
+ Preview: str(om, "preview"),
+ })
+ }
+ }
+ out = append(out, q)
+ }
+ return out
+ }
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse.go has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

- case "Skill":
- return str(in, "skill")
+ case "Skill":
+ return str(in, "skill")
+ case "AskUserQuestion":
+ return askQuestionSummary(in)

Claude488 tok · ~$0.07
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse.go
- func buildDiff(name string, in map[string]any) *model.Diff {
+ // askQuestionSummary derives the collapsed-card header for an AskUserQuestion
+ // call: the questions' headers joined with " · " (falling back to the question
+ // text when a header is missing).
+ func askQuestionSummary(in map[string]any) string {
+ raw, ok := in["questions"].([]any)
+ if !ok {
+ return ""
+ }
+ var headers []string
+ for _, rq := range raw {
+ qm, ok := rq.(map[string]any)
+ if !ok {
+ continue
+ }
+ h := str(qm, "header")
+ if h == "" {
+ h = firstLine(str(qm, "question"))
+ }
+ if h != "" {
+ headers = append(headers, h)
+ }
+ }
+ return strings.Join(headers, " · ")
+ }
+
+ func buildDiff(name string, in map[string]any) *model.Diff {

- case tc.IsAgent() && tc.AgentPrompt != "":
- // The agent prompt is markdown and often long; render it readably
- // instead of dumping the raw one-line JSON input.
- b.WriteString(`<div class="agent-prompt">` + string(Markdown(tc.AgentPrompt)) + `</div>`)
- case tc.InputJSON != "":

- }

- if tc.IsAgent() {
- // A subagent's result is markdown too; render it rather than showing
- // a monospace block — but keep the error affordance for failed runs.
- cls := "agent-result-body"
- if tc.Result.IsError {
- cls += " tool-result-error"
- }

+ case tc.IsAgent() && tc.AgentPrompt != "":
+ // The agent prompt is markdown and often long; render it readably
+ // instead of dumping the raw one-line JSON input.
+ b.WriteString(`<div class="agent-prompt">` + string(Markdown(tc.AgentPrompt)) + `</div>`)
+ case tc.IsAskUserQuestion() && len(tc.Questions) > 0:
+ // Render the questions and options as a readable list, highlighting the
+ // answer chosen, instead of dumping the raw JSON input.
+ b.WriteString(renderQuestions(tc.Questions, resultContent(tc)))
+ case tc.InputJSON != "":
+ b.WriteString(`<pre class="tool-input">` + html.EscapeString(tc.InputJSON) + `</pre>`)
+ }
+ if tc.Result != nil && tc.Result.Content != "" {
+ switch {
+ case tc.IsAgent():
+ // A subagent's result is markdown too; render it rather than showing
+ // a monospace block — but keep the error affordance for failed runs.
+ cls := "agent-result-body"
+ if tc.Result.IsError {
+ cls += " tool-result-error"
+ }
+ b.WriteString(`<div class="` + cls + `">` + string(Markdown(tc.Result.Content)) + `</div>`)
+ case tc.IsAskUserQuestion() && len(tc.Questions) > 0:
+ // Turn the "Your questions have been answered…" sentence into a tidy
+ // header → answer list; fall back to the raw text if none parse.
+ if ans := renderAnswers(tc.Questions, tc.Result.Content); ans != "" {
+ b.WriteString(ans)
+ } else {
+ b.WriteString(`<pre class="tool-result">` + html.EscapeString(StripANSI(tc.Result.Content)) + `</pre>`)
+ }
+ default:
+ cls := "tool-result"
+ if tc.Result.IsError {
+ cls += " tool-result-error"
+ }
+ b.WriteString(`<pre class="` + cls + `">` + html.EscapeString(StripANSI(tc.Result.Content)) + `</pre>`)
+ }
+ }

Now the helper functions in render.go:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go
- func turnPlainText(t model.Turn) string {
+ // resultContent returns a tool call's result text, or "" when it has no result.
+ func resultContent(tc *model.ToolCall) string {
+ if tc.Result != nil {
+ return tc.Result.Content
+ }
+ return ""
+ }
+
+ // renderQuestions renders an AskUserQuestion prompt as a list of questions, each
+ // with its header, question text, and options. The option the user picked (found
+ // in the result string) is marked selected.
+ func renderQuestions(qs []model.Question, result string) string {
+ var b strings.Builder
+ b.WriteString(`<div class="ask">`)
+ for _, q := range qs {
+ b.WriteString(`<div class="ask-q">`)
+ if q.Header != "" {
+ b.WriteString(`<span class="ask-header">` + html.EscapeString(q.Header) + `</span>`)
+ }
+ if q.Prompt != "" {
+ b.WriteString(`<div class="ask-prompt">` + string(Markdown(q.Prompt)) + `</div>`)
+ }
+ b.WriteString(`<ul class="ask-options">`)
+ for _, o := range q.Options {
+ cls := "ask-option"
+ if optionChosen(result, o.Label) {
+ cls += " ask-option-selected"
+ }
+ b.WriteString(`<li class="` + cls + `">`)
+ b.WriteString(`<div class="ask-option-label">` + html.EscapeString(o.Label) + `</div>`)
+ if o.Description != "" {
+ b.WriteString(`<div class="ask-option-desc">` + string(Markdown(o.Description)) + `</div>`)
+ }
+ if o.Preview != "" {
+ b.WriteString(`<pre class="ask-option-preview">` + html.EscapeString(o.Preview) + `</pre>`)
+ }
+ b.WriteString(`</li>`)
+ }
+ b.WriteString(`</ul></div>`)
+ }
+ b.WriteString(`</div>`)
+ return b.String()
+ }
+
+ // renderAnswers renders the chosen answers as a header → answer list. It returns
+ // "" when no answer could be parsed, so the caller can fall back to raw text.
+ func renderAnswers(qs []model.Question, result string) string {
+ var rows strings.Builder
+ any := false
+ for _, q := range qs {
+ ans := answerFor(result, q.Prompt)
+ if ans == "" {
+ continue
+ }
+ any = true
+ label := q.Header
+ if label == "" {
+ label = preview(q.Prompt, 40)
+ }
+ rows.WriteString(`<li><span class="ask-answers-q">` + html.EscapeString(label) +
+ `</span><span class="ask-answers-a">` + html.EscapeString(ans) + `</span></li>`)
+ }
+ if !any {
+ return ""
+ }
+ return `<div class="ask-answers"><div class="ask-answers-title">answered</div><ul>` +
+ rows.String() + `</ul></div>`
+ }
+
+ // optionChosen reports whether an option label appears as a chosen answer in an
+ // AskUserQuestion result string, which encodes answers as `="<label>"`.
+ func optionChosen(result, label string) bool {
+ return label != "" && strings.Contains(result, `="`+label+`"`)
+ }
+
+ // answerFor extracts the answer for a given question prompt from an
+ // AskUserQuestion result string of the form `…"<prompt>"="<answer>"…`. It returns
+ // "" when the prompt is not found.
+ func answerFor(result, prompt string) string {
+ if prompt == "" {
+ return ""
+ }
+ marker := `"` + prompt + `"="`
+ i := strings.Index(result, marker)
+ if i < 0 {
+ return ""
+ }
+ rest := result[i+len(marker):]
+ if j := strings.Index(rest, `"`); j >= 0 {
+ return rest[:j]
+ }
+ return ""
+ }
+
+ func turnPlainText(t model.Turn) string {

Now the CSS. Let me add styles after the agent-result rules:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/assets/styles.css
- .agent-result-body { font-size: .9rem; }
- .agent-result-body::before { content: "result"; }
+ .agent-result-body { font-size: .9rem; }
+ .agent-result-body::before { content: "result"; }
+ .ask { margin: .2rem 0 .4rem; }
+ .ask-q { margin-bottom: 1rem; }
+ .ask-q:last-child { margin-bottom: 0; }
+ .ask-header { display: inline-block; background: var(--accent-soft); color: var(--accent); font-size: .68rem; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .05em; padding: .1rem .5rem; border-radius: 999px; margin-bottom: .35rem; }
+ .ask-prompt { font-size: .9rem; margin-bottom: .5rem; }
+ .ask-prompt > :first-child { margin-top: 0; }
+ .ask-prompt > :last-child { margin-bottom: 0; }
+ .ask-options { list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: .4rem; }
+ .ask-option { border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 8px; padding: .5rem .7rem; background: var(--surface); }
+ .ask-option-selected { border-color: var(--accent); background: var(--accent-soft); }
+ .ask-option-label { font-weight: 600; font-size: .88rem; }
+ .ask-option-selected .ask-option-label::before { content: "✓ "; color: var(--accent); }
+ .ask-option-desc { color: var(--muted); font-size: .82rem; margin-top: .15rem; }
+ .ask-option-desc > :first-child { margin-top: 0; }
+ .ask-option-desc > :last-child { margin-bottom: 0; }
+ .ask-option-preview { background: var(--code-bg); border-radius: 6px; padding: .45rem .6rem; margin: .35rem 0 0; font-family: var(--mono); font-size: .76rem; overflow-x: auto; white-space: pre; }
+ .ask-answers { margin-top: .3rem; }
+ .ask-answers-title { color: var(--muted); font-size: .7rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .05em; margin-bottom: .2rem; }
+ .ask-answers ul { list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: .25rem; }
+ .ask-answers li { display: flex; gap: .5rem; align-items: baseline; font-size: .85rem; }
+ .ask-answers-q { color: var(--muted); flex: 0 0 auto; }
+ .ask-answers-q::after { content: "→"; margin-left: .5rem; color: var(--border); }
+ .ask-answers-a { font-weight: 600; color: var(--accent); }

{
  "command": "sed -n '1,60p' internal/render/render_test.go",
  "description": "Read start of render_test.go"
}
package render

import (
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"
	"unicode/utf8"

	"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/model"
	"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/usage"
)

func TestMarkdownRendersHeadingAndCode(t *testing.T) {
	out := string(Markdown("# Title\n\n```go\nfmt.Println(\"hi\")\n```"))
	if !strings.Contains(out, "<h1") {
		t.Errorf("expected <h1> in %q", out)
	}
	if !strings.Contains(out, "Println") {
		t.Errorf("expected highlighted code in output")
	}
	if !strings.Contains(out, "<span") {
		t.Errorf("expected chroma-highlighted <span> tokens in code output, got %q", out)
	}
}

func TestDiffHTMLMarksLines(t *testing.T) {
	out := string(DiffHTML(&model.Diff{Path: "x.txt", OldText: "a", NewText: "b"}))
	if !strings.Contains(out, "diff-del") || !strings.Contains(out, "diff-add") {
		t.Errorf("diff HTML missing add/del classes: %q", out)
	}
}

func TestStripANSI(t *testing.T) {
	cases := []struct{ in, want string }{
		{"\x1b[1mOpus 4.8\x1b[22m", "Opus 4.8"},   // SGR bold on/off
		{"\x1b[0;31mred\x1b[0m text", "red text"}, // colour codes
		{"plain text", "plain text"},              // fast path, unchanged
		{"array[1m] index", "array[1m] index"},    // literal, no ESC → kept
		{"\x1b]0;title\x07done", "done"},          // OSC + BEL
		{"a\x1b[Kb", "ab"},                        // erase-line CSI
	}
	for _, c := range cases {
		if got := StripANSI(c.in); got != c.want {
			t.Errorf("StripANSI(%q) = %q, want %q", c.in, got, c.want)
		}
	}
}

func TestRenderStripsANSIFromReport(t *testing.T) {
	s := model.Session{
		ID:    "x",
		Title: "T",
		Turns: []model.Turn{
			{Kind: model.TurnUser, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockText, Text: "set model to \x1b[1mOpus\x1b[22m now"}}},
			{Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: &model.ToolCall{
				Name: "Bash", Summary: "echo hi", Result: &model.ToolResult{Content: "\x1b[32mgreen\x1b[0m output"},
			}}}},
		},

{

292	func TestAgentToolCardRendersPromptAndResultAsMarkdown(t *testing.T) {
293		tc := &model.ToolCall{
294			Name:        "Agent",
295			Summary:     "Implement Task 1: scaffold",
296			AgentPrompt: "# Do the thing\n\nWith **emphasis**.",
297			InputJSON:   `{"prompt":"# Do the thing"}`,
298			Result:      &model.ToolResult{Content: "**Status:** DONE"},
299		}
300		turn := model.Turn{Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc}}}
301		out := string(renderTurnBody(turn, newAgentLinks(nil, "")))
302	
303		if !strings.Contains(out, `class="agent-prompt"`) {
304			t.Errorf("expected agent-prompt block, got %q", out)
305		}
306		if !strings.Contains(out, "<strong>emphasis</strong>") {
307			t.Errorf("prompt should be rendered as markdown: %q", out)
308		}
309		if !strings.Contains(out, `class="agent-result-body"`) {
310			t.Errorf("expected agent-result-body block: %q", out)
311		}
312		if !strings.Contains(out, "<strong>Status:</strong>") {
313			t.Errorf("result should be rendered as markdown: %q", out)
314		}
315		if strings.Contains(out, `class="tool-input"`) {
316			t.Errorf("agent card must not dump raw JSON input: %q", out)
317		}
318		if strings.Contains(out, `class="tool-result"`) {
319			t.Errorf("agent result must not use the monospace tool-result block: %q", out)
320		}
321		if !strings.Contains(out, "Implement Task 1: scaffold") {
322			t.Errorf("tool header should show the agent description: %q", out)
323		}
324	}
325	
326	func TestNonAgentToolResultStaysMonospace(t *testing.T) {
327		tc := &model.ToolCall{
328			Name:      "Bash",
329			Summary:   "ls",
330			InputJSON: `{"command":"ls"}`,
331			Result:    &model.ToolResult{Content: "file1\nfile2"},
332		}
333		turn := model.Turn{Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc}}}
334		out := string(renderTurnBody(turn, newAgentLinks(nil, "")))
335		if !strings.Contains(out, `class="tool-result"`) {
336			t.Errorf("non-agent result should keep the monospace tool-result block: %q", out)
337		}
338		if strings.Contains(out, `class="agent-result-body"`) {
339			t.Errorf("non-agent result must not be markdown-rendered: %q", out)
340		}
341	}
342	
343	func TestAgentErrorResultKeepsErrorAffordance(t *testing.T) {
344		tc := &model.ToolCall{
345			Name:   "Agent",
346			Result: &model.ToolResult{Content: "**Status:** BLOCKED", IsError: true},
347		}
348		turn := model.Turn{Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc}}}
349		out := string(renderTurnBody(turn, newAgentLinks(nil, "")))
350		if !strings.Contains(out, `class="agent-result-body tool-result-error"`) {
351			t.Errorf("agent error result should keep the tool-result-error affordance: %q", out)
352		}
353	}

- func TestAgentErrorResultKeepsErrorAffordance(t *testing.T) {
+ func TestAskUserQuestionCardRendersOptionsAndAnswers(t *testing.T) {
+ tc := &model.ToolCall{
+ Name: "AskUserQuestion",
+ Summary: "Language",
+ Questions: []model.Question{{
+ Header: "Language",
+ Prompt: "Go or explore an alternative?",
+ Options: []model.QuestionOption{
+ {Label: "Go (Recommended)", Description: "Single static **binary**."},
+ {Label: "Rust", Description: "Also single-binary.", Preview: "$ cargo build"},
+ },
+ }},
+ InputJSON: `{"questions":[...]}`,
+ Result: &model.ToolResult{Content: `Your questions have been answered: "Go or explore an alternative?"="Go (Recommended)". You can now continue.`},
+ }
+ turn := model.Turn{Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc}}}
+ out := string(renderTurnBody(turn, newAgentLinks(nil, "")))
+
+ if !strings.Contains(out, `class="ask-header"`) || !strings.Contains(out, ">Language<") {
+ t.Errorf("expected header chip with the question header: %q", out)
+ }
+ if !strings.Contains(out, "<strong>binary</strong>") {
+ t.Errorf("option description should render as markdown: %q", out)
+ }
+ if !strings.Contains(out, `class="ask-option-preview"`) || !strings.Contains(out, "cargo build") {
+ t.Errorf("expected preview block for the option that has one: %q", out)
+ }
+ if !strings.Contains(out, `class="ask-option ask-option-selected"`) {
+ t.Errorf("the chosen option should be marked selected: %q", out)
+ }
+ if !strings.Contains(out, `class="ask-answers"`) || !strings.Contains(out, `class="ask-answers-a">Go (Recommended)<`) {
+ t.Errorf("expected an answers summary with the chosen answer: %q", out)
+ }
+ if strings.Contains(out, `class="tool-input"`) {
+ t.Errorf("AskUserQuestion card must not dump raw JSON input: %q", out)
+ }
+ if strings.Contains(out, "questions have been answered") {
+ t.Errorf("raw result sentence should be replaced by the answers summary: %q", out)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestAskUserQuestionFallsBackToRawResultWhenUnparsed(t *testing.T) {
+ // A result whose prompt does not match the question falls back to raw text.
+ tc := &model.ToolCall{
+ Name: "AskUserQuestion",
+ Questions: []model.Question{{
+ Header: "Scope",
+ Prompt: "What scope?",
+ Options: []model.QuestionOption{{Label: "Small"}},
+ }},
+ Result: &model.ToolResult{Content: "some unexpected result text"},
+ }
+ turn := model.Turn{Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc}}}
+ out := string(renderTurnBody(turn, newAgentLinks(nil, "")))
+ if !strings.Contains(out, `class="tool-result"`) || !strings.Contains(out, "some unexpected result text") {
+ t.Errorf("unparseable result should fall back to the raw monospace block: %q", out)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestAgentErrorResultKeepsErrorAffordance(t *testing.T) {

Now the parse test:

Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript/parse_test.go
- func TestBuildToolCallSkillSummaryIsSkillName(t *testing.T) {
+ func TestBuildToolCallAskUserQuestionSummaryAndQuestions(t *testing.T) {
+ b := apiBlock{
+ Type: "tool_use",
+ ID: "t3",
+ Name: "AskUserQuestion",
+ Input: json.RawMessage(`{"questions":[
+ {"header":"Language","question":"Go or Rust?","multiSelect":false,
+ "options":[
+ {"label":"Go","description":"single binary","preview":"$ go build"},
+ {"label":"Rust","description":"also single binary"}]},
+ {"header":"Scope","question":"How big?","multiSelect":true,"options":[]}]}`),
+ }
+ tc := buildToolCall(b)
+ if tc.Summary != "Language · Scope" {
+ t.Errorf("Summary = %q, want the joined question headers", tc.Summary)
+ }
+ if !tc.IsAskUserQuestion() {
+ t.Error("AskUserQuestion tool should report IsAskUserQuestion")
+ }
+ if len(tc.Questions) != 2 {
+ t.Fatalf("got %d questions, want 2", len(tc.Questions))
+ }
+ q := tc.Questions[0]
+ if q.Header != "Language" || q.Prompt != "Go or Rust?" || q.MultiSelect {
+ t.Errorf("question 0 = %+v", q)
+ }
+ if len(q.Options) != 2 || q.Options[0].Label != "Go" || q.Options[0].Preview != "$ go build" {
+ t.Errorf("question 0 options = %+v", q.Options)
+ }
+ if !tc.Questions[1].MultiSelect {
+ t.Errorf("question 1 should be multiSelect")
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestBuildToolCallSkillSummaryIsSkillName(t *testing.T) {

ccwhid (cc-what-have-i-done) renders a Claude Code session transcript into a self-contained static HTML report. Run with no flags to browse sessions in an interactive TUI.

Usage:
  ccwhid [flags]

Flags:
      --force               overwrite a non-empty output directory
  -h, --help                help for ccwhid
      --include-subagents   include subagent work: inline Task sidechains and linked agent-session pages (default true)
      --latest              render the most recent interactive session (skips agent transcripts)
      --no-redact           disable secret redaction
      --open                open the report in a browser when done
      --out string          output directory (default ./ccwhid-report/<session-short>)
      --project string      project path or name; scopes --latest, or opens the TUI focused on it
      --session string      session id or unambiguous prefix to render
      --title string        override the report title
      --usage               include a token-usage & estimated-cost section
  -v, --version             version for ccwhid

{
  "command": "cd ~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done \u0026\u0026 python3 - \u003c\u003c'PY'\nimport json,glob\nfor fn in glob.glob(\"*.jsonl\"):\n    n=0; sub=0\n    for line in open(fn):\n        try: r=json.loads(line)\n        except: continue\n        msg=r.get(\"message\")\n        if not isinstance(msg,dict): continue\n        c=msg.get(\"content\")\n        if not isinstance(c,list): continue\n        for b in c:\n            if isinstance(b,dict) and b.get(\"type\")==\"tool_use\" and b.get(\"name\")==\"AskUserQuestion\":\n                n+=1\n                if r.get(\"isSidechain\"): sub+=1\n    if n: print(f\"{fn}: {n} AskUserQuestion tool_use ({sub} in sidechain)\")\nPY",
  "description": "Find sessions with real AskUserQuestion tool_use blocks"
}

Claude1k tok · ~$0.11

Done. The AskUserQuestion card now renders structurally instead of dumping raw JSON. Verified end-to-end against the real session (13 such cards):

What changed

  1. Header in the card title — toolSummary now emits the question header(s) for AskUserQuestion, joined with · for multi-question calls (e.g. Language, --project alone · Root filter scope).
  2. Options as a formatted list — each question renders a header chip, the question text (markdown), and a card per option showing the label, its description (markdown), and the optional monospace preview block. The option the user picked is highlighted (accent border + ✓).
  3. The result sentence, cleaned up — the Your questions have been answered: … string is parsed into a tidy header → answer summary list. If a result can't be parsed (unexpected shape), it falls back to the raw monospace block.

Files

  • internal/model/model.go — Question/QuestionOption types, ToolCall.Questions, IsAskUserQuestion().
  • internal/transcript/parse.go — parseQuestions, askQuestionSummary, wired into buildToolCall/toolSummary.
  • internal/render/render.go — renderQuestions, renderAnswers, answerFor, optionChosen, plus the two new renderTool switch branches.
  • internal/render/assets/styles.css — .ask* styles (theme-aware).
  • Tests added in render_test.go (rendering + fallback) and parse_test.go (summary + structured parse).

gofmt, go vet, and the full go test ./... are all green, and a real report render confirms the headers land in titles, chosen answers extract correctly (including multi-question / multi-select), and no raw JSON input remains for these cards.

Claude2k tok · ~$0.13

All 37 are [user] — the stale Go module path, which is the documented out-of-scope case (real name in a module path / already public via commit authorship; \bmarkus\b correctly doesn't match inside it). Let me confirm this isn't a new regression by comparing against the already-committed demo:

Claude
Bashecho "[user] in committed (HEAD) demo:"; git show HEAD:docs/session-report/index.html | grep -c "[user]"; echo "bare-standalone '[user]' (word-boundary, not [user]) in new demo:"; grep -oE '(^|[^a-zA-Z])[user]([^a-zA-Z]|$)' docs/session-report/index.html | grep -vi "[user]" | wc -l | tr -d ' '; echo "--- scratchpad/tmp path leaks from this session? ---"; grep -oE 'claude-501|/private/tmp/[^ "<]*ccwhid' docs/session-report/index.html | sort -u | head

  "description": "Confirm [user] is pre-existing, no bare [user], no tmp leaks"
}

Claude829 tok · ~$0.11

Clean — [user] (module path) is the pre-existing out-of-scope identifier (21→37 as this session's transcript grew), zero bare standalone [user], and scratchpad paths are correctly scrubbed to -Users-[user]-. Now let me commit — feature code first, then the demo:

Claude
Bashgit add internal/ && git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
{
  "command": "git add internal/ \u0026\u0026 git commit -q -F - \u003c\u003c'EOF'\nfeat: render AskUserQuestion cards as readable option lists\n\nPreviously an AskUserQuestion tool call dumped its raw JSON input and the\n\"Your questions have been answered…\" result sentence verbatim. Now:\n\n- the card title carries the question header(s), joined with \" · \" for\n  multi-question calls;\n- the expanded card renders each question as a header chip, the question\n  text, and a card per option (label, markdown description, optional\n  monospace preview), highlighting the option the user chose;\n- the result sentence becomes a tidy header → answer summary, falling back\n  to the raw text when it cannot be parsed.\n\nAdds Question/QuestionOption to the model, parseQuestions/askQuestionSummary\nto the transcript parser, and the rendering + styles, with parser and\nrender tests.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 \u003cnoreply@anthropic.com\u003e\nEOF\ngit log --oneline -1",
  "description": "Commit feature code"
}
8ef8960 feat: render AskUserQuestion cards as readable option lists
Claude223 tok · ~$0.09
Bashgit add docs/session-report && git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
{
  "command": "git add docs/session-report \u0026\u0026 git commit -q -F - \u003c\u003c'EOF'\ndocs: regenerate demo report with AskUserQuestion cards\n\nRegenerated from session 41f7e8f4 (--include-subagents --usage) so the\ndemo shows the new readable AskUserQuestion option-list rendering.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 \u003cnoreply@anthropic.com\u003e\nEOF\ngit log --oneline -3",
  "description": "Commit regenerated demo"
}
a276e0d docs: regenerate demo report with AskUserQuestion cards
8ef8960 feat: render AskUserQuestion cards as readable option lists
dda7ee0 docs: regenerate demo report with account-name scrubbing

Claude881 tok · ~$0.10

Now open the PR:

Claude
Bashgh pr create --title "Render AskUserQuestion cards as readable option lists" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
{
  "command": "gh pr create --title \"Render AskUserQuestion cards as readable option lists\" --body \"$(cat \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n## Summary\n\n`AskUserQuestion` tool cards previously dumped their raw JSON input and the verbatim `Your questions have been answered…` result sentence. This makes them first-class, like the earlier Agent/Skill card work:\n\n- **Header in the title** — the collapsed card summary now shows the question `header`(s), joined with `·` for multi-question calls (e.g. `--project alone · Root filter scope`).\n- **Options as a list** — each question renders a header chip, the question text (markdown), and a card per option: **label**, markdown description, and the optional monospace `preview` block. The option the user picked is highlighted (accent border + ✓).\n- **Answers summary** — the result sentence is parsed into a tidy header → answer list, with a graceful fallback to the raw monospace block when a result can't be parsed.\n\n## Changes\n\n- `internal/model/model.go` — `Question`/`QuestionOption` types, `ToolCall.Questions`, `IsAskUserQuestion()`.\n- `internal/transcript/parse.go` — `parseQuestions`, `askQuestionSummary`, wired into `buildToolCall`/`toolSummary`.\n- `internal/render/render.go` — `renderQuestions`, `renderAnswers`, `answerFor`, `optionChosen`, plus two `renderTool` branches.\n- `internal/render/assets/styles.css` — theme-aware `.ask*` styles.\n- Tests in `render_test.go` (rendering + fallback) and `parse_test.go` (summary + structured parse).\n- `docs/session-report/` regenerated from session 41f7e8f4 to demo the new rendering.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `gofmt -l .`, `go vet ./...`, `go test ./...` all clean.\n- End-to-end render of the real session: 13 cards, headers land in titles (incl. multi-question joins), chosen answers extract correctly (incl. multi-select), previews render, and no raw JSON/answer sentence remains for these cards.\n- Demo redaction re-checked: no bare account-name leaks; `[user]` remains the documented out-of-scope module-path identifier.\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\" 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -3",
  "description": "Create the pull request"
}
https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/pull/9

  "description": "Request Copilot review"
}

{
  "command": "for i in $(seq 1 20); do\n  n=$(gh api repos/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/pulls/9/reviews --jq '[.[]|select(.user.login==\"Copilot\")]|length' 2\u003e/dev/null)\n  if [ \"$n\" != \"0\" ] \u0026\u0026 [ -n \"$n\" ]; then echo \"Copilot reviews present: $n\"; break; fi\n  echo \"waiting ($i)... reviews by copilot: ${n:-0}\"; sleep 15\ndone",

  "timeout": 360000
}

=== reviews (all) ===
{"state":"COMMENTED","submitted":"2026-07-12T21:17:20Z","user":"copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]"}
=== still-requested reviewers ===
=== inline comments by Copilot ===
{"body":"Option selection is currently determined by scanning the entire result string for `=\"\u003clabel\u003e\"`, which can incorrectly mark options as selected in *other* questions when multiple questions share the same label (e.g. common \"Yes\" / \"No\" choices). Selection should be computed per-question using the parsed answer for that question prompt when available, falling back to the broad scan only if the answer can't be parsed.","line":429,"path":"internal/render/render.go"}
Claude5k tok · ~$0.20

Copilot found one valid correctness issue: optionChosen scans the whole result string, so shared option labels (e.g. "Yes"/"No") across questions can cross-mark. Let me first understand the real data — the demo showed 21 selections across 17 questions, so I need to know if those extras are genuine multi-select or false positives:

Claude
Bashcd ~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done && python3 - <<'PY'
{
  "command": "cd ~/.claude/projects/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done \u0026\u0026 python3 - \u003c\u003c'PY'\nimport json\n# find AskUserQuestion calls, check for shared labels across questions in same call, and multiSelect\nfor line in open(\"41f7e8f4-aaf3-4cc6-b237-7184bd309128.jsonl\"):\n    try: r=json.loads(line)\n    except: continue\n    msg=r.get(\"message\")\n    if not isinstance(msg,dict): continue\n    c=msg.get(\"content\")\n    if not isinstance(c,list): continue\n    for b in c:\n        if isinstance(b,dict) and b.get(\"type\")==\"tool_use\" and b.get(\"name\")==\"AskUserQuestion\":\n            qs=b.get(\"input\",{}).get(\"questions\",[])\n            labels=[]\n            for q in qs:\n                for o in q.get(\"options\",[]):\n                    labels.append(o.get(\"label\"))\n                if q.get(\"multiSelect\"):\n                    print(\"MULTISELECT question header:\",q.get(\"header\"))\n            dupes=set(x for x in labels if labels.count(x)\u003e1)\n            if dupes:\n                print(\"SHARED LABELS across questions in one call:\",dupes)\nPY",
  "description": "Check for multiSelect and shared labels in real data"
}
Shell cwd was reset to ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
Claude1k tok · ~$0.15
BashSCR=/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done/41f7e8f4-aaf3-4cc6-b237-7184bd309128/scratchpad; python3 - <<PY
{
  "command": "SCR=/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done/41f7e8f4-aaf3-4cc6-b237-7184bd309128/scratchpad; python3 - \u003c\u003cPY\nimport re\nhtml=open(\"$SCR/out41/index.html\").read()\n# split into ask cards\ncards=re.split(r'(?=\u003cdiv class=\"ask\"\u003e)', html)\ntotal_sel=0; total_q=0\nfor ci,card in enumerate(cards[1:], 1):\n    card=card.split('\u003c/div\u003e\u003c/details\u003e')[0]\n    qs=card.count('class=\"ask-q\"')\n    sels=re.findall(r'ask-option-selected\"\u003e.*?\u003cdiv class=\"ask-option-label\"\u003e([^\u003c]*)\u003c', card)\n    total_q+=qs; total_sel+=len(sels)\n    if len(sels)!=qs:\n        print(f\"card{ci}: {qs} questions, {len(sels)} selected -\u003e {sels}\")\nprint(\"TOTAL questions:\",total_q,\"selected:\",total_sel)\nPY",
  "description": "Find cards where selected count differs from question count"
}
TOTAL questions: 17 selected: 17
Claude2k tok · ~$0.14

Clarified: the demo actually has exactly 17/17 (my earlier grep -oc miscounted), and the real data has no multi-select or shared labels — but Copilot's finding is a valid latent bug for multi-question calls with shared labels. I'll fix it per their guidance: prefer the per-question parsed answer, fall back to the broad scan only when unparsed.

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go
- for _, q := range qs {
- b.WriteString(`<div class="ask-q">`)
- if q.Header != "" {
- b.WriteString(`<span class="ask-header">` + html.EscapeString(q.Header) + `</span>`)
- }
- if q.Prompt != "" {
- b.WriteString(`<div class="ask-prompt">` + string(Markdown(q.Prompt)) + `</div>`)
- }
- b.WriteString(`<ul class="ask-options">`)
- for _, o := range q.Options {
- cls := "ask-option"
- if optionChosen(result, o.Label) {
- cls += " ask-option-selected"
- }
+ for _, q := range qs {
+ // Resolve the answer for THIS question up front so option selection is
+ // scoped per-question — otherwise a label shared with another question in
+ // the same call could be cross-marked.
+ answer := answerFor(result, q.Prompt)
+ b.WriteString(`<div class="ask-q">`)
+ if q.Header != "" {
+ b.WriteString(`<span class="ask-header">` + html.EscapeString(q.Header) + `</span>`)
+ }
+ if q.Prompt != "" {
+ b.WriteString(`<div class="ask-prompt">` + string(Markdown(q.Prompt)) + `</div>`)
+ }
+ b.WriteString(`<ul class="ask-options">`)
+ for _, o := range q.Options {
+ cls := "ask-option"
+ if optionChosen(o.Label, answer, result) {
+ cls += " ask-option-selected"
+ }

Claude486 tok · ~$0.12
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go
- // optionChosen reports whether an option label appears as a chosen answer in an
- // AskUserQuestion result string, which encodes answers as `="<label>"`.
- func optionChosen(result, label string) bool {
- return label != "" && strings.Contains(result, `="`+label+`"`)
- }
+ // optionChosen reports whether an option was chosen for its question. It prefers
+ // the answer parsed for that specific question — so a label shared between two
+ // questions in the same call is not cross-marked — and only falls back to
+ // scanning the whole result string when that question's answer could not be
+ // parsed. The answer may be a ", "-joined list for multi-select questions.
+ func optionChosen(label, answer, result string) bool {
+ if label == "" {
+ return false
+ }
+ if answer != "" {
+ for _, part := range strings.Split(answer, ", ") {
+ if strings.TrimSpace(part) == label {
+ return true
+ }
+ }
+ return false
+ }
+ return strings.Contains(result, `="`+label+`"`)
+ }

Claude907 tok · ~$0.11

Now add a regression test for the cross-question shared-label case:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render_test.go
- func TestAskUserQuestionFallsBackToRawResultWhenUnparsed(t *testing.T) {
+ func TestAskUserQuestionSelectionIsScopedPerQuestion(t *testing.T) {
+ // Two questions share the option labels "Yes"/"No". The result picks "Yes"
+ // for the first and "No" for the second; each question must mark only its
+ // own answer, not the label shared with the other question.
+ tc := &model.ToolCall{
+ Name: "AskUserQuestion",
+ Questions: []model.Question{
+ {Header: "Cache", Prompt: "Enable cache?", Options: []model.QuestionOption{{Label: "Yes"}, {Label: "No"}}},
+ {Header: "Verbose", Prompt: "Verbose logs?", Options: []model.QuestionOption{{Label: "Yes"}, {Label: "No"}}},
+ },
+ Result: &model.ToolResult{Content: `Your questions have been answered: "Enable cache?"="Yes", "Verbose logs?"="No". Continue.`},
+ }
+ turn := model.Turn{Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc}}}
+ out := string(renderTurnBody(turn, newAgentLinks(nil, "")))
+
+ // Exactly two options selected across the whole card (one per question), not
+ // four (which the old whole-result scan would have produced).
+ if n := strings.Count(out, "ask-option-selected"); n != 2 {
+ t.Errorf("expected exactly 2 selected options (one per question), got %d: %q", n, out)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestAskUserQuestionFallsBackToRawResultWhenUnparsed(t *testing.T) {

{
  "command": "git add internal/ \u0026\u0026 git commit -q -F - \u003c\u003c'EOF'\nfix: scope AskUserQuestion option selection per question\n\noptionChosen scanned the whole result string for `=\"\u003clabel\u003e\"`, which could\ncross-mark options when two questions in one call share an option label\n(e.g. Yes/No). Resolve each question's answer via answerFor first and match\noptions against that, falling back to the broad scan only when the answer\ncannot be parsed. Handles \", \"-joined multi-select answers too.\n\nAddresses Copilot review feedback on PR #9; adds a regression test.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 \u003cnoreply@anthropic.com\u003e\nEOF\ngit add docs/session-report \u0026\u0026 git commit -q -F - \u003c\u003c'EOF'\ndocs: regenerate demo report\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 \u003cnoreply@anthropic.com\u003e\nEOF\ngit push 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -3",
  "description": "Commit fix and demo, push"
}

{"body":"`answerFor` builds a marker string that does not match the documented AskUserQuestion result format `\"\u003cprompt\u003e\"=\"\u003canswer\u003e\"` (it currently produces `\"\u003cprompt\u003e=`). As a result, the prompt is never found and the function returns an empty answer, which breaks option highlighting and the rendered answers summary.","created":"2026-07-12T21:24:03Z","line":512,"path":"internal/render/render.go"}
{"body":"Local variable name `any` shadows Go's predeclared identifier `any` (alias for `interface{}`), which can be confusing when scanning the code. Renaming it to something like `hasAny` avoids the shadowing and makes the boolean intent clearer.","created":"2026-07-12T21:24:03Z","line":473,"path":"internal/render/render.go"}

Claude1k tok · ~$0.12

Two round-2 comments. Let me assess:

  1. Line 512 (answerFor marker) — a false positive. The code is marker := "+ prompt +"="``, which produces "<prompt>"=" — exactly the documented format. Copilot misread it as "<prompt>=. The end-to-end render proves it works (all 17 answers extract correctly). I'll reject with explanation.
  2. Line 473 (any shadows builtin) — valid minor style nit. Worth fixing.

Let me fix the any shadowing:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go
- var rows strings.Builder
- any := false
- for _, q := range qs {
- ans := answerFor(result, q.Prompt)
- if ans == "" {
- continue
- }
- any = true
+ var rows strings.Builder
+ hasAny := false
+ for _, q := range qs {
+ ans := answerFor(result, q.Prompt)
+ if ans == "" {
+ continue
+ }
+ hasAny = true

Claude2k tok · ~$0.14

Round-3 finding is valid and minor: .ask-header uppercases, which mangles case-sensitive headers (--project alone → --PROJECT ALONE). Let me drop the uppercase transform:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/assets/styles.css
- .ask-header { display: inline-block; background: var(--accent-soft); color: var(--accent); font-size: .68rem; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .05em; padding: .1rem .5rem; border-radius: 999px; margin-bottom: .35rem; }
+ .ask-header { display: inline-block; background: var(--accent-soft); color: var(--accent); font-size: .72rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: .02em; padding: .1rem .5rem; border-radius: 999px; margin-bottom: .35rem; }

Claude1k tok · ~$0.12

All three Copilot review rounds are handled — I've reached the 3-round cap from the usual flow. Here's where PR #9 stands:

PR #9 — ready for your merge decision

https://github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/pull/9

Feature: AskUserQuestion cards now render as readable option lists — header(s) in the card title, each option as a card (label + markdown description + optional preview) with the chosen one highlighted, and the result sentence turned into a header → answer summary.

Copilot review rounds (3, the cap):

Round Finding Verdict Action
1 Option selection scanned the whole result → could cross-mark shared labels across questions ✅ valid Fixed — per-question resolution via answerFor, broad scan only as fallback; regression test added (3f57649/fbd0a1c)
2 answerFor marker "doesn't match the format" ❌ false positive Rejected with evidence — marker is "<prompt>"=", exactly correct; verified end-to-end
2 local any shadows the builtin ✅ valid (minor) Renamed to hasAny (71b1f19)
3 .ask-header uppercased case-sensitive headers (--project → --PROJECT) ✅ valid (minor) Removed text-transform: uppercase (3f57649)

Every reply is posted on the PR, including the reasoned rejection of the false positive.

State: gofmt/go vet/go test ./... all green; demo regenerated and redaction re-verified (no bare account-name leaks; [user] remains the documented out-of-scope module-path identifier).

Want me to merge it (and then cut a release like last time), or would you rather review/merge yourself first?

You
