This loses the existing error styling/affordance that other tool results get via `tool-result-error` and makes failed AskUserQuestion runs harder to notice.","created":"2026-07-12T21:34:28Z","id":3567167873,"line":380,"path":"internal/render/render.go"}
Claude1k tok · ~$0.13

Round 4 has one genuinely new, valid finding: the AskUserQuestion result fallback <pre> hardcodes class="tool-result" and ignores tc.Result.IsError, dropping the error affordance for failed runs. Let me fix it:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go
- if ans := renderAnswers(tc.Questions, tc.Result.Content); ans != "" {
- b.WriteString(ans)
- } else {
- b.WriteString(`<pre class="tool-result">` + html.EscapeString(StripANSI(tc.Result.Content)) + `</pre>`)
- }
+ if ans := renderAnswers(tc.Questions, tc.Result.Content); ans != "" {
+ b.WriteString(ans)
+ } else {
+ cls := "tool-result"
+ if tc.Result.IsError {
+ cls += " tool-result-error"
+ }
+ b.WriteString(`<pre class="` + cls + `">` + html.EscapeString(StripANSI(tc.Result.Content)) + `</pre>`)
+ }
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render.go has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
Claude951 tok · ~$0.13

Now add a test covering the error affordance in the fallback:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render/render_test.go
- turn := model.Turn{Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc}}}
- out := string(renderTurnBody(turn, newAgentLinks(nil, "")))
- if !strings.Contains(out, `class="tool-result"`) || !strings.Contains(out, "some unexpected result text") {
- t.Errorf("unparseable result should fall back to the raw monospace block: %q", out)
- }
- }
+ turn := model.Turn{Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc}}}
+ out := string(renderTurnBody(turn, newAgentLinks(nil, "")))
+ if !strings.Contains(out, `class="tool-result"`) || !strings.Contains(out, "some unexpected result text") {
+ t.Errorf("unparseable result should fall back to the raw monospace block: %q", out)
+ }
+ }
+
+ func TestAskUserQuestionErrorFallbackKeepsErrorAffordance(t *testing.T) {
+ // An errored result that also can't be parsed must keep the error affordance.
+ tc := &model.ToolCall{
+ Name: "AskUserQuestion",
+ Questions: []model.Question{{Header: "Scope", Prompt: "What scope?", Options: []model.QuestionOption{{Label: "Small"}}}},
+ Result: &model.ToolResult{Content: "interrupted by user", IsError: true},
+ }
+ turn := model.Turn{Kind: model.TurnAssistant, Blocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: tc}}}
+ out := string(renderTurnBody(turn, newAgentLinks(nil, "")))
+ if !strings.Contains(out, `class="tool-result tool-result-error"`) {
+ t.Errorf("errored AskUserQuestion fallback should keep the tool-result-error affordance: %q", out)
+ }
+ }

3567171785
Claude550 tok · ~$0.11

Done — the round-4 finding is fixed and pushed (f8add44), and I've replied on the PR.

Finding (valid): the AskUserQuestion result fallback <pre> (used only when the result can't be parsed into answers) hardcoded class="tool-result" and ignored tc.Result.IsError, so a failed/interrupted prompt lost the error affordance every other tool result gets.

Fix: the fallback now appends tool-result-error when the result is an error, consistent with the agent/generic result branches. Added TestAskUserQuestionErrorFallbackKeepsErrorAffordance to lock it in.

gofmt/go vet/go test ./... all green; demo regenerated and redaction re-verified clean.

We're now past the usual 3-round cap (this was round 4), all findings addressed:

  • R1: per-question selection scoping — fixed
  • R2: any shadowing — fixed · answerFor "bug" — false positive, rejected with evidence
  • R3: header uppercase — fixed
  • R4: error affordance in fallback — fixed

Want me to merge PR #9 (and cut a release as before), keep waiting for another Copilot pass, or hold for you to review?

You

merge it and cut a release

  • Published (not draft/prerelease), GoReleaser workflow green — only the usual non-blocking Node 20 deprecation warning.
  • All 7 assets attached: darwin/linux/windows × amd64/arm64 + checksums.txt.
go install github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid@v0.3.2

End to end: AskUserQuestion cards now render as readable option lists (header in the title, options with descriptions/previews, chosen answer highlighted, and a clean header → answer summary) — merged after 4 Copilot rounds (all valid findings fixed, one false positive rejected with evidence) and shipped as v0.3.2.

The only lingering optional item remains the [user] module-path scrub, still available whenever you want it.

You

Let's tackle this lingering module-path scrub, before you or the reviewer brings it up for the 23rd time. What do you suggest how to solve it?

Claude4k tok · ~$0.27

Here's the situation, grounded in what I just checked:

  • The leak is the concatenated form [user] (the stale github.com/[user]/ccwhid module path, ~37×). The current account-name scrub is \bmarkus\b — the word boundary deliberately stops it from matching inside [user], which is why it survives.
  • The real fix is that the redactor only knows one PII token (the account name, [user], from $HOME). It doesn't know the full name, which is the thing that leaks into module paths, commit trailers, and git log output. On this machine both os/user (GECOS) and git config user.name cleanly yield [user] — so we have a dependency-light source that parallels how homeDir is already passed in.

What I suggest

Add full-name scrubbing to internal/redact as a second known-PII source alongside homeDir, not a fuzzy heuristic:

  1. The CLI resolves the display name (os/user.Current().Name, with a --redact-name override) and passes it in — I'd refactor redact.Session(s, homeDir) → redact.Session(s, redact.Config{HomeDir, UserName}) (only 2 call sites).
  2. From "[user]" derive a small set of literal forms and replace each with [user]:
    • verbatim [user]
    • [user], [user], [user], [user]
    • → github.com/[user]/ccwhid becomes github.com/[user]/ccwhid
  3. Guards against over-redaction (this is what makes it safe as a general feature): only multi-part concatenations (inherently long/distinctive, ~zero collision) and the verbatim spaced name; each token ≥4 chars; same system-name denylist as accountName. The first name is already covered by the existing \bmarkus\b pass.

Why this and not the alternatives: dropping the \b (substring-matching [user]) would mangle unrelated words for short account names — it's unsafe as a general default. Generic GitHub-username scrubbing is out (it'd destroy the legitimate github.com/saigyo/... refs). Scrubbing the specific known identity threads that needle. Honest caveat: the name is already public via commit authorship, so this is defense-in-depth — but it stops the tool's own demo from undercutting its redaction claims, and it generalizes to every user's reports.

Two genuine knobs are yours to pick before I build it:

Claude
AskUserQuestionDefault · Match scope
Default

How should full-name redaction be activated?

  • Auto, on by default

    Derive the name from the OS/git identity automatically (like homeDir already is), with --redact-name to override and --no-redact-name to disable. Zero-config: it just works when you regenerate the demo. Consistent with account-name scrubbing being on by default.

  • Opt-in via --redact-name

    Off unless you explicitly pass a name. Most conservative — no chance of surprising over-redaction from an auto-detected name — but you must remember the flag, and CI/other environments won't scrub unless told.

Match scope

How aggressively should the name be matched?

  • Path/module forms + full name

    Scrub [user], [user], [user], and the verbatim '[user]'. Kills the module-path leak with essentially no over-redaction risk. Leaves the standalone surname in prose/emails alone.

  • Also standalone surname

    Additionally scrub 'Ackermann' on its own — catches it in prose and inside emails (acm.ackermann@… → acm.[user]@…), but higher chance of mangling legitimate text that happens to contain the surname.

answered
  • DefaultAuto, on by default
  • Match scopePath/module forms + full name

{

1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"os"
6		"os/exec"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"runtime"
9	
10		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/discovery"
11		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/redact"
12		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/render"
13		"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/transcript"
14	)
15	
16	func resolveOutDir(opts *options, si discovery.SessionInfo) string {
17		if opts.out != "" {
18			return opts.out
19		}
20		short := si.ID
21		if len(short) > 8 {
22			short = short[:8]
23		}
24		return filepath.Join("ccwhid-report", short)
25	}
26	
27	// resolveSession picks a session from flags. It returns needTUI=true when the
28	// user gave no selector.
29	func resolveSession(opts *options, root string) (discovery.SessionInfo, bool, error) {
30		switch {
31		case opts.session != "":
32			si, err := discovery.FindSession(root, opts.session)
33			return si, false, err
34		case opts.latest:
35			return latestSession(opts, root)
36		default:
37			return discovery.SessionInfo{}, true, nil
38		}
39	}
40	
41	// latestSession returns the most recent interactive (non-agent) session,
42	// optionally scoped to a project. Agent-spawned transcripts are skipped so
43	// --latest targets the user's own sessions.
44	func latestSession(opts *options, root string) (discovery.SessionInfo, bool, error) {
45		groups, err := discovery.Scan(root)
46		if err != nil {
47			return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, err
48		}
49		if opts.project != "" {
50			idx, err := discovery.FindProject(groups, opts.project)
51			if err != nil {
52				return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, err
53			}
54			groups = groups[idx : idx+1]
55		}
56		// Pick the newest interactive session by modification time. Group order
57		// alone is insufficient: groups are sorted by their newest session
58		// *including* agent transcripts, so a recent agent run in one project
59		// could otherwise mask a newer human session in another.
60		bestGI, bestSI := -1, -1
61		for gi := range groups {
62			for si := range groups[gi].Sessions {
63				if groups[gi].Sessions[si].IsAgent {
64					continue
65				}
66				if bestGI < 0 || groups[gi].Sessions[si].ModTime.After(groups[bestGI].Sessions[bestSI].ModTime) {
67					bestGI, bestSI = gi, si
68				}
69			}
70		}
71		if bestGI < 0 {
72			return discovery.SessionInfo{}, false, fmt.Errorf("no sessions found")
73		}
74		return groups[bestGI].Sessions[bestSI], false, nil
75	}
76	
77	// generate parses, redacts, and renders a session, returning the output dir.
78	func generate(opts *options, si discovery.SessionInfo) (string, error) {
79		outDir := resolveOutDir(opts, si)
80		if err := ensureOutDir(outDir, opts.force); err != nil {
81			return "", err
82		}
83		sess, err := transcript.ParseFile(si.FilePath, transcript.Options{
84			IncludeSubagents: opts.includeSubagents,
85		})
86		if err != nil {
87			return "", err
88		}
89		if si.ID != "" {
90			sess.ID = si.ID
91		}
92		if sess.SkippedLines > 0 {
93			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "warning: skipped %d malformed line(s)\n", sess.SkippedLines)
94		}
95		if opts.includeSubagents {
96			agents, err := transcript.LoadAgentSessions(si.FilePath, transcript.Options{
97				IncludeSubagents: opts.includeSubagents,
98			})
99			if err != nil {
100				return "", err
101			}
102			sess.Agents = agents
103		}
104		if !opts.noRedact {
105			home, _ := os.UserHomeDir()
106			redact.Session(&sess, home)
107		}
108		if err := render.Site(sess, outDir, render.Options{Title: opts.title, Usage: opts.usage}); err != nil {
109			return "", err
110		}
111		return outDir, nil
112	}
113	
114	func ensureOutDir(dir string, force bool) error {
115		entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
116		if err != nil {
117			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
118				return os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755)
119			}
120			return err
121		}
122		if len(entries) == 0 {
123			return nil
124		}
125		if !force {
126			return fmt.Errorf("output directory %q is not empty (use --force to overwrite)", dir)
127		}
128		// --force: clear existing entries so the report reflects only this run and
129		// no stale files (e.g. renamed assets) are left behind.
130		for _, e := range entries {

{

16	
17	// options holds all CLI flag values.
18	type options struct {
19		session          string
20		project          string
21		latest           bool
22		out              string
23		title            string
24		includeSubagents bool
25		noRedact         bool
26		force            bool
27		open             bool
28		usage            bool
29	}
30	
31	func newRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
32		opts := &options{}
33		cmd := &cobra.Command{
34			Use:   "ccwhid",
35			Short: "Turn a Claude Code session transcript into a browsable HTML report",
36			Long: "ccwhid (cc-what-have-i-done) renders a Claude Code session " +
37				"transcript into a self-contained static HTML report. Run with no " +
38				"flags to browse sessions in an interactive TUI.",
39			Version:      version,
40			SilenceUsage: true,
41			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
42				return run(cmd, opts)
43			},
44		}
45		f := cmd.Flags()
46		f.StringVar(&opts.session, "session", "", "session id or unambiguous prefix to render")
47		f.StringVar(&opts.project, "project", "", "project path or name; scopes --latest, or opens the TUI focused on it")
48		f.BoolVar(&opts.latest, "latest", false, "render the most recent interactive session (skips agent transcripts)")
49		f.StringVar(&opts.out, "out", "", "output directory (default ./ccwhid-report/<session-short>)")
50		f.StringVar(&opts.title, "title", "", "override the report title")
51		f.BoolVar(&opts.includeSubagents, "include-subagents", true, "include subagent work: inline Task sidechains and linked agent-session pages")
52		f.BoolVar(&opts.noRedact, "no-redact", false, "disable secret redaction")
53		f.BoolVar(&opts.force, "force", false, "overwrite a non-empty output directory")
54		f.BoolVar(&opts.open, "open", false, "open the report in a browser when done")
55		f.BoolVar(&opts.usage, "usage", false, "include a token-usage & estimated-cost section")
56		return cmd
57	}
58	
59	func run(cmd *cobra.Command, opts *options) error {
60		root, err := discovery.DefaultRoot()
61		if err != nil {
62			return err
63		}
64		si, needTUI, err := resolveSession(opts, root)
65		if err != nil {
66			return err
67		}
68		if needTUI {
69			groups, err := discovery.Scan(root)
70			if err != nil {
71				return err
72			}
73			focusIdx := -1
74			if opts.project != "" {
75				focusIdx, err = discovery.FindProject(groups, opts.project)
76				if err != nil {
77					return err
78				}
79			}
80			sel, err := tui.Run(groups, focusIdx)
81			if err != nil {
82				return err
83			}
84			if sel.Canceled {
85				fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "canceled")
86				return nil
87			}
88			si = sel.Session
89			opts.includeSubagents = sel.IncludeSubagents
90			opts.noRedact = !sel.Redact

Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/redact/redact.go
- // Redactor applies redaction rules, a home-directory rewrite, and account-name
- // heuristics.
- type Redactor struct {
- homeDir string
- userRe *regexp.Regexp // standalone mentions of our own account name; nil when unknown/unsafe
- }
-
- func New(homeDir string) *Redactor {
- r := &Redactor{homeDir: homeDir}
- if name := accountName(homeDir); name != "" {
- r.userRe = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)\b` + regexp.QuoteMeta(name) + `\b`)
- }
- return r
- }
+ // Config holds the known identity a Redactor scrubs. Both fields are optional;
+ // an empty field disables the corresponding rule.
+ type Config struct {
+ HomeDir string // the user's home directory, rewritten to ~ and mined for the account name
+ UserName string // the user's display name, e.g. "[user]"; its path/module forms are scrubbed
+ }
+
+ // Redactor applies redaction rules, a home-directory rewrite, and account-name
+ // and display-name heuristics.
+ type Redactor struct {
+ homeDir string
+ userRe *regexp.Regexp // standalone mentions of our own account name; nil when unknown/unsafe
+ nameRe *regexp.Regexp // path/module/verbatim forms of the display name; nil when unknown/unsafe
+ }
+
+ func New(cfg Config) *Redactor {
+ r := &Redactor{homeDir: cfg.HomeDir}
+ if name := accountName(cfg.HomeDir); name != "" {
+ r.userRe = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)\b` + regexp.QuoteMeta(name) + `\b`)
+ }
+ if forms := nameForms(cfg.UserName); len(forms) > 0 {
+ // Match longer, more-specific forms first so "[user]" wins over
+ // any shorter overlap.
+ sort.Slice(forms, func(i, j int) bool { return len(forms[i]) > len(forms[j]) })
+ alts := make([]string, len(forms))
+ for i, f := range forms {
+ alts[i] = regexp.QuoteMeta(f)
+ }
+ r.nameRe = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)\b(?:` + strings.Join(alts, "|") + `)\b`)
+ }
+ return r
+ }
+
+ // nameForms derives the literal strings to scrub from a display name: the
+ // verbatim name plus its concatenated and separator-joined lowercase forms
+ // (module paths, usernames). It returns nil for single-token names — those are
+ // already covered by the account-name rule, and scrubbing a lone word (which
+ // could be a common noun or company name) risks mangling unrelated text.
+ func nameForms(full string) []string {
+ parts := strings.Fields(full)
+ if len(parts) < 2 {
+ return nil
+ }
+ lower := make([]string, len(parts))
+ for i, p := range parts {
+ lower[i] = strings.ToLower(p)
+ }
+ candidates := []string{
+ strings.Join(parts, " "), // "[user]"
+ strings.Join(lower, ""), // [user]
+ strings.Join(lower, "-"), // [user]
+ strings.Join(lower, "."), // [user]
+ strings.Join(lower, "_"), // [user]
+ }
+ var forms []string
+ seen := map[string]bool{}
+ for _, c := range candidates {
+ if len(c) < 4 || seen[c] {
+ continue
+ }
+ seen[c] = true
+ forms = append(forms, c)
+ }
+ return forms
+ }

+ home, _ := os.UserHomeDir()

Claude473 tok · ~$0.13
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/cmd/ccwhid/run.go
- func ensureOutDir(dir string, force bool) error {
+ // redactUserName resolves the display name to scrub from the report. An explicit
+ // --redact-name wins; otherwise it falls back to the OS account's display name
+ // and then to git's configured user.name. Returns "" when name redaction is
+ // disabled or no name can be found.
+ func redactUserName(opts *options) string {
+ if opts.noRedactName {
+ return ""
+ }
+ if opts.redactName != "" {
+ return opts.redactName
+ }
+ if u, err := user.Current(); err == nil {
+ if name := strings.TrimSpace(u.Name); name != "" {
+ return name
+ }
+ }
+ if out, err := exec.Command("git", "config", "--get", "user.name").Output(); err == nil {
+ if name := strings.TrimSpace(string(out)); name != "" {
+ return name
+ }
+ }
+ return ""
+ }
+
+ func ensureOutDir(dir string, force bool) error {

Now let me add tests for the new name-scrubbing behavior. Let me see the end of the test file for style:

Claude
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done; tail -30 internal/redact/redact_test.go
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done; tail -30 internal/redact/redact_test.go",
  "description": "Read tail of redact_test.go"
}
	if !strings.Contains(got, "[user] staff") {
		t.Errorf("expected scrubbed owner column: %q", got)
	}
}

func TestRedactKeepsHomeTilde(t *testing.T) {
	// The friendly ~ rewrite for our own home must survive.
	r := New(Config{HomeDir: "~"})
	if got := r.String("~/IdeaProjects/app"); got != "~/IdeaProjects/app" {
		t.Errorf("home tilde rewrite broken: %q", got)
	}
}

func TestAccountNameSkipsSystemAndShortNames(t *testing.T) {
	for _, h := range []string{"/root", "/home/[user]/var/empty", ""} {
		if got := accountName(h); got != "" {
			t.Errorf("accountName(%q) = %q, want empty (system/short)", h, got)
		}
	}
	if got := accountName("~"); got != "[user]" {
		t.Errorf("accountName = %q, want [user]", got)
	}
}

func TestRedactDoubledBackslashWindowsPath(t *testing.T) {
	r := New(Config{HomeDir: "~"})
	if got := r.String(`C:\\Users\\[user]\\file`); strings.Contains(got, "bob") {
		t.Errorf("doubled-backslash Windows path not scrubbed: %q", got)
	}
}

{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done; cat \u003e\u003e internal/redact/redact_test.go \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n\nfunc TestRedactFullNameModulePathForms(t *testing.T) {\n\tr := New(Config{HomeDir: \"~\", UserName: \"[user]\"})\n\tcases := []struct{ in, notWant string }{\n\t\t{\"import github.com/[user]/ccwhid/internal/model\", \"[user]\"},\n\t\t{\"see github.com/[user]/x\", \"[user]\"},\n\t\t{\"user [user] pushed\", \"[user]\"},\n\t\t{\"Signed-off-by: [user] \u003cx@y\u003e\", \"[user]\"},\n\t}\n\tfor _, c := range cases {\n\t\tgot := r.String(c.in)\n\t\tif strings.Contains(got, c.notWant) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"String(%q) = %q, still contains %q\", c.in, got, c.notWant)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !strings.Contains(got, userPlaceholder) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"String(%q) = %q, expected %q\", c.in, got, userPlaceholder)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// The canonical module-path leak collapses to the placeholder, keeping the rest.\n\tif got := r.String(\"github.com/[user]/ccwhid\"); got != \"github.com/[user]/ccwhid\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"module path scrub = %q, want github.com/[user]/ccwhid\", got)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestRedactFullNameLeavesLegitimateRefsIntact(t *testing.T) {\n\tr := New(Config{HomeDir: \"~\", UserName: \"[user]\"})\n\t// The real module path must be untouched — only the known name is scrubbed.\n\tif got := r.String(\"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done\"); got != \"github.com/saigyo/cc-what-have-i-done\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"legitimate ref altered: %q\", got)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestRedactSingleTokenNameNotScrubbed(t *testing.T) {\n\t// A one-word display name is too risky to scrub broadly (could be a common\n\t// word) and is left to the account-name rule; nameForms returns nothing.\n\tif forms := nameForms(\"Apple\"); forms != nil {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"single-token name should yield no forms, got %v\", forms)\n\t}\n\tr := New(Config{HomeDir: \"~\", UserName: \"Apple\"})\n\tif got := r.String(\"the Apple falls\"); got != \"the Apple falls\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"single-token name over-redacted: %q\", got)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestRedactNoUserNameKeepsNameForms(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Without a configured name, compound-name forms are not scrubbed.\n\tr := New(Config{HomeDir: \"~\"})\n\tif got := r.String(\"github.com/[user]/ccwhid\"); !strings.Contains(got, \"[user]\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"no UserName should leave [user] intact: %q\", got)\n\t}\n}\nEOF\ngofmt -w internal/redact/redact_test.go internal/redact/redact.go cmd/ccwhid/run.go cmd/ccwhid/main.go \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/redact/ -run 'FullName|SingleToken|NoUserName' -v 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -20",
  "description": "Add name-scrubbing tests and run them"
}

Claude1k tok · ~$0.15

Found it — and it's a real bug worth having caught. Those two stragglers are in my AskUserQuestion option text, which means the Questions field bypasses redaction entirely. redactTool scrubs Summary, InputJSON, AgentPrompt, Result, etc. — but I never added the new Questions field (introduced in PR #9). Same class as the AgentPrompt gap Copilot caught before. Let me close it:

Claude

Now add a regression test for the Questions redaction gap, then rebuild and regenerate:

Claude
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done; cat >> internal/redact/redact_test.go <<'EOF'
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done; cat \u003e\u003e internal/redact/redact_test.go \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n\nfunc TestRedactScrubsAskUserQuestionFields(t *testing.T) {\n\t// AskUserQuestion cards render from the structured Questions, which must be\n\t// scrubbed just like every other user-visible field.\n\ts := \u0026model.Session{Turns: []model.Turn{{\n\t\tKind: model.TurnAssistant,\n\t\tBlocks: []model.Block{{Type: model.BlockToolUse, Tool: \u0026model.ToolCall{\n\t\t\tName: \"AskUserQuestion\",\n\t\t\tQuestions: []model.Question{{\n\t\t\t\tHeader: \"Path for [user]\",\n\t\t\t\tPrompt: \"Use ~/secret?\",\n\t\t\t\tOptions: []model.QuestionOption{{\n\t\t\t\t\tLabel:       \"Yes, [user]\",\n\t\t\t\t\tDescription: \"keep ~/notes\",\n\t\t\t\t\tPreview:     \"cat ~/.env\",\n\t\t\t\t}},\n\t\t\t}},\n\t\t}}},\n\t}}}\n\tSession(s, Config{HomeDir: \"~\", UserName: \"[user]\"})\n\tq := s.Turns[0].Blocks[0].Tool.Questions[0]\n\tblob := q.Header + \"\\n\" + q.Prompt + \"\\n\" + q.Options[0].Label + \"\\n\" + q.Options[0].Description + \"\\n\" + q.Options[0].Preview\n\tfor _, leak := range []string{\"[user]\", \"~\"} {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(blob, leak) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"AskUserQuestion field still leaks %q: %q\", leak, blob)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\nEOF\ngofmt -w internal/redact/redact_test.go\nSCR=/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-cc-what-have-i-done/41f7e8f4-aaf3-4cc6-b237-7184bd309128/scratchpad\ngo test ./internal/redact/ -run AskUserQuestion -v 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -4\ngo build -o \"$SCR/ccwhid\" ./cmd/ccwhid \u0026\u0026 \"$SCR/ccwhid\" --session 41f7e8f4 --include-subagents --usage --out docs/session-report --force 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -1\necho \"=== [user] in demo (want 0) ===\"; grep -roc \"[user]\" docs/session-report/ | grep -v ':0'; echo \"(no output above = 0)\"\necho \"=== '[user]' in demo (want 0) ===\"; grep -rc \"[user]\" docs/session-report/ | grep -v ':0'; echo \"(no output above = 0)\"",
  "description": "Add test, rebuild, regenerate, verify zero leaks"
}

{

78	| `--session <id>` | Session id or unambiguous prefix to render |
79	| `--project <path\|name>` | Scope `--latest` to a project, or open the TUI on it (matches full path, basename, or unambiguous substring) |
80	| `--latest` | Render the most recent interactive session (skips agent transcripts) |
81	| `--out <dir>` | Output directory |
82	| `--title <str>` | Override the report title |
83	| `--include-subagents` | Include subagent work: inline Task sidechains and linked agent-session transcript pages under `subagents/` (default true; `--include-subagents=false` to omit) |
84	| `--no-redact` | Disable secret redaction |
85	| `--force` | Overwrite a non-empty output directory |
86	| `--open` | Open the report in a browser when done |
87	| `--usage` | Include a token-usage & estimated-cost section (default off) |
88	
89	## Redaction
90	
91	By default, ccwhid scrubs common secret shapes (AWS keys, API tokens, JWTs,
92	`KEY=[REDACTED:assignment] assignments) and rewrites your home directory to `~`.
93	It also scrubs account names from home-directory paths in any form — plain
94	(`/Users/[user]/home/[user]\Users\[user]-encoded project dirs (`-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-…`) — plus standalone
96	mentions of your own login name (e.g. the owner column of `ls -l` output),
97	replacing them with `[user]`. This is best-effort defense-in-depth — **review
98	generated reports before committing them** (a real name embedded in a URL or
99	module path, for instance, is out of scope). Disable with `--no-redact`.
Claude292 tok · ~$0.13
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md
- | `--no-redact` | Disable secret redaction |
- | `--force` | Overwrite a non-empty output directory |
+ | `--no-redact` | Disable secret redaction |
+ | `--redact-name <str>` | Display name to scrub from output (default: your OS/git display name) |
+ | `--no-redact-name` | Disable display-name redaction (account/path redaction still applies) |
+ | `--force` | Overwrite a non-empty output directory |

Claude603 tok · ~$0.13
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md
- dash-encoded project dirs (`-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-…`) — plus standalone
- mentions of your own login name (e.g. the owner column of `ls -l` output),
- replacing them with `[user]`. This is best-effort defense-in-depth — **review
- generated reports before committing them** (a real name embedded in a URL or
- module path, for instance, is out of scope). Disable with `--no-redact`.
+ dash-encoded project dirs (`-Users-[user]-IdeaProjects-…`) — plus standalone
+ mentions of your own login name (e.g. the owner column of `ls -l` output),
+ replacing them with `[user]`.
+
+ It also scrubs your **display name** (resolved from your OS account, or
+ `git config user.name`, or `--redact-name`) in the forms that leak into module
+ paths, usernames, and commit trailers — the verbatim name plus its concatenated
+ and separator-joined variants (`Jane Doe`, `janedoe`, `jane-doe`, `jane.doe`),
+ so `github.com/janedoe/project` becomes `github.com/[user]/project`. Only
+ multi-word names are matched, and only the known name — generic GitHub usernames
+ are left intact. Turn this off with `--no-redact-name`.
+
+ This is best-effort defense-in-depth — **review generated reports before
+ committing them.** Disable all redaction with `--no-redact`.
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/README.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

Now commit the code + README, then the demo, and push:

Claude
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ngit add internal/redact/redact.go internal/redact/redact_test.go cmd/ccwhid/main.go cmd/ccwhid/run.go internal/render/render_e2e_test.go README.md\ngit commit -q -F - \u003c\u003c'EOF'\nfeat(redact): scrub the user's display name from reports\n\nThe redactor knew only the account name (from $HOME), whose word-boundary\nrule deliberately misses the name when it is concatenated into a Go module\npath or username — so \"github.com/[user]/ccwhid\" leaked through.\n\nTeach the redactor a second known-PII source: the display name, resolved by\nthe CLI from --redact-name, else the OS account, else `git config user.name`,\nand passed in via the new redact.Config. From it we derive literal forms to\nscrub — the verbatim name plus concatenated/separator-joined lowercase\nvariants (janedoe, jane-doe, jane.doe, jane_doe) — so the module path becomes\n\"github.com/[user]/project\". Guards keep it safe: only multi-word names are\nmatched (a lone word could be a common noun/company), each form is \u003e= 4 chars,\nand only the known name is touched, so generic usernames and the legitimate\ngithub.com/saigyo/... refs stay intact. On by default; --no-redact-name opts\nout, --redact-name overrides the detected name.\n\nAlso close a redaction gap the AskUserQuestion feature introduced: cards\nrender from the structured Questions field, which redactTool did not scrub,\nso question/option text bypassed redaction. Scrub every Questions field too.\n\nredact.New/Session now take a redact.Config; call sites and tests updated.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 \u003cnoreply@anthropic.com\u003e\nEOF\ngit add docs/session-report \u0026\u0026 git commit -q -F - \u003c\u003c'EOF'\ndocs: regenerate demo report with display-name redaction\n\nThe stale github.com/[user]/ccwhid module path and the verbatim\nname are now scrubbed to [user]; legitimate github.com/saigyo refs are intact.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 \u003cnoreply@anthropic.com\u003e\nEOF\ngit push -u origin feature/redact-full-name 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -3",
  "description": "Commit code + demo and push branch"
}

Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done\ngh pr create --title \"Redact the user's display name from reports\" --body \"$(cat \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n## Summary\n\nCloses the long-standing gap where the user's real name leaked through redaction as a concatenated Go module path (`github.com/[user]/ccwhid`). The account-name rule (`\\bmarkus\\b`) deliberately can't match inside `[user]`, so the name survived.\n\n**Fix:** teach the redactor a second known-PII source — the **display name** — alongside `$HOME`:\n\n- The CLI resolves it from `--redact-name`, else the OS account display name, else `git config user.name`, and passes it via the new `redact.Config`.\n- From `\"Jane Doe\"` we derive literal scrub forms: the verbatim name plus concatenated/separator-joined lowercase variants (`janedoe`, `jane-doe`, `jane.doe`, `jane_doe`) → `github.com/janedoe/x` becomes `github.com/[user]/x`.\n- **Guards** keep it safe as a default: only multi-word names are matched (a lone word could be a common noun/company), each form ≥4 chars, and only the *known* name is scrubbed — generic GitHub usernames and the legit `github.com/saigyo/…` refs stay intact.\n- On by default; `--no-redact-name` opts out, `--redact-name` overrides the detected name.\n\n**Also fixes a redaction gap from PR #9:** AskUserQuestion cards render from the structured `Questions` field, which `redactTool` never scrubbed — so question/option text bypassed redaction. Now every `Questions` field is scrubbed (this is what still leaked the name in the demo until fixed).\n\n## Changes\n\n- `internal/redact/redact.go` — `Config` struct, `nameForms`, display-name regex, `Questions` scrubbing; `New`/`Session` now take `redact.Config`.\n- `cmd/ccwhid/{main,run}.go` — `--redact-name`/`--no-redact-name` flags and name resolution (OS account → git config).\n- `README.md` — documents display-name redaction and the flags.\n- Tests: name-form scrubbing, single-token safety, legit-ref preservation, and the AskUserQuestion `Questions` regression; call sites migrated to `Config`.\n- `docs/session-report/` regenerated — `[user]`/`[user]` now 0 (were 37), `saigyo` refs intact.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `gofmt -l .`, `go vet ./...`, `go test ./...` all clean.\n- Demo sweep: 0 `[user]`, 0 verbatim name, 0 bare `[user]`, 0 unscrubbed `-Users-\[user]\u003e`; 458 legit `saigyo` refs intact; module path renders as `github.com/[user]/ccwhid`.\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\" 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -2",
  "description": "Open the PR"
}

{"body":"## Pull request overview\n\nThis PR closes a privacy gap in report generation by extending redaction beyond `$HOME`/account-name heuristics to also scrub the user’s **display name** (including common module-path/username forms), and by ensuring structured `AskUserQuestion` content is redacted.\n\n**Changes:**\n- Introduces `redact.Config` and updates the redactor to optionally scrub display-name–derived forms (verbatim + concatenated/separator variants) and to scrub structured `ToolCall.Questions`.\n- Adds CLI support for `--redact-name` / `--no-redact-name`, with automatic display-name discovery (OS account → git config) when enabled.\n- Updates docs, tests, and regenerates the demo session report artifacts to reflect the new redaction behavior.\n\n### Reviewed changes\n\nCopilot reviewed 27 out of 28 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eShow a summary per file\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n| File | === inline comments ===
{"body":"The display-name redaction regex uses `\\b` word boundaries, which in Go's `regexp` are ASCII-only. That can cause non-ASCII display names (e.g. accented letters) to *not* match as whole words, leaving the name unredacted in reports.\n\nConsider switching to explicit Unicode-aware boundaries (e.g. using `\\pL`/`\\pN`) and preserving the surrounding delimiter characters during replacement, since RE2 doesn't support lookarounds. Adding a test case with a non-ASCII name would help prevent regressions.","id":3567211878,"line":67,"path":"internal/redact/redact.go"}
{"body":"README: the display-name resolution order here is ambiguous and currently reads like `--redact-name` is the *last* fallback, while the implementation makes `--redact-name` take precedence. Also, the docs list separator variants but omit the underscore form (`jane_doe`) which the implementation scrubs.","id":3567211880,"line":105,"path":"README.md"}
Claude6k tok · ~$0.30

Both round-1 findings are valid:

  1. \b is ASCII-only in RE2 — a display name with accented letters at a form boundary can escape redaction. I'll switch to Unicode-aware delimiter capture ([^\pL\pN]), matching the pattern userPathRe already uses.
  2. README nits — resolution order reads backwards (--redact-name is highest precedence, not last) and omits the jane_doe form.

Let me fix the regex first:

Claude
Edit~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/redact/redact.go
- alts := make([]string, len(forms))
- for i, f := range forms {
- alts[i] = regexp.QuoteMeta(f)
- }
- r.nameRe = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)\b(?:` + strings.Join(alts, "|") + `)\b`)
+ alts := make([]string, len(forms))
+ for i, f := range forms {
+ alts[i] = regexp.QuoteMeta(f)
+ }
+ // Delimit with Unicode letter/number classes rather than \b: RE2's \b is
+ // ASCII-only, so it mishandles accented display names (e.g. a form ending
+ // in "á"). Capture the surrounding delimiters and re-emit them, since RE2
+ // has no lookarounds.
+ r.nameRe = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)(^|[^\pL\pN])(?:` + strings.Join(alts, "|") + `)($|[^\pL\pN])`)
The file ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done/internal/redact/redact.go has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

- s = r.nameRe.ReplaceAllString(s, userPlaceholder)
- }
+ if r.nameRe != nil {
+ s = r.nameRe.ReplaceAllString(s, "${1}"+userPlaceholder+"${2}")
+ }

Now add a non-ASCII regression test that genuinely exercises the boundary fix (a name ending in an accented letter), and verify:

Claude
Bashcd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done; cat >> internal/redact/redact_test.go <<'EOF'
{
  "command": "cd ~/IdeaProjects/cc-what-have-i-done; cat \u003e\u003e internal/redact/redact_test.go \u003c\u003c'EOF'\n\nfunc TestRedactFullNameUnicodeBoundaries(t *testing.T) {\n\t// A name ending in an accented letter would slip past RE2's ASCII-only \\b;\n\t// the Unicode delimiter capture must still scrub it and keep the delimiters.\n\tr := New(Config{HomeDir: \"/home/[user]\", UserName: \"Ana Peñá\"})\n\tcases := []struct{ in, want string }{\n\t\t{\"repo github.com/anapeñá/x\", \"repo github.com/[user]/x\"},\n\t\t{\"by Ana Peñá.\", \"by [user].\"},\n\t\t{\"user ana-peñá pushed\", \"user [user] pushed\"},\n\t}\n\tfor _, c := range cases {\n\t\tif got := r.String(c.in); got != c.want {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"String(%q) = %q, want %q\", c.in, got, c.want)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\nEOF\ngofmt -w internal/redact/redact_test.go internal/redact/redact.go\ngo test ./internal/redact/ -run 'FullName|Unicode|AskUserQuestion' -v 2\u003e\u00261 | grep -E \"PASS|FAIL|ok\" | head",
  "description": "Add unicode test and run redact tests"
}
